column1: [iOS] Save entire sync queue json in database on flush
column41:
h3. Summary

The issue involves the app deleting all local database data when a user switches sites, resulting in the loss of unsynced offline edits. The expected behavior is to retain local data per site and ensure unsynced data is not deleted during site switches.

h3. Context

Currently, when switching sites, the app clears all local data, which leads to unsynced edits becoming unprocessable. The goal is to improve data retention and syncing behavior across multiple sites.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* Site A's data remains intact when switching to Site B and back.
* Sync queue items from Site A are preserved after switching to Site B and synced when returning to Site A.
* Data from multiple sites coexists in the local database without leakage.
* Each site switch fetches data from the server using an upsert strategy.
* Sync queue processes only items for the active site.
* No cross-site data leakage occurs on any screen.
* The "Clear Database" option in settings clears all sites' data and the sync queue.
* Logging out clears all data.

h3. Other information

*Current Behavior:*  
When a user switches sites, the app deletes ALL local database data ({{clearAllSLDData()}} in {{SLDService.kt}}) and fetches fresh data for the new site. Any unsynced offline edits (sync queue items) lose their referenced entities, making them unprocessable and effectively lost.

*Expected Behavior:*

* Local data retained per-site, mapped by {{sldId}} — not deleted on site switch.
* Always re-fetch target site data from server on switch (upsert, not delete + insert).
* Unsynced data never deleted on site switch.
* Sync queue processes only active site's items.
* Unsynced data removed only after: successful sync, manual Clear Database, or app uninstall.

*Technical Scope:*

||Phase||Details||
|Schema (DB v24→25)|Add {{sldId}} to {{sync_queue_item}} + {{quotes}} tables|
|SyncQueueItem|Add {{sldId}} field, update all factory methods + ~15 call sites|
|SLDService rewrite|Remove {{clearAllSLDData()}} from switch flow; keep for Settings/logout only|
|Sync processing|Filter {{FlushSyncQueueUseCase}} by active sldId|
|DAO queries|Add site-scoped queries to QuoteDao, AttachmentDao, SLDViewDao, SyncQueueItemDao|
|ViewModel audit|Ensure all queries filter by active sldId|

*Key Files:* {{SLDService.kt}}, {{SyncQueueItem.kt}}, {{Quote.kt}}, {{SyncQueueItemDao.kt}}, {{QuoteDao.kt}}, {{DatabaseMigrations.kt}}, {{AppDatabase.kt}}, {{FlushSyncQueueUseCase.kt}}, {{SyncQueueForegroundService.kt}}, {{SelectSiteViewModel.kt}}, + ~15 use cases

*Testing:* Migration test, round-trip switch, sync queue preservation, active-site-only sync, no data leakage, upsert idempotency, clear database, logout.

column1: Web | Welcome email: Improve credential copy experience with triple-click select
column41:
h2. Problem Users face difficulty copying their email and password from the Welcome email. Currently, credentials are not visually isolated, making it hard to select and copy them accurately — especially on mobile devices. Since email clients do not support JavaScript, adding a "Copy" button is *not feasible*. h2. Solution Update the Welcome email (SendGrid HTML template) to make credentials *visually isolated and easy to triple-click-select*: h3. Design Changes # *Place email and password each on their own dedicated line* within a styled block (e.g., a bordered/shaded container) # *Each credential value should be on its own separate block-level element* ({{

}} or {{

}}) so that a *triple-click selects only that value* — not the label or surrounding text # Use a *monospace font* (e.g., {{Courier New}}) for the password to improve readability and distinguish it from surrounding text # Ensure the email is displayed as *plain text* (not a {{mailto:}} link) so it can be selected easily without triggering the email client h3. Add User Instruction Below the credentials box, include a short helper tip: {quote}*Tip:* Triple-click (or long-press on mobile) on the email or password to quickly select and copy it.{quote} h3. Example Layout {noformat}┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ EMAIL │ │ mukul+espohio@egalvanic.com │ ← triple-click selects only this line │ │ │ PASSWORD │ │ V2Nr0-slq1aE! │ ← triple-click selects only this line └──────────────────────────────────────────┘ 💡 Tip: Triple-click (or long-press on mobile) on the email or password to quickly select and copy it.{noformat} h3. Technical Notes * Use *inline CSS only* (SendGrid / email client compatibility) * Use *Handlebars variables* for dynamic values * Each credential value must be in its own *block-level element* (not inline with the label) to enable clean triple-click selection * Avoid {{}} for the email — render as plain text * Use {{font-family: 'Courier New', monospace}} for password for visual distinction h2. Reference See attached screenshot of the current Welcome email for context.

column1: Android | Sign-in page: Remove T&C checkbox and update consent text
column41:
h2. Context

Currently the sign-in page has a checkbox for "I agree to the Terms and Conditions and Privacy Policy" that the user must check before signing in. This adds unnecessary friction to the login flow.

h2. Requirement

* *Remove* the "I agree to the Terms and Conditions and Privacy Policy" checkbox from the sign-in page
* *Replace* with inline consent text below the Sign In button:

  > "By signing in, you agree to our [Terms and Conditions] and [Privacy Policy]."

* The "Terms and Conditions" and "Privacy Policy" links should remain clickable and open the respective pages
* No user action (checkbox tick) should be required — tapping Sign In implies acceptance

h2. Reference

See attached screenshot of the current sign-in page (Web) for context. Apply the same change on Android.

column1: Replace issue report template with updated BCES-IQ template
column41:
h2. Objective

Replace the existing issue report template with the updated reference template (attached).

h2. Details

* The new template file {{bces-iq_issue_new.docx}} is attached to this ticket
* This is a Jinja2-based DOCX template used to generate Equipment Issue Reports
* The template includes sections for:

  - Asset info (QR code, label, class, location)

  - Proposed Resolution, Status, Priority

  - Description with Immediate Hazard / Customer Notified checkboxes

  - Photos

  - Thermal Analysis (for Thermal Anomaly issues)

  - Current Draw / Voltage Drop measurements

  - Infrared Photos

  - Repair Details / Replacement Details

  - Possible Consequences, Recommendations For Repair, Corrective Actions

h2. Important Note

*Use Calibri font at all places.* If the font changes at some places during implementation, ensure it is corrected back to Calibri throughout the template.

h2. Attachments

* {{bces-iq_issue_new.docx}} — Reference template to use as replacement

column1: Quoting Price Multiplier for Additional Scopes of Work
column41:
[https://app.devrev.ai/egalvanic/works/ISS-3762|https://app.devrev.ai/egalvanic/works/ISS-3762|smart-link] 
When quoting through the Opportunities feature, we'd like to see an additional multiplier applied when multiple procedures are performed on the same asset in a single work order. Since the time associated with a procedure is an average of time arrive on set, setup, teardown, etc. the time estimate when performing multiple forms of work is often an overestimate. For example, when performing and AF Data Collection & an IR/Visual on the same asset the actual time taken is not just a sum of these two procedure timings, but more accurately expressed as:

Total Time/Asset = 0.75(AF Time + IR/Visual Time)

# Navigate to *Panel Schedule*.
# Select any existing asset and click on *Edit*.
# Go to the option to link *Existing OCP*.
# Click on *Select All Existing*.
# Observe the time taken for data to be added.

*Actual Result:*

* The system takes around *7–8 minutes* to add the data after selecting “Select All Existing”.
* In some cases, the process *fails or does not complete successfully*.

*Expected Result:*

* Data should be added *within an acceptable response time* (few seconds).
* The process should complete *successfully without failure*.
* Overall performance should be optimized for bulk selection operations.

column1: Z | Android | “Condition of Maintenance” Not Set by Default for Assets Created via Quick Count and SLD (Mobile)
column41:
*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open the mobile application
# Create an asset using *Quick Count* or from *SLD*
# Save the asset
# Check the “Condition of Maintenance” field on mobile
# Verify the same asset on the web application

*Description:*
When an asset is created using Quick Count or SLD on mobile, the “Condition of Maintenance” field remains blank (no default value is set). However, on the web application, the default value is shown as *“1 (Serviceable)”*. This creates inconsistency between mobile and web behavior.

*Expected Result:*
The “Condition of Maintenance” field should be set to *“1 (Serviceable)” by default* on mobile as well, ensuring consistency with the web application.

*Description:*

column41:
*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Navigate to the SLD section
# Create a new asset from SLD
# Save the asset
# Open the web application
# Check the created asset details

*Description:*
When an asset is created from the SLD in the mobile application, the “Condition of Maintenance” field is set to a non-serviceable value by default. This is incorrect and does not match the expected default behavior.

*Expected Result:*
The “Condition of Maintenance” field should be set to *“1” (Serviceable)* by default when an asset is created from SLD.

column1: Z| Android | Fix Language Index Handling for SLD (French ↔ English Switching)
column41:
h3. *Description*

Currently, in the SLD module, when switching the application language between French and English, only *index id 1* is being passed. Due to this, the language mapping is incorrect:

* When the app language is set to French, SLD content is displayed in English
* When the app language is set to English, SLD content is displayed in French

This indicates that the correct language index is not being passed dynamically. The system should pass the appropriate index IDs based on the selected application language to ensure proper translation.

The task is to update the implementation so that *multiple or correct index IDs are passed dynamically*, ensuring that the SLD module reflects the selected application language accurately.

column1: Z | FE | Mismatch in Issue Count and Pagination Not Working in Issue Listing
column41:
h3. *Description*

On the dashboard, the issue count is displayed as *400*, but when navigating to the Issue tab, only *25 issues* are shown in the listing. Additionally, when the page size is changed, the forward (next) button becomes disabled, and no API call is triggered. The Network tab still shows the total issue count as 400, indicating a discrepancy between the displayed data and pagination behavior.

----

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Login to the application
# Navigate to the Dashboard
# Note the total issue count (e.g., 400)
# Go to the Issue tab
# Observe the number of issues listed (e.g., 25)
# Change the page size (e.g., increase items per page)
# Click on the forward/next button

----

h3. *Actual Result*

* Only 25 issues are displayed despite a total count of 400
* Forward/next button is disabled
* No API call is triggered on pagination
* Network tab still shows total issue count as 400

----

h3. *Expected Result*

* Issue listing should match the total count (400) with proper pagination
* Changing page size should update the listing accordingly
* Forward/next button should be enabled when more records are available
* API calls should be triggered correctly on pagination actions

Sort Issues by Room - iOS
column41:
Implement the ability to sort/organize issues by room (location) in the iOS mobile app.

*Parent Story:* ZP-1397 - Sort Issues by Room

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* Users can sort issues by room/location within the iOS app
* Sorting should be accessible and intuitive in the issue list view
* Behavior should be consistent with the overall sorting functionality defined in the parent story

*Unit Testing:*

* Write unit tests to verify correct sorting logic by room/location
* Test edge cases: empty room values, duplicate room names, special characters
* Test sort order toggling (ascending/descending)
* Ensure unit tests cover data layer and ViewModel/Presenter logic
* All unit tests must pass before moving to code review
column211:
09/Apr/26 2:56 AM;712020:9881bb6e-cbf7-47a6-bf7a-838a383b2866;[~accountid:712020:9c81fbcc-e424-4396-a25b-01f80fc942eb] 

 *Testing* *Plan:* *Room-Based* *Sorting* *(ZP-1752)*



  *Prerequisites*



* Have an SLD with issues linked to nodes

* Some nodes should be assigned to rooms (with Building > Floor > Room hierarchy)

* Some nodes should have *no* *room* assigned

* Have at least one active IR Session with linked issues



  ---

  *Test* *1:* *Main* *Issues* *List* *—* *Room* *Sort* *Option* *Appears*



# Open the main *Issues* *List* (via UnifiedIssueListView)

# Tap the *sort* *icon* (arrow up/down) in the top toolbar

# Verify *5* *sort* *options* appear: Created Date, Modified Date, Title, Status, *Room*

# Verify the Room option shows the *door* *icon* (door.left.hand.open)



  *Expected:* Room sort option is visible with correct label and icon.



  ---

  *Test* *2:* *Main* *Issues* *List* *—* *Room* *Sort* *Groups* *Issues*



# From the sort menu, select *Room*

# Verify issues are now displayed in *grouped* *sections* (not flat list)

# Each section should have a *header* with a door icon and location breadcrumb (e.g. "Building A > Floor 1 > Room 101")

# Verify issues within each group belong to nodes in that room

# Verify groups are sorted *alphabetically* by room path

# If any issues have no room, verify they appear in a *"No* *Room"* section *at* *the* *bottom*



  *Expected:* Issues grouped by room with correct headers and alphabetical ordering. "No Room" group is last.



  ---

  *Test* *3:* *Main* *Issues* *List* *—* *Other* *Sort* *Options* *Still* *Work*



# Switch sort to *Created* *Date* — verify flat list, newest first

# Switch to *Modified* *Date* — verify flat list, most recently modified first

# Switch to *Title* — verify flat list, alphabetical A-Z

# Switch to *Status* — verify flat list, Open > In Progress > Resolved > Closed



  *Expected:* All 4 original sorts work exactly as before, flat list (no grouping).



  ---

  *Test* *4:* *Main* *Issues* *List* *—* *Filters* *+* *Room* *Sort*



# Select the *Room* sort

# Apply different filter chips (All, Open, In Progress, Resolved, etc.)

# Verify the room grouping respects the active filter (only matching issues shown, empty groups should disappear)

# Clear filters and verify all groups return



  *Expected:* Filtering and room grouping work together correctly.



  ---

  *Test* *5:* *Main* *Issues* *List* *—* *Search* *Includes* *Room* *Location*



# With *any* *sort* selected, use the search bar

# Type a *building* *name* (e.g. "Building A")

# Verify issues whose nodes are in rooms within that building appear in results

# Type a *floor* *name* — verify matching issues appear

# Type a *room* *name* — verify matching issues appear

# Clear search and verify all issues return



  *Expected:* Search now matches against "Building > Floor > Room" path.



  ---

  *Test* *6:* *Session* *Issues* *Tab* *—* *Sort* *Menu* *Appears*



# Open a *Session* *Detail* screen

# Navigate to the *Issues* *tab*

# Verify a *sort* *icon* (arrow up/down) appears to the right of the "Issues" header

# Tap it and verify *5* *sort* *options* appear: Created Date, Modified Date, Title, Status, Room



  *Expected:* Sort dropdown is visible and shows all options.



  ---

  *Test* *7:* *Session* *Issues* *Tab* *—* *Default* *Sort* *(Created* *Date)*



# On the Issues tab, do *not* change the sort

# Verify issues display in *Open* */* *Closed* *sections* (existing behavior)

# Verify issues within each section are sorted by *created* *date* *descending* (newest first)



  *Expected:* Default behavior is unchanged.



  ---

  *Test* *8:* *Session* *Issues* *Tab* *—* *Room* *Sort* *Groups* *Issues*



# Tap the sort menu and select *Room*

# Verify the *Open* */* *Closed* *sections* *disappear*

# Issues should now be grouped by *room* with section headers showing "Building > Floor > Room"

# "No Room" group should appear at the bottom for issues on nodes without a room

# Each section should have a *door* *icon*, bold label, and a divider



  *Expected:* Room grouping replaces Open/Closed sections.



  ---

  *Test* *9:* *Session* *Issues* *Tab* *—* *Switch* *Back* *from* *Room* *Sort*



# After using Room sort, switch back to *Created* *Date* or *Status*

# Verify the room grouping disappears

# Verify *Open* */* *Closed* *sections* reappear with correct issue separation



  *Expected:* Switching away from Room sort restores normal Open/Closed layout.



  ---

  *Test* *10:* *Session* *Issues* *Tab* *—* *Other* *Sorts* *Apply*



# Select *Title* sort — verify issues are alphabetical within Open/Closed sections

# Select *Status* sort — verify ordering by status priority within the list

# Select *Modified* *Date* — verify ordering by modification date



  *Expected:* All sort types correctly sort the session issues while keeping Open/Closed grouping.



  ---

  *Test* *11:* *Session* *Issues* *Tab* *—* *QR* *Filter* *+* *Room* *Sort*



# Scan a QR code to filter issues to a specific node

# Select *Room* sort

# Verify only the filtered issues are shown, grouped by room

# Clear the QR filter — verify all session issues return, still grouped by room



  *Expected:* QR filter and room sort work together.

 *Test* *12:* *Edge* *Cases*



  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

  │                    Scenario                     │                      Expected                       │

  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

  │ Issue on a node with room but *no* *floor/building* │ Group header shows just the room name               │

  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

  │ Issue on a node with *no* *room*                    │ Appears under "No Room" group                       │

  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

  │ Issue with *no* *node* at all                       │ Appears under "No Room" group                       │

  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

  │ Session with *0* *issues*                           │ "No issues" empty state shown, no sort menu visible │

  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

  │ All issues in the *same* *room*                     │ Single group section shown                          │

  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

  │ All issues with *no* *room*                         │ Single "No Room" group shown                        │

  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘



  ---

  *Test* *13:* *French* *Language*



# Switch device language to *French*

# Open sort menu — verify "Room" shows as *"Salle"*

# Select Room sort — verify the "No Room" fallback shows as *"Aucune* *salle"*



  *Expected:* French translations display correctly.



  ---

  *Test* *14:* *Performance*



# Open an SLD with a *large* *number* *of* *issues* (50+)

# Select Room sort in both the main issues list and session issues tab

# Verify no noticeable lag when switching sort options or scrolling



  *Expected:* Sorting and grouping perform smoothly.

column1: Android | Inline Core Attributes entry during Connection Creation
column41:
h2. Android — Inline Core Attributes on Connection Creation

h3. Context

Parent: ZP-1398 — Currently on Android, users must create a connection, go back to the list, then tap into the connection again to fill Core Attributes. This sub-task embeds the Core Attributes form directly within the connection creation flow.

h3. Requirements

# *Extend the Create Connection screen* to include a collapsible "Core Attributes" section below existing connection fields
# *Fetch Core Attribute schema* from the API after the user selects a connection class — render fields dynamically using existing attribute field components
# *Reuse existing Core Attribute UI components* (Material dropdowns, TextInputLayouts, number pickers) from the Edit Connection flow
# *Make Core Attributes optional* — user can collapse/skip the section and save just the connection
# *Single save flow* — on tap Save, persist connection + Core Attributes. Handle partial failure: if connection saves but attributes fail, show a Snackbar/dialog error without losing the connection
# *Offline support* — Core Attribute data entered during creation should be added to the sync queue alongside the connection entity, syncing together when connectivity is restored
# *Keyboard and scroll handling* — ensure the NestedScrollView or RecyclerView scrolls correctly when the soft keyboard opens for Core Attribute input fields
# *Tablet layout* — Core Attributes section should render properly on tablet screen sizes

h3. Offline Considerations

* Cache the Core Attribute schema in the local Room/SQLite database per connection class
* When creating a connection offline, Core Attributes should be stored alongside the connection in the local DB and synced together on next sync flush
* Ensure sync queue JSON includes Core Attribute payload (ref: ZP-1265 sync queue architecture)

h3. Unit Test Plan

||#||Test Case||Expected Result||
|1|Create Connection → select class|Core Attributes section appears with correct fields|
|2|Change connection class mid-creation|Core Attribute fields refresh to match new class|
|3|Fill connection + Core Attributes → Save (online)|Both saved successfully in single flow|
|4|Save connection without filling Core Attributes|Connection created, attributes remain empty|
|5|Invalid Core Attribute value → Save|Inline validation error on the field|
|6|Create connection + Core Attributes offline → sync|Both synced together without data loss|
|7|Core Attribute schema not cached, device offline|Section shows "Available when online" message|
|8|Soft keyboard opens for text field in Core Attributes|Screen scrolls so active field remains visible|
|9|Tablet landscape layout|Core Attributes section renders correctly|
|10|Rotate device mid-creation with Core Attributes filled|Form state preserved after configuration change|

column1: FE | Inline Core Attributes entry during Connection Creation
column41:
h2. Frontend (Web) — Inline Core Attributes on Connection Creation

h3. Context

Parent: ZP-1398 — Currently, users must create a connection first, then reopen it to fill in Core Attributes. This sub-task adds the Core Attributes form directly into the connection creation flow on Web.

h3. Requirements

# *Extend the Create Connection modal/side-panel* to include a Core Attributes section below the existing connection fields
# *Fetch the Core Attribute schema* for the selected connection class dynamically (attributes vary by class)
# *Render Core Attribute form fields inline* — reuse existing Core Attribute field components (dropdowns, text inputs, number fields)
# *Make Core Attributes optional* — users can skip and save just the connection, matching current behaviour
# *Single save action* — persist both connection and Core Attributes together; handle partial failure gracefully
# *Validation* — apply same field validation rules as the existing Edit Connection Core Attributes screen
# *Loading state* — show loader while Core Attribute schema is fetched after class selection
# *Responsive layout* — ensure expanded form works at both 100% and 75% zoom

h3. API Integration

* Reuse existing Core Attribute schema endpoint (fetch fields per connection class)
* Reuse existing connection creation + Core Attribute update endpoints
* Handle partial failure: connection created but attributes failed → show error, don't lose connection

h3. Unit Test Plan

||#||Test Case||Expected Result||
|1|Open Create Connection → select connection class|Core Attribute fields load dynamically below|
|2|Change connection class after fields load|Core Attribute fields refresh to match new class|
|3|Fill all fields + Core Attributes → Save|Connection created with Core Attributes populated|
|4|Fill connection fields only, skip Core Attributes → Save|Connection created successfully (attributes optional)|
|5|Enter invalid value in Core Attribute field → Save|Validation error shown on specific field|
|6|API failure on Core Attribute save after connection created|Error toast shown, connection persists, retry prompt|
|7|Slow network — schema fetch|Loader displayed, fields appear once loaded|
|8|Layout at 100% zoom|No overflow or scrollbar issues|

column1: DCF-406: AWS S3 Object-Level Logging for Read & Write Events
column41:
h2. SOC 2 — AWS S3 Object-Level Logging for Read & Write Events

h3. Failing Test

Drata infrastructure test detecting that S3 buckets do not have object-level logging enabled for both read and write events.

h3. What Needs to Be Done

# Navigate to *AWS CloudTrail* → Trails
# Select the active trail (or create a data events trail)
# Under *Data events*, add *S3* as a data event source
# Enable logging for *both Read and Write* events on all S3 buckets (or specify production buckets)
# Save the trail configuration
# Verify events are being captured in CloudTrail logs
# Capture evidence screenshot and upload to the [SOC2 compliance folder|https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1olSqolXVOdvuA6TtDukZLNivFm2LmGIN]

h3. Important Note

This is separate from S3 server access logging — CloudTrail data events provide the detailed object-level audit trail required by SOC 2.

h3. Acceptance Criteria

* CloudTrail data events enabled for S3 (Read + Write) on all production buckets
* Drata infrastructure test passes

column1: [Android] Add Swap Source/Target button in Edit Connection view
column41:
h2. Overview

Add a "Swap" button between the Source and Target node pickers in the Edit Connection screen that swaps source and target (including their terminals) in one tap.

h2. Implementation Steps

# *Add a swap button* between Source and Target fields in the Edit Edge/Connection screen:

   - Use Material Icon {{swap_vert}} or {{compare_arrows}}

   - Style consistently with the existing connection detail UI

# *On tap, swap:*

   - Source node ID ↔ Target node ID

   - Source terminal ID ↔ Target terminal ID

   - Source node display object ↔ Target node display object

# *Validation* — existing checks (same-node, duplicate connection) should automatically apply to swapped values. No changes needed.

# *Save/Sync* — the existing save/sync flow should handle persisting the change. No backend or API changes needed.

# *Disable* the swap button while saving is in progress.

h2. No API/backend changes required

Swapping just modifies the source/target UUIDs and terminal UUIDs on the existing edge, which the existing update flow already supports.

----

h2. QA Test Plan

||#||Test Case||Expected Result||
|1|Open an existing connection → tap Swap button|Source and Target nodes visually swap positions, arrow direction updates|
|2|After swap → verify terminal dropdowns|Source terminal shows the old target's terminal, and vice versa|
|3|After swap → Save|Connection saves successfully. Reopening shows swapped values persisted|
|4|Swap → verify on SLD diagram|The connection arrow direction reflects the swap on the diagram|
|5|Swap a connection where reversed version already exists|Duplicate connection validation warning should appear, Save should be disabled|
|6|Swap a self-connection (source == target)|Should remain disabled/not possible (self-connections already prevented)|
|7|Swap → Discard changes (back without saving)|Original source/target should be preserved (no change)|
|8|Swap while offline → sync when back online|Swap should save locally, then sync correctly when network is restored|
|9|Swap button disabled during save|Button should not be tappable while save is in progress|
|10|Swap twice (double swap)|Should return to original source/target configuration|

# *Save/Sync* — the existing {{saveAndSync()}} flow handles persisting via {{EdgeService.updateEdge()}} and syncing to server. No backend or API changes needed.

h2. No API/backend changes required

Swapping just modifies the source/target UUIDs and terminal UUIDs on the existing edge, which the existing update flow already supports.

10/Apr/26 5:03 AM;712020:9881bb6e-cbf7-47a6-bf7a-838a383b2866;[~accountid:712020:9c81fbcc-e424-4396-a25b-01f80fc942eb] 

h2. Test Plan: Source/Target Swap in Edit Connection (ZP-1730)

h3. Prerequisites

* Have an SLD with multiple nodes
* Nodes should have various terminal configurations:
** *Node A*: Has both LOAD and LINE terminals (e.g., 1 LOAD, 1 LINE)
** *Node B*: Has both LOAD and LINE terminals (e.g., 2 LOAD, 2 LINE)
** *Node C*: Has only LOAD terminals (no LINE)
** *Node D*: Has only LINE terminals (no LOAD)
** *Node E*: Has no terminals at all
* Existing connections between some of these nodes
* Test in both English and French language settings

----

h3. 1. Swap Button Visibility & State

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|1.1|Swap button appears|Open edit screen for any connection|arrow.up.arrow.down icon visible between source and target sections (replaces old arrow.down)|
|1.2|Swap enabled for compatible nodes|Open edit for connection A -> B (both have LOAD and LINE)|Swap button is blue and tappable|
|1.3|Swap disabled for self-connection|Change source to same node as target|Swap button turns gray and is disabled|
|1.4|Swap disabled - target has no LOAD terminals|Open edit for connection A -> D (D has only LINE, no LOAD)|Swap button is gray/disabled (D can't become source)|
|1.5|Swap disabled - source has no LINE terminals|Open edit for connection C -> B (C has only LOAD, no LINE)|Swap button is gray/disabled (C can't become target)|
|1.6|Swap enabled - node with no terminals|Open edit for connection involving Node E (no terminals)|Swap button is blue and enabled (terminals are optional)|
|1.7|Swap disabled while saving|Tap save on a valid swapped connection, observe swap button during save|Swap button is disabled during save operation|

----

h3. 2. Swap Functionality

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|2.1|Basic swap|Open A -> B, tap swap|Source shows Node B, target shows Node A. Header label updates to "B -> A"|
|2.2|Terminal auto-selection (single eligible)|Open A -> B (A has 1 LOAD, 1 LINE; B has 1 LOAD, 1 LINE), tap swap|Source terminal auto-selects B's LOAD terminal. Target terminal auto-selects A's LINE terminal|
|2.3|Terminal cleared (multiple eligible)|Open A -> B (B has 2 LOAD terminals), tap swap|Source terminal shows "Select terminal" (not auto-selected since B has 2 LOAD terminals). User must manually pick one|
|2.4|Terminal cleared (no eligible on that side)|Open A -> E (E has no terminals), tap swap|Source becomes E with no terminal picker shown. Target becomes A with LINE terminal options|
|2.5|Swap then swap back restores originals|Open A -> B with terminals T1 (source) and T2 (target). Tap swap. Tap swap again|Source = A, target = B, source terminal = T1, target terminal = T2. Save bar disappears (hasChanges = false)|
|2.6|hasChanges after swap|Open A -> B, tap swap|Save bar appears at bottom (hasChanges = true)|
|2.7|Discard changes after swap|Open A -> B, tap swap, tap back button|"Discard changes?" alert appears. Tap discard - returns without saving|

----

h3. 3. Terminal Validation

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|3.1|No source terminals warning|Swap to put a node with no LOAD terminals as source (but has other terminals)|Orange triangle warning: "Source node has no source-eligible terminals". Save button disabled|
|3.2|No target terminals warning|Swap to put a node with no LINE terminals as target (but has other terminals)|Orange triangle warning: "Target node has no target-eligible terminals". Save button disabled|
|3.3|Missing source terminal selection|Swap where new source has 2+ LOAD terminals, don't select one|Orange circle warning: "Please select a source terminal". Save button disabled|
|3.4|Missing target terminal selection|Swap where new target has 2+ LINE terminals, don't select one|Orange circle warning: "Please select a target terminal". Save button disabled|
|3.5|Validation clears after terminal selected|From 3.3, select a source terminal from dropdown|Warning disappears. Save button becomes enabled (if all other validations pass)|
|3.6|Multiple validation messages|Create a state where both source and target terminals are missing|Both "Please select a source terminal" and "Please select a target terminal" warnings shown|
|3.7|Same-node + terminal warnings|Change source to match target (self-connection)|"Source and target cannot be the same node" warning shown. Terminal warnings may also appear. Save disabled|

----

h3. 4. Save & API Verification

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|4.1|Save swapped connection (online)|Open A -> B, swap to B -> A, select terminals, tap save|Save succeeds. Connection list shows B -> A. Server receives correct source, target, source_node_terminal_id, target_node_terminal_id, source_handle, target_handle|
|4.2|Verify handle codes in API payload|Monitor network request after saving a swapped connection|source_handle = handle_code of the LOAD terminal on the new source node. target_handle = handle_code of the LINE terminal on the new target node|
|4.3|Save swapped connection (offline)|Switch to offline mode, open A -> B, swap, select terminals, save|Save succeeds locally. Sync queue entry created. When going online, sync sends correct swapped values|
|4.4|WebView updates after swap save|Save a swapped connection, observe the SLD graph|Edge direction visually updates in the WebView to reflect swapped source/target|
|4.5|Reopen after save|Save a swapped connection B -> A. Close and reopen the edge detail screen|Shows B -> A with correct terminals. Original values are now B -> A (no unsaved changes shown)|

----

h3. 5. Interaction with Other Edit Features

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|5.1|Swap + change edge class|Swap source/target, then change the edge class, then save|Both changes saved correctly. Core attributes reflect the new edge class|
|5.2|Swap + edit core attributes|Swap, change a core attribute value, save|Both swap and attribute change saved correctly|
|5.3|Change source via picker then swap|Open A -> B. Change source to C via picker. Tap swap|Source = B, target = C. Terminals re-evaluated for the new positions|
|5.4|Swap then change node via picker|Swap A -> B to B -> A. Then change target via picker to C|Source = B, target = C. Terminal picker updates for node C|
|5.5|Duplicate connection after swap|A -> B exists and B -> A exists. Open A -> B, tap swap|Shows "A connection already exists between these nodes" (duplicate B -> A detected). Save disabled|

----

h3. 6. Edge Cases

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|6.1|Node with deleted terminals|Node has 2 terminals but 1 is soft-deleted, leaving 1 LOAD active|Terminal auto-selects. Deleted terminal not shown in picker|
|6.2|Rapid double-tap swap|Tap swap button twice quickly|Swaps and swaps back. Returns to original state. No crash|
|6.3|Swap with context node|Open edge edit from a node detail view (contextNode set)|Swap works. Node pickers still show appropriate filtered nodes|
|6.4|Connection between nodes with no terminals|Both source and target have no terminals at all. Swap|Swap works. No terminal pickers shown. No validation warnings. Save enabled|

----

h3. 7. Localization

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|7.1|English strings|Set language to English, trigger all validation states|All messages display in English|
|7.2|French strings|Set language to French, trigger all validation states|Messages display in French|
|7.3|Swap button accessibility|Enable VoiceOver, navigate to swap button|Reads "Swap source and target" (EN) or "Intervertir la source et la cible" (FR)|

----

h3. 8. Regression - Existing Edit Flow (No Swap)

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|8.1|Edit terminal only|Open edge, change terminal selection, save|Saves correctly. Handle codes updated to match new terminal|
|8.2|Edit edge class only|Open edge, change edge class, save|Saves correctly. No terminal validation issues if terminals already selected|
|8.3|Edit source node only|Open edge, change source via picker, save|Works as before. Terminal picker updates for new source node|
|8.4|Edit with no changes then back|Open edge, make no changes, tap back|Navigates back immediately (no discard alert)|
|8.5|Existing edge with terminals pre-selected|Open an edge that has terminals already set|Terminal pickers show correct selections. Save bar not visible. No validation warnings|
|8.6|Existing edge - node with single terminal|Open edge where source has exactly 1 LOAD terminal|Terminal auto-selected and shown as read-only with green checkmark|

EG Forms - Redo it properly
column41:
h2. EG Forms — Redo It Properly

h3. Context

Parent: ZP-1583 — Reporting Engine V2. This task is a comprehensive rework of the EG Forms system to support the V2 reporting engine. The original EG Forms implementation needs to be rebuilt with a proper V2 schema that includes JSONB references, new form types (enum eg_form_types 5, 6, 7), and the {{version: 2}} marker for all V2 forms.

h3. Requirements

* Redesign EG Forms data model to use the V2 schema with JSONB references
* Support new form types: type 5, type 6, type 7 (added to {{enum_eg_form_types}})
* Add {{version: 2}} marker to all EG Forms that use the new schema
* Migrate relevant existing forms to V2 schema in dev environment
* Change form type 3 from "NETA" to "NETA Class-Level" naming
* Ensure form rendering works correctly with the new schema on web
* Support references between forms (JSONB references field)
* Update form CRUD operations to handle V2 schema validation
* Maintain backward compatibility with V1 forms

h3. API Integration

* Updated EG Forms CRUD endpoints with V2 schema support
* Form validation endpoint for V2 schema
* Migration endpoint/script to upgrade forms to V2
* Seeding for new enum_eg_form_types (5, 6, 7)

h3. Unit Test Plan

||#||Test Case||Expected Result||
|1|Create V2 form with type 5|Form created with version:2 and type 5|
|2|Create V2 form with type 6|Form created with version:2 and type 6|
|3|Create V2 form with type 7|Form created with version:2 and type 7|
|4|Add JSONB references to form|References stored and retrievable|
|5|Render V2 form on web|Form displays all fields correctly|
|6|Load V1 form (backward compatibility)|V1 form renders without errors|
|7|Validate V2 form with missing required fields|Validation error returned|
|8|Form type 3 renamed to NETA Class-Level|Updated name shown in UI and API|
|9|Query forms filtered by version|Only matching version forms returned|

h3. QA Test Plan

* Verify all three new form types (5, 6, 7) can be created, edited, and deleted
* Confirm V2 forms render correctly in work orders and report generation
* Test JSONB references resolve correctly between linked forms
* Validate form type 3 displays as "NETA Class-Level" everywhere
* Verify V1 forms continue to work without modification
* Test form search/filter by type and version
* End-to-end: create V2 form → fill in work order → generate report
* Verify seeding scripts add new form types correctly

_Note: Git commit details unavailable via API — descriptions generated from epic context. Please verify accuracy with the dev team._

07/Apr/26 4:20 AM;712020:19109706-a11c-4efd-abee-5ca72d900e45;Hi [~accountid:623b9df4866b810069e4062a] , Visualizer vs Planned Work Orders quarter mismatch

I've been looking into the discrepancy between the Visualizer tab and the Planned Work Orders tab. They're showing items in different quarters for the same assets/procedures. Here's what I found:

*Root cause:* The Visualizer currently uses quote_line.window_end to determine which quarter a checkmark appears in, while the Planned Work Orders tab uses the work order's start_date. These dates are often very different.

*Example — Disconnect 01, Visual Inspection:*
window_end = 2026-04-01 → Visualizer shows it in Q2 2026
Work order start_date = 2029-04-01 → Planned WOs tab shows it in Q2 2029

Many quote lines have window_end set to 2026-04-01 regardless of when the work order is actually scheduled, so the Visualizer ends up stacking everything into Q2 2026.

Proposed fix: Update the Visualizer to use the planned work order's date (planned_workorder.start_date or end_date) for quarter placement instead of window_end. This would make the Visualizer consistent with the Planned Work Orders tab.

Can you confirm:

* Should the Visualizer reflect the planned work order schedule (WO start/end date) rather than the compliance window (window_end)?
* For lines that don't have a planned work order assigned yet, should we fall back to window_end?

Thanks!

column1: Clarification on redefining Non-Serviceable and Limited Service equipment classifications
column41:
[https://app.devrev.ai/egalvanic/works/TKT-762|https://app.devrev.ai/egalvanic/works/TKT-762|smart-link] 
The user has raised a query regarding the current process for marking equipment as Non-Serviceable, especially in situations where access is restricted due to high cal or LOTO reasons. They have proposed adjustments to the definitions of Non-Serviceable and Limited Service classifications to include access limitations as a primary reason, not just technical issues.

Proposed definitions:

* Non-Serviceable: Equipment that has a problem or presents a safety condition that is detrimental to proper electrical or mechanical operation, or that cannot be safely opened due to arc-flash hazards or LOTO limitations, shall be designated as non-serviceable. Access to non-serviceable equipment by unqualified persons shall be restricted.
* Limited Service: Equipment that has minor deficiencies or conditions that are not detrimental to the protective operation or characteristics of the equipment, and that can be safely accessed under controlled conditions, shall be designated as limited service.

The user seeks clarification on whether an issue needs to be created for each piece of equipment that cannot be accessed due to high cal or LOTO, or if the definition can be adjusted to cover these instances. Please provide guidance or escalate to the appropriate team for further assistance.
column211: 05/Apr/26 5:33 AM;712020:b43bccaf-6e8f-47ef-82f7-5439c6765fdb;*Limited Service* (proposed new definition): Equipment with minor deficiencies _that can be safely accessed under controlled conditions_.

column1: iOS Performance Problems
column41:
[https://app.devrev.ai/egalvanic/works/TKT-759|https://app.devrev.ai/egalvanic/works/TKT-759|smart-link] 

videos link : [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1mJ22339G07XywJG2zhzbXwDU429c-Ad-/view?usp=sharing|https://drive.google.com/file/d/1mJ22339G07XywJG2zhzbXwDU429c-Ad-/view?usp=sharing|smart-link] 

*Priority: Blocker*

We've talked back and forth with Mukul about this but I'd just like to open a ticket to hold all of our findings in one place. In brief, a lot of our field techs are experiencing significant slowdown on iOS versions of IQ. I'll attach videos of the problem experienced from multiple sources to illustrate this point. Our findings can be broken down into 2 investigatory sessions. The first is an interview I held with one of our techs Eric Gravel. Here are my notes from this session:

* Experienced it multiple days, problem seemed to arise when shifting to offline mode, they fully switched to offline mode when this happened, doesn’t seem to be an issue of coming in and out of internet access
* Laptop was working fine, but with internet access didn’t use much iPad. Didn’t experience lag when working online with the iPad
* Tried syncing, tried restarting the app, had to switch back to TEGG
* Believe he was running version 1.24, could not verify as the app crashed consistently on start-up when I called him
* We had him update the app, now on iOS Version 1.25
* 1.6Mb in his image cache
* Happened on the 16th & 17th

The second is testing Derek & I performed manually the other day to attempt to recreate the issue. Here are my notes from that session:
There are 3 main behaviours exhibited resulting an overall slowdown:

# Slow appearance of the dropdown for certain selections
# Slow population of data entered from the dropdown
# Lost inputs such as menu scrolling, or dropdown selections. This seems to result when an input is entered while the system is processing a previous input which can result in lost keystrokes

* The issue is exclusive to iOS, we tried the same steps on Android devices and found no comparable slow down
* We tested on iOS Ver. 1.24, and 1.26 and found the same behaviour
* The issue doesn't seem to be an issue with the iPads themselves or anything downloaded on them, as we also tried with a personal iOS phone and got the same results.
* It is quite difficult to recreate as each keystroke produces a different timing for a response. It's inconsistent when the slowdown happens
* The issue seems to arise primarily only when a certain type of dropdown is used. This is best exhibited in the videos, but one type of dropdown seems to operate fully as expected while the other seems to result in significant slowdown. This is most easily seen when entering data for an issue.

In summary: The slowdown we're encountering seems to be tied to a specific type of dropdown. This might be a jump, but I believe that the dropdown is not working is unique to the iOS UI. I believe that the dropdown itself is part of the iOS architecture.

 

I spoke with Mukul last night, about these results and we believe it may be down to one of the dropdowns causing this slowdown. If this dropdown could be switched to the one that is specific to IQ in all locations in the app I believe that would be a good starting place to see if this can be resolved. Again I'll go ahead an attach all of the videos we have so far for your review. Thank you!

column41:
h2. Introduce NEW_QUERY_META to Lambdas and Configure BE and FE to Use It

h3. Context

Parent: ZP-1583 — Reporting Engine V2. This task introduces a new query metadata format ({{NEW_QUERY_META}}) to the reporting lambdas and updates both the backend and frontend to use it. This supports the V2 reporting engine's need for richer query shape definitions, including EG Forms references (JSONB) and the new form types (5, 6, 7).

h3. Requirements

* Define the {{NEW_QUERY_META}} structure that supports V2 reporting query shapes
* Update reporting lambdas to accept and process the new query metadata format
* Maintain backward compatibility with existing query format during transition
* Update backend to send {{NEW_QUERY_META}} in reporting API calls
* Update frontend to construct and pass {{NEW_QUERY_META}} when invoking reports
* Support the Eaton-driven query shape changes (ref: ZP-1596)
* Include {{version: 2}} flag to distinguish V2 queries from legacy

h3. API Integration

* Lambda endpoints updated to accept {{NEW_QUERY_META}} parameter
* Backend reporting endpoints pass through the new metadata format
* Frontend constructs {{NEW_QUERY_META}} from ReportingConfig and user selections
* Fallback to legacy format when {{version}} flag is absent

h3. Unit Test Plan

||#||Test Case||Expected Result||
|1|Lambda receives NEW_QUERY_META (v2)|Processes query correctly with v2 logic|
|2|Lambda receives legacy query format|Falls back to v1 processing|
|3|FE constructs NEW_QUERY_META from config|Correct metadata structure sent to BE|
|4|BE passes NEW_QUERY_META to lambda|Lambda receives expected format|
|5|NEW_QUERY_META with EG Form references|References resolved correctly in query|
|6|NEW_QUERY_META with form type 5, 6, or 7|Correct form type processing|
|7|Malformed NEW_QUERY_META|Lambda returns validation error|
|8|Mixed v1 and v2 queries in same session|Each query processed with correct version logic|

h3. QA Test Plan

* Verify reports generated with NEW_QUERY_META produce correct output
* Compare V1 vs V2 output for equivalent queries to ensure parity where expected
* Test all three new form types (5, 6, 7) through the full pipeline
* Validate backward compatibility — legacy reports still generate correctly
* Test lambda performance with new metadata format (no degradation)
* Confirm FE correctly populates NEW_QUERY_META for each report type
* End-to-end test: create config → select parameters → generate report

_Note: Git commit details unavailable via API — descriptions generated from epic context. Please verify accuracy with the dev team._

column41:
h3. Summary

Port the iOS commit {{d1169b1}} to Android. This involves implementing an equipment library picker with offline support and various settings configurations.

h3. Context

The task requires the development of features that include local caching, UI for equipment selection, and AI suggestions. These features are crucial for enhancing user experience in asset creation.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* Implement equipment library local caching using Room entity and DAO.
* Create an offline-capable dropdown/dialog for the equipment library picker in the asset creation flow.
* Develop the UI and data model for trip unit settings selection.
* Develop the UI and data model for frame size/settings selection.
* Integrate AI-powered equipment suggestions using {{suggestedShortcutId}} and nameplate extraction.
* Implement a sync queue for offline-first create/update functionality.

h3. Other information

Key files involved in this implementation include:

* {{data/local/database/entity/NodeShortcut.kt}}
* {{presentation/assets/components/ShortcutPickerDialog.kt}}
* {{data/remote/ApiEndpoints.kt}}
* {{presentation/assets/AddAssetScreen.kt}}
* {{data/local/database/migrations/}}

|6.6|Successful suggestion|Tap sparkle on a node with photos|Loading spinner shown → equipment auto-selected (including SST trip unit resolution if applicable)|
|6.7|No match|Tap sparkle when AI finds no match|"No matching equipment found" error shown in red|
|6.8|API failure|Simulate error (bad network)|"AI suggestion failed: ..." error shown in red|
|6.9|Suggestion replaces existing|Have equipment selected, tap sparkle|Previous selection replaced by AI suggestion|

----

h3. 7. SLD Sync — eqp_lib Field

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|7.1|eqp_lib syncs to server on save|Select equipment, save node, trigger sync|{{eqp_lib}} sent as JSON object (not string) in node update payload|
|7.2|eqp_lib syncs from server|Refresh SLD from server where node has {{eqp_lib}} set|{{eqp_lib}} field populated on local node; Engineering section shows the selection|
|7.3|eqp_lib null handling|Clear equipment selection, save, sync|{{eqp_lib}} sent as {{null}} to server|
|7.4|eqp_lib as JSON object in response|Server returns {{eqp_lib}} as raw JSON object|Parsed correctly into string for local storage|
|7.5|eqp_lib as JSON string in response|Server returns {{eqp_lib}} as JSON string|Parsed correctly|
|7.6|use_eqp_lib on node class|Sync node classes from server|{{use_eqp_lib}} flag properly parsed and stored on NodeClass|
|7.7|eqp_lib in WebView DTO|Open SLD in WebView|{{eqp_lib}} field included in all 4 {{createDTO()}} overloads passed to WebView|

----

h3. 8. Add Asset Flow

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|8.1|Equipment section on add|Create new node with class that has {{use_eqp_lib = true}}|Engineering section shown|
|8.2|Select equipment during creation|Pick equipment, fill other fields, save|Node created with {{eqp_lib}} set|
|8.3|No AI suggest on add|Open add asset|Sparkle button not shown (no {{node}} reference yet)|
|8.4|Equipment section hidden|Create node with class where {{use_eqp_lib = false}}|No Engineering section|

----

h3. 9. Sync Execution — Empty Queue Fix

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|9.1|Empty sync queue|Trigger sync with no pending changes|No sync job API call made; log shows "Sync queue empty — skipping sync job audit"|
|9.2|Non-empty sync queue|Make changes, trigger sync|Sync job API call proceeds normally|

----

h3. 10. Offline / Edge Cases

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|10.1|Offline equipment browsing|Download library while online, go offline, open picker|Picker works fully offline (all categories, items, styles, search)|
|10.2|Offline trip unit settings|Go offline, configure trip unit settings|All dropdowns work from cached SwiftData|
|10.3|Offline frame settings|Go offline, configure frame settings|All dropdowns work from cached SwiftData|
|10.4|Large library performance|Download full library|No UI freeze; progress bar updates smoothly; app remains responsive|
|10.5|App upgrade with new models|Install update (first launch with new SwiftData models)|Migration succeeds; no crash on launch|
|10.6|Multiple SLDs|Switch between SLDs, edit nodes|Equipment selections are per-node, not shared|

----

h3. Notes for QA

* The Equipment Library section only appears on nodes whose *node class* has {{use_eqp_lib = true}} — coordinate with backend to ensure the right classes have this flag set.
* The {{eqp_lib}} field is stored as a JSON string locally but must be sent as a raw JSON *object* to the backend (not a string). Verify via network proxy (e.g., Charles) if possible.
* French translations were *not* added in this commit for the new strings — flag this as a known gap if French localization is required.

column1: Improvements to Agent
column41:
h2. Improvements to Agent

h3. Context

Parent: ZP-1583 — Reporting Engine V2. This task covers various improvements to the AI agent sidecar service that powers template editing and form/config creation. The agent runs as a separate ECS Fargate service (port 8899) communicating with the Anthropic API and the backend.

h3. Requirements

* Improve agent response quality and consistency for template operations
* Add better error handling and retry logic for Anthropic API failures
* Implement request/response logging for debugging agent interactions
* Optimize agent prompt engineering for reporting-specific tasks
* Add health check improvements and monitoring metrics
* Support streaming responses for better UX during long operations
* Improve context window management — pass only relevant template/form sections to reduce token usage
* Add rate limiting to prevent abuse

h3. API Integration

* Agent health endpoint: {{GET /health}} on port 8899
* Agent request endpoint: accepts template context + user instruction
* Backend integration via {{AGENT_BACKEND_URL}} (port 5001)
* Anthropic API via {{ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}} secret

h3. Unit Test Plan

||#||Test Case||Expected Result||
|1|Send valid template edit request|Agent returns well-formed template update|
|2|Send request with invalid template context|Agent returns error with helpful message|
|3|Anthropic API rate limited|Retry with exponential backoff, eventual response or error|
|4|Anthropic API down|Graceful failure, error propagated to caller|
|5|Health check endpoint|Returns 200 with service status|
|6|Large template context (near token limit)|Agent truncates/summarizes context, still produces valid response|
|7|Concurrent requests to agent|All requests handled without interference|
|8|Streaming response for long operation|Partial results delivered progressively|

h3. QA Test Plan

* Verify agent responds within acceptable time (< 15s for standard operations)
* Test agent with various template types and complexity levels
* Confirm error messages are user-friendly and actionable
* Validate agent logging captures request/response for debugging
* Test agent under concurrent load (5+ simultaneous requests)
* Verify health check reports correct status
* Confirm agent recovery after temporary Anthropic API outage

h2. Give Users Something to Start With for Page Templates

h3. Context

Parent: ZP-1583 — Reporting Engine V2. This task provides starter/seed page templates so users are not faced with a blank canvas when creating reports. Pre-built templates for common report types (NETA, inspection summaries, asset condition reports) give users a foundation to customize.

h3. Requirements

* Create a library of starter ReportingPageTemplate records for common use cases
* Starter templates should include: cover page, summary page, detail/loop page, appendix page
* Templates should use the company's style guide (branding colors, logos) when available
* Provide a "Start from template" option in the report creation flow
* Users can duplicate a starter template and customize it
* Starter templates should be read-only (company cannot modify the originals)
* Seed starter templates during company onboarding or feature enablement

h3. API Integration

* Endpoint to list available starter templates (filtered by form type or report type)
* Duplicate/clone endpoint to create a user-editable copy from a starter
* Seeding scripts for initial template data

|3|Duplicate starter template|Editable copy created in user's template list|
|4|Attempt to edit original starter template|Edit blocked — read-only|
|5|Duplicated template renders correctly|Copy produces same output as original|
|6|Starter templates use company branding|Logos and colors from company style guide applied|
|7|New company — seed templates|Starter templates available after feature enablement|

h3. QA Test Plan

* Verify all starter templates render without errors
* Confirm duplication creates an independent copy (edits don't affect original)
* Test starter templates with different company brandings
* Validate cover page, summary, detail loop, and appendix templates individually
* Ensure starter templates work end-to-end in report generation

h2. Branding and Asset (Media) Management

h3. Context

Parent: ZP-1583 — Reporting Engine V2. This task introduces branding and media asset management for the reporting system. Companies need to upload and manage their logos, style guides (primary/accent colors), and other media assets that are used in generated reports via the {{CompanyAsset}} and {{Company}} (style guide) models.

h3. Requirements

* Build a Branding & Assets management UI under company settings
* Allow uploading company logos (small_logo, large_logo) with image preview
* Support style guide configuration: primary_color, accent_color, font preferences
* Media asset library for uploading, organizing, and deleting report assets (images, icons)
* Assets should be stored and served via the existing file storage infrastructure
* Preview how branding appears in report templates (live preview)
* Support image format validation (PNG, JPG, SVG) and size limits
* Seed default style guides for new companies

h3. API Integration

* CRUD endpoints for CompanyAsset records
* Update Company record with style guide fields
* File upload endpoint for media assets with validation
* Thumbnail generation for uploaded images

h3. Unit Test Plan

||#||Test Case||Expected Result||
|1|Upload small logo (valid PNG)|Logo saved and displayed in preview|
|2|Upload large logo (valid SVG)|Logo saved and displayed in preview|
|3|Set primary and accent colors|Colors saved and reflected in branding preview|
|4|Upload asset exceeding size limit|Validation error shown|
|5|Upload unsupported file format|Rejection with clear error message|
|6|Delete an asset used in a template|Warning shown, soft delete or block|
|7|Browse asset library with pagination|Paginated asset grid loads correctly|
|8|Preview branding in sample report template|Template renders with uploaded logos and colors|

h3. QA Test Plan

* Verify logo upload and retrieval for both small and large variants
* Confirm style guide colors apply correctly in report generation
* Test asset library with 50+ uploaded assets (pagination, search)
* Validate file type restrictions are enforced on both FE and BE
* Test branding preview across different report templates
* Verify default style guide is seeded for new companies
* Check image quality after upload (no compression artifacts)

column1: AI-driven Form/Template creation
column41:
h2. AI-Driven Form/Template Creation

h3. Context

Parent: ZP-1583 — Reporting Engine V2. This task enables users to create new EG Forms and ReportingPageTemplates using AI. Instead of manually building forms/templates from scratch, users describe what they need in natural language and the AI agent generates the initial structure.

h3. Requirements

* Provide a "Create with AI" entry point in both the EG Forms and Page Template creation flows
* Accept natural language descriptions of desired form/template (e.g., "Create a NETA MTS inspection form with fields for voltage, current, and resistance")
* AI generates a complete form/template structure based on the description
* Generated output should follow existing schema conventions (EG Forms v2 schema, ReportingPageTemplate structure)
* Allow users to review and edit the AI-generated output before saving
* Support iterative refinement — users can ask the AI to modify the generated result
* Include form type selection (enum eg_form_types: 5, 6, 7) in the creation flow

h3. API Integration

* Send creation request to the agent sidecar service (port 8899)
* Agent returns structured form/template JSON conforming to the appropriate schema
* Validate generated output against schema before presenting to user
* Save via existing form/template CRUD endpoints

h3. Unit Test Plan

||#||Test Case||Expected Result||
|1|Describe a simple form and generate|Valid EG Form structure returned|
|2|Describe a page template and generate|Valid ReportingPageTemplate structure returned|
|3|Generate form with specific field types (text, number, select, date)|All requested field types present in output|
|4|Refine generated form with follow-up instruction|AI modifies form based on feedback|
|5|Review and save AI-generated form|Form saved correctly to database|
|6|Generate form with vague description|AI produces reasonable default with editable suggestions|
|7|Agent service timeout during generation|Error message shown, user can retry or create manually|
|8|Generated output fails schema validation|Error shown, user prompted to refine or edit manually|

h3. QA Test Plan

* Verify AI-generated forms conform to EG Forms v2 schema
* Test generation for each form type (5, 6, 7)
* Confirm generated templates render correctly in the template previewer
* Validate iterative refinement preserves previously accepted changes
* Test with complex descriptions (10+ fields, nested sections)
* Verify saved AI-generated forms work in report generation pipeline
* Check that generation works on both desktop and tablet viewports

_Note: Git commit details unavailable via API — descriptions generated from epic context. Please verify accuracy with the dev team._

h2. Test Plan: 100MB Attachment Upload Size Validation

h3. Prerequisites

* iOS device or simulator with the app installed
* Test files of varying sizes:
** Small file: < 50 MB (e.g., 10 MB PDF, 25 MB image)
** Boundary file (just under): ~99.9 MB file
** Boundary file (exactly at): 100 MB file
** Boundary file (just over): ~100.1 MB file
** Large file: > 100 MB (e.g., 102.8 MB video, 150 MB ZIP)
* Network connectivity (online mode)
* Offline mode available for offline tests

----

h3. Module 1: Attachments Tab (Work Order Details)

*TC-1.1: Upload file under 100MB*

# Open a Work Order → Navigate to Attachments tab
# Tap "+" button → Select a file under 100 MB
# *Expected:* AttachmentConfigurationView sheet appears, upload proceeds successfully

*TC-1.2: Upload file over 100MB*

# Open a Work Order → Navigate to Attachments tab
# Tap "+" button → Select a file over 100 MB (e.g., 102.8 MB .mp4)
# *Expected:* Upload is blocked, error alert shows "File too large. Maximum size is 100MB."

*TC-1.3: Upload file exactly at 100MB*

# Select a file that is exactly 100,000,000 bytes
# *Expected:* Upload proceeds successfully (limit is inclusive: <=)

*TC-1.4: Upload file at 100.1MB*

# Select a file that is ~100,100,000 bytes
# *Expected:* Upload is blocked with the error message

*TC-1.5: Empty state "Browse Files" button with oversized file*

# Open Attachments tab with no attachments (empty state)
# Tap "Browse Files" → Select a file over 100 MB
# *Expected:* Upload is blocked with the error message

----

h3. Module 2: IR Photos Tab — File Picker ("From Files")

*TC-2.1: Upload image file under 100MB*

# Open a Work Order → Navigate to IR Photos tab
# Tap Upload IR Photos → Select "From Files"
# Select an image file under 100 MB
# *Expected:* Image is compressed and uploaded successfully

*TC-2.2: Upload single image file over 100MB*

# Tap Upload IR Photos → Select "From Files"
# Select a single image file over 100 MB
# *Expected:* Upload is blocked, error alert shows "File too large. Maximum size is 100MB."

*TC-2.3: Upload multiple files where one exceeds 100MB*

# Tap Upload IR Photos → Select "From Files"
# Select multiple images — include one that exceeds 100 MB
# *Expected:* Entire batch is blocked with the error message (no partial upload)

*TC-2.4: Upload exactly 20 files all under 100MB*

# Select 20 image files, all under 100 MB
# *Expected:* All files are uploaded successfully

----

h3. Module 3: IR Photos Tab — Photo Picker ("From Photos")

*TC-3.1: Upload photos under 100MB from photo library*

# Open a Work Order → Navigate to IR Photos tab
# Tap Upload IR Photos → Select "From Photos"
# Select photos under 100 MB from the library
# *Expected:* Photos are compressed and uploaded successfully

*TC-3.2: Upload photo over 100MB from photo library*

# Tap Upload IR Photos → Select "From Photos"
# Select a photo/video that exceeds 100 MB (e.g., ProRAW image, large screenshot stitching)
# *Expected:* Upload is blocked with the error message

*TC-3.3: Upload multiple photos where one exceeds 100MB*

# Select multiple photos — include one exceeding 100 MB
# *Expected:* Upload stops at the oversized photo and shows the error message

----

h3. Module 4: Service-Level Validation (AttachmentUploadService)

*TC-4.1: Offline → Online sync of oversized attachment*

# Switch to Offline mode
# Attempt to add an attachment (if possible to bypass UI check via queued sync)
# Switch back to Online mode and trigger sync
# *Expected:* Service-level validation in AttachmentUploadService catches and rejects the file with fileTooLarge error

----

h3. Module 5: Localization

*TC-5.1: Error message in English*

# Set device language to English
# Attempt to upload an oversized file
# *Expected:* Error message displays "File too large. Maximum size is 100MB."

*TC-5.2: Error message in French*

# Set device language to French
# Attempt to upload an oversized file
# *Expected:* Error message displays "Fichier trop volumineux. Taille maximale : 100 Mo."

----

h3. Module 6: Edge Cases

*TC-6.1: Corrupted/unreadable file*

# Select a file whose size cannot be determined (permissions issue, broken symlink)
# *Expected:* App does not crash; graceful error handling

*TC-6.2: Network disconnect during upload of valid file*

# Select a valid file under 100 MB → Begin upload
# Disconnect network mid-upload
# *Expected:* Upload fails gracefully, attachment is queued for sync with uploadNeeded = true

*TC-6.3: Cancel and retry with valid file*

# Attempt to upload an oversized file → See error
# Dismiss error → Select a valid file under 100 MB
# *Expected:* Upload proceeds normally; previous error does not persist
