h2. Test Plan: ZP-1648 — Performance Fix for App Hang

h3. Devices to Test

* iPhone 13 (or similar lower-end device)
* iPad
* iPhone 15/16 Pro (high-end baseline)

h3. Pre-requisites

* An SLD with multiple nodes, edges, issues, and tasks
* At least 5+ issue classes with calculated fields and auto-fill rules
* At least 5+ node classes with subtypes
* An active IR session
* Both online and offline mode available

----

h3. 1. Issue Detail Screen

h4. 1.1 Picker Responsiveness (Primary Bug Fix)

* Open an existing issue → tap Status picker → verify sheet opens immediately (no lag)
* Select a status → verify sheet dismisses and status updates in both the header badge and the picker field
* Rapidly switch status 5+ times (Open → In Progress → Pending → Resolved → Open) → verify no hang or stutter
* Tap Priority picker → verify sheet opens → select a priority → verify color updates
* Rapidly switch priority 5+ times → verify smooth
* Tap Subtype picker (when type is set) → verify sheet opens → select → verify updates
* Tap Issue Class picker → verify sheet opens with full list of classes
* Select an issue class with calculated fields → verify calculated field values appear within ~1 second
* Rapidly switch issue class 3+ times → verify no hang, only the last class is applied
* Tap the X button on Issue Class picker → verify class clears and Core Attributes section disappears

h4. 1.2 Status Picker — Cannot Clear

* Verify the Status picker does NOT show an X (clear) button — status is required
* Verify Priority picker DOES show an X button and can be cleared
* Verify Subtype picker DOES show an X button and can be cleared

h4. 1.3 Change Detection (Save Button)

* Open issue with no changes → verify "Save Changes" button is NOT visible
* Change any field (status, priority, title, etc.) → verify "Save Changes" appears immediately
* Revert the change back to original value → verify "Save Changes" disappears after ~150ms
* Change status → verify Cancel button appears in toolbar (replacing Close)

h4. 1.4 Core Attributes

* Select an issue class → verify Core Attributes section appears with correct properties
* Edit a text core attribute → verify value is retained
* Edit a select/dropdown core attribute → verify selection sheet opens
* Edit a field that triggers auto-fill → verify dependent fields auto-populate
* Edit a calculated field manually → verify orange "overridden" border appears
* Tap refresh on overridden calculated field → verify recalculates from inputs
* Change issue class → verify attributes from previous class are preserved where names match

h4. 1.5 Safety & Notification

* Toggle Immediate Hazard → verify toggle updates and save button appears
* Toggle Customer Notified → verify toggle updates

h4. 1.6 Description & Proposed Resolution

* Edit description text → verify cursor stays in place (no jumps)
* Edit proposed resolution → verify cursor stays in place
* Type in description while rapidly changing other pickers → verify no cursor jumps or text loss

h4. 1.7 Photos

* Add a photo → verify it appears in the photo list
* Delete a photo → verify it disappears
* Add a photo → verify "Save Changes" appears
* Tap a photo → verify full-screen viewer opens

h4. 1.8 IR Photos (if session exists)

* Verify IR Photos section appears when session is active
* Link/unlink IR photos → verify changes tracked

h4. 1.9 Save Flow

* Make changes → tap "Save Changes" → verify saving overlay appears
* Verify all changed fields persist after save (reopen the issue)
* Verify issue class + core attributes + descriptions all saved correctly
* Verify photos added/deleted correctly

h4. 1.10 Delete Flow

* Tap Delete Issue → verify confirmation alert
* Confirm delete → verify issue is removed

h4. 1.11 Discard Flow

* Make changes → tap Cancel → verify "Discard Changes?" alert
* Tap Discard → verify view dismisses without saving
* Make changes → tap Cancel → tap "Cancel" on alert → verify stays on screen with changes intact

h4. 1.12 Status History

* Tap clock icon in toolbar → verify status history sheet opens

----

h3. 2. Issue Creation Screen

h4. 2.1 Issue Class Picker

* Open new issue creation → tap Issue Class picker → verify picker responds without lag
* Select an issue class with calculated fields → verify Core Attributes section appears
* Rapidly switch issue class 3+ times → verify no hang
* Select a class → verify title auto-populates (when creating from a node)

h4. 2.2 Field Entry

* Enter title → verify validation (required field)
* Select priority → verify selection
* Select node (when not fixed) → verify selection

h4. 2.3 Core Attributes on Creation

* Select issue class → fill in core attributes → verify they persist through class changes (where names match)
* Verify auto-fill works on creation screen

h4. 2.4 Safety Fields

* Toggle immediate hazard → verify state
* Toggle customer notified → verify state

h4. 2.5 Create Flow

* Fill all required fields → tap Create → verify issue is created
* Verify all fields (title, priority, class, core attributes, safety) saved correctly

----

h3. 3. Asset Detail Screen

h4. 3.1 Picker Responsiveness (Primary Bug Fix)

* Open an asset → tap Node Class picker → verify sheet opens immediately (no lag)
* Select a node class → verify subtypes refresh to match the selected class
* Rapidly switch node class 3+ times → verify no hang, subtypes always match last selected class
* Tap Node Subtype picker → verify sheet opens with correct subtypes for current class
* Tap Serviceability picker → verify sheet opens → select → verify updates
* Tap the X button on Serviceability → verify clears and note field disappears
* Select "Non-Serviceable" → verify serviceability note field appears

h4. 3.2 Voltage Picker

* Tap primary Voltage picker → verify sheet opens with all 17 voltage options
* Select a voltage (e.g., "480V") → verify picker shows selected value
* Tap the X button → verify voltage clears to nil
* For nodes with primary_secondary_voltage: verify both pickers appear and work independently
* For nodes without dual voltage: verify only single voltage picker appears

h4. 3.3 Change Detection

* Open asset with no changes → verify save button is NOT visible
* Change asset name → verify save button appears immediately
* Change node class → verify save button appears
* Change serviceability → verify save button appears
* Change voltage → verify save button appears
* Change serviceability note only → verify save button appears
* Delete a task → verify save button appears
* Create an issue from asset → verify save button appears
* Revert all changes → verify save button disappears

h4. 3.4 Basic Info Fields

* Edit asset name → verify updates in header
* Change room (top-level node) → verify room picker works
* For child nodes: verify parent/enclosure label is shown (not room picker)
* Change QR code → verify field updates
* Tap QR scanner button → verify scanner opens
* Change COM level (1/2/3) → verify visual feedback

h4. 3.5 Core Attributes

* Select a node class → verify Core Attributes section appears
* Edit attributes → verify save includes them
* For nodes with nameplate photos: verify extraction button works (when online)

h4. 3.6 Equipment Library

* For node classes with use_eqp_lib: verify Equipment Library section appears
* For other classes: verify section does NOT appear

h4. 3.7 Notes

* Edit notes → verify cursor stays in place
* Verify notes only visible in full (.all) focus mode

h4. 3.8 Photos

* Add a photo → verify appears in list and save button shows
* Delete a photo → verify removed
* Set a photo as default → verify profile header updates
* Delete the default photo → verify fallback to another photo

h4. 3.9 IR Photos

* With active session: verify IR Photos section appears
* Enter IR photo key + visual photo key → tap Add → verify staged photo appears
* Verify next photo keys auto-increment
* Delete a staged IR photo → verify removed
* For FLIR-IND sessions: verify visual photo field is disabled
* Without active session but with existing IR photos: verify section still shows existing photos

h4. 3.10 Tasks

* Verify tasks card appears (in .all and .tasks focus modes)
* Tap a task → verify task detail opens
* Delete a task → verify save button appears
* Create a task → verify creation sheet opens

h4. 3.11 Issues

* Verify issues card appears (in .all and .issues focus modes)
* Tap an issue → verify issue detail opens
* Delete an issue → verify save button appears
* Create an issue → verify creation sheet opens

h4. 3.12 Child Nodes / OCP

* For enclosure nodes (box == true, no parent): verify Child Nodes section appears
* Tap a child node → verify asset detail opens recursively
* Save child node → verify parent shows loading overlay during save
* Create child → verify creation sheet opens
* Create multiple OCP → verify sheet opens
* OCP photo walkthrough → verify fullScreenCover opens
* Link existing → verify fullScreenCover opens

h4. 3.13 Connections / Edges

* For non-child nodes: verify Connections section appears
* Tap an edge → verify edge detail opens
* Save edge → verify parent shows loading overlay during save

h4. 3.14 Copy Asset Details

* Tap ellipsis menu → "Copy Details From" → verify picker opens with same-class nodes
* Select a node → select fields → verify attributes/subtype/serviceability copied
* Verify success alert shows
* Tap ellipsis menu → "Copy Details To" → verify picker opens
* Select target → select fields → verify target opens with prefilled data

h4. 3.15 Save Flow

* Make changes → tap Save → verify saving overlay
* Verify all changed fields persist (reopen the asset)
* Verify node class, subtype, core attributes, voltage, serviceability, notes, photos all saved
* Verify IR photos converted from staged data to actual IRPhoto objects

h4. 3.16 Discard Flow

* Make changes → tap Cancel → verify discard alert → confirm → verify dismisses

h4. 3.17 Focus Modes

* Open in .all mode → verify all sections visible
* Open in .coreAttributes mode → verify only photos + equipment + voltage + core attributes
* Open in .tasks mode → verify only tasks section + auto-opens task creation
* Open in .irPhotos mode → verify only IR photos section
* Open in .issues mode → verify only issues section + auto-opens issue creation
* Open in .ocp mode → verify only child nodes section
* Open in .connections mode → verify only edges section
* Open in .collectAFData mode → verify basic info (no shortcut/QR/COM/serviceability) + photos + equipment + voltage + core attributes + child nodes + edges
* Open in .collectCOMData mode → verify basic info (full) + photos

----

h3. 4. Cross-Screen Interactions

* Open asset → create issue from asset → edit the issue → save → return to asset → verify issue count updated
* Open asset → open child node → save child → verify parent loading overlay appeared and dismissed
* Open issue from asset detail → verify issue detail opens correctly → edit and save → return to asset

----

h3. 5. Performance Validation

* [ ] *iPhone 13 / iPad*: Open Issue Detail → rapidly switch status 10 times → switch priority 10 times → open issue class → verify NO hang
* [ ] *iPhone 13 / iPad*: Open Asset Detail → rapidly switch node class 5 times → verify NO hang, subtypes always correct
* [ ] *iPhone 13 / iPad*: Open Issue Creation → select issue class → verify NO picker lag
* [ ] Verify TextEditor cursor does not jump when changing pickers on the same screen

----

h3. 6. Offline Mode

* Switch to offline mode → open Issue Detail → make changes → save → verify queued
* Switch to offline mode → open Asset Detail → make changes → save → verify queued
* Verify IR photo presigned URLs gracefully handled when offline

column1: iOS - Newly Created Task Shows "Open" Status But Changes to "Overdue" After Page Refresh

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Navigate to *Tasks* page ({{/tasks}}) or any Asset > Tasks tab

# Observe the newly created task in the list — Status shows *"Open"* ✅
# *Refresh the page* (F5 or Ctrl+R)

# Observe the status

*Expected Result:*

* After page refresh, the task status should remain *"Open"* (or "Pending" depending on workflow)

*Steps to Reproduce:*

*At 100% Zoom (BUG):*

# Open [https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai|https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai|smart-link]  in Chrome browser

# Set browser zoom to *100%* (Ctrl+0 / Cmd+0 to reset)
# Observe the Login page — look for a Language selection button/dropdown
# Scroll down the entire page

*At 70% Zoom (WORKAROUND):*

# Same Login page
# Decrease browser zoom to *70%* (Ctrl+minus / Cmd+minus multiple times)
# The *Language button now becomes visible* at the bottom of the login form or in the footer area
# Button is functional — clicking it opens language options

----

*Expected Result:*

* Language selection button should be *fully visible and accessible* at the default 100% browser zoom on all standard screen resolutions
* Button should be positioned within the visible viewport — either below the login form, in the footer, or as a dropdown in the header
* Users should NOT need to change zoom level to access language settings

*Actual Result:*

* Language button is *completely hidden/clipped* at 100% zoom
* Only appears when zoom is reduced to approximately 70%
* No indication to the user that a language option exists
* The button is rendered below the visible viewport and the page does not scroll to reveal it

h3. *Bug 1: No Redirect After Issue Deletion*

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Login to [https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai|https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai|smart-link] 
# Navigate to *Issues* page ({{/issues}})
# Click on any issue to open the Issue Detail page (e.g., {{/issues/{issue-uuid}}})
# Click the *Delete* action (via Actions menu or delete button)
# Confirm the deletion
# Observe the page after deletion

*Expected Result:*

* After successful deletion, user should be *automatically redirected to the Issues list* ({{/issues}})
* A success toast/notification should confirm: "Issue deleted successfully"
* The deleted issue should no longer appear in the Issues list

*Actual Result:*

* After deletion, user *remains on the same page* — no redirect to Issues list
* The page shows a broken/stale state of the deleted issue
* User must manually navigate back to {{/issues}} to continue working

----

h3. *Bug 2: Deleted Issue Still Accessible via Direct URL*

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Before deleting an issue, *copy its URL* from the browser address bar (e.g., {{https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai/issues/abc12345-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx}})
# Delete the issue (via Steps above)
# Open a new browser tab
# *Paste the copied URL* of the deleted issue and press Enter
# Observe the page

*Expected Result:*

* Page should show a *"Issue not found"* error message or redirect to Issues list
* The deleted issue data should NOT be accessible or visible
* HTTP response should be 404 for the deleted resource

*Actual Result:*

* The *deleted issue is still fully visible* — all its data (title, description, asset, priority, status) is displayed as if it was never deleted
* No indication that the issue has been deleted
* The issue appears to still exist in the system

column1: Frontend - Pass Accept-Language header for backend i18n support
column41:
*Objective:* Pass the user's selected language code to the backend via the {{Accept-Language}} HTTP header on all API requests, so the backend can respond with localized messages (English/French).

*Related:* [ZP-1618|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/browse/ZP-1618] (Backend i18n), [ZP-1421|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/browse/ZP-1421] (Language Selector), [ZP-1599|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/browse/ZP-1599] (Frontend - Add French language support)

----

h2. Implementation Details

* Set the {{Accept-Language}} header based on the user's language selection from the language selector
* Supported values: {{en}} (English), {{fr}} (French)
* Default: {{en}} if no language is selected

h2. Frontend-specific Approach

# *Axios interceptor/defaults* - Add the header to the Axios instance defaults or request interceptor (e.g., {{axios.defaults.headers.common['Accept-Language'] = lang}}) so all API calls include it automatically
# *Language switch handling* - When the user switches language via the language selector, update the header value in the shared API client
# *Persistence* - Persist the selected language in {{localStorage}} so it's restored on page reload

h2. Example

{noformat}Accept-Language: fr{noformat}

h2. Notes

* {{Accept-Language}} is a CORS-safelisted header - no backend CORS configuration changes are needed
* Browsers send it without requiring it in {{Access-Control-Allow-Headers}}
* If the header is missing, the backend defaults to English (no breaking change)

----

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] All API requests include the {{Accept-Language}} header with the user's selected language
* [ ] Header value updates immediately when user changes language
* [ ] Selected language persists across page reload
* [ ] Default value is {{en}} when no language has been selected

column1: Backend - Add French language support for API response messages
column41:
h3. Summary

Add French language support for API response messages based on the {{Accept-Language}} header in requests.

h3. Context

The objective is to ensure the backend responds with messages in English or French, depending on the language code provided.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* [ ] Requests with {{Accept-Language: fr}} return French messages.
* [ ] Requests with {{Accept-Language: en}} or no header return English messages.
* [ ] Unsupported language codes fall back to English.
* [ ] Permission denied, auth errors, and validation messages are translated.
* [ ] No breaking changes for existing API consumers.

h3. Other information

*Scope (Phase 1):*

* Create translation system in {{app/translations.py}}:
** Translation dictionary with {{en}} and {{fr}} keys.
** {{t(key, **kwargs)}} helper function for message lookup with placeholder support.
** Default fallback to English for missing keys or unsupported languages.
* Create i18n middleware in {{app/i18n_middleware.py}}:
** {{before_request}} hook to parse {{Accept-Language}} header.
** Set {{flask.g.lang}} for the request lifecycle.
** Supported languages: {{en}}, {{fr}} (default: {{en}}).
* Register middleware in {{app/__init__.py}}:
** Slot after {{subdomain_middleware}}, before {{mutation_middleware}}.
* Update hardcoded messages to use {{t()}} in:
** {{app/decorators/permissions.py}} (~8 messages).
** {{app/version_check_middleware.py}} (~2 messages).
** {{app/constants.py}} - deprecation note on {{ErrorMessages}} class.

*Out of Scope (Phase 2 - future):*

* Migrate inline error messages across ~44 controller files to use {{t()}}.

*Technical Details:*

* Language detection via {{Accept-Language}} HTTP header.
* Translation storage in-memory Python dict.
* Fallback to English for missing keys or unsupported language codes.
* No breaking changes; missing header defaults to English.

*Translation Keys (initial set):*

||Key||English||French||
|auth.required|Authentication required|Authentification requise|
|auth.invalid|Invalid authentication|Authentification invalide|
|auth.unauthorized|Unauthorized access|Acces non autorise|
|error.missing_field|Missing required field: {field}|Champ obligatoire manquant : {field}|
|error.not_found|{resource} not found|{resource} introuvable|
|error.invalid_input|Invalid input provided|Donnees saisies invalides|
|error.internal|An internal error occurred|Une erreur interne est survenue|
|error.database|Database operation failed|Echec de l'operation de base de donnees|
|auth.invalid_token|Invalid or expired token|Jeton invalide ou expire|
|permission.denied|You don't have permission to perform this action|Vous n'avez pas la permission d'effectuer cette action|

*Files to Create:*

* {{app/translations.py}}
* {{app/i18n_middleware.py}}

*Files to Modify:*

* {{app/__init__.py}}
* {{app/decorators/permissions.py}}
* {{app/version_check_middleware.py}}
* {{app/constants.py}}

*Testing:*

* Send requests with {{Accept-Language: fr}} and verify French response messages.
* Send requests without the header and verify English (default) messages.
* Send requests with unsupported language (e.g., {{de}}) and verify English fallback.
* Test permission denied / auth error scenarios in both languages.

column1: Frontend - Add French language support
column41: Add French language support to the frontend web application. Implement localization for all UI text strings to support English/French language switching for Quebec staff.
column212:
25/Mar/26 2:07 AM;712020:b43bccaf-6e8f-47ef-82f7-5439c6765fdb;*Related Backend Task:* [ZP-1618|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/browse/ZP-1618] - Backend i18n support

*Action Required:* Pass the user's selected language code to the backend via the {{Accept-Language}} HTTP header on all API requests.

*Implementation Details:*

* Set the {{Accept-Language}} header based on the user's language selection (from the language selector)
* Supported values: {{en}} (English), {{fr}} (French)
* Default: {{en}} if no language is selected
* This header should be added globally to the HTTP client (Axios/Fetch interceptor) so all API calls include it automatically

*Example:*

{noformat}Accept-Language: fr{noformat}

*Frontend-specific approach:*

* Add the header to the Axios instance defaults or request interceptor (e.g., {{axios.defaults.headers.common['Accept-Language'] = lang}})
* When the user switches language via the language selector, update the header value in the shared API client
* Persist the selected language in {{localStorage}} so it's restored on page reload

*Note:* {{Accept-Language}} is a CORS-safelisted header - no backend CORS configuration changes are needed. Browsers send it without requiring it in {{Access-Control-Allow-Headers}}. If the header is missing, the backend defaults to English (no breaking change).

column41:
h2. Template Editing Agent

h3. Context

Parent: ZP-1583 — Reporting Engine V2. This task implements an AI-powered agent that assists users in editing {{ReportingPageTemplate}} records. The agent leverages the Anthropic API (via the agent sidecar service on port 8899) to allow natural-language-driven template modifications.

h3. Requirements

* Integrate the AI agent into the template editing workflow
* Allow users to describe desired template changes in natural language (e.g., "add a summary table at the top", "change the header font to bold")
* Agent translates natural language instructions into template structure updates
* Preview changes before applying them to the template
* Support undo/revert for agent-made changes
* Agent should understand template structure: header, body sections, footer, page breaks, style properties
* Handle agent service unavailability gracefully with fallback to manual editing

h3. API Integration

* Communicate with the agent sidecar service ({{AGENT_SERVICE_URL}} on port 8899)
* Send template context + user instruction to the agent
* Receive structured template diff/update from agent response
* Apply updates to the template via existing CRUD endpoints

h3. Unit Test Plan

||#||Test Case||Expected Result||
|1|Send natural language edit instruction to agent|Agent returns valid template modification|
|2|Preview agent-suggested changes|Diff view shows before/after comparison|
|3|Apply agent changes to template|Template updated correctly, preview matches|
|4|Undo agent-applied change|Template reverted to previous state|
|5|Agent service unavailable|Graceful error message, manual editing still works|
|6|Ambiguous instruction sent to agent|Agent asks for clarification or provides best-guess with explanation|
|7|Large template editing|Agent handles complex templates without timeout|
|8|Concurrent edits — agent + manual|No data loss, last write wins with warning|

h3. QA Test Plan

* Verify agent can modify header, body, and footer sections independently
* Test agent with various instruction phrasings for the same change
* Confirm preview accurately reflects what will be applied
* Validate undo restores template to exact previous state
* Test agent response time under normal load (< 10s)
* Verify agent interactions are logged for debugging
* Check that agent cannot break template structure (malformed output)

column1: Z | FE | Migrate frontend auth from explicit token management to HTTP-only cookie-based auth
column41:
h3. Summary

Migrate frontend authentication from explicit token management to HTTP-only cookie-based authentication.

h3. Context

This issue involves significant changes to the authentication process in the frontend application. The goal is to enhance security by using HTTP-only cookies instead of storing tokens in memory.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* Refactor the auth store in {{src/store/auth.js}}:
** Remove {{accessToken}} and {{idToken}} from Zustand state.
** Eliminate the {{setTokens()}} action.
** Modify {{login()}} and {{respondToChallenge()}} to rely on cookies set by the backend.
** Update {{getCurrentUser()}} to use cookie auth.
** Change {{logout()}} to send cookies via {{credentials: 'include'}}.
** Implement a new {{initializeAuth()}} flow that tries {{GET /me}} first.
* Update request handling in {{src/utils/apiClient.js}}:
** Remove {{Authorization: Bearer}} header injection in {{request()}}.
** Ensure {{refreshToken()}} does not store returned tokens.
** Simplify {{processQueue()}} for automatic cookie handling.
* Modify {{src/App.jsx}} to check the {{isAuthenticated}} flag instead of {{accessToken}}.
* Adjust {{src/pages/ReportingPages.jsx}} to remove {{Authorization}} header from SSE fetch calls and add {{credentials: 'include'}}.
* Update {{src/store/ui.js}} to remove {{accessToken}} and {{idToken}} from user context.

h3. Other information

* Files changed: {{auth.js}}, {{apiClient.js}}, {{App.jsx}}, {{ReportingPages.jsx}}, {{ui.js}}.
* Suggested Jira title: Migrate FE auth from explicit token management to HTTP-only cookie-based authentication.

h3. Context

h3. Requirements

* Ensure backward compatibility with existing reports during migration
* Add template versioning support so existing reports continue to reference their original template version
* Remove deprecated/inline template definitions after migration
* Update any template references in ReportingConfig records

h3. API Integration

* Update report generation endpoints to resolve templates from the centralized table
* Ensure template lookup is performant (indexed queries)
* Handle missing template gracefully with fallback or clear error

h3. Unit Test Plan

||#||Test Case||Expected Result||
|1|Generate report using migrated template|Report renders identically to pre-migration output|
|2|Generate report with versioned template|Correct template version used for rendering|
|3|Reference a non-existent template ID|Meaningful error returned, no crash|
|4|Create new template in centralized table|Template available for report generation|
|5|Update template — existing reports unaffected|Old reports use original version, new reports use updated version|
|6|Delete template referenced by active reports|Deletion blocked or soft-deleted with warning|
|7|List all templates with pagination|Paginated list returned correctly|

h3. QA Test Plan

* Verify all existing report types generate correctly post-refactor
* Compare pre-migration and post-migration report output for visual parity
* Confirm new templates can be created and used in report generation
* Test template CRUD operations from the UI
* Validate no orphaned template references exist
* Check report generation performance is not degraded

h2. Reporting Config Explorer / Tester

h3. Context

Parent: ZP-1583 — Reporting Engine V2. This task introduces a UI for exploring and testing ReportingConfig records. Admins need a way to browse, search, preview, and validate reporting configurations before they are used in production report generation.

h3. Requirements

* Build an explorer/tester interface for ReportingConfig records
* List all available reporting configs with search and filter capabilities
* Allow previewing a config's query shape, parameters, and output structure
* Provide a "test run" mode that executes a config against sample data and displays results
* Show config metadata: name, description, associated form types, version, created/updated timestamps
* Validate config syntax and flag errors before saving
* Support JSON viewer for raw config inspection

h3. API Integration

* Reuse existing ReportingConfig CRUD endpoints
* Integrate with query execution endpoint for test runs
* Handle large result sets with pagination
* Display meaningful error messages for failed test executions

h3. Unit Test Plan

||#||Test Case||Expected Result||
|1|Load config explorer page|All reporting configs listed with metadata|
|2|Search configs by name|Filtered results match search term|
|3|Filter configs by form type|Only configs for selected form type shown|
|4|Click a config to preview|Config details panel shows query shape and parameters|
|5|Run test execution on valid config|Results displayed in table/JSON format|
|6|Run test execution on invalid config|Error message displayed with details|
|7|View raw JSON of config|JSON viewer shows full config structure|
|8|Empty state — no configs exist|Appropriate empty state message shown|

h3. QA Test Plan

* Verify config explorer loads without errors on the reporting page
* Confirm search returns accurate results and handles special characters
* Test pagination when more than 20 configs exist
* Validate test execution returns correct data for known configs
* Verify error handling for malformed configs

{noformat}# Agent Sidecar ECS Setup Checklist

Per-environment AWS setup required before the CI/CD workflow can deploy the agent sidecar.

Reference implementation: **dev** (completed 2026-04-06)

---

## 1. ECR Repository

```bash
aws ecr create-repository --repository-name eg-pz-{env}-agent-ohio --region us-east-2
```

## 2. Security Group

```bash
# Create SG in the environment's VPC
aws ec2 create-security-group \
  --group-name eg-pz-{env}-agent-sg-ohio \
  --description "Agent sidecar ECS service - {env}" \
  --vpc-id {VPC_ID} \
  --region us-east-2

# Inbound: port 8899 from backend SG only
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
  --group-id {AGENT_SG_ID} \
  --protocol tcp --port 8899 \
  --source-group {BACKEND_SG_ID} \
  --region us-east-2

# Outbound: HTTPS for Anthropic API
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-egress \
  --group-id {AGENT_SG_ID} \
  --protocol tcp --port 443 \
  --cidr 0.0.0.0/0 \
  --region us-east-2

# Outbound: port 5001 to backend SG (future MCP)
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-egress \
  --group-id {AGENT_SG_ID} \
  --protocol tcp --port 5001 \
  --source-group {BACKEND_SG_ID} \
  --region us-east-2

# Remove default allow-all egress rule
aws ec2 describe-security-group-rules \
  --filter Name=group-id,Values={AGENT_SG_ID} \
  --region us-east-2 \
  --query 'SecurityGroupRules[?IsEgress==`true` && IpProtocol==`-1`].SecurityGroupRuleId'
# Then:
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-egress \
  --group-id {AGENT_SG_ID} \
  --security-group-rule-ids {DEFAULT_EGRESS_RULE_ID} \
  --region us-east-2
```

## 3. Cloud Map Service

```bash
# Find the environment's namespace ID
aws servicediscovery list-namespaces --region us-east-2 \
  --query 'Namespaces[?Name==`egpz.{env}`].Id'

# Register "agent" service
aws servicediscovery create-service \
  --name agent \
  --namespace-id {NAMESPACE_ID} \
  --dns-config 'NamespaceId={NAMESPACE_ID},DnsRecords=[{Type=A,TTL=10}]' \
  --health-check-custom-config FailureThreshold=1 \
  --region us-east-2
```

## 4. Secrets Manager

```bash
# Copy ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from the backend secret
ANTHROPIC_KEY=$(aws secretsmanager get-secret-value \
  --secret-id {BACKEND_SECRET_ARN} --region us-east-2 \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.loads(json.loads(sys.stdin.read())['SecretString'])['ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'])")

aws secretsmanager create-secret \
  --name eg-pz-{env}-agent-ohio \
  --description "Agent sidecar service secrets - {env}" \
  --secret-string "{\"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\":\"$ANTHROPIC_KEY\"}" \
  --region us-east-2
```

## 5. IAM Policy Update

```bash
# Get the current policy
aws iam get-role-policy \
  --role-name eg-pz-{env}-ecs-ohio-task-execution-role \
  --policy-name eg-pz-{env}-ecs-ohio-task-execution-secrets-policy

# Add the new agent secret ARN to the Resource array, then:
aws iam put-role-policy \
  --role-name eg-pz-{env}-ecs-ohio-task-execution-role \
  --policy-name eg-pz-{env}-ecs-ohio-task-execution-secrets-policy \
  --policy-document '{...updated policy with agent secret ARN...}'
```

## 6. CloudWatch Log Group

```bash
aws logs create-log-group \
  --log-group-name /ecs/eg-pz-{env}-agent-ohio \
  --region us-east-2
```

## 7. Task Definition

```bash
aws ecs register-task-definition --region us-east-2 --cli-input-json '{
  "family": "eg-pz-{env}-agent-td-ohio",
  "networkMode": "awsvpc",
  "requiresCompatibilities": ["FARGATE"],
  "cpu": "512",
  "memory": "1024",
  "executionRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::165183897698:role/eg-pz-{env}-ecs-ohio-task-execution-role",
  "taskRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::165183897698:role/eg-pz-{env}-ecs-ohio-task-role",
  "containerDefinitions": [
    {
      "name": "eg-pz-{env}-agent-ohio",
      "image": "165183897698.dkr.ecr.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/eg-pz-{env}-agent-ohio:latest",
      "essential": true,
      "portMappings": [
        {"containerPort": 8899, "hostPort": 8899, "protocol": "tcp"}
      ],
      "environment": [
        {"name": "AGENT_SERVICE_PORT", "value": "8899"},
        {"name": "AGENT_BACKEND_URL", "value": "http://agent.egpz.{env}:5001"},
        {"name": "AGENT_API_BASE_URL", "value": "http://agent.egpz.{env}:5001"}
      ],
      "secrets": [
        {
          "name": "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY",
          "valueFrom": "{AGENT_SECRET_ARN}:ANTHROPIC_API_KEY::"
        }
      ],
      "logConfiguration": {
        "logDriver": "awslogs",
        "options": {
          "awslogs-group": "/ecs/eg-pz-{env}-agent-ohio",
          "awslogs-region": "us-east-2",
          "awslogs-stream-prefix": "agent"
        }
      },
      "healthCheck": {
        "command": ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -f http://localhost:8899/health || exit 1"],
        "interval": 30,
        "timeout": 5,
        "retries": 3,
        "startPeriod": 10
      }
    }
  ]
}'
```

## 8. ECS Service

```bash
aws ecs create-service --region us-east-2 \
  --cluster eg-pz-{env}-ecs-ohio \
  --service-name eg-pz-{env}-agent-service-ohio \
  --task-definition eg-pz-{env}-agent-td-ohio:1 \
  --desired-count 1 \
  --launch-type FARGATE \
  --network-configuration 'awsvpcConfiguration={subnets=[{SUBNET_1},{SUBNET_2},{SUBNET_3}],securityGroups=[{AGENT_SG_ID}],assignPublicIp=ENABLED}' \
  --service-registries 'registryArn={CLOUD_MAP_SERVICE_ARN}'
```

## 9. Backend Secret Update

```bash
# Add AGENT_SERVICE_URL to the backend's Secrets Manager secret
# Fetch current, add key, update:
#   AGENT_SERVICE_URL = http://agent.egpz.{env}:8899
```

## 10. Backend Task Definition Update

```bash
# Add to the backend task definition's secrets array:
# {
#   "name": "AGENT_SERVICE_URL",
#   "valueFrom": "{BACKEND_SECRET_ARN}:AGENT_SERVICE_URL::"
# }
# Register new revision, then update the backend ECS service to use it.
```

## 11. GitHub Secrets

| Secret | Value |
|--------|-------|
| `ECR_REPOSITORY_AGENT_{ENV}` | `eg-pz-{env}-agent-ohio` |
| `ECS_AGENT_SERVICE_NAME_{ENV}` | `eg-pz-{env}-agent-service-ohio` |

## 12. Workflow Update

Add agent build + deploy steps to the environment's job in `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` (copy from the dev job pattern).

---

## Dev Environment Reference Values

| Resource | Value |
|----------|-------|
| ECR Repo | `eg-pz-dev-agent-ohio` |
| Security Group | `sg-019a1f246d214a2a2` (eg-pz-dev-agent-sg-ohio) |
| Cloud Map Service | `srv-zfp632znm3wkmogi` in `egpz.dev` (ns-7atokwlz6ktoh3ut) |
| Agent Secret | `eg-pz-dev-agent-ohio-MZlKzJ` |
| Log Group | `/ecs/eg-pz-dev-agent-ohio` |
| Task Definition | `eg-pz-dev-agent-td-ohio:1` |
| ECS Service | `eg-pz-dev-agent-service-ohio` |
| Backend SG | `sg-059f8e285100f3310` |
| VPC | `vpc-076cda6609559fe6e` |
| Subnets | `subnet-0d272f94aa6d910ee`, `subnet-09de044d3b50c8352`, `subnet-050c88e6da4540de7` |

column1: FE/BE Implement Folder Structure and Storage Management for Attachments Module
column41:
h2. Overview

The Attachments module currently stores files in a flat list per site, with per-file visibility (Internal/Public), a document type tag (Third-Party Report, SLD, Floor Plan, General Documentation, Product Manual, Reference, Other), and no storage controls. This story introduces two capabilities:

# *Folder hierarchy* — virtual folders (DB-only, S3 keys unchanged) allowing users to organize attachments into nested structures per site, with cascading visibility.
# *Storage management* — per-customer configurable storage quotas and file size limits, with usage tracking visible at the customer, site, and upload levels.

The existing document *type* field is retained as metadata (classifies _what_ a file is). Folders are a separate organizational axis (_where_ a file lives). A file can be a "Floor Plan" (type) inside a "Permits & Compliance" folder (location). Users can filter by type within any folder.

----

h2. Data Model

h3. New table: {{attachment_folders}}

||Field||Type||Description||
|{{id}}|UUID|Primary key|
|{{site_id}}|UUID|FK to {{sites}}|
|{{parent_folder_id}}|UUID (nullable)|FK to self; null = root-level folder|
|{{name}}|VARCHAR(255)|Folder display name|
|{{visibility}}|ENUM({{Internal}}, {{Public}})|Cascades to all children|
|{{sort_order}}|INTEGER|Display ordering within parent|
|{{created_by}}|UUID|FK to {{users}}|
|{{created_at}}|TIMESTAMP (TZ)|Auto-set|
|{{updated_at}}|TIMESTAMP (TZ)|Auto-update|
|{{deleted_at}}|TIMESTAMP (TZ, nullable)|Soft delete|

h3. New table: {{customer_storage_config}}

||Field||Type||Description||
|{{id}}|UUID|Primary key|
|{{customer_id}}|UUID|FK to {{customers}}, UNIQUE|
|{{max_file_size_mb}}|INTEGER|Max allowed size per individual file upload|
|{{storage_quota_mb}}|BIGINT|Total storage cap across all sites for this customer|
|{{storage_used_bytes}}|BIGINT|Cached aggregate of all attachment file sizes; updated on upload/delete|
|{{created_at}}|TIMESTAMP (TZ)|Auto-set|
|{{updated_at}}|TIMESTAMP (TZ)|Auto-update|

h3. Modify existing {{attachments}} table

* Add {{folder_id}} UUID (nullable, FK to {{attachment_folders}}). Null = root/unfiled.
* When {{folder_id}} is set, the file's effective visibility is inherited from its folder. The file-level visibility column becomes read-only (derived).
* The existing {{type}} field (Third-Party Report, SLD, Floor Plan, General Documentation, Product Manual, Reference, Other) is *unchanged* and remains independent metadata. Type classifies _what_ a file is; folder determines _where_ it lives.
* Confirm {{file_size_bytes}} (or equivalent) already exists and is reliably populated on upload. If not, add and backfill from S3 metadata.

h3. Constraints

* Unlimited nesting depth (self-referential tree via {{parent_folder_id}})
* Folder names must be unique within the same parent + site combination
* Deleting a folder is blocked if it contains files or subfolders
* Every customer MUST have a {{customer_storage_config}} row before uploads are allowed

----

h2. Storage Management

h3. Configuration

* Each customer has individually configured storage limits in {{customer_storage_config}}.
* Both {{max_file_size_mb}} and {{storage_quota_mb}} are set per customer by platform admins. There are no system-wide defaults — limits must be explicitly configured for each customer.
* Limits are editable via customer admin settings.

h3. Enforcement

* *Per-file limit:* Upload is rejected with a clear error message if the file exceeds the customer's {{max_file_size_mb}}. Validation occurs client-side (pre-upload) and server-side (on receipt).
* *Quota limit:* Upload is rejected if {{storage_used_bytes}} + incoming file size would exceed {{storage_quota_mb}}. Server-side enforcement is authoritative.
* Soft-deleted attachments do NOT count toward quota. Restoring a soft-deleted file re-checks quota before allowing restore.

h3. Tracking

* {{storage_used_bytes}} is updated atomically on every upload and delete operation.
* If {{storage_used_bytes}} drifts (e.g., due to manual S3 operations), provide an admin endpoint to recalculate from actual attachment records.

h3. Visibility

Storage usage is surfaced at three levels:

*1. Customer Admin Settings:*

* Display: total quota, total used, percentage used, remaining
* Visual: progress bar with color thresholds (green < 70%, yellow 70–90%, red > 90%)
* Per-site breakdown table showing each site's contribution to total usage
* Edit controls for {{max_file_size_mb}} and {{storage_quota_mb}}

*2. Site-level Attachments page:*

* Subtle indicator in the page header showing site storage used and customer-wide remaining quota
* Format: "Site: 245 MB | Customer: 3.2 GB / 5 GB used"

*3. Upload dialog:*

* Show max allowed file size (e.g., "Max file size: 50 MB")
* Show remaining customer quota (e.g., "1.8 GB remaining")
* If file selected exceeds either limit, show inline error before upload attempt
* If remaining quota is below 10%, show a warning banner

----

h2. Default Folder Template

On site creation (and backfilled for existing sites), auto-generate these root-level folders:

||Folder||Default Visibility||
|Coordination Studies|Internal|
|As-Built Drawings|Internal|
|Permits & Compliance|Internal|
|Site Photos|Public|
|Proposals & Contracts|Internal|

The default folder set is configurable at the customer level. Customers can customize which folders are auto-created for new sites. Individual site users can rename or delete default folders after creation — they are a starting point, not enforced structure.

----

h2. Visibility Rules

* Folder visibility is the single source of truth for all files within it.
* Changing a folder's visibility updates the effective visibility of all direct and nested child files and subfolders.
* Files at root level (no folder) retain independent per-file visibility controls.
* Moving a file into a folder automatically adopts the folder's visibility.
* Moving a file out of a folder to root restores independent visibility (defaults to the folder's value at time of move).

----

h2. UI/UX

h3. Navigation

* Replace flat file list with folder tree + file list layout.
* Left panel or inline folder tree with expand/collapse. Root level shows an "Unfiled" pseudo-folder for files without a folder.
* Breadcrumb bar above file list (e.g., {{Site > Permits & Compliance > 2025}}). Truncate gracefully for deep nesting (show first and last two levels with ellipsis).
* Clicking a folder filters the file list to that folder's contents.

h3. Folder CRUD

* "New Folder" button available at root and within any folder.
* Kebab menu on folders: Rename, Move, Change Visibility, Delete.
* Delete is blocked when folder contains files or subfolders. User must move or delete contents first.

h3. File Operations

* "Move to Folder" action on file rows (single and bulk select).
* Upload Attachment flow adds a "Destination Folder" dropdown defaulting to current folder context.
* Visibility column on file rows shows inherited value with a lock icon when folder-controlled. Root-level files show editable visibility as today.
* Type filter remains available within any folder view.

h3. Search

* Search operates across all folders within the site.
* Results display folder path as context (e.g., {{Permits & Compliance > 2025 > filename.pdf}}).
* Filterable by type and folder.

----

h2. API Endpoints

h3. Folders

||Method||Endpoint||Description||
|POST|{{/api/v1/sites/:site_id/folders}}|Create folder|
|GET|{{/api/v1/sites/:site_id/folders}}|List folder tree for site|
|PATCH|{{/api/v1/sites/:site_id/folders/:id}}|Rename, move, change visibility|
|DELETE|{{/api/v1/sites/:site_id/folders/:id}}|Soft-delete (blocked if non-empty)|

h3. File Operations

||Method||Endpoint||Description||
|PATCH|{{/api/v1/attachments/:id/move}}|Move file to folder or to root|
|POST|{{/api/v1/attachments/bulk-move}}|Bulk move files to a folder|

h3. Storage

||Method||Endpoint||Description||
|GET|{{/api/v1/customers/:customer_id/storage}}|Get storage config, usage, and per-site breakdown|
|PATCH|{{/api/v1/customers/:customer_id/storage}}|Update storage limits (admin only)|
|POST|{{/api/v1/customers/:customer_id/storage/recalculate}}|Recalculate {{storage_used_bytes}} from actual records (admin only)|

----

h2. Migration Strategy

* Create {{attachment_folders}} table.
* Create {{customer_storage_config}} table. Populate a row for every existing customer (limits must be set by admin before uploads resume — or set temporary permissive values and flag for review).
* Add {{folder_id}} column to {{attachments}} as nullable (no breaking change).
* Existing {{type}} field is untouched — no migration needed.
* Confirm/add {{file_size_bytes}} on {{attachments}} and backfill from S3 {{Content-Length}} metadata if missing.
* Compute and populate {{storage_used_bytes}} for each customer from existing attachment records.
* Run backfill job: generate default folder set for every existing site.
* Existing files remain at root (unfiled). No forced file migration.

----

h2. Out of Scope

* Drag-and-drop file-to-folder (stretch goal, separate ticket)
* Folder-level permissions beyond visibility (e.g., per-role edit/view)
* Cross-site shared folders
* Folder-level storage quotas (only customer-level quotas in v1)
* Per-site storage quotas
* Changes to the existing document type taxonomy

----

{quote}⚠️ *Cross-System Impact:* Run migration on BOTH Egalvanic + BCES-IQ databases{quote}

*Cross-System Checklist:*

* [ ] Changes tested on DEV
* [ ] Changes tested on QA
* [ ] BCES-IQ migration planned (DB change — {{attachment_folders}}, {{customer_storage_config}}, {{attachments.folder_id}})
* [ ] Verified both PROD environments after deployment

📄 Reference: [Cross-System Validation Guide|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENGR/pages/102826000]

----

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] New {{attachment_folders}} table exists with self-referential parent hierarchy supporting unlimited nesting
* [ ] New {{customer_storage_config}} table exists with per-customer {{max_file_size_mb}}, {{storage_quota_mb}}, and {{storage_used_bytes}}
* [ ] Every customer has a {{customer_storage_config}} row (backfilled for existing customers)
* [ ] {{attachments}} table has nullable {{folder_id}} FK column
* [ ] Existing {{type}} field is unchanged and functions independently of folder assignment
* [ ] {{file_size_bytes}} is reliably stored on every attachment record (backfilled if needed)
* [ ] Default folder set (Coordination Studies, As-Built Drawings, Permits & Compliance, Site Photos, Proposals & Contracts) is auto-created on new site creation
* [ ] Default folder set is configurable at the customer level
* [ ] Existing sites are backfilled with default folders
* [ ] Folder CRUD operations (create, rename, move, delete) function correctly
* [ ] Folder delete is blocked when folder contains files or subfolders
* [ ] Folder visibility cascades to all child files and nested subfolders
* [ ] Moving a file into a folder adopts folder visibility; moving to root restores independent visibility
* [ ] Upload is rejected client-side and server-side if file exceeds customer's {{max_file_size_mb}}
* [ ] Upload is rejected server-side if cumulative storage would exceed customer's {{storage_quota_mb}}
* [ ] {{storage_used_bytes}} updates atomically on upload and delete
* [ ] Storage usage is displayed in customer admin settings with per-site breakdown and progress bar
* [ ] Storage usage is displayed on site-level Attachments page header
* [ ] Upload dialog shows max file size, remaining quota, and inline errors when limits exceeded
* [ ] Warning banner appears in upload dialog when remaining quota is below 10%
* [ ] Admin recalculate endpoint correctly recomputes {{storage_used_bytes}} from actual records
* [ ] UI displays folder tree with expand/collapse and breadcrumb navigation
* [ ] Type filter works within any folder view
* [ ] Upload flow includes destination folder selection
* [ ] Single and bulk file move to folder works
* [ ] Search returns results across all folders with folder path context displayed
* [ ] All API endpoints return correct responses and enforce constraints
* [ ] Changes verified on BCES-IQ environment

column1: Display Web App Version at the bottom of the Left Navigation Menu
column41:
h2. Overview

Add the application semantic version number (e.g., v1.18.2) to the bottom of the left sidebar navigation menu so users can easily identify the deployed version.

h2. Current State

* The left sidebar is rendered in {{src/components/Layout.jsx}}
* The app uses a build timestamp ({{__APP_VERSION__}}) defined in {{vite.config.js}} for update detection
* {{package.json}} has {{"version": "0.0.0"}} (not actively maintained)
* No version is currently visible to users in the UI

h2. Requirements

* Display the semantic version (e.g., v1.18.2) at the bottom of the left sidebar menu
* Version should be visible in both expanded and collapsed sidebar states
* Version text should be subtle/muted so it doesn't distract from navigation
* Version should be readable but secondary to navigation items

h2. Technical Approach

# Define {{VITE_APP_VERSION}} env variable or use {{__APP_VERSION__}} build-time define in {{vite.config.js}} with a semantic version
# In {{Layout.jsx}}, add a version display element at the bottom of the sidebar Drawer content
# Handle both expanded (show full "v1.18.2") and collapsed (show "v1.18" or icon) sidebar states

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Version number is visible at the bottom of the left sidebar
* [ ] Version displays correctly in both expanded and collapsed sidebar states
* [ ] Version updates automatically with each build/release
* [ ] Version text has muted/subtle styling

column41:
*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Login to [https://acme.dev.egalvanic.ai|https://acme.dev.egalvanic.ai|smart-link] 
# Navigate to Accounts > Egalvanic > Goals tab
# Observe the Revenue goal: Operator = "=", Target = $7, *Current = $0*, Cadence = Weekly, Status = *"Needs Attention"*
# Click the Edit (pencil) icon on that goal
# In the Edit Goal modal, change *only* the Time Period to "Custom" with Start Date = 03/16/2026, Due Date = 12/31/2026
# Do NOT change Goal Type, Operator, or Target Value
# Click *"Save Changes"*
# Observe the goal row after save

----

*Expected Result:*

* Current value should remain *$0* (or recalculate correctly for the new period)
* Only the Cadence and Period columns should change to reflect the new time period

*Actual Result:*

* Current value *silently changed from $0 to $426,736* — a completely different number
* The user never touched the Current value — this was an automatic and incorrect recalculation
* $426,736 appears to be the *total platform revenue across ALL accounts/ALL time* — not specific to this goal's period or account

The endpoint is GET /issue/<issue_id>/status-history. Here's what to share:

{noformat}curl -X GET \                                                                                                                                                 
    '<https://<api-base-url>>/issue/3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6/status-history' \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <access_token>' \
    -H 'X-Subdomain: <subdomain>'{noformat}

  Response — 200 OK (with history)

  

{noformat}{
    "success": true,
    "data": [
      {
        "id": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
        "issue_id": "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6",

        "changed_at": "2026-03-18T09:00:00Z",
        "change_reason": null

----

* Results are ordered by changed_at descending (most recent first)
* old_status: null means it's the initial status set at issue creation
* changed_by_name returns full name (first + last); falls back to email if no name set; null if user not found

column1: Web — Goals Table Has No Visible Horizontal Scrollbar — Edit Button and First Column Hidden Off-Screen
column41:
*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Login to [https://acme.dev.egalvanic.ai|https://acme.dev.egalvanic.ai|smart-link] 
# Navigate to *Goals* page ({{/goals}})
# Observe the Goals table — 7 goals are listed (1–7 of 7)
# Look at the *first column* — Goal Names are truncated: shows only "c", "nft", "c", "nft", "EG-ACME A", "EG-ACME A", "c" — full names not visible
# Look at the *right side* of the table — there is no visible horizontal scrollbar
# Try to find the *Edit button* or *Actions column* for any goal — it is NOT visible
# A new user would have *no idea* that they can scroll right to see more columns or an Edit button

----

*Expected Result:*

* A visible *horizontal scrollbar* should appear at the bottom of the table when content overflows
* OR a *scroll indicator/arrow* on the right edge showing "more content →"
* The *Edit/Actions button* should be in a *fixed/sticky last column* that is always visible regardless of horizontal scroll position
* The *Goal Name (first column)* should also be *sticky/frozen* so it remains visible while scrolling right
* All column data should be fully readable without horizontal scrolling — or at minimum, the scroll affordance should be obvious

*Actual Result:*

* *No horizontal scrollbar* visible on the table
* *No scroll indicator* — nothing tells the user there is more content to the right
* *Edit/Actions button is completely hidden* — users cannot edit or manage their goals
* *First column (Goal Name) is severely truncated* — shows only 1-3 characters ("c", "nft") making goals unidentifiable
* *Column headers are also truncated*: "Operat..." instead of "Operator"
* User must know to use *Shift+scroll* or *trackpad horizontal swipe* to discover hidden content — new users will not know this

!image-20260318-102450.png|width=671,alt="image-20260318-102450.png"!

column1: Web - Create Goal Allows Start Date After Due Date — No Date Range Validation
column41:
*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Login 
# Navigate to *Opportunities* page
# Click *"+ Create Goal"* button
# Fill in the form:
#* Goal Scope: *Account*
#* Account: *Default EG-ACME Account*
#* Goal Type: *Overdue Work Orders*
#* Operator: *Less Than*
#* Target Value: *-9* (also a bug — negative accepted, see related ticket)
# In Time Period section, click *"Custom"*
# In *Start Date* field, enter: *03/31/2026*
# In *Due Date* field, enter: *03/03/2026*
# Observe — Start Date (March 31) is 28 days AFTER Due Date (March 3)
# Click *"Create"* button

----

*Expected Result:*

* Form should *immediately show a validation error* when Start Date > Due Date: "Start Date cannot be after Due Date"
* Both date fields should show *red border* indicating the error
* The *"Create" button should be disabled* until the date range is valid
* Alternatively, the Due Date picker should *not allow selecting a date before the Start Date* (and vice versa — Start Date should not allow selecting a date after Due Date)

*Actual Result:*

* *No validation error* appears — form looks completely normal
* Both date fields show no red border, no warning, no tooltip
* *"Create" button is fully active* and clickable
* The goal is created with an *impossible date range* (starts after it's due)
* The created goal will show in the dashboard with a nonsensical timeframe

!image-20260318-074850.png|width=560,alt="image-20260318-074850.png"!

column1: Web - Create Goal Allows Negative Target Value (-5555) — No Input Validation
column41:
*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Login to [https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai|https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai|smart-link] 
# Navigate to *Opportunities* page
# Click the *"+ Create Goal"* button (visible at 75% zoom — see related bug)
# In the Create Goal modal, fill in the following:
#* Goal Scope: *Account*
#* Account: *Default EG-ACME Account*
#* Goal Type: *Pull Through Work*
#* Operator: *Greater Than*
#* Target Value: Type *-5555*
# Select Time Period: *This Week* (Mar 16, 2026 – Mar 22, 2026)
# Click *"Create"* button

----

*Expected Result:*

* Target Value field should *reject negative numbers* — show validation error: "Target value must be a positive number"
* Target Value field should have a *minimum value of 1* (or 0 depending on business logic)
* Target Value field should have a *reasonable maximum limit* to prevent absurd targets
* The "Create" button should be *disabled* or show validation error until a valid positive target is entered

*Actual Result:*

* *-5555 is accepted* with no validation error, no red border, no warning
* The goal is created successfully with Target Value = -5555
* The goal "Pull Through Work *Greater Than -5555*" is logically meaningless — it is always achieved because any work value > -5555
* No min/max boundary check on the field at all

!image-20260318-072721.png|width=671,alt="image-20260318-072721.png"!

column1: Web - "Create Goals" Button Only Visible at 75% Browser Zoom — Hidden at Default 100% Zoom
column41:
*Steps to Reproduce:*

*At 100% Zoom (BUG):*

# Login
# Navigate to the Goals page
# Set browser zoom to *100%* (Ctrl+0 to reset to default)
# Look for the *"Create Goals"* button
# Scroll the page — button is *NOT visible* anywhere

*At 75% Zoom (WORKAROUND):*

# Same page as above
# Decrease browser zoom to *75%* (Ctrl + minus key, or Ctrl+scroll down)
# The *"Create Goals"* button now *becomes visible*
# Button is functional — clicking it opens the create goals form

----

*Expected Result:*

* "Create Goals" button should be *fully visible and accessible* at the default 100% browser zoom
* Button should be positioned in the toolbar area (top right, near other action buttons) or in a clearly visible location
* Users should NOT need to change zoom level to access core functionality

*Actual Result:*

* Button is *completely hidden/clipped* at 100% zoom
* Only appears when zoom is reduced to 75%
* No indication to the user that the button exists or that they need to zoom out

!image-20260318-071638.png|width=671,alt="image-20260318-071638.png"!

column1: Create separate Cognito user pools for ACME Dev, QA, and Stage environments
column41:
h2. Objective

Create dedicated AWS Cognito user pools for ACME in the Dev, QA, and Stage environments instead of sharing a single user pool.

h2. Scope

* *Dev* – Create a new Cognito user pool for ACME Dev
* *QA* – Create a new Cognito user pool for ACME QA
* *Stage* – Create a new Cognito user pool for ACME Stage
* *Production* – No changes; keep the existing user pool as-is

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* Each non-production environment (Dev, QA, Stage) has its own isolated ACME Cognito user pool
* Application configuration is updated to point to the correct user pool per environment
* Production user pool remains unchanged and unaffected

column1: Z | BE | Monitor and log MFA events
column41:
As a security engineer, I want to implement monitoring and logging for all MFA-related events, so that suspicious activities can be detected and audited.

Acceptance Criteria:

* All MFA enrollment, verification, and recovery events are logged.
* Logs include relevant metadata (user, timestamp, method, outcome).
* Alerts are configured for suspicious or failed MFA attempts.
* Logging and monitoring are tested and verified in staging.

column1: Z | BE | Document MFA setup and troubleshooting steps
column41:
As a technical writer, I want to document the steps for setting up and troubleshooting MFA, so that users and support staff can easily resolve issues related to MFA.

Acceptance Criteria:

* Documentation covers setup, enrollment, verification, and recovery.
* Troubleshooting steps for common issues are included.
* Documentation is reviewed by engineering and support teams.
* Final documentation is published in the knowledge base.

column1: Z | BE | Test MFA implementation for security vulnerabilities
column41:
As a QA engineer, I want to thoroughly test the MFA implementation for potential security vulnerabilities, so that we can ensure the system is robust and secure.

Acceptance Criteria:

* Security tests cover all MFA endpoints and flows.
* Common vulnerabilities (e.g., brute force, replay attacks) are tested.
* Issues found are documented and prioritized for resolution.
* All critical vulnerabilities are resolved before release.

column1: Z | FE | Create MFA recovery and backup options
column41:
As a user, I want to have secure recovery and backup options for MFA, so that I can regain access to my account if I lose my primary MFA device.

Acceptance Criteria:

* Users can set up backup codes or alternative MFA methods.
* Recovery options are clearly explained and easy to use.
* Security measures are in place to prevent unauthorized recovery.
* Recovery flows are tested for usability and security.

column1: Z | FE | Develop MFA enrollment process
column41:
As a user, I want a secure and user-friendly process to enroll in MFA, so that I can add an extra layer of security to my account.

Acceptance Criteria:

* Users can enroll in any supported MFA method from their account settings.
* The enrollment process includes clear instructions and feedback.
* Users receive confirmation upon successful enrollment.
* The process is accessible and responsive across devices.

column1: Z | BE | Integrate MFA with user authentication flow
column41:
As a user, I want MFA to be seamlessly integrated into the login and account recovery flows, so that my account remains secure during authentication and recovery processes.

Acceptance Criteria:

* MFA is required during login and account recovery as per security policy.
* Users are prompted for MFA after entering correct credentials.
* Failed MFA attempts are logged and handled securely.
* Integration is tested end-to-end with all supported MFA methods.

column1: Z | BE | Implement backend support for MFA methods
column41:
As a developer, I want to implement backend logic for supporting multiple MFA methods (e.g., SMS, authenticator app, email), so that users can choose their preferred method for authentication.

Acceptance Criteria:

* Backend APIs for enrolling, verifying, and managing MFA methods are implemented.
* Support for at least SMS, authenticator app, and email MFA is provided.
* All MFA data is securely stored and transmitted.
* Unit and integration tests are written and pass successfully.

column1: Z | FE | Design MFA user interface
column41:
As a user, I want a clear and intuitive interface for MFA enrollment and verification, so that I can easily secure my account with minimal friction.

Acceptance Criteria:

* MFA enrollment and verification screens are designed according to UX guidelines.
* All supported MFA methods are represented in the UI.
* Error and success states are clearly communicated to the user.
* Designs are reviewed and approved by the product owner.

31/Mar/26 7:33 AM;712020:9881bb6e-cbf7-47a6-bf7a-838a383b2866;[~accountid:712020:9c81fbcc-e424-4396-a25b-01f80fc942eb] 

h1. IR Photos Tab — QA Test Plan

h2. Prerequisites

* A session with existing IR photos (for viewing/edit/delete tests)
* A session with no IR photos (for empty state tests)
* An active (open) session and a closed session
* Device with photos in the photo library
* Image files accessible via the Files app
* Stable internet connection (for online tests)
* Access to toggle offline mode

----

h2. 1. Tab Navigation & Layout

h3. TC-1.1: IR Photos tab is visible in session details

*Steps:*

# Open any session from the session list
# Observe the bottom tab bar

*Expected:* 6 tabs visible — Details, Assets, Tasks, Issues, *IR*, Files. "IR" tab shows a camera icon.

h3. TC-1.2: Tap IR tab to navigate

*Steps:*

# Open a session with existing IR photos
# Tap the "IR" tab

*Expected:* IR Photos tab loads. Header shows "{count} IR Photos" with an "Upload IR Photos" button.

h3. TC-1.3: Swipe between tabs

*Steps:*

# Navigate to the Issues tab (index 3)
# Swipe left

*Expected:* IR Photos tab appears. Swiping left again shows the Files tab.

h3. TC-1.4: Files/Attachments tab still works at new index

*Steps:*

# Tap the "Files" tab (last tab)

*Expected:* Attachments tab loads correctly. Upload, download, delete attachment functionality still works as before.

----

h2. 2. IR Photos List Display

h3. TC-2.1: Empty state

*Steps:*

# Open a session with no IR photos
# Navigate to the IR tab

*Expected:* Empty state shown — camera icon, "No IR Photos" text, descriptive message. No crash.

h3. TC-2.2: Photo list with existing photos

*Steps:*

# Open a session with IR photos
# Navigate to the IR tab

*Expected:*

* List of IR photo pairs displayed
* Each row shows: IR thumbnail (orange "IR" label), VIS thumbnail (blue "VIS" label)
* Photo keys shown below each thumbnail
* Asset name, location, captured date shown to the right
* Overflow menu (ellipsis icon) on the right
* Photos sorted by captured date (newest first)

h3. TC-2.3: Thumbnails load correctly

*Steps:*

# Open a session with IR photos (online mode)
# Navigate to the IR tab
# Wait for thumbnails to load

*Expected:* Both IR and VIS thumbnails load with actual images. Loading spinner shown during fetch.

h3. TC-2.4: Linked issue displayed

*Steps:*

# Open a session where an IR photo is linked to an issue
# Navigate to the IR tab

*Expected:* The linked issue title appears in orange text below the location in the photo row.

h3. TC-2.5: Scroll performance with many photos

*Steps:*

# Open a session with 50+ IR photos
# Navigate to the IR tab
# Scroll up and down rapidly

*Expected:* Smooth scrolling. Thumbnails load progressively as rows appear. No freezing or stuttering.

h3. TC-2.6: Offline thumbnail display

*Steps:*

# Open a session with IR photos while online — let thumbnails load
# Switch to offline mode
# Navigate away from the IR tab, then back

*Expected:* Previously loaded thumbnails still visible from cache. Photos not yet cached show an offline icon.

----

h2. 3. Upload IR Photos — From Photo Library

h3. TC-3.1: Upload single photo from library

*Steps:*

# Open an active session, navigate to IR tab (online)
# Tap "Upload IR Photos" button
# Select "From Photos"
# Pick 1 photo from the library
# Wait for upload to complete

*Expected:*

* Upload progress overlay appears ("Preparing upload...", "Uploading 1-1/1...", "Refreshing...")
* After completion, the new photo appears in the list
* No error alert

h3. TC-3.2: Upload multiple photos from library

*Steps:*

# Tap "Upload IR Photos" > "From Photos"
# Select 5 photos
# Wait for upload

*Expected:*

* Progress shows batch info ("Uploading 1-5/5...")
* All 5 photos appear in the list after refresh
* Thumbnails load for the new photos

h3. TC-3.3: Maximum selection limit (20 photos)

*Steps:*

# Tap "Upload IR Photos" > "From Photos"
# Try to select more than 20 photos

*Expected:* The photo picker prevents selecting more than 20 photos (native iOS limit enforced).

h3. TC-3.4: Cancel photo selection

*Steps:*

# Tap "Upload IR Photos" > "From Photos"
# Tap "Cancel" without selecting any photos

*Expected:* No upload initiated. No error. Tab remains as-is.

----

h2. 4. Upload IR Photos — From Files

h3. TC-4.1: Upload single file

*Steps:*

# Tap "Upload IR Photos" > "From Files"
# Pick 1 image file (JPEG or PNG)
# Wait for upload

*Expected:* File uploads successfully. New photo appears in list after refresh.

h3. TC-4.2: Upload multiple files

*Steps:*

# Tap "Upload IR Photos" > "From Files"
# Select 3 image files
# Wait for upload

*Expected:* All 3 files upload. Progress overlay shows batch progress. Photos appear in list.

h3. TC-4.3: Exceed 20 file limit

*Steps:*

# Tap "Upload IR Photos" > "From Files"
# Select more than 20 image files

*Expected:* Error alert: "You can upload a maximum of 20 files at once." No upload initiated.

h3. TC-4.4: Upload non-image file

*Steps:*

# Tap "Upload IR Photos" > "From Files"
# Attempt to select a PDF or text file

*Expected:* File picker only shows image files (non-image files should be grayed out or hidden).

h3. TC-4.5: Upload HEIC image

*Steps:*

# Tap "Upload IR Photos" > "From Photos" or "From Files"
# Select a HEIC format photo (common on iPhone)

*Expected:* HEIC image is compressed/converted to JPEG before upload. Upload succeeds.

h3. TC-4.6: Cancel file selection

*Steps:*

# Tap "Upload IR Photos" > "From Files"
# Tap "Cancel"

*Expected:* No upload. No error.

----

h2. 5. Upload — Edge Cases & Error Handling

h3. TC-5.1: Upload button disabled when offline

*Steps:*

# Switch to offline mode
# Navigate to the IR tab

*Expected:* "Upload IR Photos" button is grayed out and not tappable.

h3. TC-5.2: Upload button disabled for closed session

*Steps:*

# Open a closed/inactive session
# Navigate to the IR tab

*Expected:* "Upload IR Photos" button is grayed out and not tappable.

h3. TC-5.3: Network failure during upload

*Steps:*

# Start uploading 5 photos
# Enable airplane mode mid-upload

*Expected:* Some uploads fail. Error alert shows: "X of 5 photos failed to upload." Successfully uploaded photos still appear after refresh.

h3. TC-5.4: Upload large photos

*Steps:*

# Select 3 large photos (5MB+ each)
# Upload

*Expected:* Photos are compressed to JPEG at 0.6 quality before upload. Upload completes (may take longer). No crash or OOM.

h3. TC-5.5: Upload progress overlay

*Steps:*

# Upload 10 photos
# Observe the overlay

*Expected:* Overlay shows sequential progress: "Preparing upload..." → "Uploading 1-5/10..." → "Uploading 6-10/10..." → "Refreshing..." → overlay dismisses.

----

h2. 6. Edit IR Photo

h3. TC-6.1: Edit photo keys

*Steps:*

# Find an IR photo in the list
# Tap the overflow menu (ellipsis icon)
# Tap "Edit IR Photo"
# Change the IR Photo Key to "Updated IR Key"
# Change the Visual Photo Key to "Updated VIS Key"
# Tap "Save"

*Expected:*

* Edit sheet dismisses
* Row updates immediately with new key labels
* Thumbnails reload (may briefly show loading state while fetching new presigned URLs)
* Changes sync to server (verify in frontend if possible)

h3. TC-6.2: Cancel edit

*Steps:*

# Tap overflow menu > "Edit IR Photo"
# Modify the keys
# Tap "Cancel"

*Expected:* Sheet dismisses. No changes saved. Original keys remain.

h3. TC-6.3: Save with empty keys

*Steps:*

# Tap overflow menu > "Edit IR Photo"
# Clear both IR Photo Key and Visual Photo Key fields
# Observe the Save button

*Expected:* Save button is disabled when both fields are empty.

h3. TC-6.4: Edit while offline

*Steps:*

# Switch to offline mode
# Edit a photo's keys and save

*Expected:* Keys update locally. Change is queued for sync. When back online, change syncs to server.

----

h2. 7. Delete IR Photo

h3. TC-7.1: Delete a photo

*Steps:*

# Tap overflow menu on any IR photo
# Tap "Delete"
# Observe the alert dialog

*Expected:* Centered alert dialog appears: "Delete IR Photo?" with confirmation message. Two buttons: "Cancel" and "Delete".

h3. TC-7.2: Confirm delete

*Steps:*

# Trigger delete alert (TC-7.1)
# Tap "Delete"

*Expected:* Alert dismisses. Photo row disappears immediately from the list. Photo count in header decreases. Change syncs to server.

h3. TC-7.3: Cancel delete

*Steps:*

# Trigger delete alert (TC-7.1)
# Tap "Cancel"

*Expected:* Alert dismisses. Photo remains in list unchanged.

h3. TC-7.4: Delete while offline

*Steps:*

# Switch to offline mode
# Delete an IR photo

*Expected:* Photo disappears from list immediately (soft delete). Deletion queued for server sync when online.

----

h2. 8. Photo Preview / Fullscreen Viewer

h3. TC-8.1: Open fullscreen viewer

*Steps:*

# Tap on an IR photo row (tap the thumbnails or metadata area, NOT the overflow menu)

*Expected:* Fullscreen viewer opens with the tapped photo displayed. Black background. Close button visible.

h3. TC-8.2: Swipe between photos in viewer

*Steps:*

# Open the fullscreen viewer
# Swipe left/right

*Expected:* Navigate between photos in the session. Photo counter updates (e.g., "2 / 5").

h3. TC-8.3: Zoom in viewer

*Steps:*

# Open the fullscreen viewer
# Pinch to zoom on a photo

*Expected:* Photo zooms in (up to 5x). Can pan around the zoomed image.

h3. TC-8.4: Close viewer

*Steps:*

# Open the fullscreen viewer
# Tap the close button (X icon)

*Expected:* Viewer dismisses. Returns to the IR Photos list.

h3. TC-8.5: VIS and IR pair display

*Steps:*

# Open the fullscreen viewer for a photo that has both VIS and IR images

*Expected:* Both VIS and IR images displayed side-by-side (or as the viewer layout dictates). Labels visible.

h3. TC-8.6: Overflow menu not triggered by row tap

*Steps:*

# Tap on the thumbnail or metadata area of a row

*Expected:* Fullscreen viewer opens. The overflow menu does NOT appear.

h3. TC-8.7: Overflow menu still accessible

*Steps:*

# Tap the ellipsis (overflow) icon on a row

*Expected:* Context menu appears with "Edit IR Photo" and "Delete" options. Fullscreen viewer does NOT open.

----

h2. 9. Session Refresh & Data Sync

h3. TC-9.1: Pull-to-refresh updates IR photos

*Steps:*

# Add IR photos from the frontend web app
# On iOS, tap the refresh button in the navigation bar
# Navigate to the IR tab

*Expected:* Newly added photos from frontend appear in the iOS list.

h3. TC-9.2: Targeted refresh after upload (not full SLD)

*Steps:*

# Upload IR photos from the IR tab
# Observe the app behavior

*Expected:* Only the session's IR photos refresh (fast). The entire SLD does NOT reload. Other tabs' data remains unchanged.

----

h2. 10. Localization

h3. TC-10.1: English strings

*Steps:*

# Set device language to English
# Navigate to the IR tab

*Expected:* All strings in English: "IR Photos", "Upload IR Photos", "From Photos", "From Files", "Edit IR Photo", "Delete IR Photo?", etc.

h3. TC-10.2: French strings

*Steps:*

# Set device language to French
# Navigate to the IR tab

*Expected:* All strings in French: "Photos IR", "Telecharger des photos IR", "Depuis les photos", "Depuis les fichiers", "Modifier la photo IR", "Supprimer la photo IR ?", etc.

----

h2. 11. Regression — Existing Functionality

h3. TC-11.1: Attachments tab still works

*Steps:*

# Navigate to the Files tab (now index 5)
# Upload, download, and delete an attachment

*Expected:* All attachment functionality works as before. No regression.

h3. TC-11.2: Other tabs unaffected

*Steps:*

# Navigate through Details, Assets, Tasks, Issues tabs

*Expected:* All tabs load and function correctly. No visual or functional changes.

h3. TC-11.3: Session close still works

*Steps:*

# Close a session from the Details tab

*Expected:* Session closes successfully. IR Photos tab shows disabled upload button for the closed session.

h3. TC-11.4: IR photos in asset detail still work

*Steps:*

# Navigate to an asset/node detail view that has IR photos
# Verify the IR photo section displays correctly

*Expected:* Existing IR photo functionality in asset details is unchanged.

----

h2. 12. Performance

h3. TC-12.1: Session with 50+ IR photos

*Steps:*

# Open a session with 50+ IR photos
# Navigate to IR tab, scroll through the entire list

*Expected:* Thumbnails load progressively. Scrolling is smooth. No crash or freeze.

h3. TC-12.2: Session with 100+ IR photos

*Steps:*

# Open a session with 100+ IR photos
# Scroll rapidly up and down

*Expected:* List renders without lag. Previously viewed thumbnails load from cache on return scroll.

h3. TC-12.3: Upload 20 photos at once

*Steps:*

# Select 20 photos and upload

*Expected:* Upload completes within reasonable time (~30-60s). Progress updates shown. All photos appear after refresh.
