⏺ *Test Plan: Attachment Delete Implementation*                                 *Prerequisites*                                                                                            

* App installed on simulator/device                                                    

* A Work Order (IR Session) with at least 2-3 uploaded attachments

* Access to toggle between online/offline mode                                                                                                                                            ---                                                                                                                                                                                                            

  *1. Delete Icon Visibility*                                 

* Open a Work Order → navigate to Files tab               

* Verify a red trash icon appears on every attachment row              

* Verify the trash icon appears next to the download icon                

* Verify layout looks correct with both icons (no overlap or clipping)                                                                                                                                         

  *2. Delete Confirmation Dialog*                                                             

* Tap the trash icon on any attachment                                              

* Verify a confirmation alert appears with title "Delete Attachment?"

* Verify the alert message says "Are you sure you want to delete this attachment? This action cannot be undone."                            

* Tap "Cancel" → verify the attachment is still in the list (not deleted)

* Tap trash icon again → tap "Delete" → verify the attachment disappears from the list immediately                                                                                                             

  *3. Online Delete*                                                                                      

* Ensure app is in online mode                                                            

* Delete an uploaded attachment

* Verify the attachment disappears from the list instantly                

* Verify the API call DELETE /attachment/delete/<id> fires (check network logs via slog)                                                                        

* Close and reopen the Work Order → verify the deleted attachment does not reappear                                                         

* Force-refresh / re-sync the session → verify the attachment stays deleted                                                                                                                                   

  *4. Offline Delete (Queued Sync)*                                                         

* Switch app to offline mode                                                                 

* Verify both download and delete icons are grayed out / disabled

* Verify tapping either button does nothing                                       

* Switch back to online mode

* Verify both icons become active again (download = blue, delete = red)                                                                                                   



  *5. Delete Not-Yet-Uploaded Attachment*                                            

* Add a new attachment while offline (or before upload completes)                                                                                          

* Verify it shows the orange upload-pending indicator     

* Switch to online mode and tap the trash icon on that pending attachment                                                                                         

* Confirm deletion → verify it disappears from the list

* Verify no API call is made (check logs — no deleteAttachment() log entry)                                                                                             

* Verify the local file is removed from disk (no orphaned files in cache)                                                                                                

                                                                                               

  *6. French Localization*                                                                           

* Change device language to French (Settings → General → Language)                                                                                          

* Open a Work Order → Files tab → tap trash icon

* Verify alert title shows "Supprimer la pièce jointe ?"                      

* Verify alert message shows "Êtes-vous sûr de vouloir supprimer cette pièce jointe ? Cette action est irréversible."                           

* Verify "Cancel" and "Delete" buttons use existing French translations ("Annuler" / "Supprimer")                                             



  *7. Edge Cases*                                                                                         

* Delete the last attachment in the list → verify the empty state view ("No Attachments") appears                                                     

* Add a new attachment after deleting all → verify it appears correctly

* Rapid tap: tap trash on two different attachments quickly → verify only one confirmation shows and no crash occurs                                                                                                             

  *8. Token Refresh (AttachmentUploadService Refactor)*                   

* Upload a new attachment while online → verify upload succeeds as before                                                                           

* Download an attachment → verify presigned URL works and file opens

* Fetch/refresh the attachments list → verify list loads correctly      

* (If possible) Let the access token expire, then perform an upload/download → verify it auto-refreshes the token and completes successfully

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Login to [https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai|https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai|smart-link] 
# Navigate to *Site Overview* (Dashboard) — {{/dashboard}}
# Set browser zoom to *100%* (default — Ctrl+0 to reset)
# Observe the top row of summary cards: Total Assets (147), Unresolved Issues (5), Pending Tasks (18), Active Work Orders (7), Opportunities Value ($0.0k), Equipment at Risk ($0.0k)
# Notice the *"Equipment at Risk"* card — the text/value is going *outside the card boundary* (overflowing the container box)
# Scroll down to look for *"Assets by Type"* chart — it is *not visible* at 100% zoom
# Scroll down further to look for *"Issues by Type"* chart — also *not visible* at 100% zoom

----

*Expected Result:*

* *Equipment at Risk* card should contain all text and values within its card boundary — no overflow
* *Assets by Type* chart should be fully visible below the summary cards at 100% zoom with proper scrolling
* *Issues by Type* chart should be fully visible below Assets by Type at 100% zoom with proper scrolling

*Actual Result:*

* *Equipment at Risk* card: The "$0.0k" value and/or "EQUIPMENT AT RISK" label text overflows outside the card's container boundary — text goes beyond the card edge
* *Assets by Type* chart: Not visible at 100% zoom — either clipped by a fixed-height parent container or pushed below the viewport with no scroll
* *Issues by Type* chart: Same issue — not visible at 100% zoom
* The dashboard main content area does not scroll properly to show the chart sections below the summary cards

column1: Frontend | AI Extraction doesn't work on staged photos
column41:
h3. Summary

AI nameplate extraction on the web frontend only works in edit mode (on saved assets). During asset creation, the "Extract from Photos" button is not available even though photos are already uploaded to S3. Users must save the asset first, then go back to extract — adding unnecessary friction.

h3. Context

Unlike iOS/Android which need to upload to a temp S3 bucket (since photos are staged locally), the *frontend does not need to use the temp bucket* — photos are already uploaded to the {{asset_photos}} S3 bucket immediately when selected via {{PhotoUploadField}}. The frontend just needs to:

# Send the already-uploaded photo S3 URLs to the new extraction endpoint
# Apply the extracted attributes directly to the form

h3. Requirements

# *Enable "Extract from Photos" button in create mode*

   - Currently the button only shows when {{mode === "edit"}} in {{AssetFormFields.jsx}}

   - Show it in create mode when: a node class is selected AND staged photos exist (not deleted, with a file object)

# *Call the new temp extraction endpoint*

   - Endpoint: {{POST /extraction/extract-temp-nameplate-data}}

   - Request body:

     ```json

     {

       "photos": ["https://...s3.amazonaws.com/photo1.jpg", ...],

       "node_class_id": "uuid",

       "core_attributes": [...],

       "overwrite_existing": false,

       "extract_name": false,

       "extract_subtype": false,

       "subtype_options": [...]

     }

     ```

   - Photos array should contain the public S3 URLs of already-uploaded photos

   - {{node_class_id}} is the selected node class ID from the form

# *Apply extracted attributes to form fields*

   - Response returns {{extracted_attributes}} array with {{{ id, name, node_class_property, value }}}

   - Map each {{node_class_property}} to the corresponding {{_core_attr_{property_id}}} form field

   - Only set values for empty fields (respect {{overwrite_existing: false}})

   - Optionally apply {{extracted_name}} → {{label}} and {{extracted_subtype}} → {{node_subtype}}

# *Show appropriate feedback*

   - Loading state while extracting

   - Success message with count of attributes applied

   - Error message if extraction fails or no data extracted

h3. Key Files

* {{src/components/unified/AssetFormFields.jsx}} — Extraction button + handler
* {{src/services/mainAPIService.js}} — New API functions ({{extractTempNameplateData}})
* {{src/services/s3Service.js}} — {{S3_BUCKETS.TEMP_PHOTOS}} bucket key (added for consistency, but frontend uses existing {{asset_photos}} URLs)

h3. Important Note

Frontend does *NOT* need to upload photos to the temp bucket. Photos are already in S3 ({{asset_photos}} bucket) when the user selects them in {{PhotoUploadField}}. The public URLs of those uploaded photos can be sent directly to the temp extraction endpoint.

h3. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] "Extract from Photos" button is visible during asset creation when photos are uploaded and a node class is selected
* [ ] Clicking the button sends photo URLs to {{POST /extraction/extract-temp-nameplate-data}}
* [ ] Extracted core attributes are populated into form fields immediately
* [ ] Existing extraction flow in edit mode remains unchanged
* [ ] Proper loading, success, and error states are shown
* [ ] Fields that already have values are not overwritten

h3. Blocked By

* ZP-1430 — Backend: Temp S3 bucket + new extraction endpoint (In Progress, PR merged)

column1: AI Extraction doesn't work on staged photos (before asset save)
column41:
h3. Summary

AI nameplate extraction currently only works on photos that have been synced to the server and exist in S3. Users cannot extract nameplate data from staged photos during asset creation — they must first save the asset, which adds unnecessary friction to the workflow.

h3. Goal

Enable AI nameplate extraction on staged/unsaved photos across all platforms (Backend, Frontend, iOS, Android) so users can extract data before saving the asset.

h3. Approach

* *Backend (ZP-1430):* Create a temp S3 bucket ({{pgz-s3-temp-photos}}) for temporary photo storage. Add a new endpoint {{POST /extraction/extract-temp-nameplate-data}} that accepts photo URLs directly (instead of node_ids). Implement cleanup cron job with configurable retention.
* *Frontend:* Enable the "Extract from Photos" button during asset creation (create mode). Call the new temp extraction endpoint with S3 URLs of already-uploaded photos. Apply extracted attributes directly to the form. _Note: Frontend does not need to use the temp bucket — photos are already uploaded to asset_photos S3 when selected._
* *iOS (ZP-1314):* Upload staged photos to the temp S3 bucket and call the temp extraction endpoint before asset save.
* *Android (ZP-1318):* Upload staged photos to the temp S3 bucket and call the temp extraction endpoint before asset save.

h3. Sub-Tasks

||Platform||Ticket||Status||
|Backend|ZP-1430|In Progress|
|iOS|ZP-1314|In Progress|
|Android|ZP-1318|To Do|
|Frontend|_TBD_|To Do|

h3. Acceptance Criteria

* Users can trigger AI nameplate extraction on photos *during asset creation* (before saving) on all platforms.
* Extracted core attributes are populated into the form fields immediately.
* Existing extraction flow on saved assets (edit mode) remains unaffected.
* Temp photos are cleaned up automatically after the configured retention period.

column1: FE - Quote Price Equalization UI
column41:
h2. Summary

Implement frontend UI for equalizing client-facing prices across selected years when editing a quote.

h2. Background

The backend will provide an API to equalize sell prices across selected years in a quote. The frontend needs to expose this functionality in the quote editing interface with year selection and visual feedback.

h2. Scope

h3. 1. Equalize Action in Quote Editor

* Add an "Equalize Pricing" button/option in the quote editing view (near the year-over-year pricing summary)
* Button should only be visible/enabled for quotes with 2+ years

h3. 2. Year Selection UI

* On clicking "Equalize Pricing", show a dialog/panel with:

  - Checkbox list of all years in the quote

  - Each year showing its current client-facing total price

  - Preview of the equalized price (total of selected years / count)

  - "Apply" and "Cancel" actions

* Minimum 2 years must be selected to enable "Apply"

h3. 3. Visual Indicators

* After equalization, mark equalized years in the pricing table (e.g., icon, badge, or subtle highlight)
* Show the original (pre-equalization) price alongside the equalized price for reference
* Tooltip or info text explaining "Price equalized across X years"

h3. 4. Reset/Undo

* Add "Reset Equalization" option to revert to original inflation-based pricing
* Confirmation dialog before resetting

h3. 5. Pricing Table Updates

* Year-over-year pricing table should reflect equalized values after applying
* Totals should recalculate based on equalized prices
* Read equalization metadata from the pricing API response ({{is_equalized}}, {{original_price}}, {{equalized_years}})

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] "Equalize Pricing" button visible on quotes with 2+ years
* [ ] Year selection dialog allows choosing specific years to equalize
* [ ] Preview shows equalized price before applying
* [ ] Equalized years are visually distinguished in the pricing table
* [ ] Original prices shown for comparison on equalized years
* [ ] "Reset Equalization" reverts to original pricing
* [ ] Proper loading/error states for API calls
* [ ] Responsive design for the equalization dialog

column1: BE - Quote Price Equalization API
column41:
h2. Summary

Implement backend API support for equalizing client-facing (sell) prices across selected years in a quote.

h2. Background

Currently, quote pricing applies 3% annual inflation, resulting in escalating prices year over year. Clients sometimes want a flat/equal price across certain years. This feature allows users to select specific years and equalize the client-facing total across them while keeping internal costs unchanged.

h2. Scope

h3. 1. New Equalization Endpoint

* *POST* {{/quote/<quote_id>/equalize-pricing}}
* Request body:

  - {{year_indices}}: array of year indices to equalize (e.g., {{[2, 3, 4]}} for years 2-4)

* Calculates the total client-facing price for the selected years, divides equally, and applies adjustments to achieve equal annual pricing

h3. 2. Equalization Logic (in {{quote_pricing_service.py}})

* Sum the total sell price across the selected years
* Divide by the number of selected years to get the equalized annual price
* Calculate the required adjustment per year to achieve the equalized price
* Apply adjustments via {{labor_adjustment}} / {{material_adjustment}} on per-workorder {{QuoteLabor}} records (not global)
* Preserve internal cost values — only sell/client-facing prices change

h3. 3. Reset/Undo Equalization

* *POST* {{/quote/<quote_id>/reset-equalization}}
* Resets per-workorder adjustments back to defaults (removes equalization)
* Alternatively, store original adjustment values before equalization for clean rollback

h3. 4. Pricing Response Update

* Update {{calculate_quote_pricing}} response to include:

  - {{is_equalized}}: boolean flag per year

  - {{equalized_years}}: list of year indices that are equalized

  - {{original_price}}: pre-equalization price per year (for UI display)

h3. 5. Validation

* At least 2 years must be selected for equalization
* Selected years must exist in the quote's year range
* Cannot equalize if quote has no pricing data

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Equalization endpoint distributes total sell price equally across selected years
* [ ] Internal costs remain unchanged
* [ ] Reset endpoint reverts equalization adjustments
* [ ] Pricing API response includes equalization metadata
* [ ] Unit tests for equalization calculation logic
* [ ] Integration tests for equalize/reset endpoints

column1: During Migration, Visual photo to generate
column41:
h2. Background

The system supports two FLIR camera configurations:

* *FLIR-SEP* (Separate): The camera produces two separate files — a visual (RGB) photo and an IR (thermal) photo. Both are uploaded independently.
* *FLIR-IND* (Indicated): The camera produces a single IR file that contains an embedded visual photo within it. The visual image must be extracted from the IR file.

The {{ir_photos}} table stores two S3 keys per record:

* {{ir_photo_key}} — the thermal/IR image
* {{visual_photo_key}} — the corresponding visual/RGB image

The {{ir_sessions}} table has a {{photo_type}} field (e.g., {{FLIR-SEP}}, {{FLIR-IND}}) that indicates which camera configuration was used.

h2. Problem

During workorder migration, FLIR-IND IR photos are imported into the system, but the embedded visual photo is *not extracted* from the IR file. This results in {{ir_photos}} records where:

* {{ir_photo_key}} is populated (the raw FLIR file is uploaded to S3)
* {{visual_photo_key}} is *empty/null* (the visual was never extracted and uploaded)

Without the visual photo, reports and UI views that rely on the visual image will be incomplete.

h2. Requirements

During the workorder migration process, add logic to:

# *Detect FLIR-IND sessions* — Check the {{photo_type}} field on the {{ir_sessions}} record. If it is {{FLIR-IND}}, the IR files contain embedded visual photos.
# *Extract the visual image* — For each IR photo in a FLIR-IND session, download the IR file from S3 and extract the embedded visual image using a FLIR extraction library (e.g., Python's {{flirimageextractor}} or similar).
# *Upload the visual to S3* — Upload the extracted visual image (as JPG) to S3. Developer to determine the appropriate S3 bucket/prefix based on existing patterns (see {{active_visual_prefix}} on the session and {{S3_BUCKET_IR_PHOTOS}} constant).
# *Update the database record* — Populate the {{visual_photo_key}} field on the {{ir_photos}} record with the new S3 key.

h2. Technical Context

* *IRPhoto model* ({{app/models/IRPhoto.py}}): {{visual_photo_key}}, {{ir_photo_key}}, {{ir_session_id}}
* *Session model* ({{app/models/Session.py}}): {{photo_type}} field stores camera config (e.g., {{FLIR-SEP}}, {{FLIR-IND}}), {{active_visual_prefix}} for S3 prefix
* *Photo normalizer* ({{app/services/photo_normalizer.py}}): Existing utility that normalizes photo formats to JPG — can be reused for the extracted visual
* *S3 bucket config* ({{app/constants.py}}): {{S3_BUCKET_IR_PHOTOS}} defaults to {{pgz-s3-ir-photos}}
* *IR Controller* ({{app/controllers/ir_controller.py}}): Handles IR photo CRUD with normalization

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* After migration completes, *all* {{ir_photos}} records linked to FLIR-IND sessions should have both {{ir_photo_key}} and {{visual_photo_key}} populated with valid S3 keys
* The extracted visual images should be accessible from S3 and viewable as standard JPG images
* Migration should log/report how many visual photos were extracted
* If extraction fails for a specific photo (e.g., corrupted file), log a warning and continue with remaining photos — do not fail the entire migration

column1: [Android] Attachment Visibility for Clients
column41:
h2. Overview

Add UI controls for managing attachment visibility (Internal / Public) on Android, so internal users can control which attachments are visible to clients in the client portal.

h2. Context

Clients should not see every attachment on a site — some are for internal use. The default should be *Internal* (hidden from clients). Internal users can explicitly mark attachments as *Public* to make them visible in the client portal.

h2. Requirements

h3. Attachment List Display

* Show a *visibility badge/indicator* on each attachment in the Attachments/Files tab:

  - {{Internal}} — default, muted/gray badge

  - {{Public}} — highlighted badge (e.g., green or blue)

* Internal users should see all attachments regardless of visibility

h3. Visibility Toggle

* Add a way to change visibility on each attachment (toggle, long-press menu, or context menu)
* Options: *Internal* (default) / *Public*
* Show confirmation when changing to Public (e.g., "This attachment will be visible to clients. Continue?")

h3. Upload Flow

* When uploading a new attachment, include a visibility selector in the upload configuration
* Default selection: *Internal*

h3. Data Model Update

* Update the local Attachment entity to include a {{visibility}} field (String: "internal" / "public")
* Default value: {{internal}}
* Sync visibility changes with backend API
* Support offline visibility changes via sync queue

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Visibility badge (Internal/Public) is shown on each attachment in the Attachments tab
* [ ] Internal users can toggle attachment visibility between Internal and Public
* [ ] Confirmation dialog is shown when making an attachment Public
* [ ] New attachment uploads default to Internal visibility
* [ ] Upload configuration includes visibility selector
* [ ] Attachment model updated with visibility field
* [ ] Visibility changes sync with backend (online and offline)
* [ ] Works on all supported Android phone and tablet screen sizes

----

h3. 2. Attachment Row — Visibility Chip Display

||#||Step||Expected Result||
|2.1|View the Files tab with existing attachments|Each row shows a visibility chip after the type label|
|2.2|Verify chip colors|"Internal" = orange background/text, "Public" = green background/text|
|2.3|Verify old attachments (created before this feature)|Show "Internal" chip by default|

----

h3. 3. Context Menu — Visibility Toggle (Online)

||#||Step||Expected Result||
|3.1|Long-press an "Internal" attachment|Context menu shows "Make Public" with globe icon|
|3.2|Tap "Make Public"|Confirmation alert: "Make Public?" / "This attachment will be visible to clients. Continue?"|
|3.3|Tap "Cancel" on the alert|Nothing changes, chip stays "Internal"|
|3.4|Long-press again → Tap "Make Public" → Tap "Make Public" button|Chip changes to green "Public"|
|3.5|Long-press the now "Public" attachment|Context menu shows "Make Internal" with lock icon|
|3.6|Tap "Make Internal"|No confirmation shown, chip changes to orange "Internal" immediately|

----

h3. 4. Context Menu — Visibility Toggle (Offline)

||#||Step||Expected Result||
|4.1|Switch to offline mode|App enters offline mode|
|4.2|Long-press an attachment → Context menu appears|"Make Public" / "Make Internal" option is enabled (not greyed out)|
|4.3|Change visibility to "Public" (confirm alert)|Chip updates to green "Public" locally|
|4.4|Check sync queue analyzer (Settings → Sync Queue)|A new .update entry for the attachment is in the pending tab|
|4.5|Switch back to online mode|Sync processes the queued update|
|4.6|Verify the attachment still shows "Public"|Visibility persisted after sync|

----

h3. 5. Delete — Online

||#||Step||Expected Result||
|5.1|Tap the trash icon on a synced attachment|Confirmation alert appears|
|5.2|Confirm delete|Attachment disappears from the list, API delete call made|

----

h3. 6. Delete — Offline (Synced Attachment)

||#||Step||Expected Result||
|6.1|Switch to offline mode|App enters offline mode|
|6.2|Tap the trash icon on a synced attachment|Delete button is enabled, confirmation alert appears|
|6.3|Confirm delete|Attachment disappears from the list (soft-deleted locally)|
|6.4|Check sync queue analyzer|A new .delete entry for the attachment is in the pending tab|
|6.5|Switch to online mode|Sync processes the delete, attachment removed from server|

----

h3. 7. Delete — Offline (Unsynced Attachment)

||#||Step||Expected Result||
|7.1|Switch to offline mode|App enters offline mode|
|7.2|Upload a new attachment (queued for sync)|Attachment appears with upload-pending indicator|
|7.3|Check sync queue analyzer|A .create entry exists for this attachment|
|7.4|Tap the trash icon on this unsynced attachment|Confirmation alert appears|
|7.5|Confirm delete|Attachment disappears from the list|
|7.6|Check sync queue analyzer|The .create entry is gone (cleaned up)|
|7.7|Switch to online mode|No orphaned sync operations, no errors|

----

h3. 8. Delete — Offline (Visibility Changed Then Deleted)

||#||Step||Expected Result||

|8.2|Upload a new attachment|Attachment appears with upload-pending indicator|

|8.4|Tap trash icon → Confirm delete|Attachment disappears|
|8.5|Check sync queue analyzer|Both .create and .update entries are cleaned up|
|8.6|Switch to online mode|No errors, clean sync|

----

h3. 9. Sync Queue Analyzer — Swipe Delete Attachment Queue Item

||#||Step||Expected Result||
|9.1|Create an attachment offline (so a .create item is queued)|.create entry visible in sync queue pending tab|
|9.2|Swipe left on the .create entry → Tap Delete|Confirmation alert: "Delete Attachment?"|
|9.3|Confirm|Queue item removed, attachment removed from Files tab, local file cleaned up|
|9.4|Create a synced attachment, then change visibility offline|.update entry visible in sync queue|
|9.5|Swipe left on the .update entry → Tap Delete|No confirmation, queue item removed directly|
|9.6|Verify the attachment still exists in Files tab|Attachment still visible (only queue item removed, entity preserved)|

----

h3. 10. Server Import — Backward Compatibility

||#||Step||Expected Result||
|10.1|Sync with server that returns attachments WITH visibility and visibility_id fields|Attachments import with correct visibility and chip displays correctly|
|10.2|Sync with server that returns attachments WITHOUT visibility field (old server)|Attachments import with "Internal" default, no crash|

----

h3. 11. French Localization

||#||Step||Expected Result||
|11.1|Switch app language to French (Settings)|App switches to French|
|11.2|Open Files tab → Tap "Add File" → Check Visibility section|Header: "Visibilité", options: "Interne" / "Public", footer in French|
|11.3|Long-press an attachment|Context menu: "Rendre public" or "Rendre Interne"|
|11.4|Tap "Rendre public"|Alert: "Rendre public ?" / "Cette pièce jointe sera visible par les clients. Continuer ?"|
|11.5|Check visibility chips|"Interne" (orange) / "Public" (green)|

----

h3. 12. Edge Cases

||#||Step||Expected Result||
|12.1|Kill and relaunch app → Check Files tab|Visibility chips persist correctly (SwiftData)|
|12.2|Change visibility online, but API fails (e.g., no network mid-request)|Change is saved locally, operation queued for retry|
|12.3|Multiple rapid visibility toggles on the same attachment|Last value wins, no crash|

column1: [Frontend] Attachment Visibility for Clients
column41:
h2. Overview

Add UI controls for managing attachment visibility (Internal / Public) on the web platform, so internal users can control which attachments are visible to clients in the client portal.

h2. Context

Clients should not see every attachment on a site — some are for internal use. The default should be *Internal* (hidden from clients). Internal users can explicitly mark attachments as *Public* to make them visible in the client portal.

h2. Requirements

h3. Attachment List Display

* Show a *visibility badge/indicator* on each attachment in the session attachments list:

  - {{Internal}} — default, muted/gray badge

  - {{Public}} — highlighted badge (e.g., green or blue)

* Internal users should see all attachments regardless of visibility

h3. Visibility Toggle

* Add a way to change visibility on each attachment (toggle, dropdown, or context menu)
* Options: *Internal* (default) / *Public*
* Show confirmation when changing to Public (e.g., "This attachment will be visible to clients. Continue?")

h3. Upload Flow

* When uploading a new attachment, include a visibility selector in the upload configuration
* Default selection: *Internal*

h3. Client Portal View

* Client portal should only display attachments marked as {{Public}}
* No visibility toggle should be shown to clients — they only see what's shared with them

h3. Bulk Actions (nice-to-have)

* Allow selecting multiple attachments and changing visibility in bulk

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Visibility badge (Internal/Public) is shown on each attachment in the list
* [ ] Internal users can toggle attachment visibility between Internal and Public
* [ ] Confirmation dialog is shown when making an attachment Public
* [ ] New attachment uploads default to Internal visibility
* [ ] Upload configuration includes visibility selector
* [ ] Client portal only shows Public attachments
* [ ] Client portal does not show visibility controls
* [ ] Responsive layout works on desktop and tablet

column1: [Backend] Attachment Visibility - API & Data Model
column41:
h2. Overview

Add backend support for controlling attachment visibility in the client portal. Attachments should have a visibility flag (Internal / Public) so that internal-only files are hidden from clients.

h2. Context

Clients should not see every attachment on a site — some attachments are for internal use only. The default visibility should be *Internal* (not visible to clients). Users can explicitly mark attachments as *Public* to make them visible in the client portal.

h2. Requirements

h3. Data Model

* Add a *visibility* field to the Attachment model with two values:

  - {{internal}} (default) — visible only to internal users

  - {{public}} — visible to both internal users and clients in the client portal

* Default value must be {{internal}} so existing attachments remain hidden from clients
* Migration: All existing attachments should default to {{internal}}

h3. API Changes

# *Create Attachment* ({{POST /attachment/create}})

   - Accept optional {{visibility}} field (default: {{internal}})

# *Update Attachment Visibility* (new endpoint or extend existing)

   - Allow changing visibility on an existing attachment

   - Input: attachment ID, visibility value

   - Only authorized internal users should be able to change visibility

# *List Attachments* ({{GET /attachment/?session_id=...}})

   - For internal users: return all attachments with their visibility flag

   - For client portal requests: filter to only return {{public}} attachments

# *Bulk Update Visibility* (optional, nice-to-have)

   - Allow changing visibility for multiple attachments at once

h3. Key Considerations

* Default must be {{internal}} — this is critical for security (attachments should not accidentally be exposed to clients)
* Existing attachments must remain hidden from clients after migration
* Client portal API must enforce visibility filtering server-side (not just UI filtering)
* Consider adding visibility to the attachment-node mapping level if per-node visibility is needed in the future

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Attachment model has a {{visibility}} field with values: {{internal}}, {{public}}
* [ ] Default visibility is {{internal}}
* [ ] Existing attachments migrated to {{internal}} visibility
* [ ] Create attachment API accepts visibility parameter
* [ ] Update attachment visibility endpoint works
* [ ] Client portal API only returns {{public}} attachments
* [ ] Internal user API returns all attachments with visibility flag
* [ ] API documented for frontend/mobile consumption

column1: Copy Asset Details - Frontend
column41:
h2. Overview

Implement "Copy Details To" and "Copy Details From" functionality in the web asset detail view, allowing field techs to quickly duplicate asset attributes from one piece of equipment to another.

h2. Context

Contractors often use identical equipment throughout a facility. Currently, techs must manually re-enter the same Core Attributes for each identical asset. This feature saves significant field time by enabling copy/paste of asset details between assets of the same class.

h2. Requirements

h3. UI - Copy Buttons

* Add *"Copy Details To"* and *"Copy Details From"* buttons in the asset detail view
* Buttons should be positioned near the Core Attributes section for easy access
* Both buttons open an asset picker/modal to select the source/target asset

h3. Asset Picker for Copy

* Allow searching by *Asset Name* and *QR Code*
* *Filter assets to the same asset class* as the current asset (copying between different classes doesn't make sense)
* Show asset name, location (room), and QR code in the picker list
* Consider filtering to assets within the same SLD/Work Order context

h3. What Gets Copied

The following fields should be copied between assets:

* *Core Attributes* — all property values defined by the asset class
* *Asset Subtype* — if applicable for the class
* *Serviceability* — GOOD, FAIR, POOR, NOT_DETERMINED
* *Node Terminals* — terminal configuration (voltage_rating, amp_rating, phase_count, etc.)

h3. What Should NOT Be Copied

* Asset Name — each asset has a unique name
* QR Code — unique per asset
* Location/Room — each asset has its own location
* Position on diagram (x, y, width, height)
* Photos, Issues, Tasks, IR Photos — specific to each asset
* COM Rating — specific to each asset's condition
* IDs and metadata (id, parent_id, sld, sync fields)

h3. Copy Behavior

* *"Copy Details From"*: Select a source asset → its copyable fields overwrite the current asset's fields
* *"Copy Details To"*: Select a target asset → the current asset's copyable fields overwrite the target's fields
* Show a confirmation dialog before overwriting (e.g., "This will overwrite Core Attributes on [Target Asset]. Continue?")
* After copy, save/sync the modified asset
* Update asset fluency completion on the target asset after copy

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] "Copy Details From" button is visible in asset detail view near Core Attributes
* [ ] "Copy Details To" button is visible in asset detail view near Core Attributes
* [ ] Asset picker/modal opens with search by Asset Name and QR Code
* [ ] Asset picker only shows assets of the same asset class
* [ ] Core Attributes are correctly copied between assets
* [ ] Node Terminals are correctly copied
* [ ] Asset Subtype and Serviceability are copied
* [ ] Asset Name, QR Code, Location, Photos, Issues, Tasks are NOT copied
* [ ] Confirmation dialog is shown before overwriting
* [ ] Modified asset is saved/synced after copy
* [ ] Asset Fluency completion updates after copy
* [ ] Responsive layout works on desktop and tablet

column1: [Backend] T&C and Privacy Policy Versioning - API & Data Model
column41:
h2. Overview

Implement backend support for Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy versioning. This is a prerequisite for the frontend (ZP-1442), iOS (ZP-1443), and Android (ZP-1444) sub-tasks, which will consume these APIs.

h2. Two Acceptance Flows

h3. Flow 1: New Sign-In

* Signing in = agreeing to the latest T&C and Privacy Policy
* On successful sign-in, the backend should automatically record acceptance of the current latest versions for the user
* No separate prompt needed

h3. Flow 2: Already Signed-In Users — Policy Update

* When a new T&C or Privacy Policy version is published, existing signed-in users must accept it before continuing to use the app
* Frontend/mobile will call the check endpoint on app launch / session resume
* If outdated, the user sees a mandatory prompt and must accept to proceed

h2. Requirements

h3. Data Model

* *T&C/Privacy Policy Version Registry*: Store versioned documents with the following fields:

  - Document type (terms_and_conditions / privacy_policy)

  - Version identifier (e.g., v1.0, v2.0)

  - Effective date

  - Content URL per alliance partner

  - Active/published flag

  - Created timestamp

* *User Acceptance Records*: Track which version each user has accepted:

  - User ID

  - Document type (terms_and_conditions / privacy_policy)

  - Accepted version

  - Accepted timestamp

  - Alliance partner context

h3. API Endpoints

# *Check Acceptance Status* (GET)

   - Input: User ID (from auth token)

   - Returns: Whether the user has accepted the latest T&C and Privacy Policy versions

   - Response should include: latest version info, user's accepted version, URLs per alliance partner, and whether acceptance is required

   - Called by frontend/mobile on app launch / session resume (Flow 2)

# *Record Acceptance* (POST)

   - Input: Document type, version accepted

   - Records the user's acceptance with timestamp

   - Used in two scenarios:

     - *Flow 1*: Called automatically on successful sign-in to record acceptance of latest versions

     - *Flow 2*: Called when existing user clicks "Accept" on the update prompt

# *Admin: Publish New Version* (POST) _(if needed)_

   - Input: Document type, version, URLs per alliance partner, effective date

   - Creates a new version entry in the registry

   - Existing signed-in users will be prompted to accept on next app launch

h3. Key Considerations

* T&C and Privacy Policy are versioned *independently* — a user may have accepted the latest T&C but not the latest Privacy Policy
* URLs are *different per alliance partner* — the version registry must store URLs per partner
* The check endpoint should be lightweight and fast since it's called on every app launch
* Ensure backward compatibility — users who signed up before versioning should be handled gracefully (treat as needing acceptance on next app launch)
* Flow 1 (sign-in auto-accept) should be atomic with the sign-in process

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Data model for T&C/Privacy Policy version registry is created
* [ ] Data model for user acceptance records is created
* [ ] GET endpoint returns whether user needs to accept latest T&C and/or Privacy Policy
* [ ] GET endpoint returns correct URLs per alliance partner
* [ ] POST endpoint records user acceptance with version and timestamp
* [ ] Sign-in flow automatically records acceptance of latest versions (Flow 1)
* [ ] T&C and Privacy Policy versions are tracked independently
* [ ] Handles users who have no prior acceptance records (pre-versioning users)
* [ ] API is documented for frontend/mobile consumption

column1: QR Code in Work Orders - Android
column41:
h2. Overview

Add QR code scanning and display support within Work Orders (IR Sessions) for asset selection when creating/managing Tasks and Issues.

h2. Requirements

* Display QR code option when selecting assets inside a Work Order context
* Ensure QR code scanner is accessible from:

  - *Tasks tab*: When creating a new task within a Work Order, the asset picker should show the QR scanner button

  - *Issues tab*: When creating a new issue within a Work Order, the asset picker should show the QR scanner button

* QR code scanning should match scanned codes against the asset's QR code field and auto-select the matching asset
* Ensure all asset selectors within Work Order context include both the QR scan button and the "Create New Asset" button

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] QR code scanner button visible in asset selector when creating a Task from within a Work Order
* [ ] QR code scanner button visible in asset selector when creating an Issue from within a Work Order
* [ ] Scanning a QR code auto-selects the matching asset
* [ ] "Create New Asset" and QR scan buttons are both visible in all asset selector contexts within Work Orders

Users can now *scan a QR code on the Tasks tab and Issues tab* inside a Work Order to *filter the list* and show only tasks/issues linked to the scanned asset.

18/Mar/26 2:36 AM;712020:9881bb6e-cbf7-47a6-bf7a-838a383b2866;[~accountid:712020:4adb279c-383d-4a86-b512-a5a829380b9f] [~accountid:712020:9c81fbcc-e424-4396-a25b-01f80fc942eb] 

 *what is the logic for the Arc Flash option?*  

# *Node must have a class*                                                              

* asset.node_class != nil — unclassified nodes are immediately *not ready*.

  *2. All af_required core attributes must be filled*                   

* asset.af_isComplete — looks at the node class's definition, filters to properties where          af_required == true, then checks that every one has a non-empty value in core_attributes. Must be  100% complete.            

  *3. Source requirement (only if node_class.needs_source == true)*

  The node needs a "source" — satisfied by *any one* of these:                               

* *Has a parent asset* (asset.parent_id != nil) — parent counts as source.
* *Has a valid inbound edge* — at least one non-deleted edge targeting this node where:
* The edge has a class (edge.edge_class != nil)
* The edge's own af_required attributes are all filled (edge.af_isComplete)
* *A descendant child has a valid inbound edge* — walks all descendants via childrenMap and checks if any child has a valid inbound edge (child's source serves as parent's source).           

  *4. Edge completeness*                                                                                

* For inbound edges used to satisfy the source requirement, the edge itself must have a class and  all its af_required attributes filled (edge.af_isComplete, which is also af_completion == 100). 

  *Visual result*                                                                                          

* *Green checkmark* — all conditions met                                                              
* *Red xmark* — any condition fails



*What is the logic for the C.O.M option?* 

 All 5 conditions must pass:                                                                              

# *Node must have a class* — asset.node_class != nil                                                 

# *Node must have a subtype* — asset.node_subtype != nil

# *Node must have a suggested shortcut* — asset.suggested_shortcut_id != nil                         

# *Node must have a COM value* — [http://asset.com|http://asset.com|smart-link]  != nil

# *All three condition assessment components must be filled* — asset.com_calculation must exist, and:

* criticalityValue > 0                                                                            

* operatingConditionsValue > 0                                                                    

* maintenanceValue > 0                                   

  *Visual result*   

* *Green checkmark* — all 5 conditions met

* *Red xmark* — any condition fails

column1: Copy Asset Details - Android
column41:
h2. Overview

Implement "Copy Details To" and "Copy Details From" functionality in the Android asset detail view, allowing field techs to quickly duplicate asset attributes from one piece of equipment to another.

h2. Context

Contractors often use identical equipment throughout a facility. Currently, techs must manually re-enter the same Core Attributes for each identical asset. This feature saves significant field time by enabling copy/paste of asset details between assets of the same class.

h2. Requirements

h3. UI - Copy Buttons

* Add *"Copy Details To"* and *"Copy Details From"* buttons in the asset detail view
* Buttons should be positioned near the Core Attributes section for easy access
* Both buttons open an asset picker to select the source/target asset

h3. Asset Picker for Copy

* Allow searching by *Asset Name* and *QR Code*
* Include QR code scanner option
* *Filter assets to the same asset class* as the current asset (copying between different classes doesn't make sense)
* Show asset name, location (room), and QR code in the picker list
* Consider filtering to assets within the same SLD/Work Order context

h3. What Gets Copied

The following fields should be copied between assets:

* *Core Attributes* — all property values defined by the asset class
* *Asset Subtype* — if applicable for the class
* *Serviceability* — GOOD, FAIR, POOR, NOT_DETERMINED
* *Node Terminals* — terminal configuration (voltage_rating, amp_rating, phase_count, etc.)

h3. What Should NOT Be Copied

* Asset Name — each asset has a unique name
* QR Code — unique per asset
* Location/Room — each asset has its own location
* Position on diagram (x, y, width, height)
* Photos, Issues, Tasks, IR Photos — specific to each asset
* COM Rating — specific to each asset's condition
* IDs and metadata (id, parent_id, sld, sync fields)

h3. Copy Behavior

* *"Copy Details From"*: Select a source asset → its copyable fields overwrite the current asset's fields
* *"Copy Details To"*: Select a target asset → the current asset's copyable fields overwrite the target's fields
* Show a confirmation dialog before overwriting (e.g., "This will overwrite Core Attributes on [Target Asset]. Continue?")
* After copy, mark the modified asset for sync
* Update asset fluency completion on the target asset after copy

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] "Copy Details From" button is visible in asset detail view near Core Attributes
* [ ] "Copy Details To" button is visible in asset detail view near Core Attributes
* [ ] Asset picker opens with search by Asset Name and QR Code
* [ ] QR code scanner is available in the asset picker
* [ ] Asset picker only shows assets of the same asset class
* [ ] Core Attributes are correctly copied between assets
* [ ] Node Terminals are correctly copied
* [ ] Asset Subtype and Serviceability are copied
* [ ] Asset Name, QR Code, Location, Photos, Issues, Tasks are NOT copied
* [ ] Confirmation dialog is shown before overwriting
* [ ] Modified asset is flagged for sync after copy
* [ ] Asset Fluency completion updates after copy

column1: Copy Asset Details - iOS
column41:
h2. Overview

Implement "Copy Details To" and "Copy Details From" functionality in the iOS asset detail view, allowing field techs to quickly duplicate asset attributes from one piece of equipment to another.

h2. Context

Contractors often use identical equipment throughout a facility. Currently, techs must manually re-enter the same Core Attributes for each identical asset. This feature saves significant field time by enabling copy/paste of asset details between assets of the same class.

h2. Requirements

h3. UI - Copy Buttons

* Add *"Copy Details To"* and *"Copy Details From"* buttons in the asset detail view ({{EditNodeDetailViewV3}})
* Buttons should be positioned near the Core Attributes section for easy access
* Both buttons open an asset picker to select the source/target asset

h3. Asset Picker for Copy

* Allow searching by *Asset Name* ({{node.label}}) and *QR Code* ({{node.qr_code}})
* Include QR code scanner option (reuse existing {{QRCodeScannerView}})
* *Filter assets to the same NodeClass* as the current asset (copying between different classes doesn't make sense)
* Show asset name, location (room), and QR code in the picker list
* Consider filtering to assets within the same SLD/Work Order context

h3. What Gets Copied

The following fields should be copied between assets:

* *Core Attributes* ({{core_attributes: [NodeProperty]}}) — all property values defined by the NodeClass
* *Node Subtype* ({{node_subtype}}) — if applicable for the class
* *Serviceability* ({{serviceability}}) — GOOD, FAIR, POOR, NOT_DETERMINED
* *Node Terminals* ({{node_terminals: [NodeTerminal]}}) — terminal configuration (voltage_rating, amp_rating, phase_count, etc.)

h3. What Should NOT Be Copied

* Asset Name ({{label}}) — each asset has a unique name
* QR Code ({{qr_code}}) — unique per asset
* Location/Room ({{room}}) — each asset has its own location
* Position on diagram ({{x}}, {{y}}, {{width}}, {{height}})
* Photos, Issues, Tasks, IR Photos — specific to each asset
* COM Rating ({{com}}, {{com_calculation}}) — specific to each asset's condition
* IDs and metadata ({{id}}, {{parent_id}}, {{sld}}, sync fields)

h3. Copy Behavior

* *"Copy Details From"*: Select a source asset → its copyable fields overwrite the current asset's fields
* *"Copy Details To"*: Select a target asset → the current asset's copyable fields overwrite the target's fields
* Show a confirmation dialog before overwriting (e.g., "This will overwrite Core Attributes on [Target Asset]. Continue?")
* After copy, mark the modified asset as {{needsSync = true}} for sync
* Update {{af_completion}} on the target asset after copy

h2. Key Files

* Asset Detail View: {{Egalvanic PZ/Views/Assets/EditNodeDetailViewV3/}}
* Asset Model: {{Egalvanic PZ/Core/Models/Entities/NodeV2.swift}}
* Class Definition: {{Egalvanic PZ/Core/Models/Entities/NodeClass.swift}}
* Core Attributes UI: {{Egalvanic PZ/Core/UI/Views/EntityCoreAttributesView.swift}}
* QR Scanner: {{Egalvanic PZ/Views/Assets/QRCodeScannerView.swift}}
* Asset Picker: {{Egalvanic PZ/Core/UI/Views/EntityCreationView.swift}} (NodeV2SingleSelectorView)
* Terminal Model: {{Egalvanic PZ/Core/Models/Entities/NodeTerminal.swift}}

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] "Copy Details From" button is visible in asset detail view near Core Attributes
* [ ] "Copy Details To" button is visible in asset detail view near Core Attributes
* [ ] Asset picker opens with search by Asset Name and QR Code
* [ ] QR code scanner is available in the asset picker
* [ ] Asset picker only shows assets of the same NodeClass
* [ ] Core Attributes are correctly copied between assets
* [ ] Node Terminals are correctly copied
* [ ] Node Subtype and Serviceability are copied
* [ ] Asset Name, QR Code, Location, Photos, Issues, Tasks are NOT copied
* [ ] Confirmation dialog is shown before overwriting
* [ ] Modified asset is flagged for sync after copy
* [ ] Asset Fluency completion updates after copy

h2. QA Test Plan — Copy Asset Details (iOS)

h3. Prerequisites

* SLD with at least 3 nodes of the *same class* (e.g., 3 "Motor" assets)
* At least 1 node of a *different class* (e.g., 1 "Panel" asset)
* At least 1 node with *no class assigned*
* Some nodes should have: core attributes filled, subtype set, serviceability set, QR codes assigned
* Some nodes should have: empty core attributes, no subtype, no serviceability

----

h3. 1. Menu Visibility

||#||Test||Expected||
|1.1|Open asset detail with a class assigned|{{...}} menu visible in toolbar with "Copy Details From..." and "Copy Details To..."|
|1.2|Open asset detail with *no class* assigned|{{...}} menu NOT visible|
|1.3|Open asset detail from a session (e.g., Collect Data mode)|{{...}} menu NOT visible|

----

h3. 2. Copy Details From — Happy Path

||#||Test||Expected||
|2.1|Tap "Copy Details From..."|Picker sheet opens with title "Copy Details From"|
|2.2|Verify picker list|Only shows same-class, non-deleted nodes (excludes current node)|
|2.3|Verify picker row display|Each row shows: icon + label + room name + QR badge (if QR exists)|
|2.4|Tap a source node|Picker dismisses, field selection sheet opens|
|2.5|Verify field selection defaults|All 3 fields checked: Core Attributes, Asset Subtype, Serviceability|
|2.6|Tap "Apply" with all fields checked|Sheet dismisses, alert shows "Details copied from [name]. Review and save."|
|2.7|Verify draft state updated|Core attributes, subtype, and serviceability match the source node|
|2.8|Verify unsaved changes indicator|Cancel button appears (red), save is possible|
|2.9|Tap Save|Node saves successfully with copied data|

----

h3. 3. Copy Details From — Field Selection

||#||Test||Expected||
|3.1|Uncheck "Core Attributes", apply|Only subtype and serviceability copied; core attributes unchanged|
|3.2|Uncheck "Asset Subtype", apply|Only core attributes and serviceability copied; subtype unchanged|
|3.3|Uncheck "Serviceability", apply|Only core attributes and subtype copied; serviceability unchanged|
|3.4|Check only "Serviceability", apply|Only serviceability copied|
|3.5|Uncheck ALL fields|"Apply" button is disabled|

----

h3. 4. Copy Details To — Happy Path

||#||Test||Expected||
|4.1|Tap "Copy Details To..."|Picker sheet opens with title "Copy Details To"|
|4.2|Select a target node|Picker dismisses, field selection sheet opens|
|4.3|Tap "Apply" with all fields checked|Field selection dismisses, target node opens full-screen with prefilled data|
|4.4|Verify target screen|Alert shows "Details copied from [source name]. Review and save."|
|4.5|Verify target draft state|Core attributes, subtype, serviceability match source node's values|
|4.6|Save target node|Saves successfully with copied data|
|4.7|Dismiss target node|Returns to source node screen; source node unchanged|

----

h3. 5. Picker — Search & QR Scanner

||#||Test||Expected||
|5.1|Type in search box by label|List filters to matching nodes|
|5.2|Type in search box by room name|List filters to matching nodes|
|5.3|Type in search box by QR code|List filters to matching nodes|
|5.4|Clear search text (X button)|Full list restored|
|5.5|Tap QR scanner → scan valid same-class node QR|Node auto-selected, picker dismisses|
|5.6|Scan QR of a *different class* node|Alert: "Scanned asset is not the same class as this asset."|
|5.7|Scan QR that matches *no* node|Alert: "No asset found with that QR code."|

----

h3. 6. Empty State

||#||Test||Expected||
|6.1|Open Copy From when node is the *only* asset of its class|Empty state: "No Same-Class Assets" with descriptive message|
|6.2|Verify search bar hidden in empty state|Search bar and QR button NOT shown|

----

h3. 7. Cancellation at Each Step

||#||Test||Expected||
|7.1|Open picker → tap Cancel|Picker dismisses, no state change|
|7.2|Open picker → select node → Cancel field selection|Field selection dismisses, no data copied|
|7.3|Copy To → select node → apply → dismiss target without saving|Returns to source, source data unchanged|
|7.4|After cancelling, retry Copy From|Fresh picker opens, no stale data from previous attempt|

----

h3. 8. Offline / Online Sync

||#||Test||Expected||
|8.1|*Online:* Copy From → save|API call sends updated node with copied fields|
|8.2|*Offline:* Copy From → save|Changes saved locally, sync op queued|
|8.3|*Offline:* Copy From → save → go online|Queued sync replays, server receives copied data|
|8.4|*Online:* Copy To → save target|Target node API call includes all copied fields|
|8.5|*Offline:* Copy To → save target → go online|Target sync op queued and replayed correctly|

----

h3. 9. Edge Cases

||#||Test||Expected||
|9.1|Source has no subtype → Copy From with subtype field|Target subtype cleared (set to nil)|
|9.2|Source has no serviceability → Copy From with serviceability field|Target serviceability cleared|
|9.3|Source has empty core attributes → Copy From|Target core attributes cleared|
|9.4|Node with existing unsaved changes → Copy From|Copied data overwrites draft; user can review all changes before save|
|9.5|Copy From then immediately Copy To (same session)|Both flows work independently, no state leakage|

----

h3. 10. Regression

||#||Test||Expected||
|10.1|Open asset detail normally (no copy)|No visible changes to existing behavior|
|10.2|All existing save flows still work|Label, room, photos, COM, QR code save correctly|
|10.3|Existing features (child nodes, edges, tasks, issues) still work|No interference from new feature|

column1: App Getbeamer Alternate
column41:
h2. Objective

Evaluate alternatives to Beamer (https://app.getbeamer.com/) which is on the higher cost side, and determine if a more cost-effective solution can meet our needs.

----

h2. Work Items

h3. 1. Find Best Alternatives to Beamer

*Beamer Current Pain Points:*

* MAU-based pricing is unpredictable; costs spike with user growth
* Feedback collection costs extra $99/mo add-on
* No AI assistance for writing changelogs
* No native Jira integration for roadmap or release notes
* Development velocity has slowed (gap in releases 2023-2024)

*Top Alternatives Identified:*

||Tool||Focus||Pricing||Best For||
|*Released*|Feedback + Roadmap + AI Release Notes (Jira native)|Per Jira user (scales down with team size)|Jira-centric teams|
|*Featurebase*|Feedback + Changelog + Roadmap|Free + $29/seat/mo|Budget-conscious all-in-one|
|*Canny*|Feedback boards + Changelog + Roadmap|Free (limited) or from $400/mo|Feature request voting|
|*Frill*|Feedback + Changelog + Roadmap|$25-$149/mo|Small teams, multi-language|
|*Productboard*|Full PM suite + Roadmap + Feedback|From $19/maker/mo|Product teams needing full PM|
|*LaunchNotes*|Enterprise changelog + Announcements|Premium (enterprise)|Large SaaS, segmented audiences|
|*Headway*|Simple changelog|Free + $29/mo|Basic changelog only|
|*Sleekplan*|Changelog + Feedback + Roadmap|Competitive pricing|Beamer-like all-in-one at lower cost|

----

h3. 2. Released vs Beamer - Detailed Comparison

*Released* (https://marketplace.atlassian.com/apps/1230872/released-feedback-portal-public-roadmap-ai-release-notes) - Atlassian Marketplace App

||Feature||Beamer||Released||
|Changelog / Release Notes|Yes (manual)|Yes (AI-generated from Jira tickets)|
|In-App Notification Widget|Yes (popups, banners, top bars)|No native widget; embeddable portal only|
|Push Notifications|Yes (web push, mobile)|No|
|Feedback Collection|Yes ($99/mo add-on)|Yes (included - portal, inbox, triage, voting)|
|Public Roadmap|Yes (basic, no Jira sync)|Yes (cross-project, Jira-synced)|
|NPS Surveys|Yes ($99/mo add-on)|No|
|User Onboarding / Tours|Yes|No|
|AI Assistance|None|Yes (AI drafts release notes from Jira data)|
|Jira Integration|None|Native (lives inside Jira)|
|Multi-Product Support|No (single product)|Yes (cross-project views)|
|Segmentation / Targeting|Yes (advanced)|Limited (audience-tailored roadmaps)|
|Analytics|Yes (engagement, reactions)|Limited|
|SSO / User Mgmt|Separate user management|Uses Atlassian identity|

*Pricing Comparison:*

_Beamer:_

* Starter: $49-59/mo (5K MAUs)
* Pro: $99-119/mo (10K MAUs)
* Scale: $249-299/mo (50K MAUs)
* Feedback add-on: +$99/mo
* NPS add-on: +$99/mo
* Min cost for changelog + feedback: ~$150/mo+

_Released:_

* Per Jira user pricing (scales down with team size)
* Standard and Advanced tiers
* Feedback, roadmap, release notes all included
* 30-day free trial available
* Significantly cheaper for small-to-mid teams

*What Released Covers Well (and Often Better):*

* Release notes / changelogs (AI-powered vs manual)
* Feedback collection (included vs $99/mo extra)
* Public roadmap (Jira-synced vs static)
* Branding and customization

*What Released Cannot Replace:*

* In-app notification widgets (no popups, banners, badges)
* Push notifications
* NPS surveys
* User onboarding / product tours
* Resource center / help widget
* Advanced user segmentation
* Email campaign-style announcements

----

h3. 3. Final Summary - Key Takeaways

*If primary use is Changelog + Feedback + Roadmap:*

* *Released is the recommended choice* - cheaper, AI-powered, natively integrated with Jira
* Eliminates manual changelog writing with AI generation from Jira tickets
* Feedback links directly to Jira work items (no context switching)
* Roadmap stays automatically in sync with Jira epics

*If in-app engagement features are needed (notifications, onboarding, NPS):*

* Beamer or Released + a lightweight notification tool is the better path
* Consider Featurebase ($29/seat/mo) as a middle-ground option

*Cost Savings:*

* Beamer with feedback: minimum ~$150/mo, scaling unpredictably with MAUs
* Released: per Jira user pricing, significantly lower for Jira-centric teams
* Potential savings: 40-70% depending on team size and MAU count

*Decision Criteria:*

# Do we primarily need changelog + feedback + roadmap? → Released
# Do we need in-app notifications and push? → Beamer or hybrid approach
# Are we Jira-centric? → Released has a strong advantage
# Is budget the primary concern? → Released or Featurebase

*Next Steps:*

* [ ] Trial Released (30-day free trial)
* [ ] Validate AI release note quality with actual Jira tickets
* [ ] Test feedback portal with internal team
* [ ] Compare actual per-user cost for our team size
* [ ] Make final recommendation

column1: [Android] Connections list pagination support
column41:
h2. Description

Update the Android mobile app to support paginated loading of connections from the new v2 API endpoint.

*Note:* API-based search is NOT in scope for mobile as the app has offline support and uses local search.

h2. Requirements

h3. Pagination

* Integrate with v2 paginated connections list API for data sync/loading
* Support incremental loading or paginated fetching of connections
* Ensure offline data store is properly populated with paginated data

h3. Offline Support

* Continue to support offline-first architecture
* Local search remains unchanged (search on locally cached data)
* Sync mechanism updated to work with paginated API

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* Android app uses the new v2 paginated endpoint for connection data loading
* All connections are properly synced/cached locally via paginated fetches
* Local/offline search continues to work as before
* No regression in offline functionality
* Performance improved for initial sync with large datasets

column1: [Android] Add Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy consent text on Sign-In screen
column41:
h2. Task

Add a consent statement on the Android Sign-In screen below the Sign-In button, with support for two T&C acceptance flows.

h2. Alliance Partner Requirement

The T&C and Privacy Policy URLs are *different per alliance partner*. The app must dynamically resolve the correct URLs based on the active alliance partner context/configuration.

h3. Sample URL Paths

||Alliance Partner||Terms & Conditions||Privacy Policy||
|Partner 1|{{https://{partner1-domain}/terms-and-conditions}}|{{https://{partner1-domain}/privacy-policy}}|
|Partner 2|{{https://{partner2-domain}/terms-and-conditions}}|{{https://{partner2-domain}/privacy-policy}}|

*Note:* Identify actual URLs from the alliance partner configuration. Above are sample reference paths.

h2. Implementation Details

* Add text: "By signing in, you agree to our [Terms & Conditions] and [Privacy Policy]"
* "Terms & Conditions" and "Privacy Policy" should be tappable links
* Dynamically set URLs based on the alliance partner config
* Links open in a Chrome Custom Tab or in-app WebView
* Use secondary/caption text style per Material Design guidelines
* Ensure proper layout on all Android phone and tablet screen sizes

h2. Two Acceptance Flows

h3. Flow 1: New Sign-In

* When a user signs in, the act of signing in constitutes agreement to the latest T&C and Privacy Policy
* On successful sign-in, automatically call the backend API to record acceptance of the latest versions
* No separate prompt needed — the consent text on the Sign-In screen is sufficient

h3. Flow 2: Already Signed-In Users — Policy Update Prompt

* On app launch / session resume, call the backend API to check if the user has accepted the latest T&C and Privacy Policy
* If the user has *not accepted the latest version*, display a mandatory acceptance prompt/modal
* The prompt should include:

  - Message indicating the T&C and/or Privacy Policy has been updated

  - Links to the updated documents (per alliance partner)

  - An "Accept" button

* User *cannot bypass* the prompt — block all app interaction until accepted
* On acceptance, call the backend API to record the acceptance (version + timestamp)
* If both T&C and Privacy Policy have new versions, prompt for both

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Consent text is visible on the Sign-In screen
* [ ] Both links are tappable and open correct URLs in in-app browser
* [ ] URLs are dynamically resolved per alliance partner (not hardcoded)
* [ ] Text styling follows Material Design guidelines (body-small/caption style)
* [ ] Works on all supported Android phone and tablet screen sizes
* [ ] Verified with both alliance partner configurations
* [ ] *Flow 1*: On sign-in, latest T&C/Privacy Policy acceptance is automatically recorded
* [ ] *Flow 2*: On app launch, checks user's accepted versions against latest
* [ ] *Flow 2*: If outdated, shows mandatory acceptance prompt with links and Accept button
* [ ] *Flow 2*: User cannot bypass the prompt or use the app until accepted
* [ ] *Flow 2*: Acceptance is recorded via backend API with version and timestamp

* On page load / session resume, call the backend API to check if the user has accepted the latest T&C and Privacy Policy
* If the user has *not accepted the latest version*, display a mandatory acceptance modal/prompt
* The prompt should include:

  - Message indicating the T&C and/or Privacy Policy has been updated

  - Links to the updated documents (per alliance partner)

  - An "Accept" button

* User *cannot bypass* the prompt — block all navigation/interaction until accepted
* On acceptance, call the backend API to record the acceptance (version + timestamp)
* If both T&C and Privacy Policy have new versions, prompt for both

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Consent text is visible on the Sign-In screen
* [ ] Both links are clickable and open correct URLs in new tab
* [ ] URLs are dynamically resolved per alliance partner (not hardcoded)
* [ ] Text styling is consistent with design system (secondary text)
* [ ] Responsive on desktop, tablet, and mobile web
* [ ] Verified with both alliance partner configurations
* [ ] *Flow 1*: On sign-in, latest T&C/Privacy Policy acceptance is automatically recorded
* [ ] *Flow 2*: On page load, checks user's accepted versions against latest
* [ ] *Flow 2*: If outdated, shows mandatory acceptance modal with links and Accept button
* [ ] *Flow 2*: User cannot bypass the modal or use the app until accepted
* [ ] *Flow 2*: Acceptance is recorded via backend API with version and timestamp

column1: Add Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy consent notice on Sign-In screen
column41:
h2. User Story

As a user, when I am on the Sign-In screen, I should see a consent statement below the Sign-In button that reads:

_"By signing in, you agree to our Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy"_

so that I am informed about the legal agreements before proceeding.

h2. Important: Alliance Partner Configuration

We have *two alliance partners*, and the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy URLs are *different per alliance partner*. The implementation must dynamically resolve the correct URLs based on the active alliance partner context.

h3. Sample URL Paths (per alliance partner)

||Alliance Partner||Terms & Conditions||Privacy Policy||
|Partner 1|{{https://{partner1-domain}/terms-and-conditions}}|{{https://{partner1-domain}/privacy-policy}}|
|Partner 2|{{https://{partner2-domain}/terms-and-conditions}}|{{https://{partner2-domain}/privacy-policy}}|

*Note:* Developers to identify the actual URLs from the respective alliance partner configurations. The URL paths above are sample references for the expected pattern.

h2. Two Acceptance Flows

There are *two distinct scenarios* for T&C and Privacy Policy acceptance:

h3. Flow 1: New Sign-In

* When a user signs in, the act of signing in itself constitutes agreement to the *latest* Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy
* The Sign-In screen displays: _"By signing in, you agree to our Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy"_ with clickable links
* On successful sign-in, the system automatically records the user's acceptance of the current latest T&C and Privacy Policy versions (no separate prompt needed)

h3. Flow 2: Already Signed-In Users — Policy Update Prompt

* When a new version of T&C or Privacy Policy is published, users who are *already signed in* (existing sessions) must be prompted to accept the updated policy
* On app launch or session resume, the system checks if the user's last accepted version matches the latest published version
* If the user has *not accepted the latest version*, a mandatory acceptance prompt/modal is displayed before allowing them to continue using the app
* The prompt should show links to the updated T&C / Privacy Policy and an "Accept" button
* The user *cannot bypass* this prompt — they must accept to continue using the app
* On acceptance, the system records the new accepted version and timestamp

h2. T&C Versioning Requirement

Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy must support *multiple versions* to enable the flows above.

h3. Versioning Requirements

* Each T&C and Privacy Policy document must have a version identifier (e.g., v1.0, v2.0)
* The system must track which version each user has accepted
* T&C and Privacy Policy are versioned *independently* — a user may need to accept one but not the other
* Once accepted, store the accepted version and timestamp against the user's profile

h3. Suggested Approach

* Backend: Maintain a T&C/Privacy Policy version registry (version number, effective date, content/URL per alliance partner)
* Backend: Store user acceptance records (user ID, document type, accepted version, accepted timestamp)
* Backend: Provide an API endpoint to check if the user has accepted the latest version and to record acceptance
* Frontend/Mobile:

  - *Flow 1 (Sign-in)*: On successful sign-in, call the API to automatically record acceptance of latest versions

  - *Flow 2 (Existing session)*: On app launch / session resume, call the API to check acceptance status; if outdated, show the mandatory acceptance prompt

h2. Acceptance Criteria

h3. Sign-In Screen

* A text statement is displayed on the Sign-In screen near/below the Sign-In button
* The text reads: "By signing in, you agree to our Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy"
* "Terms & Conditions" is a clickable hyperlink that opens the correct T&C page based on the alliance partner
* "Privacy Policy" is a clickable hyperlink that opens the correct Privacy Policy page based on the alliance partner
* Links must be *dynamically resolved* based on the alliance partner (not hardcoded)
* Links should open in a new tab/browser (web) or in-app browser (mobile)
* The statement should be visible without scrolling on standard screen sizes
* Font size should be smaller/secondary text style, consistent with industry standards

h3. Flow 1: New Sign-In Acceptance

* On successful sign-in, the latest T&C and Privacy Policy versions are automatically recorded as accepted for the user
* No separate prompt is needed — signing in = agreeing

h3. Flow 2: Existing User Policy Update

* When T&C or Privacy Policy is updated, already signed-in users see a mandatory acceptance prompt on next app launch
* Prompt includes links to the updated documents and an "Accept" button
* User cannot bypass the prompt or use the app until accepted
* Acceptance is recorded via backend API with version and timestamp

h3. General

* T&C and Privacy Policy versioning tracked independently
* Must be implemented across all platforms: Web, iOS, and Android

h2. References

* Similar to Google, Microsoft, and other SaaS sign-in flows
* Ensure compliance with SOC2, GDPR, and standard privacy regulations

column1: [Pentest F-001] Weak Content Security Policy (CSP)
column41:
h2. Overview

*Pentest Finding ID:* F-001

*Severity:* Low

*Category:* WSTG-CONF-12 – Test for Content Security Policy

*OWASP Top 10:* A05:2021 – Security Misconfiguration

*CWE:* CWE-693: Protection Mechanism Failure

h2. Description

The application defines a Content Security Policy (CSP) that only prevents clickjacking ({{frame-ancestors 'none'}}) but does not meaningfully restrict other types of active content or external resources. The CSP does not provide broader protections against XSS, data exfiltration, or abuse of inline scripts/third-party libraries.

h2. Affected URL

* All pages: {{https://acme.egalvanic.ai/*}}

h2. Current CSP Header

{{Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none'}}

h2. Reproduction Steps

# Log in as any user and use any feature while proxying through Burp Suite
# Observe the Content-Security-Policy header is only {{frame-ancestors 'none'}}
# This only prevents clickjacking but does not mitigate XSS attacks

h2. Remediation Recommendations

* Design a restrictive, application-wide CSP with {{default-src}} directive limiting to trusted origins
* Extend policy beyond {{frame-ancestors}} to include {{script-src}}, {{style-src}}, {{img-src}}, {{connect-src}}, etc.
* Use nonce-based or hash-based execution instead of {{unsafe-inline}}
* Apply strict CSP to styles and other active content
* Deploy new policy in {{Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only}} mode first, then enforce once stable

column1: [Pentest F-002] Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
column41:
h2. Overview

*Pentest Finding ID:* F-002

*Severity:* Medium

*Category:* WSTG-BUSL-09 – Test Upload of Malicious Files

*OWASP Top 10:* A05:2021 – Security Misconfiguration

*CWE:* CWE-434: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type

h2. Description

The file upload functionality does not enforce adequate server-side validation of file types. Executable files (e.g., .exe) can be uploaded by bypassing client-side checks. The server accepts and stores the file in the S3 bucket without restriction. While uploaded files are not directly executed on the web server, the platform could be used to host and distribute malicious content.

h2. Affected Endpoint

* *Presigned Upload:* {{/api/attachment/presigned-upload}}

h2. Reproduction Steps

# Log in as any user with a role other than client portal user
# Navigate to Attachments section and click Upload Attachments
# Select a file with a dangerous type (e.g., net.exe)
# Observe the application accepts and stores the file without rejecting it based on its extension or content type

h2. Remediation Recommendations

* Enforce a strict server-side allowlist for file types (e.g., PDF, DOCX, PNG, JPEG)
* Validate file content type by magic numbers (binary signature inspection)
* Normalize and sanitize file names and extensions (strip special characters, collapse multiple extensions)
* Integrate malware/content scanning for uploads
* Enforce file size and count limits
* Serve stored files with {{Content-Disposition: attachment}} header
* Provide clear feedback on rejected uploads

column1: [Pentest F-003] Unauthenticated APIs - Endpoints Accessible Without Authentication
column41:
h2. Overview

*Pentest Finding ID:* F-003

*Severity:* High

*Category:* WSTG-ATHN-04 – Testing for Bypassing Authentication Schema

*OWASP Top 10:* A01:2021 – Broken Access Control

*CWE:* CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

h2. Description

Certain APIs can be accessed without requiring a valid user session or authentication. Backend endpoints respond to requests from unauthenticated users and return structured data that should only be available to logged-in users. Removing the Authorization header still returns valid responses.

h2. Affected Endpoints

* *Attachments:* {{/api/lookup/attachments/{id}}} - Returns attachments without authentication
* *Work Order Details:* Work order detail data accessible without Authorization header

h2. Reproduction Steps

# Log in as any user and navigate to Attachments section
# Intercept the request using Burp Suite
# Remove the Authorization header and resend the request
# Observe the API still returns the attachments data
# Repeat for Work Order detail endpoints - same behavior observed

h2. Remediation Recommendations

* Enforce server-side authentication on all affected APIs, rejecting requests without valid Authorization header
* Implement centralized authorization checks for business data endpoints
* Restrict anonymous access to API routes at the gateway/reverse proxy level
* Validate user-to-resource access (tenant/project/role check) on the backend
* Align API security with frontend expectations (if UI requires login, backend must too)
* Review and classify all existing API endpoints as public or private

column1: [Pentest F-004] Improper Authorization - Client Portal Users Can Access Admin-Only Endpoints
column41:
h2. Overview

*Pentest Finding ID:* F-004

*Severity:* High

*Category:* WSTG-ATHZ-02 – Testing for Bypassing Authorization Schema

*OWASP Top 10:* A01:2021 – Broken Access Control

*CWE:* CWE-258: Improper Authorization

h2. Description

The application does not consistently enforce authorization checks on certain server-side functions, allowing users to access functionality or data restricted based on their role/permissions. By manipulating parameters and directly requesting specific endpoints, Client Portal users can perform actions reserved for Admin users.

h2. Affected Endpoints

* *Audit Log:* {{/api/mutations}} - Client Portal user can retrieve audit logs
* *Attachments:* File upload endpoint accessible by Client Portal user
* *Work Order Planning:* Work orders retrieval and update accessible by Client Portal user
* *Settings > Users:* Company user information accessible by Client Portal user
* *Settings > PM > Test Equipment Library:* Test equipment data accessible by Client Portal user
* *Settings > PM > Equipment Library:* Equipment data accessible by Client Portal user

h2. Reproduction Steps

# Log in as a Client Portal user and capture the auth token
# Use the Client Portal user token to call Admin-only endpoints (Audit Log, Attachments, Work Orders, Users, Equipment)
# Observe that requests are processed successfully despite the user not having UI access to these features

h2. Remediation Recommendations

* Enforce Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) with least privilege on all endpoints
* Implement server-side authorization checks on every API endpoint
* Use resource-level (object-level) access controls
* Centralize authorization logic in a middleware layer
* Apply deny-by-default policy configuration
* Harden administrative interfaces to restrict admin-only endpoints
* Validate and ignore client-side authorization flags
