column1: Temp S3 Bucket for Staged Photo AI Nameplate Extraction
column41:
h3. Summary

A temporary S3 bucket is needed for staged photo AI nameplate extraction. Currently, users must save photos to S3 before extraction, causing workflow delays.

h3. Context

AI nameplate extraction only works on photos synced to S3. Users cannot extract nameplate data from staged photos until after saving the asset, which adds friction to the workflow.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* New S3 bucket created and accessible from the backend.
* Environment variables added to {{.env.example}} and loaded in {{app/config.py}}.
* Cron job runs daily at 12 AM PST and deletes temp photos older than the configured retention period.
* Retention period is configurable via {{TEMP_PHOTOS_RETENTION_DAYS}}.
* Cleanup job logs deletion count.
* Existing extraction flow is not affected.

h3. Other information

*Requirements:*

# New Temp S3 Bucket
#* Create a new S3 bucket for temporary/staged photo storage (e.g., {{pgz-s3-temp-photos}}).
#* Bucket should follow the same region ({{us-east-2}}) and access patterns as existing buckets.
#* Bucket naming convention should follow existing pattern: {{pgz-s3-<purpose>}}.
# Environment Configuration
#* Add the following environment variables:
#** {{S3_BUCKET_TEMP_PHOTOS}} — Temp bucket name (e.g., {{pgz-s3-temp-photos}}).
#** {{TEMP_PHOTOS_RETENTION_DAYS}} — Number of days to retain temp photos before cleanup (default: {{7}}, configurable).
#** {{TEMP_PHOTOS_CLEANUP_ENABLED}} — Toggle for the cleanup cron job (default: {{true}}).
#* *Files to update:*
#** {{.env.example}} — Add new env vars with defaults.
#** {{app/config.py}} — Load new env vars into app config.
# Cron Job for Temp Photo Cleanup
#* Implement a scheduled cleanup job with the following behavior:
#** *Schedule:* Runs daily at *12:00 AM PST* (08:00 UTC).
#** *Action:* Deletes all objects in the temp bucket that were created more than {{TEMP_PHOTOS_RETENTION_DAYS}} ago (default: 7 days).
#** *Configurable:* Retention period should be configurable via environment variable.
#** *Logging:* Log the number of objects deleted per run.
#* *Implementation Notes:*
#** The current backend has no cron framework — uses a poll-based mutation worker ({{mutation_worker.py}}) for background tasks.
#** Consider introducing *APScheduler* (lightweight, Flask-compatible) or a similar scheduling library.
#** Alternatively, this could be implemented as an *AWS EventBridge rule + Lambda* for serverless cleanup.
#** S3 Lifecycle Policies could also handle expiration natively (simplest approach).

*Technical Context:*

* *Existing S3 Setup:*
** S3 client: {{app/services/s3_service.py}} (boto3, presigned URLs, batch operations).
** Current buckets: {{S3_BUCKET_BRANDING}}, {{S3_BUCKET_IR_PHOTOS}}, {{S3_BUCKET_ONBOARDING}}, {{S3_BUCKET_ATTACHMENTS}}, {{S3_BUCKET_ASSET_PHOTOS}}, {{S3_BUCKET_REPORTING}}, {{S3_BUCKET_SLDS}}.
* *Extraction Flow:*
** Route: {{POST /extraction/extract-nameplate-data}} ({{app/routes/extraction_routes.py}}).
** Controller: {{app/controllers/extraction_controller.py}} — parses S3 URLs, builds prompts, invokes Lambda.
** Lambda: {{EXTRACTION_LAMBDA_NAME}} env var (default: {{eg-pz-prod-structured-photo-extraction-ohio}}).

column1: Send Quotes to Multiple Recipients
column41:
h3. *Medium Priority*

When sending to Printed Quotes to recipients through the Quote (Status from Draft -> Sent to Customer) it would be useful to be able to select multiple recipients.

----

User story

* As a user, when sending Printed Quotes, I want to select multiple recipients so that I can efficiently send quotes to all relevant parties.

h3. Context

The issue arises when sending Printed Quotes. Currently, the system only allows sending to a single recipient. This feature would enhance usability by enabling multiple recipients selection.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* Users can select multiple recipients when sending Printed Quotes.
* The system should allow the status to change from Draft to Sent to Customer for all selected recipients.

h3. Other information

* Priority: Medium

column1: Z | iOS | Asset Photos Fail to Sync After Offline Creation Showing "Entry No Longer Exist" Error
column41:
*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Turn *offline mode ON* in the application.
# Go to *Asset* and create a new asset.
# Add photos in the following sections:
#* Asset Photo
#* Nameplate Photo
#* Panel Schedule Photo
#* Arc Flash Photo
# Save the asset while still in *offline mode*.
# Wait for some time (around *1 hour*).
# Turn *online mode ON* and perform *data sync*.
# Open the *Sync Queue Analyzer* and check the photo sync status.

*Description:*
When an asset is created in offline mode and photos are added (Asset Photo, Nameplate Photo, Panel Schedule Photo, and Arc Flash Photo), the asset saves successfully. However, after going online and syncing the data later, the photos fail to sync. In the *Sync Queue Analyzer*, the photos show *sync failure* with the error message *"Entry No Longer Exist."*

*Expected Result:*
All photos added to the asset in *offline mode* should *sync successfully* when the device goes online and data synchronization is performed, without any error.

[BE] Auto-generate FLIR Visual Image when FLIR-IND is selected in Work Order
column41:
h2. Backend Implementation — Auto-generate FLIR Visual Image

h3. Objective

Implement the backend API and processing logic to extract and generate a visual image from a FLIR IR radiometric image when the FLIR-IND work order configuration is active.

h3. Scope of Work

h4. 1. FLIR Radiometric Image Processing

* Implement a service to parse FLIR radiometric JPEG (RJPEG) files
* Extract the embedded visual image from the FLIR IR image metadata/APP1 marker
* FLIR cameras store both thermal (IR) and visual images within a single radiometric JPEG file — the visual image is embedded as a secondary image in the EXIF/APP1 segment
* Support standard FLIR radiometric formats (RJPEG with embedded visual)
* Handle edge cases where the visual image is not embedded (older cameras, different configurations)

h4. 2. API Endpoint

* Create an API endpoint to trigger visual image extraction from a FLIR IR image

  - *POST* {{/api/v1/work-orders/{workOrderId}/flir-visual-extract}} (or similar)

  - Input: FLIR IR image reference (image ID or file path)

  - Output: Generated visual image URL, metadata (dimensions, format, extraction status)

* Implement validation to ensure:

  - The work order has FLIR-IND configuration active

  - The uploaded image is a valid FLIR radiometric file

  - The file contains embedded visual data

h4. 3. Storage & Association

* Store the extracted visual image in S3 (or existing image storage service)
* Create a database record linking the visual image to:

  - The parent work order

  - The source IR image

  - The asset / inspection point

* Add metadata fields to distinguish auto-generated visual images from manually uploaded ones
* Implement proper file naming convention for generated visual images

h4. 4. Auto-Generation Trigger

* When an IR image is uploaded to a work order with FLIR-IND configuration:

  1. Detect that FLIR-IND is active for the work order

  2. Parse the uploaded FLIR radiometric file

  3. Extract the embedded visual image

  4. Store the visual image and create the association

  5. Return the visual image data to the frontend

* Consider implementing this as an async process (background job) for large files
* Support batch processing for existing work orders that need retroactive visual generation

h4. 5. Error Handling

* Return appropriate error responses:

  - {{400}} — Invalid file format (not a FLIR radiometric image)

  - {{404}} — No embedded visual data found in the FLIR image

  - {{422}} — Work order does not have FLIR-IND configuration

  - {{500}} — Internal processing error

* Log errors for monitoring and debugging
* Implement retry logic for transient failures

h4. 6. Testing

* Unit tests for FLIR radiometric image parsing and visual extraction
* Unit tests for API endpoint validation and response formatting
* Integration tests for the full flow (upload → extract → store → respond)
* Test with various FLIR camera models and image formats
* Test edge cases (corrupted files, missing visual data, oversized files)
* Performance tests for large FLIR radiometric files

h3. Technical Notes

* FLIR radiometric JPEG files contain embedded visual images in the EXIF APP1 segment or as a secondary JPEG stream
* Libraries to consider: {{exiftool}} (CLI), {{Pillow}} with EXIF support, or a dedicated FLIR SDK
* The extracted visual image is typically a standard JPEG
* File sizes: IR+visual combined files can be 2-10MB; extracted visual is typically 0.5-2MB

h3. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] API endpoint extracts visual image from FLIR radiometric files
* [ ] Visual image is stored in S3 and linked to the work order and source IR image
* [ ] Auto-generation triggers when IR image is uploaded with FLIR-IND configuration active
* [ ] Proper error handling for invalid files, missing visual data, and incorrect configurations
* [ ] Database records correctly associate generated visual images with their source IR images
* [ ] API response includes visual image URL and metadata for frontend consumption
* [ ] Batch/retroactive processing supported for existing FLIR-IND work orders

column1: [FE] Auto-generate FLIR Visual Image when FLIR-IND is selected in Work Order
column41:
h2. Frontend Implementation — Auto-generate FLIR Visual Image

h3. Objective

Implement the frontend changes to support auto-generation and display of FLIR visual images when the FLIR-IND work order configuration is selected.

h3. Scope of Work

h4. 1. Work Order Configuration Detection

* Detect when *FLIR-IND* is selected as the work order configuration
* Trigger the visual image auto-generation flow upon FLIR IR image upload when FLIR-IND is active

h4. 2. API Integration

* Call the backend API to extract the visual image from the uploaded FLIR IR image
* Handle the API response containing the generated visual image URL/data
* Implement proper error handling for cases where visual extraction fails (e.g., no embedded visual data)
* Show loading/progress indicator while the visual image is being generated

h4. 3. UI/UX Implementation

* Display the auto-generated visual image alongside the IR image in the work order detail view
* Implement side-by-side or tabbed view for IR and visual image comparison
* Add a visual indicator/badge showing the image was auto-generated
* Support image zoom, pan, and full-screen viewing for both IR and visual images
* Handle empty states when visual image generation is not possible
* Ensure responsive layout for different screen sizes

h4. 4. Image Upload Flow

* When a FLIR IR image is uploaded and FLIR-IND is configured:

  1. Upload the IR image as normal

  2. Trigger backend API call to extract the visual image

  3. Display both images once the visual extraction completes

  4. Show appropriate status messages during processing

* Support drag-and-drop and file picker upload methods

h4. 5. Testing

* Unit tests for FLIR-IND configuration detection logic
* Unit tests for visual image generation trigger flow
* Integration tests for API calls to backend visual extraction endpoint
* UI tests for side-by-side image display
* Test error scenarios (no embedded visual, corrupt files, network failures)
* Cross-browser testing (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)

h3. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] FLIR-IND selection in work order triggers visual image auto-generation on IR image upload
* [ ] Generated visual image displays alongside the IR image
* [ ] Loading states shown during visual image generation
* [ ] Error states handled gracefully with user-friendly messages
* [ ] Image viewer supports zoom and full-screen for both IR and visual images
* [ ] Responsive design works across desktop and tablet breakpoints
column223: Auto-generate FLIR Visual Image when FLIR-IND is selected in Work Order

column1: Auto-generate FLIR Visual Image when FLIR-IND is selected in Work Order
column41:
h2. User Story

*As a* field technician / inspector,

*I want* a FLIR visual image to be automatically generated when the FLIR-IND work order configuration is selected,

*So that* I can have both the IR (infrared) and visual reference images available without manually capturing a separate visual photo, improving inspection efficiency and report completeness.

h2. Background

In the legacy platform, when the *FLIR-IND* configuration is selected for a work order, the system automatically generates a corresponding visual image from the IR (infrared) image captured by the FLIR camera. This feature needs to be replicated in the PZ platform.

FLIR cameras capture both infrared and embedded visual data in the same radiometric image file. The visual image can be extracted from the FLIR radiometric file metadata/embedded data and displayed alongside the IR image.

h2. Scope

* When *FLIR-IND* is selected as the work order configuration, the system should automatically extract and generate the visual image from the uploaded FLIR IR image
* The generated visual image should be stored and associated with the same work order / asset / inspection point
* The visual image should be displayed alongside the IR image in the work order detail view
* This should work for both new image uploads and existing FLIR IR images where FLIR-IND is configured

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] When FLIR-IND configuration is selected in a work order, uploading a FLIR IR image automatically generates the corresponding visual image
* [ ] The auto-generated visual image is stored in the system and linked to the same work order/asset
* [ ] Both IR and visual images are displayed side by side in the work order detail view
* [ ] The visual image extraction works for standard FLIR radiometric image formats (RJPEG)
* [ ] If the FLIR image does not contain embedded visual data, the system handles it gracefully with an appropriate message
* [ ] Existing work orders with FLIR-IND configuration can retroactively generate visual images
* [ ] The feature mirrors the legacy platform behavior as shown in the reference video

h2. Reference

* Legacy platform video attached showing the FLIR-IND visual image auto-generation behavior
* Related existing work: ZP-1158 (Display IR Photos on Mobile App), ZP-1157 (Android IR Photos)

column1: Dashboard Overview Pages - Performance & UX Improvements (Ops Overview, Sales Overview)
column41:
h2. Overview

Multiple performance and UX improvements needed across the Ops Overview and Sales Overview dashboard pages to improve data loading, navigation, and usability.

----

h2. 1. Ops Overview - Open Issues & Due Soon Sections

*Current Behavior:* All issues are being loaded at once on the Ops Overview page.

*Expected Behavior:*

* Load a maximum of *15–20 issues* initially
* Add a *"View More"* option that redirects the user to the Issues list page
* Make each issue *clickable* so it redirects to the issue detail page

  - Add an *underline* (or similar visual cue) so users know items are clickable

* *Same behavior applies to the "Due Soon / Unscheduled" section*

----

h2. 2. Ops Overview - Action Items

*Current Behavior:* All Action Items are loaded at once (currently showing 110 items).

*Expected Behavior:*

* Implement a *"Load More"* option instead of loading all action items at once
* Initial load should display a reasonable batch, with the ability to load more on demand

----

h2. 3. Sales Overview - Goals Section

*Current Behavior:* When more than 5 goals are added, the entire container/page becomes scrollable.

*Expected Behavior:*

* Only the *Goals section itself* should be scrollable (not the full page)
* Load a *maximum of 5 goals* initially (currently loads all goals)
* Provide a *redirection link to the Goals Tab* for viewing all goals

----

h2. 4. Sales Overview - PM Role: Goal Setting Permissions

*Current Behavior:* Project Managers (PM role) are unable to set Goals.

*Expected Behavior:*

* PMs should have the ability to *create and set Goals*
* Verify and update role-based permissions accordingly

----

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Ops Overview: Open Issues limited to 15-20 with "View More" redirect to Issues page
* [ ] Ops Overview: Issues are clickable with underline, redirecting to detail page
* [ ] Ops Overview: Due Soon section follows the same pattern as Open Issues
* [ ] Ops Overview: Action Items load incrementally with "Load More" option
* [ ] Sales Overview: Goals section scrolls independently (max 5 loaded initially)
* [ ] Sales Overview: Goals section has redirection to Goals Tab
* [ ] Sales Overview: PM role can set/create Goals

column1: Z | Android | Deleted node mappings incorrectly counted as incomplete when completing task
column41:
*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open a *task* that has linked nodes.
# Delete one or more of the linked *nodes* (from SLD).
# Change the task status to *Complete*.
# Tap *Save*.
# Observe the incomplete nodes confirmation dialog.

*Description:*
When completing a task, the {{getTaskNodeMappingsForTask}} DAO query filters out deleted mappings ({{isDeleted = 0}}) but does not check whether the linked *node itself* is deleted in the {{nodes_v2}} table. This causes deleted nodes to be counted as incomplete, showing an incorrect count in the confirmation dialog.

*Expected Result:*
Deleted nodes should not be counted as incomplete. Only active (non-deleted) node mappings linked to active nodes should be considered.

column1: iOS - "Proposed Resolution" Field Missing on Create New Issue Screen + No Validation Message for Issue Class
column41:
*Issue 1: Proposed Resolution Field Missing*

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open the Z Platform iOS app
# Login with valid credentials and select a site
# Navigate to *Issues* (via Dashboard > Issues or bottom tab)
# Tap the *"+"* button to create a new issue
# Observe the *New Issue* screen — visible fields are:
#* Issue Class dropdown
#* Title text field
#* Priority dropdown
#* Asset selection (under ASSIGNMENT)
# Scroll down through the entire form
# Look for a "Proposed Resolution" text field

*Expected:* A "Proposed Resolution" text box should be visible on the Create New Issue screen *Actual:* The field is *completely absent*. It only appears when you create the issue first, then open it and tap "Edit".

----

*Issue 2: No Validation Message for Issue Class*

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open the Z Platform iOS app
# Tap "+" to create a new issue
# Fill in the *Title* field (e.g., "Test Issue")
# Select an *Asset* (e.g., "1abcd")
# Leave *Issue Class* as "None" (do NOT select any class)
# Tap *"Create Issue."*

*Expected:* A validation error message should appear below the Issue Class dropdown: "Issue Class is required" (with red text, similar to how "Asset is required" shows on web when Asset is missing) *Actual:* *No validation message appears* for Issue Class. Either:

* The issue is created with Issue Class = "None" (no classification), OR
* The button does nothing with no feedback — the user has no idea why it's not working

column1: Web - Issue Class and Asset fields are not enforced as mandatory, allowing incomplete issues to be created.
column41:
*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Login to [https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai|https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai|smart-link]  with valid credentials
# Navigate to *Issues* page ({{/issues}})
# Click the *"+ Create Issue"* button (top right)
# In the Create Issue modal, fill in *only* these two fields:
#* *Title:* "Test Issue No Class No Asset"
#* *Proposed Resolution:* "test resolution"
# Leave *Issue Class* as "None" (default)
# Leave *Asset* field empty (no asset selected)
# Leave *Priority* as "None" (default)
# Click *"Create Issue"* button

*Expected Result:*

* Issue Class should be a *mandatory field* — form should show validation error: "Issue Class is required"
* Asset should be a *mandatory field* — form should show validation error: "Asset is required"
* The "Create Issue" button should remain *disabled* or show validation errors until both Issue Class and Asset are selected
* An issue without a class and asset has no context — it cannot be categorized, tracked, or associated with any equipment

*Actual Result:*

* Issue is created *successfully* with no validation errors
* Issue Class is saved as "None" — issue has no classification
* Asset field is saved as empty — issue is not linked to any asset
* The new issue appears in the Issues list with no class, no asset, and no priority
* No warning or confirmation prompt about missing critical fields

h2. iOS Test Plan: AI Extraction from Staged Nameplate Photos

----

h3. TC-1: AddAsset — Extract from staged nameplate photos

# Tap the *sparkles* button
# Choose *"Overwrite existing values"*
# *Expected:*
#* Spinner shows during extraction
#* 1 batch presigned URL call + 4 S3 PUT uploads to {{temp_photos}} bucket
#* Core attribute fields populate with extracted values
#* Success alert shows count of extracted attributes
#* Photos still show as staged (not synced)

h3. TC-2: AddAsset — Extract with "Keep existing values"

# Open Add Asset → select a node class → switch to Detailed mode
# Manually fill *Manufacturer* = "Test"
# Add *2 nameplate photos*
# Tap sparkles → choose *"Keep existing values"*
# *Expected:*
#* "Manufacturer" stays as "Test" (not overwritten)
#* Empty fields get populated with extracted values

h3. TC-3: AddAsset — Extract again after first extraction

# Complete TC-1 (extraction fills attributes)
# Tap sparkles again → choose "Overwrite existing values"
# *Expected:*
#* Staged photos re-uploaded to temp bucket (expected, matches Android)
#* Extraction runs again, attributes may update
#* No crash or error

h3. TC-4: EditNode — Mixed staged + uploaded nameplate photos

# Open an existing asset that has *3 uploaded nameplate photos*
# Add *1 new nameplate photo* (staged, not yet synced)
# Tap sparkles → choose "Overwrite existing values"
# *Expected:*
#* Takes *temp path* (because 1 photo is staged)
#* Only the 1 staged photo uploaded to temp bucket
#* API receives 4 URLs (1 temp + 3 existing)
#* Core attributes update and persist to SwiftData
#* No false "unsaved changes" flag after extraction

h3. TC-5: EditNode — All nameplate photos already uploaded

# Open an existing asset with *2 uploaded nameplate photos* (all synced)
# Tap sparkles → choose any option
# *Expected:*
#* Takes *standard path* (extractNameplateData with nodeId)
#* No temp S3 upload
#* Fetches enriched node after extraction
#* Attributes update and persist

h3. TC-6: Non-nameplate photo doesn't trigger temp path

# Open an existing asset with *2 uploaded nameplate photos*
# Add *1 new profile photo* (staged)
# Tap sparkles
# *Expected:*
#* Takes *standard path* (not temp)
#* Profile photo is ignored — only nameplate photos are considered
#* Extraction uses nodeId-based API

# *Expected:* Sparkles button appears immediately

#* No sparkles button

h3. TC-11: Extraction API failure

# *Expected:*
#* Spinner stops
#* Error alert shows with error message
#* Form state unchanged (no partial attribute writes)

h3. TC-12: Token expired during extraction

# Open Add Asset with nameplate photos

# Tap sparkles
# *Expected:*

#* Retries successfully

h3. TC-13: Save asset after extraction (AddAsset)

# Complete TC-1 (extraction fills core attributes)

h3. TC-14: Cancel after extraction (AddAsset)

# Complete TC-1 (extraction fills attributes)

# *Expected:*
#* No node created
#* Staged photo local files cleaned up
#* Temp bucket files remain (server-side cleanup)

h3. TC-15: EditNode — "Keep existing" preserves user values

column1: Z | Android | Terminal-related issues when changing node type in SLD
column41:
*Issues:*

# *Edge updates synced before node update when changing node class*
#* When a node's class is changed (e.g., UPS → ATS), the terminal reassignment logic was updating and syncing edges with new terminal IDs _before_ the node itself was saved and synced.
#* This caused the server to receive edge updates referencing terminal IDs it didn't know about yet, since the node update (which carries the new terminals) hadn't been sent.
# *Missing terminal ID resolution when creating edges*
#* When creating a new edge, if {{sourceNodeTerminalId}} or {{targetNodeTerminalId}} were null but {{sourceHandle}}/{{targetHandle}} were provided, the terminal IDs were not being resolved from the node terminal table.

----

*Fix:*

# *Split terminal reassignment into two phases (UpdateNodesUseCase)*
#* _Phase 1_: Soft-delete old terminals, create new terminals, and compute edge-to-terminal mapping — but do NOT update edges yet.
#* Save and sync the node (server now knows about the new terminals).
#* _Phase 2_: Apply pending edge updates with new terminal IDs (server already has the terminals).
# *Resolve terminal IDs from handle codes during edge creation (CreateEdgeUseCase)*
#* Added {{NodeTerminalDao.getByNodeIdAndHandleCode()}} query.
#* When {{sourceNodeTerminalId}}/{{targetNodeTerminalId}} is null and a handle code is provided, resolve the terminal ID by looking up the terminal via nodeId + handleCode.

----

*Files Changed:*

* {{NodeTerminalDao.kt}} — Added {{getByNodeIdAndHandleCode()}} query
* {{UpdateNodesUseCase.kt}} — Split {{reassignTerminalsForNode}} into {{swapTerminalsForNode}} (Phase 1) + {{applyPendingEdgeUpdates}} (Phase 2); reordered flow so node is saved/synced before edges
* {{CreateEdgeUseCase.kt}} — Added terminal ID resolution from handle codes when creating edges
* {{SldViewModel.kt}} — Removed redundant edge points update after edge creation

column1: Offline Sync Fails After Session Expiry — "Entity no longer exists" and "Not authenticated" Errors on All Queued Mutations
column41:
*Priority:* P0 — CRITICAL

*Module:* Offline Mode > Sync Queue

*Platform:* iOS (Z Platform mobile app)

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open the Z Platform iOS app and select a site (online mode)
# Go Offline (Settings > Go Offline)
# While offline, create multiple items: assets, tasks, connections, etc.
# Leave the device idle/unused for *1+ hour* so the authentication token expires
# Go back Online (Settings > Go Online)
# The app attempts to sync queued offline mutations
# Navigate to Settings > Sync Queue Analyzer > History tab

*Expected Result:*

* App should automatically refresh the auth token before attempting sync
* All queued offline operations should sync successfully
* If token refresh fails, show a clear "Please log in again to sync your changes" prompt — and *preserve the pending queue* so data is not lost after re-login

*Actual Result:*

* Sync attempts fire with an *expired token*
* *"node - create"* mutations fail with *"Not authenticated"* (takes 11.99s to fail — server timeout)
* *"userTask - create"* mutations fail with *"Entity no longer exists"* (0.01s — instant rejection)
* All 44 mutations permanently moved to *History as failed* — the data created offline is *lost forever*
* No prompt to re-authenticate, no option to re-queue failed items

column1: SLD Performance — Moving Connected Assets Causes Lag on Large Layouts (1800+ Assets)
column41:
*Problem:* When dragging/moving an asset that has connections (lines) to other assets in a large SLD layout (1800+ assets), the interface lags significantly. The asset movement is not smooth — it stutters and takes 3–4 seconds for the canvas to update, making it very difficult to reposition connected assets accurately.

*Expected Behavior:*

* Moving/dragging a connected asset should be smooth and responsive regardless of the number of assets in the layout
* Connection lines should redraw in real-time as the asset is dragged, without freezing or stuttering

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open an SLD Layout containing 1800+ assets
# Unlock the SLD for editing
# Select an asset that has one or more connections (lines) to other assets
# Click and drag the asset to move it to a new position
# Observe the lag/stutter — the asset movement is not smooth, and connection lines take 3–4 seconds to update

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* Optimize asset drag/move operation for connected assets in large SLD layouts (1800+ assets)
* Connection lines should redraw smoothly in real-time during drag operations
* No functional regression in SLD behavior after optimization
* Test with layouts containing 1800+ assets to verify performance improvements

column1: FE: Create centralized date/time formatting utility with timezone support
column41:
h2. Objective

Create a reusable date/time formatting utility that converts UTC dates from the API to the company's configured timezone for display.

h2. Changes Required

h3. 1. Date Formatting Utility

Create a centralized utility module (e.g., {{utils/dateTime.ts}} or {{utils/timezone.ts}}) with functions:

{noformat}// Core functions
formatDateTime(utcDate: string, format?: string): string  // e.g., "Mar 05, 2026 2:30 PM"
formatDate(utcDate: string, format?: string): string       // e.g., "Mar 05, 2026"
formatTime(utcDate: string, format?: string): string       // e.g., "2:30 PM"
formatDateShort(utcDate: string): string                   // e.g., "03-05-26"
formatDateLong(utcDate: string): string                    // e.g., "March 5th, 2026"
formatRelative(utcDate: string): string                    // e.g., "2 hours ago"{noformat}

* All functions read the company timezone from global app state
* If no company timezone is set, default to {{'UTC'}}
* Use a timezone-capable library: {{dayjs}} + {{dayjs/plugin/timezone}} + {{dayjs/plugin/utc}}, OR {{date-fns-tz}}, OR {{luxon}}

h3. 2. Library Selection Considerations

* *dayjs* - Lightweight (~2KB + plugins), popular, moment.js-compatible API
* *date-fns-tz* - Tree-shakeable, functional approach
* *luxon* - Rich API, built-in timezone support, slightly heavier

Choose based on what's already in use in the project or team preference.

h3. 3. Date Input Conversion (UTC to send to API)

Also include a utility for converting user-entered dates back to UTC:

{noformat}toUTCString(localDate: Date | string): string  // Convert company-tz date to UTC ISO string{noformat}

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Centralized formatting utility exists and is importable across the app
* [ ] All functions respect the company timezone setting
* [ ] Utility handles null/undefined dates gracefully
* [ ] Utility handles edge cases (DST transitions, invalid dates)
* [ ] Default fallback to UTC if company timezone is not set
* [ ] Reverse conversion utility for sending dates to API

column1: FE: Add timezone setting UI in company/admin settings

h2. Objective

Provide an admin UI for setting the company's timezone.

h2. Changes Required

h3. 1. Timezone Setting Component

* Add a timezone selector in the Company Settings / Admin Settings page
* Use a searchable dropdown with all IANA timezones grouped by region (e.g., Americas, Europe, Asia)
* Show current selection with UTC offset (e.g., {{America/New_York (UTC-05:00)}})
* Pre-populate with the company's current timezone from the API

h3. 2. API Integration

* Call {{PATCH /api/company/timezone}} to save the selected timezone
* Show success/error toast on save
* Handle validation errors (400 response for invalid timezone)

h3. 3. Store Company Timezone in App State

* After login or app initialization, store the company's {{timezone}} value from the company API response in global app state (e.g., Redux store, Context, or equivalent)
* This value will be consumed by all date formatting utilities across the app

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Admin users can see and change the company timezone in settings
* [ ] Timezone selector shows all IANA timezones with UTC offsets
* [ ] Selected timezone persists after page reload
* [ ] Non-admin users cannot access/modify the timezone setting
* [ ] Company timezone is available in global app state after login

column1: BE: Normalize legacy datetime.utcnow to datetime.now(timezone.utc)
column41:
h2. Objective

Standardize all datetime default values to use the modern timezone-aware pattern {{datetime.now(timezone.utc)}} instead of the deprecated {{datetime.utcnow}}.

h2. Background

{{datetime.utcnow()}} returns a *naive* datetime (no timezone info), deprecated in Python 3.12+. The codebase has a mix of both patterns.

h2. Files to Update

h3. Models using legacy {{datetime.utcnow}}:

||Model||File||Lines||Fields||
|*Session*|{{app/models/Session.py}}|Line 21|{{date_created}}|
|*IRPhoto*|{{app/models/IRPhoto.py}}|Line 21|{{date_created}}|
|*Quote*|{{app/models/Quote.py}}|Lines 19, 25|{{created_date}}, {{modified_date}}|
|*Task*|{{app/models/Task.py}}|Lines 31, 36, 41|{{submitted_at}}, {{created_at}}, {{due_date}}|
|*Issue*|{{app/models/Issue.py}}|Lines 19, 46|{{created_date}}, {{modified_date}}|
|*Opportunity*|{{app/models/Opportunity.py}}|Lines 15-16|{{created_at}}, {{modified_at}}|

h3. Controllers/routes using {{datetime.utcnow()}}:

||File||Lines||
|{{app/routes/pdf_export_routes.py}}|Lines 74, 213, 782, 1233, 1471|
|{{app/controllers/utility_controller.py}}|Line 21|
|{{app/controllers/task_controller.py}}|Line 915|

h2. Change Pattern

*Models - Before:* {{default=datetime.utcnow}}

*Models - After:* {{default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc)}}

*Controllers - Before:* {{datetime.utcnow()}}

*Controllers - After:* {{datetime.now(timezone.utc)}}

h2. Notes

* Non-breaking change: serialization layer already handles both naive/aware datetimes
* No migration needed - only Python default values change

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] All models use {{datetime.now(timezone.utc)}} for defaults
* [ ] All controller/route code uses {{datetime.now(timezone.utc)}}
* [ ] No remaining references to {{datetime.utcnow}} in the codebase
* [ ] Existing tests pass without changes

column1: BE: Apply company timezone to email notifications
column41:
h2. Objective

Email notifications that include dates/times should display them in the company's configured timezone.

h2. Changes Required

h3. 1. Scheduling Notification Service

*File:* {{app/services/scheduling_notification_service.py}}

*Current (line 40-47):*

{code:python}def _format_datetime_for_email(dt):
    if dt is None:
        return None
    try:
        return dt.strftime('%b %d, %Y %I:%M %p')
    except Exception:
        return str(dt){code}

This formats the UTC datetime directly without any timezone conversion.

*Fix:*

* Update {{_format_datetime_for_email()}} to accept a timezone parameter
* Convert UTC datetime to company timezone before formatting
* Include timezone abbreviation in the output (e.g., {{Mar 05, 2026 02:30 PM EST}})
* The company is already fetched at line 85: {{company = Company.query.get(user.company_id)}}

h3. 2. Audit Other Email Senders

Check any other email-sending functions that include date/time values and apply the same timezone conversion.

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Schedule details in emails show times in company timezone
* [ ] Timezone abbreviation is included (e.g., {{EST}}, {{PST}})
* [ ] All email notification paths use company timezone for date display

column1: Add company-level timezone configuration for localized date/time display
column41:
h2. Background

Currently, the system stores all dates in UTC and returns them to the frontend as UTC ISO strings (e.g., {{2025-07-08T14:30:00Z}}). There is *no company-level timezone setting*, so the frontend has no way to know which timezone to use when displaying dates/times to users. All times are displayed in UTC or the user's browser timezone, which may not match the company's operating timezone.

h2. Objective

Allow a *timezone to be configured at the company level*, so the frontend can display all date/time values in the company's preferred timezone.

----

h2. Scope of Work

h3. 1. Backend - Company Model Change

*File:* {{app/models/Company.py}}

* Add a new {{timezone}} column (String, nullable, default {{'UTC'}}) to the {{companies}} table
* Valid values: IANA timezone identifiers (e.g., {{America/New_York}}, {{US/Eastern}}, {{Asia/Kolkata}})
* Include {{timezone}} in {{Company.to_dict()}} API response
* Create an Alembic migration for the new column

h3. 2. Backend - API Endpoint for Setting Timezone

*File:* {{app/routes/account_routes.py}} or new company settings route

* Add a PATCH/PUT endpoint to update company timezone
* Validate the timezone string against {{pytz}} / {{zoneinfo}} standard library
* Restrict to admin roles only

h3. 3. Frontend - Consume Company Timezone

The frontend must use the {{timezone}} value from the company API response to convert UTC dates for display. Every place where a date/time is shown to the user must apply this conversion.

----

h2. Affected Date/Time Touchpoints (Backend Analysis)

The following areas return date/time values to the frontend and will be impacted:

h3. Category 1: Model Serialization (to_dict methods) - 20+ models

All models serialize datetime fields to UTC ISO strings. The frontend will use the company timezone to convert these for display.

||Model||DateTime Fields Returned||File||
|*Session*|{{date_created}}, {{date_closed}}, {{start_date}}, {{due_date}}, {{created_at}}, {{modified_at}}|{{app/models/Session.py}}|
|*SessionWorkBlock*|{{start_time}}, {{end_time}}, {{created_at}}, {{modified_at}}|{{app/models/SessionWorkBlock.py}}|
|*PlannedWorkorder*|{{start_date}}, {{end_date}}, {{created_at}}, {{modified_at}}|{{app/models/PlannedWorkorder.py}}|
|*PlannedWorkorderLine*|{{window_start}}, {{window_end}}, {{created_at}}, {{modified_at}}|{{app/models/PlannedWorkorderLine.py}}|
|*Quote*|{{created_date}}, {{modified_date}}, {{emp_start}}|{{app/models/Quote.py}}|
|*Job*|{{created_at}}, {{modified_at}}|{{app/models/Job.py}}|
|*Task*|{{submitted_at}}, {{created_at}}, {{due_date}}|{{app/models/Task.py}}|
|*Issue*|{{created_date}}, {{modified_date}}|{{app/models/Issue.py}}|
|*User*|{{created_at}}, {{modified_at}}|{{app/models/User.py}}|
|*SLD*|{{created_at}}, {{modified_at}}|{{app/models/SLD.py}}|
|*Photo / IRPhoto*|{{created_at}}, {{modified_at}} / {{date_created}}|{{app/models/Photo.py}}, {{app/models/IRPhoto.py}}|
|*Attachment*|{{created_at}}, {{modified_at}}|{{app/models/Attachment.py}}|
|*FormInstance*|{{created_at}}, {{modified_at}}|{{app/models/FormInstance.py}}|
|*MutationInbox*|{{client_timestamp}}, {{received_at}}, {{next_attempt_at}}|{{app/models/MutationInbox.py}}|
|*ReportingSnapshot*|{{snapshot_date}}, {{created_at}}|{{app/models/ReportingSnapshot.py}}|
|*Opportunity*|{{created_at}}, {{modified_at}}|{{app/models/Opportunity.py}}|

h3. Category 2: Scheduling Module (High Priority)

These are the most user-visible dates and most impacted by timezone:

* *Session start/due dates* - {{app/models/Session.py}} (lines 26-27)
* *Work block start/end times* - {{app/models/SessionWorkBlock.py}} (lines 11-12)
* *Work block date validation* - {{app/routes/session_work_block_routes.py}} (lines 71-86) - compares work block dates against session date range
* *Planned workorder dates* - {{app/models/PlannedWorkorder.py}} (lines 24-30)
* *Planned workorder line windows* - {{app/models/PlannedWorkorderLine.py}} - {{window_start}}, {{window_end}}

h3. Category 3: Quotes & EMP

* *Quote EMP start date and window calculations* - {{app/controllers/quote_controller.py}} (lines 43-97) - uses {{relativedelta}} for month-based window calculations
* *Quote created/modified dates* - {{app/models/Quote.py}}

h3. Category 4: PDF Exports & Document Rendering

Dates rendered in server-side PDFs currently use UTC. These need to use company timezone:

* *PDF footer "Printed: {date}"* - {{app/services/pdf_export_simple.py}} (line 1753) - uses {{datetime.now().strftime("%B %d, %Y")}} (local server time!)
* *S3 export date prefix* - {{app/routes/pdf_export_routes.py}} (line 74) - {{datetime.utcnow().strftime('%Y/%m/%d')}}
* *Render dataset service* - {{app/services/render_dataset_service.py}} (line 60) - {{current_date_formatted}} and {{current_date_short}} use UTC

h3. Category 5: Email Notifications

* *Scheduling assignment emails* - {{app/services/scheduling_notification_service.py}} (line 40-47) - {{_format_datetime_for_email()}} formats dates without timezone conversion for email display
* Schedule details sent to users show UTC times

h3. Category 6: Issues & Tasks

* *Issue dates* - {{app/models/Issue.py}} - {{created_date}}, {{modified_date}} (uses naive datetime.utcnow)
* *Task dates* - {{app/models/Task.py}} - {{submitted_at}}, {{created_at}}, {{due_date}} (uses naive datetime.utcnow)

----

h2. Technical Debt / Inconsistencies Found

These should be addressed as part of this work:

# *Mixed datetime patterns* - Some models use {{datetime.utcnow}} (naive, deprecated pattern) while others use {{datetime.now(timezone.utc)}} (timezone-aware, correct pattern):

   - *Legacy (naive):* Task, Quote, Issue, IRPhoto, Session.date_created, Opportunity

   - *Modern (aware):* User, Job, Attachment, FormInstance, PlannedWorkorder, SLD, Company

# *Mixed serialization* - Some models use {{.isoformat()}} (Photo, Opportunity) while most use {{.strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ')}}

# *Server local time used in PDF export* - {{pdf_export_simple.py}} line 1753 uses {{datetime.now()}} (server local time) instead of UTC

----

h2. Recommended Approach

# *Backend stays UTC* - Continue storing all dates in UTC in the database. Do NOT convert to local time in API responses.
# *Add `timezone` field to Company* - Return it in the company API response so the frontend always knows the company's timezone.
# *Frontend converts for display* - The frontend should use a library (e.g., {{dayjs}} with timezone plugin, {{date-fns-tz}}, {{luxon}}) to convert UTC dates to the company timezone for display.
# *Server-side rendering (PDFs, Emails)* - For PDFs and emails, the backend should convert UTC to company timezone before rendering using {{pytz}} or {{zoneinfo}}.
# *Normalize legacy datetime patterns* - Optionally standardize all models to use {{datetime.now(timezone.utc)}} instead of {{datetime.utcnow}}.

----

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Company model has a {{timezone}} column with default {{'UTC'}}
* [ ] Company {{to_dict()}} includes {{timezone}} in response
* [ ] Admin API endpoint exists to update company timezone with validation
* [ ] Frontend displays all dates/times in the company's configured timezone
* [ ] PDF exports use company timezone for "Printed" date and all date fields
* [ ] Email notifications display dates in company timezone
* [ ] Scheduling module (sessions, work blocks) displays times in company timezone
* [ ] Quote/EMP dates display in company timezone

h2. Test Plan: S3 Sync Job Snapshot (Pre/Post Dedup Payload) — iOS

h3. Scope

Changes touch 3 files — {{APIEndpoints.swift}}, {{APIClient.swift}}, {{SyncExecutionService.swift}}. All changes are within the sync flow, specifically *before* queue processing begins. The existing sync processing (Step 2+) is untouched.

----

h3. Area 1: Happy Path — Normal Sync With Queue Items

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|1.1|*Sync with mixed entity types*|Create a few nodes, edges, photos, issues offline. Go online and trigger sync.|Sync completes successfully. All items sync to backend as before. Logs show "Sync job stored successfully" with correct pre/post counts.|
|1.2|*Sync with deduplication*|Create a node, update the same node 3 times offline. Trigger sync.|{{queue_count_pre}} > {{queue_count_post}} in logs (dedup removed duplicates). Sync completes normally.|
|1.3|*Sync with no deduplication needed*|Create 3 different nodes (no overlapping operations). Trigger sync.|{{queue_count_pre}} == {{queue_count_post}}. Sync completes normally.|
|1.4|*Sync with mapping operations*|Create a task-node mapping, issue-task mapping offline. Trigger sync.|Mappings sync successfully. Snapshot payload contains {{mapping_data}} field for those items.|
|1.5|*Sync with single item*|Make one small change offline (e.g., update a node label). Trigger sync.|Snapshot created with 1 item, sync completes.|

----

h3. Area 2: Empty Queue

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|2.1|*Sync with empty queue*|Ensure no pending changes exist. Trigger sync (if possible).|Sync job posted with empty {{pre_dedup_payload}} and {{post_dedup_payload}}, both counts = 0. No errors. Sync completes with (0, 0).|

----

h3. Area 3: Sync Job API Failure (Gating Behavior)

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|3.1|*Backend* {{/sync-jobs}} returns 500|Simulate backend error (if test env allows). Trigger sync with queued items.|Log: "Sync job creation failed with status 500 — aborting sync". Sync returns (0, 0). *Queue items remain intact* — not lost.|
|3.2|*Backend* {{/sync-jobs}} unreachable (network cut mid-sync)|Start sync, kill network after connectivity check passes but before sync job POST completes.|Log: "Failed to store sync job — aborting sync". Items stay in queue. Next sync attempt retries everything.|
|3.3|*Retry after sync job failure*|After 3.1 or 3.2, restore network/backend. Trigger sync again.|Sync job succeeds this time. All queued items process normally. No items were lost from previous abort.|

----

h3. Area 4: Existing Sync Behavior Is Unchanged (Regression)

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|4.1|*Node create + update sync*|Create a node offline, update its label. Go online, sync.|Node created on backend with correct label. Same behavior as before this change.|
|4.2|*Photo upload during sync*|Take a photo offline. Trigger sync.|Photo uploads successfully via presigned URL flow. No regression.|
|4.3|*Edge create with terminals*|Create a connection between two nodes offline. Sync.|Edge created on backend with correct source/target and terminal IDs.|
|4.4|*Delete operation sync*|Delete a node offline (soft delete). Sync.|Update sent with {{is_deleted: true}}. Entity removed from local storage after sync.|
|4.5|*Sync retry on transient failure*|Cause a transient failure (e.g., timeout) during sync of one item.|Item stays in queue with incremented retry count. Next sync retries it. This logic is untouched but should be verified.|
|4.6|*Session expiry during sync*|Let auth token expire mid-sync.|Sync stops gracefully, items remain in queue without retry count increment. Behavior unchanged.|
|4.7|*Building/Floor/Room sync*|Create location hierarchy offline. Sync.|All three levels sync to backend correctly.|
|4.8|*Quote and Issue sync*|Create a quote and issue offline. Sync.|Both sync correctly with all fields.|
|4.9|*Form instance sync*|Fill out a form instance offline. Sync.|Form instance syncs. Snapshot payload shows "payload omitted due to size constraints" for this item (expected).|
|4.10|*Bulk task-node completion sync*|Complete multiple task-node mappings at once. Sync.|Bulk completion syncs in one API call as before.|

----

h3. Area 5: Network Edge Cases

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|5.1|*No network at sync start*|Turn off network. Trigger sync.|Aborted at Step 0 (network check) — never reaches sync job creation. Same as before.|
|5.2|*Cellular/expensive connection*|Switch to cellular data. Trigger sync.|Warning logged about expensive connection. Sync proceeds (including sync job creation).|
|5.3|*Large queue (50+ items)*|Accumulate many offline changes across different entity types. Sync.|Snapshot payload serializes all items. No timeout or memory issue. Sync completes.|

----

h3. Area 6: Entity Payload Accuracy (Snapshot Content Validation)

_If you have access to backend logs or can inspect the_ {{/sync-jobs}} payload:

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|6.1|*Node payload completeness*|Queue a node create. Check snapshot payload on backend.|Payload includes: id, type, label, sld_id, x, y, width, height, node_class, node_subtype, core_attributes, node_terminals, com, qr_code, room_id, suggested_shortcut.|
|6.2|*Edge payload completeness*|Queue an edge create. Check snapshot.|Payload includes: id, source, target, sld_id, source_handle, target_handle, terminal IDs, edge_class, core_attributes, points, algorithm.|
|6.3|*Deleted entity in queue*|Delete an entity, then immediately trigger sync before the delete processes.|{{buildEntityPayload}} returns error dict for that item — doesn't crash, doesn't block snapshot.|
|6.4|*Mapping items*|Queue mapping operations. Check snapshot.|Payload shows {{["message": "see mapping_data field"]}} and {{mapping_data}} contains the actual mapping JSON.|

----

h3. Priority Ranking

||Priority||Tests||Rationale||
|*P0 — Must test*|1.1, 3.1, 3.3, 4.1, 4.2|Core happy path + gating behavior + no regression on most common sync operations|
|*P1 — Should test*|1.2, 2.1, 3.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5, 5.1|Dedup validation, empty queue, network failures, delete/retry regression|
|*P2 — Nice to test*|1.3, 1.4, 1.5, 4.6–4.10, 5.2, 5.3, 6.1–6.4|Completeness, edge cases, payload validation|

----

h3. What Does NOT Need Testing

* *UI changes* — none made
* *Offline data creation* — unaffected
* *Authentication flow* — unaffected
* *Photo download/caching* — unaffected
* *SLD rendering* — unaffected

----

h3. Implementation Summary

* *Files changed*: {{APIEndpoints.swift}} (+5 lines), {{APIClient.swift}} (+12 lines), {{SyncExecutionService.swift}} (~300 lines added)
* *Pattern*: Matches Android implementation (commit c188a89) and iOS hotfix/ZP-1234 (commit 93fff1a)
* *Gating*: Hard gate — if {{/sync-jobs}} POST fails, sync aborts. Queue items remain intact for retry.
* *Safe fallbacks*: Node, Edge, UserTask have per-entity fallback builders if DTO encoding fails (matches Android pattern)

column1: Optimize Redundant Data Storage in Sync Flow
column41:
h2. User Story

*As a* developer maintaining the sync infrastructure,

*I want to* reduce redundant data storage in the sync flow,

*So that* we minimize database growth, reduce storage costs, and simplify data management.

----

h2. Background

Every mutation/sync request is currently being stored redundantly:

* *S3* — individual mutation entries stored on each Mutation Inbox request
* *Mutation Inbox table* — stores mutation entries in the database
* *Sync Job table* — serializes the entire queue as {{preDedupPayload}} and {{postDedupPayload}} JSON columns before syncing begins

This results in *two database tables* (Mutation Inbox and Sync Job) holding duplicate data for different purposes, causing the database size to steadily increase over time.

----

h2. Approved Changes

h3. 1. Move Queue Serialization to S3 (Sync Job Table)

Instead of saving the entire queue as JSON blobs ({{preDedupPayload}} / {{postDedupPayload}}) directly in the Sync Job database table, serialize and upload the payloads to *S3* and store only the *S3 file path/key* in the database.

h3. 2. Keep S3 Storage on Individual Mutation Inbox Requests (For Now)

The S3 storage save on individual Mutation Inbox requests will *not* be removed at this time. It can be revisited and removed in a future iteration once we confirm individual entry tracking is no longer required.

----

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] {{preDedupPayload}} and {{postDedupPayload}} are stored in S3 instead of directly in the Sync Job table
* [ ] Sync Job table stores S3 file paths/keys referencing the payload files
* [ ] Existing sync flow functionality remains unaffected
* [ ] Database growth from large JSON payloads in the Sync Job table is eliminated
* [ ] S3 storage on individual Mutation Inbox requests remains unchanged (to be addressed later)

column1: [Android] Save entire sync queue json in database on flush
column41:
h3. Summary

The issue involves the app deleting all local database data when a user switches sites, resulting in the loss of unsynced offline edits. The expected behavior is to retain local data per site and ensure unsynced data is not deleted during site switches.

h3. Context

Currently, when switching sites, the app clears all local data, which leads to unsynced edits becoming unprocessable. The goal is to improve data retention and syncing behavior across multiple sites.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* Site A's data remains intact when switching to Site B and back.
* Sync queue items from Site A are preserved after switching to Site B and synced when returning to Site A.
* Data from multiple sites coexists in the local database without leakage.
* Each site switch fetches data from the server using an upsert strategy.
* Sync queue processes only items for the active site.
* No cross-site data leakage occurs on any screen.
* The "Clear Database" option in settings clears all sites' data and the sync queue.
* Logging out clears all data.

h3. Other information

*Current Behavior:*  
When a user switches sites, the app deletes ALL local database data ({{clearAllSLDData()}} in {{SLDService.kt}}) and fetches fresh data for the new site. Any unsynced offline edits (sync queue items) lose their referenced entities, making them unprocessable and effectively lost.

*Expected Behavior:*

* Local data retained per-site, mapped by {{sldId}} — not deleted on site switch.
* Always re-fetch target site data from server on switch (upsert, not delete + insert).
* Unsynced data never deleted on site switch.
* Sync queue processes only active site's items.
* Unsynced data removed only after: successful sync, manual Clear Database, or app uninstall.

*Technical Scope:*

||Phase||Details||
|Schema (DB v24→25)|Add {{sldId}} to {{sync_queue_item}} + {{quotes}} tables|
|SyncQueueItem|Add {{sldId}} field, update all factory methods + ~15 call sites|
|SLDService rewrite|Remove {{clearAllSLDData()}} from switch flow; keep for Settings/logout only|
|Sync processing|Filter {{FlushSyncQueueUseCase}} by active sldId|
|DAO queries|Add site-scoped queries to QuoteDao, AttachmentDao, SLDViewDao, SyncQueueItemDao|
|ViewModel audit|Ensure all queries filter by active sldId|

*Key Files:* {{SLDService.kt}}, {{SyncQueueItem.kt}}, {{Quote.kt}}, {{SyncQueueItemDao.kt}}, {{QuoteDao.kt}}, {{DatabaseMigrations.kt}}, {{AppDatabase.kt}}, {{FlushSyncQueueUseCase.kt}}, {{SyncQueueForegroundService.kt}}, {{SelectSiteViewModel.kt}}, + ~15 use cases

*Testing:* Migration test, round-trip switch, sync queue preservation, active-site-only sync, no data leakage, upsert idempotency, clear database, logout.

column1: [Android] Update node doesn't contain node terminal information (important if class is switching)
column41:
When a node's class changes, we need to remove its old Node Terminals and create new ones based on the new class's Node Orientation. The iOS app handles this correctly on the local side (old terminals removed, new terminals created, edges re-routed to new terminals).                                                                       

  What we are NOT doing:                                                                                            

* Syncing the new Node Terminals to the server. The updateNode API payload does not include node_terminals (unlike createNode, which does). So the server never learns about the new terminals created during a class change.
* Removing the old Node Terminals on the server. The old terminals are soft-deleted locally but the server is never told to delete them.

column1: [Android] Preserve scroll position when navigating back from detail views
column41:
h2. Description

Implement scroll position preservation in the Android app so that when a user navigates back from a detail view (or after saving), the list returns to the same scroll position they were at before.

h2. Scope

* Work Orders tab (line items, assets, connections sub-views)
* Assets tab (asset lists within site visits)
* Connections tab (connection lists)
* Any other scrollable list views where detail navigation occurs

h2. Requirements

* Store the current scroll offset before navigating to a detail view
* Restore the scroll offset when the user navigates back (via "Back" button or after "Save")
* Reset scroll position when navigating to a different parent entity (e.g., switching to a different work order)

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Tapping an asset in a list, viewing the detail, and tapping "Back" returns to the same scroll position
* [ ] Editing an asset and tapping "Save" returns to the same scroll position
* [ ] Scroll position is preserved for Work Order line item lists
* [ ] Scroll position is preserved for Connection lists
* [ ] Scroll position is preserved for Asset lists
* [ ] Scroll position resets when navigating to a different parent entity

column1: [Frontend] Connections list pagination & API-based search
column41:
h2. Description

Update the web frontend connections list to use the new paginated v2 API endpoint. Replace client-side filtering with API-based server-side search.

h2. Requirements

h3. Pagination

* Integrate with v2 paginated connections list API
* Add pagination controls (page navigation, page size selector)
* Display total count and current page info

h3. API-Based Search

* Replace client-side search/filter with API-based search
* Debounce search input to avoid excessive API calls
* Show loading state during search

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Connections list uses the new v2 paginated endpoint
* [ ] Pagination controls displayed and functional
* [ ] Search input triggers API-based search (not client-side filtering)
* [ ] Loading states shown during pagination and search
* [ ] Smooth UX with no flickering or jarring transitions
* [ ] Works correctly with large connection datasets

column1: [API] Connections list v2 endpoint - Pagination & Search
column41:
h2. Description

Create a new versioned (v2) API endpoint for the connections list that supports server-side pagination and search. The existing v1 endpoint must remain unchanged to ensure backward compatibility with older app versions.

h2. Requirements

h3. Pagination

* Support {{page}} and {{page_size}} query parameters
* Return total count, current page, total pages in response metadata
* Default page size TBD (e.g., 50 or 100)

h3. Search

* Support {{search}} query parameter for server-side filtering
* Search should cover key connection fields (name, type, from asset, to asset, etc.)
* Search should be case-insensitive

h3. Backward Compatibility

* Existing v1 endpoint remains unchanged (returns all connections)
* New v2 endpoint is created with pagination and search support

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] New v2 connections list endpoint created with pagination support
* [ ] Search/filter query parameter implemented
* [ ] Response includes pagination metadata (total, page, page_size, total_pages)
* [ ] v1 endpoint continues to work unchanged
* [ ] Endpoint performs well with large connection datasets
* [ ] API documentation updated

column1: Connections List Pagination & Search - Support for large datasets
column41:
h2. User Story

*As a* user with a large number of connections,

*I want* the connections list to be paginated with server-side search,

*So that* the application performs well and I can quickly find specific connections without loading the entire dataset.

h2. Context

Similar to assets, the current connections list loads all connections at once, which causes performance issues for customers with large datasets (e.g., Koch). This story introduces:

# *Server-side pagination* for the connections list
# *API-based search* for connections (web only; mobile has offline support)
# *New API version (v2)* to ensure backward compatibility with older app versions

h2. Scope

h3. In Scope

* New versioned API endpoint (v2) for connections list with pagination and search
* Frontend (web) integration with paginated API and server-side search
* Mobile (iOS & Android) pagination support
* Backward compatibility - existing v1 endpoint remains unchanged

h3. Out of Scope

* Mobile API-based search (mobile uses offline-first with local search)

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] *AC1:* New v2 connections list endpoint supports pagination (page, page_size parameters)
* [ ] *AC2:* New v2 connections list endpoint supports server-side search (search/filter query parameter)
* [ ] *AC3:* Old v1 endpoint continues to work unchanged for older app versions
* [ ] *AC4:* Web frontend uses paginated API with pagination controls

column1: Assets List Pagination & Search - Support for large datasets (6000+ assets)
column41:
h2. User Story

*As a* user with a large number of assets (e.g., Koch with 6000+ assets),

*I want* the assets list to be paginated with server-side search,

*So that* the application performs well and I can quickly find specific assets without loading the entire dataset.

h2. Context

Current assets list loads all assets at once, which causes performance issues for customers with large datasets (e.g., Koch with 6000+ assets). This story introduces:

# *Server-side pagination* for the assets list
# *API-based search* for assets (web only; mobile has offline support)
# *New API version (v2)* to ensure backward compatibility with older app versions

h2. Scope

h3. In Scope

* New versioned API endpoint (v2) for assets list with pagination and search
* Frontend (web) integration with paginated API and server-side search
* Mobile (iOS & Android) pagination support
* Backward compatibility - existing v1 endpoint remains unchanged

h3. Out of Scope

* Mobile API-based search (mobile uses offline-first with local search)

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] *AC1:* New v2 assets list endpoint supports pagination (page, page_size parameters)
* [ ] *AC2:* New v2 assets list endpoint supports server-side search (search/filter query parameter)
* [ ] *AC3:* Old v1 endpoint continues to work unchanged for older app versions
* [ ] *AC4:* Web frontend uses paginated API with pagination controls
* [ ] *AC5:* Web frontend uses API-based search instead of client-side filtering
* [ ] *AC6:* Mobile iOS supports paginated loading of assets
* [ ] *AC7:* Mobile Android supports paginated loading of assets
* [ ] *AC8:* Performance is acceptable with 6000+ assets (list loads within reasonable time)

column1: Mobile Preserve scroll position when navigating back from detail views in Work Orders, Assets, and Connections
column41:
As a field technician using the mobile app, I need the application to preserve my scroll position when navigating back from a detail view (e.g., after viewing or saving an asset), so that I can continue working from where I left off without manually scrolling to find my place.

Currently, when a user taps on an item in a list (assets, connections, work order line items, etc.), views or edits the detail, and then saves or navigates back, the list resets to the top position. This forces users to scroll back through potentially long lists to find their previous location, causing friction and wasted time during field data entry.

_Scope:_

* Work Orders tab (line items, assets, connections sub-views)
* Assets tab (asset lists within site visits)
* Connections tab (connection lists)
* Any other scrollable list views within the mobile app where detail navigation occurs

_Acceptance Criteria:_

* When a user taps an asset in a list, views the detail, and taps "Back", the list returns to the same scroll position
* When a user edits an asset and taps "Save", the list returns to the same scroll position
* Scroll position is preserved for Work Order line item lists
* Scroll position is preserved for Connection lists
* Scroll position is preserved for Asset lists
* Behavior is consistent on both iOS and Android platforms
* Scroll position resets appropriately when navigating to a different parent entity (e.g., different work order)

column1: Update node doesn't contain node terminal information (important if class is switching)
column41:
When a node's class changes, we need to remove its old Node Terminals and create new ones based on the new class's Node Orientation. The iOS app handles this correctly on the local side (old terminals removed, new terminals created, edges re-routed to new terminals).                                                                       

  What we are NOT doing:                                                                                            

* Syncing the new Node Terminals to the server. The updateNode API payload does not include node_terminals (unlike createNode, which does). So the server never learns about the new terminals created during a class change.
* Removing the old Node Terminals on the server. The old terminals are soft-deleted locally but the server is never told to delete them.

column1: Issues Report Generation - Generate PDF reports for Issues similar to Work Order reports
column41:
h2. User Story

*As a* user viewing the Issues list page,

*I want to* generate PDF reports for issues,

*So that* I can download and share issue documentation in a standardized report format.

----

h2. Context

Currently, report generation exists for Work Orders (via {{SessionDetail.jsx}} using {{/reporting/generate_simple}} API and {{ReportGenerationModal}}). This story extends the same report generation capability to the *Issues list page*, but scoped specifically to *issues reports only* (not all entities like Work Orders target).

----

h2. Scope

* *In Scope:* Issues report generation from the Issues list page
* *Out of Scope:* Work Order reports (already implemented), individual Issue detail page report generation

----

h2. UI/UX Requirements

h3. 1. Generate Button Placement

* Add a *"Generate Report"* button on the *Issues list page* ({{src/pages/Issues.jsx}})
* Position the button *to the left of the search bar* using the {{rightActions}} prop of {{MainDataGrid}} (same pattern used in {{Assets.jsx}} for the Reset Filter button)
* Button style: {{variant="contained"}}, {{color="success"}}, with {{DescriptionIcon}} as {{startIcon}}
* Button label: *"Generate Report"*

h3. 2. Report Generation Modal

* Reuse the existing {{ReportGenerationModal}} component ({{src/components/ReportGenerationModal.jsx}})
* Fetch report configurations from *`GET /reporting/configs?type=issue`* (new config type for issues)
* Modal should allow:

* Selection of report template/configuration

* Output format selection (PDF / DOCX)

* Dynamic variable input based on selected config

h3. 3. Report Generation Flow

* Follow the same async pattern as Work Order reports:

# *POST `/reporting/generate_simple`* with {{type: 'issue'}} and relevant issue filter/selection parameters

# *Poll `GET /reporting/status/{encodedArn}`* for completion (5s interval, 10min timeout)

# *Download* the generated report via S3 presigned URL

* Show loading/generating state on the button or via a snackbar/indicator while report is being generated
* Handle errors gracefully with user-friendly messages

h3. 4. PDF Report Format

* Similar like formate we used for the work order - Issues.

----

h2. Technical Implementation Details

h3. Frontend Changes

[*File: `src/pages/Issues.jsx*|File: `src/pages/Issues.js]*`*

* Import {{ReportGenerationModal}} and {{DescriptionIcon}}
* Add state variables: {{reportModalOpen}}, {{reportingConfigs}}, {{generatingReport}}, {{reportError}}
* Add {{fetchReportingConfigs()}} function calling {{GET /reporting/configs?type=issue}}
* Add {{handleGenerateReport()}} to open the modal
* Add {{handleGenerateReportWithOptions()}} to POST to {{/reporting/generate_simple}}
* Add {{pollReportStatus()}} and {{downloadReport()}} functions (same pattern as {{SessionDetail.jsx}})
* Pass Generate Report button via {{rightActions}} prop to {{MainDataGrid}}:

{noformat}rightActions={[
  <Button
    key="generate-report"
    size="small"
    variant="contained"
    color="success"
    startIcon={<DescriptionIcon />}
    onClick={handleGenerateReport}
    disabled={generatingReport}
  >
    {generatingReport ? 'Generating...' : 'Generate Report'}
  </Button>
]}{noformat}

*Reused Components:*

* {{ReportGenerationModal}} (no changes needed if backend provides configs for type=issue)
* {{POLLING}} constants from {{src/constants/timeouts.js}}

h3. Backend Requirements (for backend team)

* Create reporting config entries with {{type: 'issue'}}
* Support {{type: 'issue'}} in {{POST /reporting/generate_simple}}
* Design the issue report template matching Work Order report styling
* Include issue-specific data: title, class, priority, asset, session, site, status, dates, description, resolution, photos

----

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] *AC1:* A "Generate Report" button is visible on the Issues list page, positioned to the left of the search bar (using {{rightActions}} pattern)
* [ ] *AC2:* Clicking the button opens the {{ReportGenerationModal}} with issue-specific report configurations
* [ ] *AC3:* User can select a report template and output format (PDF/DOCX)
* [ ] *AC4:* After clicking "Generate Report" in the modal, the system initiates report generation via {{/reporting/generate_simple}} with {{type: 'issue'}}
* [ ] *AC5:* The button shows a loading/disabled state ("Generating...") while the report is being generated
* [ ] *AC6:* The generated PDF downloads automatically upon completion
* [ ] *AC7:* The PDF report format matches the Work Order report style (same layout, fonts, branding)
* [ ] *AC8:* Report content includes all issue-specific fields: title, class, priority, asset, session, site, status, dates, description, resolution, and photos
* [ ] *AC9:* Error states are handled gracefully (generation failure, timeout after 10 minutes, network errors)
* [ ] *AC10:* The feature respects existing permission controls (same as issue management permissions)

----

h2. Dependencies

* Backend: New reporting config type {{issue}} and report template creation
* Existing: {{ReportGenerationModal}}, {{apiClient}}, {{POLLING}} constants

h2. References

* Work Order report generation: {{src/pages/SessionDetail.jsx}} (lines 2034-2149)
* Report modal: {{src/components/ReportGenerationModal.jsx}}
* Issues page: {{src/pages/Issues.jsx}}
* MainDataGrid rightActions pattern: {{src/components/MainDataGrid.jsx}} (lines 387-448)
* Quote print pattern: {{src/components/QuotePrintModal.jsx}}

column1: Enforce Database SSL Connection (sslmode=require) across all environments
column41:
h2. Task

Enforce SSL on PostgreSQL database connections to ensure data is encrypted in transit between the application and RDS.

h2. Steps

# Append {{?sslmode=require}} to the {{DATABASE_URL}} environment variable in each environment:

   - dev

   - qa

   - stage

   - prod

   - bces-prod

   Example: {{postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/db}} → {{postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/db?sslmode=require}}

# Verify AWS RDS instance has *"Encryption at rest"* enabled:

   - RDS Console → Instance → Configuration → Encryption

h2. Notes

* *Environment variable change only* — no application code changes
* AWS RDS supports SSL by default; this change ensures the client *enforces* it
* SOC2 Control: _"Data must be encrypted at rest and in transit"_

h2. Verification

* After updating, deploy to dev and confirm the application connects successfully
* Check RDS connection logs to verify SSL is being used
