When an issue is created in offline mode and then deleted from the sync section before performing the sync operation, after coming online and syncing the data, a *Dead Letter error* is generated. The error is visible in the Web Audit Log.

----

h3. *Expected Result:*

If an offline-created issue is deleted before sync, the system should properly handle the deletion and should *not generate any Dead Letter error* after sync. The sync process should complete successfully without any audit errors.

# Immediately tap Node B → edit core attributes → Save

# Immediately tap Node C → edit core attributes → Save

# *Expected:* No crash. All three saves succeed. API calls complete in background.



  *Test* *2:* *Rapid* *Node* *Position* *Dragging*

# Open an SLD in "All Nodes" mode, *online*

# Drag Node A to a new position

# Immediately drag Node B to a new position

# Immediately drag Node C to a new position

# *Expected:* No crash. Positions update in WebView. Debounced API calls fire after 150ms.



  *Test* *3:* *Rapid* *Node* *Deletion* *(Z-PLATFORM-56* *regression)*

# Open an SLD with 5+ nodes, *online*

# Delete Node A → confirm

# Immediately delete Node B → confirm

# *Expected:* No crash. Nodes removed from graph. Deletion synced to server.



  *Test* *4:* *Node* *Reparent* *+* *Delete*

# Open an SLD, *online*

# Drag a node into an enclosure (reparent)

# Immediately delete another node

# *Expected:* No crash. Both operations succeed.



  *Test* *5:* *Edge* *Routing* *Update*

# Open an SLD in "All Nodes" mode, *online*

# Drag an edge's breakpoints to change its routing

# Immediately drag another edge's breakpoints

# *Expected:* No crash. Routing updates saved and synced.



  *Test* *6:* *Offline* *Create* *Node* *→* *Sync*

# Switch device to *offline* (airplane mode)

# Create a new node in the SLD

# Verify it appears in the graph

# Switch back to *online*

# Trigger sync (or wait for auto-sync)

# *Expected:* Node created on server. Verify in web app or API.



  *Test* *7:* *Offline* *Create* *Edge* *→* *Sync*

# Switch device to *offline*

# Create a new edge between two nodes

# Switch back to *online*

# Trigger sync

# *Expected:* Edge created on server. Verify in web app.



  *Test* *8:* *Offline* *Edit* *Node* *→* *Sync*

# Switch device to *offline*

# Edit a node's label and core attributes → Save

# Switch back to *online*

# Trigger sync

# *Expected:* Updated data synced to server correctly.



  *Test* *9:* *Offline* *Create* *→* *Online* *Edit* *Before* *Sync*

# Switch device to *offline*

# Create a new node

# Switch back to *online*

# *Before* *sync* *queue* *processes*, tap the new node → edit attributes → Save

# Let sync queue run

# *Expected:* Node created on server, then updated with edits. No crash.



  *Test* *10:* *View-Specific* *Mode* *Operations*

# Open an SLD, select a specific *View* (not "All Nodes")

# Drag nodes, reparent nodes, create edges

# *Expected:* View mappings update correctly. No crash.



  *Test* *11:* *Navigate* *Away* *During* *Pending* *Syncs*

# Open an SLD, *online*

# Rapidly drag 3-4 nodes (triggers debounced syncs)

# Immediately tap Back to leave the SLD view

# *Expected:* flushPendingNodeSyncs() fires. No crash. All pending updates sent to server.



  *Test* *12:* *Create* *Child* *Node* *(Parent* *Type* *Change)*

# Open an SLD, *online*

# Create a child node inside an enclosure

# *Expected:* Parent node type changes to "group" and syncs. Child node created and syncs. No crash.



  *Test* *13:* *Enclosure* *Creation* *with* *Terminals*

# Open an SLD, *online*

# Create a new enclosure node (with terminals)

# *Expected:* Enclosure and terminals saved. Synced to server.



  *Test* *14:* *Edge* *Delete*

# Open an SLD, *online*

# Delete an edge → confirm

# Immediately delete another edge

# *Expected:* No crash. Edges soft-deleted and synced.

column1: BE | API to serve SLD Viewer version and download URL for mobile apps
column41:
h2. Context

The mobile apps (iOS - [ZP-524|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/browse/ZP-524]) need a backend API to fetch the current SLD Viewer version and its download URL. This enables dynamic SLD viewer updates without requiring a new app release. The mobile app will call this endpoint on login, compare versions locally, and download the viewer ZIP if a newer version is available.

Currently, no backend endpoint exists to serve {{sld_viewer_version}} and {{sld_viewer_download_url}}.

Reference: [Dynamic SLD - Confluence|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENGR/pages/68943873/Dynamic+SLD]
h2. 

h2. Scope

h3. 1. Database Model - {{sld_viewer_versions}} table

Create a new {{SLDViewerVersion}} model with the following fields:

|| Field || Type || Description ||
| {{id}} | UUID (PK) | Primary key, auto-generated |
| {{version}} | String | Semantic version (e.g., "1.0.0", "1.2.3") |
| {{platform}} | String | Target platform: {{ios}}, {{android}}, or {{all}} |
| {{s3_key}} | String | S3 object key for the viewer ZIP file in {{pgz-s3-slds}} bucket |
| {{file_size}} | BigInteger | Size of the ZIP file in bytes |
| {{checksum}} | String | SHA-256 hash of the ZIP for integrity verification on mobile |
| {{is_active}} | Boolean | Whether this version is the currently served version (only one active per platform) |
| {{release_notes}} | Text | Optional notes about what changed in this version |
| {{uploaded_by}} | UUID (FK -> users) | User who uploaded this version |
| {{is_deleted}} | Boolean | Soft delete flag (default: false) |
| {{created_at}} | DateTime (TZ) | Record creation timestamp |
| {{modified_at}} | DateTime (TZ) | Last modification timestamp |

h3. 2. Alembic Migration
* Create migration for the {{sld_viewer_versions}} table
* Add index on {{(platform, is_active)}} for efficient lookup
* Follow existing idempotent migration pattern (check before adding)

h3. 3. API Endpoint

*{{GET /sld-viewer/latest}}*

Returns the currently active SLD viewer version and a presigned S3 download URL.

*Headers:*
* {{Authorization: Bearer <jwt_token>}} (requires {{@require_auth}})

*Query Parameters:*
* {{platform}} (required): {{ios}} or {{android}}

*Success Response (200):*
{code:json}{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "sld_viewer_version": "1.2.0",
    "sld_viewer_download_url": "https://pgz-s3-slds.s3.amazonaws.com/...",
    "file_size": 5242880,
    "checksum": "sha256:abc123...",
    "release_notes": "Bug fixes and performance improvements"
  }
}
{code}

*No active version found (200):*
{code:json}{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "sld_viewer_version": null,
    "sld_viewer_download_url": null
  }
}
{code}

*Error Response (400/500):*
{code:json}{
  "success": false,
  "error": "Platform parameter is required. Accepted values: ios, android"
}
{code}

*Implementation Notes:*
* Use {{@require_auth}} decorator (no specific permission needed - all authenticated users can fetch viewer info)
* Generate a presigned S3 read URL for the download (use existing {{S3Service}} with {{pgz-s3-slds}} bucket)
* Set presigned URL expiry to {{S3_URL_EXPIRY_READ}} (default 1 hour)
* Query: {{SELECT * FROM sld_viewer_versions WHERE platform IN (:platform, 'all') AND is_active = true AND is_deleted = false ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1}}
* If platform-specific and {{all}} versions both exist, prefer platform-specific
* Also register at {{/api/sld-viewer/latest}} for API prefix compatibility (follow existing dual-route pattern)

h3. 4. Route, Controller, and Model Files

Follow existing project patterns:
* *Route:* {{app/routes/sld_viewer_routes.py}} - Register in {{app/routes/__init__.py}}
* *Controller:* {{app/controllers/sld_viewer_controller.py}} - Static method returning {{(data, status_code)}} tuple
* *Model:* {{app/models/SLDViewerVersion.py}} - With {{to_dict()}} serialization method
h2. 

h2. Acceptance Criteria
* [ ] {{sld_viewer_versions}} table created via Alembic migration
* [ ] {{GET /sld-viewer/latest?platform=ios}} returns active viewer version and a valid presigned S3 download URL
* [ ] {{GET /sld-viewer/latest?platform=android}} returns active viewer version for android or {{all}} platform
* [ ] Returns {{null}} values gracefully when no active version exists (not 404)
* [ ] Endpoint is protected by {{@require_auth}} (valid JWT token required)
* [ ] Presigned download URL is valid and allows the mobile app to download the ZIP
* [ ] {{checksum}} and {{file_size}} are included in the response for mobile-side integrity verification
* [ ] Response follows the standard {{{"success": true/false, "data": {...}}}} pattern
* [ ] Platform query parameter is validated ({{ios}}, {{android}} only)
h2. 

h2. Technical Notes
* *Existing pattern reference:* Similar to {{AppVersionRule}} model and {{/version-check}} endpoint in {{app/routes/version_management_routes.py}}
* *S3 Bucket:* Use existing {{pgz-s3-slds}} bucket for storing viewer ZIPs
* *Presigned URLs:* Use existing {{S3Service.generate_presigned_url()}} with read expiry
* *Seeding initial data:* The first viewer version record will need to be inserted manually (or via a seed script) until admin APIs are built in a future ticket
* *Rollback support:* Setting a previous version's {{is_active = true}} (and deactivating the current one) effectively rolls back to that version - mobile apps will pick it up on next login

column1: Z | iOS | Delta T and Severity Fields Not Calculated in Backward Compatibility (V1.20) – Showing “Waiting for Input”
column41:
h3. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open the application in *Backward Compatibility mode (Version 1.20)*.
# Navigate to the *Thermal Issue* module.
# Create or open an existing thermal issue.
# Enter the required temperature values (e.g., Reference Temp and Measured Temp).
# Save the issue.

----

h3. *Actual Result:*

* The *Delta T* field is not getting calculated automatically.
* The *Severity* field is showing *“Waiting for Input”* instead of calculated severity.
* Calculated values are not appearing in the respective fields.

----

h3. *Expected Result:*

* The *Delta T* should be automatically calculated based on the entered temperature values.
* The *Severity* should be auto-calculated according to the Delta T value.
* Both fields should display calculated values instead of “Waiting for Input”.
!Video_20260220_172638_537_1.mp4|width=592,alt="Video_20260220_172638_537_1.mp4"!
column211:
24/Feb/26 6:16 AM;712020:506eab12-6be8-4af7-8629-0824e8bb2fb2;[~accountid:712020:b43bccaf-6e8f-47ef-82f7-5439c6765fdb] The user will be required to update the app (optional update will need to be made mandatory), and we will need to inform the client about this.

In the old iOS build with the new web setup, the *Delta T* feature does not function properly because the expression references *[Problem Temp Unit]* and *[Reference Temp Unit]*, which are virtual fields and not actual stored properties.

The iOS app does not store temperature units and treats temperature fields as regular text fields. As a result, the evaluator is unable to resolve the unit values, returns nil, and Delta T appears empty.

Since *Severity* depends on both Delta T and Problem Temp Unit, it also fails and appears empty.

Therefore, old issues created before the temperature unit feature was introduced will always display empty Delta T and Severity values in the iOS app.

Because of this limitation, users must update to the latest iOS version to ensure proper functionality.

column214:
23/Feb/26 8:36 AM;623b9df4866b810069e4062a;[~accountid:712020:506eab12-6be8-4af7-8629-0824e8bb2fb2] [~accountid:712020:9881bb6e-cbf7-47a6-bf7a-838a383b2866] [~accountid:712020:b43bccaf-6e8f-47ef-82f7-5439c6765fdb] Watch this please; exact same behavior produces different results in this video (v 1.20)

!ScreenRecording_02-23-2026 06-33-55_1.mp4|thumbnail!
column215:
24/Feb/26 6:27 AM;712020:9881bb6e-cbf7-47a6-bf7a-838a383b2866;[~accountid:712020:b43bccaf-6e8f-47ef-82f7-5439c6765fdb] The root cause was a race condition in the iOS SLD bridge — when "distribute children" fires, it sends both nodePositionChanged and enclosureSizeUpdated events for the same panelboard node nearly simultaneously. Each event was triggering an independent API call with the full node state, but updateNodePosition would read the node before the size update was saved to SwiftData,sending stale width/height values. Due to the backend's last-write-wins mutation processing, the stale data from the position update could overwrite the correct size from the distribute operation.

The fix consolidates concurrent updates per node using a 150ms debounce — when multiple events arrive for the same node in quick succession, only one API call is sent with the final state. Additionally,pending API calls are flushed immediately when navigating away from the SLD view to ensure the backend has the latest data before any refresh occurs. This has been committed to the bugfix/ZP-1042-distribute-children-not-working-in-mobile-single-line branch.

column1: FE Define default start width/height dimensions for switchgear and panelboard elements on SLD
column41:
When users add switchgear or panelboard assets to a Single Line Diagram, the initial element dimensions should be consistent and appropriately sized based on the asset type. Currently, new elements may render at arbitrary or inconsistent sizes, requiring manual adjustment every time.

This enhancement establishes predefined default dimensions for each asset category, ensuring visual consistency across diagrams and reducing the manual effort required to resize elements after placement.

*Business Value:*

* Reduces time spent manually resizing newly placed elements
* Ensures visual consistency across all SLDs created by different users
* Establishes a foundation for asset-type-specific rendering rules

*Scope:*

* Define default width and height values for switchgear elements
* Define default width and height values for panelboard elements
* Apply defaults when elements are first placed on the canvas
* Existing elements on saved diagrams remain unchanged

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* When a switchgear element is added to an SLD, it renders at the defined default width and height
* When a panelboard element is added to an SLD, it renders at the defined default width and height
* Default dimensions are visually appropriate and proportional to typical diagram layouts
* Users can still manually resize elements after placement
* Existing saved diagrams load without dimension changes to their elements
* Default dimension values are configurable for future adjustments

column1: FE Quote Issue Report Generation for Pull-Through Work
column41:
Enable sales and dispatch teams to generate quote issue reports that consolidate findings from proactive site visits and convert them into actionable pull-through work opportunities.

*Overview:*
When technicians perform energized scans, arc flash audits, or routine maintenance, they identify issues that represent follow-up work opportunities. This feature provides a streamlined workflow to capture those issues in a quote and generate a professional report for customer presentation.

*Capabilities:*

* Generate a Quote Issue Report from a work order or standalone
* Bulk import issues discovered during work orders into a quote
* Display consolidated issue list within the quote report
* Support proactive site visit types: energized scan, arc flash audit, routine maintenance

*Business Value:*

* Captures pull-through revenue from issues identified during proactive visits
* Provides customers with clear documentation of findings and recommended remediation
* Reduces manual effort in transferring work order findings to quotes

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* [ ] User can initiate quote issue report generation from a work order
* [ ] User can generate a standalone quote issue report (not tied to specific WO)
* [ ] User can bulk add issues from a work order to a quote
* [ ] Quote report displays all associated issues with descriptions, severity, and recommendations
* [ ] Report clearly identifies source site visit type (energized scan, arc flash audit, routine maintenance)
* [ ] Report is exportable/printable for customer delivery
* [ ] Issues maintain traceability link to originating work order

column1: Z | Frontend | Clear Unwanted Console Logs and Duplicate Script Tag
column41:
h3. Impact

Excessive debug-level console logs and a duplicate PLuG SDK script tag in the frontend codebase are causing clutter in the browser console and network tab. This makes it difficult for users to debug real issues.

h3. Expected behaviour

The frontend should not output unnecessary debug logs in production. The PLuG SDK script tag should only be included once, and only genuine errors should trigger {{console.error}} calls.

h3. Actual behaviour

The frontend currently outputs a large volume of debug logs, leaks internal details, and has a duplicate PLuG SDK script tag in {{index.html}}. This results in a polluted console and network tab.

h3. Steps to reproduce

* Run the app locally.
* Check the browser console for debug logs.
* Inspect the network tab for duplicate PLuG SDK script tags.

h3. Environment

The bug is present in the production environment of the frontend codebase.

h3. Workarounds

N/A

h3. Other information

* Files changed:
** {{index.html}}: Removed duplicate PLuG SDK script tag
** {{src/App.jsx}}: Removed 3 test logs + 7 PLuG debug logs
** {{src/utils/apiClient.js}}: Removed 8 request/refresh verbose logs
** {{src/services/brandingService.js}}: Removed ~20 branding fetch/override logs
** {{src/context/BrandingContext.jsx}}: Removed 7 context loading/reloading logs
** {{src/services/windowEventService.js}}: Removed ~20 event listener debug logs
* *Total:* 6 files changed, *118 lines removed*, 1 line added
* Out of Scope (External SDK Noise):
** {{version.json}}: Retool embedded dashboards
** {{track-events.publish}}: Retool telemetry
** {{rev-users.self.get}}: Retool
** {{startSession/}}, {{addMetadata/}}, {{setIdentifier/}}, {{plug-config}}: PLuG SDK (DevRev)
** {{envelope/?sentry_version=7}}: Sentry SDK
** {{webmonitoringapi.userexperior.online}}: UserExperior monitoring
* Testing:
*# Run the app locally and verify the browser console is clean of debug logs.
*# Verify PLuG widget still initializes correctly for authenticated users.
*# Verify branding loads correctly (logos, colors, dynamic API URL).
*# Verify window event service still handles modal open/close events.
*# Confirm {{console.error}} still fires on actual failures.

column1: Z | Android | IR Photos not linking; in-session Task Linking concept
column41:
h3. Impact

The bug affects the linking of IR Photos to tasks within the session room. Users are unable to effectively link tasks to nodes, which disrupts their workflow.

h3. Expected behaviour

Users should be able to:

* Add IR Photos through the session room asset context menu.
* Link tasks via a context menu item and a modal for linking/unlinking tasks to nodes.
* Use an ephemeral "listening" mode for tasks that auto-link to new or pulled-in nodes.

h3. Actual behaviour

Currently, the system does not allow users to link tasks to nodes. The expected context menu items and functionalities are missing.

h3. Steps to reproduce

* Open the session room.
* Attempt to add "Add IR Photos" to the asset context menu.
* Look for the "Link Task" context menu item.
* Check for the NodeTaskPickerView modal for linking/unlinking tasks.
* Test the listening mode for tasks to see if they auto-link to new nodes.

h3. Environment

N/A

h3. Workarounds

N/A

h3. Other information

* Ephemeral "listening" mode for tasks is not functioning.

column1: Z | FE | Removed Field Technician Still Visible in Work Block After Removal from Work Order
column41:
h3. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Login to the web application.
# Navigate to the *Scheduling* module.
# Open any Work Order.
# Add a *Field Technician* to the Work Order.
# Add a Work Block and verify that only the added Field Technician appears in the “Search Person” list (this is correct behavior).
# Now remove the same Field Technician from the Work Order.
# Open the existing Work Block.
# Check the assigned technician in the Work Block.

----

h3. *Description:*

When a Field Technician is added to a Work Order, the same technician correctly appears in the Work Block selection list.

However, if the Field Technician is removed from the Work Order, the technician still remains assigned in the existing Work Block and is not automatically removed.

This creates data inconsistency between the Work Order and its Work Blocks.

----

When a Field Technician is removed from the Work Order:

* The technician should automatically be removed from all associated Work Blocks.
*OR*
* The system should prevent removal if the technician is already assigned to a Work Block (with a validation message).

The Work Block data should always stay in sync with the Work Order.

column1: Z | iOS | Today” Button in Scheduler
column41:
h2. *Task 1: Add “Today” Button in Scheduler*

h3. *Title:*

Add “Today” Button Next to Day View in Scheduler

h3. *Description:*

In the Scheduler module, a *“Today”* button needs to be added next to the *Day* view button.

Currently, when a user navigates to any future date in Day, Week, Month, or Year view, there is no quick option to return to the current date.

h3. *Expected Result:*

* A “Today” button should be visible next to the Day view button.
* Clicking the “Today” button should redirect the scheduler to the current day.
* It should work from Day, Week, Month, and Year views.

----

h2. *Task 2: Show Selected Date Schedule Below Calendar in month view* 

h3. *Description:*

In the Scheduler, when a user selects any date from any month:

* Currently, selected date schedules are not clearly shown in month.

h3. *Expected Result:*

* When a user selects any date:
** If schedules exist → They should be displayed below.
** If no schedules exist → “No Schedule Available” message should be displayed.

----

h2. *Task 3: Limit Scheduling Listing to Next 3 Months*

h3. *Description:*

In the Scheduling listing screen, only upcoming 3 months of data should be displayed in the “Later” section.

h3. *Expected Result:*

* The “Later” section should display schedules only for the next 3 months.
* Data beyond 3 months should not be shown.

----

h2. *Task 4: Show Overdue Tag for Past Date Selection*

h3. *Description:*

When a user selects a past date in the Scheduler:

* If any schedules are pending or incomplete for that date,
* An *“Overdue”* tag should be displayed, similar to how it is shown in the web version.

h3. *Expected Result:*

* If a user selects a past date:
** Pending schedules should show an *“Overdue”* tag.

column1: Z | iOS | Work Order Created in Offline Mode Fails to Sync and Shows Retry Error in Sync Analysis

h3. *Description:*

When a user creates a Work Order the Work Order is successfully saved locally. However, after reconnecting to the internet and attempting to sync, the Work Order does not sync to the server. In the Sync Analysis section, a retry error message is displayed.

This prevents the offline-created Work Order from being uploaded to the system.

----

h3. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open the mobile application.
# Create a new Work Order in offline mode.
# Save the Work Order.
# Turn on the internet connection.
# Trigger Sync manually or wait for auto-sync.
# Navigate to Sync Analysis and check the sync status.

----

h3. *Actual Result:*

The Work Order does not sync to the server and shows a retry error in Sync Analysis.

----

h3. *Expected Result:*

The Work Order created in offline mode should successfully sync to the server once the internet connection is restored, without any retry errors.

column1: Z | Android | Rename OCPD/OCP to Sub-Components, Add Voice-to-Text, Dropdown Refactors, COM in Photo Walkthrough
column41:
h3. User story
* As a user, I want OCPD/OCP terminology updated to Sub-Component so that the app uses consistent, updated terminology.
* As a user, I want voice-to-text input for asset labels and core attributes so that I can quickly enter information hands-free.
* As a user, I want pickers to display long option names without truncation so that I can see full descriptions.
* As a user, I want asset name, required core attributes, and COM picker available in the photo walkthrough classification so that I can complete asset classification efficiently.

h3. Context

This issue focuses on multiple improvements to the Android application including terminology updates, voice input capabilities, dropdown/picker refactors, and enhanced photo walkthrough classification. This is the Android equivalent of ZP-1019 (iOS).

h3. Acceptance criteria
* Update default string resource values from OCPD/OCP to Sub-Component terminology throughout the app (in {{res/values/strings.xml}} and any related constants).
* Implement a {{SpeechRecognitionManager}} using Android's {{SpeechRecognizer}} API for voice-to-text input.
* Create a {{SpeechTextField}} composable for voice-enabled text fields.
* Create a {{VoiceAttributeRow}} composable for voice input on attribute rows.
* Refactor subtype pickers to use {{GenericDropdownMenu}} with bottom sheet presentation ({{useSheet = true}}) in {{AddAsset}} and {{CreateChildNode}} screens.
* Refactor shortcut picker from a dropdown menu to a sheet-based selection (bottom sheet).
* Add asset name, required core attributes, and COM picker to the photo walkthrough classification screen.
* Add select/dropdown mode-switching for voice input in entity core attributes row view.
* Add microphone and speech recognition permissions to {{AndroidManifest.xml}} ({{RECORD_AUDIO}}, {{INTERNET}}).
* Request runtime permissions for microphone access before initiating voice input.

h3. Other information
* iOS reference ticket: ZP-1019

* Bug 2 - Collapsed enclosures as drop targets (useDragAndDrop.jsx + GraphEditor.jsx): Added hiddenNodeIds parameter to the hook and filtered the enclosureNodes memo to exclude hidden nodes (!hiddenNodeIds.has([n.id|http://n.id])). Passed hiddenNodeIds from GraphEditor.jsx where it's already available.          
* Bug 3 - SLD link edges on DynamicNodes (App.jsx): Updated getDefaultSourceHandle and getDefaultTargetHandle to check node.data.node_terminals for database-driven terminal handle codes (LOAD side for source, LINE side for target) before falling back to hardcoded handle IDs.

column1: Z | Android | Temperature Units & Delta T — Add unit field to issue details with °F/°C selector and calculated field support
column41:
h2. Context

Per the Temperature Units & Delta T spec, issue detail properties must now include a {{unit}} field when saved to the database. Temperature fields need a °F/°C selector, and calculated fields (e.g., Delta T) must inherit the unit from referenced temperature fields. This is the Android equivalent of iOS changes being made in ZP-1015, and web frontend changes made in {{IssueFormFields.jsx}}, {{IssueEditor.jsx}}, {{IssueCreationForm.jsx}}, and {{useCalculatedFields.js}}.

Reference tickets: ZP-828, ZP-1015

h2. Current State (Android)
* {{IssueProperty}} (in {{Issue.kt}}) has: {{id}}, {{issueId}}, {{issueClassPropertyId}}, {{name}}, {{value}} — *no {{unit}} field*
* {{IssuePropertyDTO}} (in {{SLDDTO.kt}}) has: {{id}}, {{name}}, {{value}}, {{issueClassProperty}} — *no {{unit}} field*
* {{IssueClassProperty}} (in {{IssueClass.kt}}) has: {{id}}, {{name}}, {{type}}, {{index}}, {{afRequired}}, {{description}}, {{options}}, {{columns}}, {{internalType}} — *no {{calculation}} or {{expression}} fields*
* {{AttributeProperty}} interface has: {{id}}, {{name}}, {{type}}, {{afRequired}}, {{options}}, {{description}} — *no {{calculation}} or {{unit}}*
* {{CoreAttributesSection}} handles types: text, string, number, integer, float, double, boolean, bool, select, dropdown, date, table_with_column_headers, table_with_row_headers — *no {{temperature}} or {{calculated}} type*
* API payloads (via {{IssueMappers.kt}}) for issue create/update do not include {{unit}}

h2. Required Changes

h3. 1. Data Model

_IssueProperty_ ({{Issue.kt}}) — Add:
{code:kotlin}
val unit: String? = null // e.g. "°F", "°C"
{code}

_IssueClassProperty_ ({{IssueClass.kt}}) — Add:
{code:kotlin}
@SerializedName("calculation")
val calculation: String? = null // JSON: {"expression": "[Problem Temp] - [Reference Temp]"}
{code}

_IssuePropertyDTO_ ({{SLDDTO.kt}}) — Add:
{code:kotlin}
@SerializedName("unit")
val unit: String? = null
{code}

_AttributeProperty_ interface ({{CoreAttributesSection.kt}}) — Add:
{code:kotlin}
val calculation: String?  // Add to interface with default null
{code}

h3. 2. Temperature Property Type UI

Add {{temperature}} case in {{CoreAttributesSection.kt}} {{AttributeFieldStyled}} when block:
* Decimal numeric input field (keyboard type: decimal)
* °F/°C segmented control / toggle (default: °F)
* Unit label displayed next to value
* Store selected unit in a {{draftUnits: Map[UUID, String]}} state map parallel to {{draftValues}}

h3. 3. Calculated Field Type with Unit Resolution

Add {{calculated}} case in {{CoreAttributesSection.kt}} {{AttributeFieldStyled}} when block:
* Read-only display with resolved unit (e.g., "10 °F")
* Manual value override supported, but unit still follows temperature fields

_Unit Resolution Logic (from spec):_
h1. If calculated property has a static {{unit}} → use it
h1. Parse expression for {{[Field Name]}} references
h1. Match against {{temperature}} type properties in same issue class
h1. Use first matched temperature field's selected unit
h1. Fallback: no unit

h3. 4. API Payload & Mappers

Update {{IssueMappers.kt}} to include {{unit}}:
{code:kotlin}
fun IssueProperty.toDTO(): IssuePropertyDTO {
    return IssuePropertyDTO(
        id = id,
        name = name,
        value = value,
        issueClassProperty = issueClassPropertyId,
        unit = unit  // ✅ Include unit
    )
}
{code}

Ensure DTO-to-entity mapping also preserves {{unit}} when parsing API responses.
Ensure offline sync queue payloads include {{unit}} (sync re-serializes from local DB, so updating the entity/DTO is sufficient).

h3. 5. Delta T Unit Auto-Sync

When user switches temperature unit (°F ↔ °C):
* Re-resolve unit for all calculated fields referencing temperature fields
* Update {{draftUnits}} for affected calculated fields
* Use a flag/ref to avoid infinite recomposition loops (similar to web's {{lastEmittedRef}} approach)

h2. Files to Modify

|| File || Change ||
| {{data/local/database/entity/Issue.kt}} | Add {{unit: String? = null}} to {{IssueProperty}} |
| {{data/local/database/entity/IssueClass.kt}} | Add {{calculation: String? = null}} to {{IssueClassProperty}} |
| {{data/remote/dto/SLDDTO.kt}} | Add {{unit: String? = null}} to {{IssuePropertyDTO}} |
| {{data/remote/dto/IssueMappers.kt}} | Map {{unit}} in {{IssueProperty.toDTO()}} and reverse mapping |
| {{presentation/common/components/CoreAttributesSection.kt}} | Add {{temperature}} and {{calculated}} type cases in {{AttributeFieldStyled}}; add {{calculation}} to {{AttributeProperty}} interface; add {{draftUnits}} state management |
| {{presentation/issues/IssueDetailsScreen.kt}} | Pass unit draft state to core attributes section |
| New: {{domain/usecase/issues/CalculatedFieldResolver.kt}} | Expression parsing + unit resolution utility |

h2. Acceptance Criteria
* [ ] {{IssueProperty}} entity has {{unit: String? = null}} field (backward compatible)
* [ ] {{IssueClassProperty}} entity has {{calculation: String? = null}} field
* [ ] DTOs serialize/deserialize {{unit}} and {{calculation}} correctly via Gson
* [ ] Temperature fields render decimal numeric input with °F/°C selector (default: °F)
* [ ] Calculated fields render as read-only with resolved unit (e.g., "10 °F")
* [ ] Unit resolution follows spec: static unit → expression parsing → first temperature match → fallback null
* [ ] Switching °F ↔ °C on temperature field auto-updates Delta T unit
* [ ] Issue create API payload includes {{unit}} for temperature and calculated fields
* [ ] Issue update API payload includes {{unit}} for temperature and calculated fields
* [ ] Offline sync queue payloads include {{unit}} (verified via local DB → DTO serialization)
* [ ] Non-temperature fields unaffected (no {{unit}} key or null)
* [ ] No infinite recomposition loops on unit changes
* [ ] Room database migration not needed ({{unit}} is stored inside JSON blob in {{details}} column)

        unit: attr.unit  // ✅ Include unit
    )
}
{code}

Ensure {{CoreAttributesService.applyCoreAttributeChanges}} persists unit from draft state.
Ensure offline sync queue payloads include {{unit}}.

h3. 5. Delta T Unit Auto-Sync

When user switches temperature unit (°F ↔ °C):
* Re-resolve unit for all calculated fields referencing temperature fields
* Update {{draftUnits}} for affected calculated fields
* Avoid infinite re-render loops (similar to web's {{lastEmittedRef}} approach)
h2. 

h2. Files to Modify

|| File || Change ||
| {{Egalvanic PZ/Core/Models/Entities/IssueClass.swift}} | Add {{unit}} to IssueProperty, {{calculation}} to IssueClassProperty |
| {{Egalvanic PZ/Core/Models/DTOs/IssueClassDTO.swift}} | Add {{unit}} to IssuePropertyDTO, {{calculation}} to IssueClassPropertyDTO |
| {{Egalvanic PZ/Core/UI/Protocols/EntityCoreAttributesProtocol.swift}} | Extend protocol + update CoreAttributesService to persist units |
| {{Egalvanic PZ/Core/UI/Views/EntityCoreAttributesRowView.swift}} | Add {{temperature}} and {{calculated}} type cases |
| {{Egalvanic PZ/Core/UI/Views/EntityCoreAttributesView.swift}} | Add {{draftUnits}} state, unit resolution, auto-sync logic |
| {{Egalvanic PZ/Views/Issues/IssueDetailView.swift}} | Pass unit draft state to core attributes section |
| {{Egalvanic PZ/Core/Singletons/APIClient.swift}} | Include {{unit}} in issue create/update payload |
| New: {{Egalvanic PZ/Core/Services/CalculatedFieldService.swift}} | Expression parsing + unit resolution service |
h2. 

h2. Acceptance Criteria
* [ ] IssueProperty entity has {{unit: String?}} field (backward compatible)
* [ ] IssueClassProperty entity has {{calculation: String?}} field
* [ ] DTOs serialize/deserialize {{unit}} and {{calculation}} correctly
* [ ] Temperature fields render numeric input with °F/°C selector (default: °F)
* [ ] Calculated fields render as read-only with resolved unit (e.g., "10 °F")
* [ ] Unit resolution follows spec: static unit → expression parsing → first temperature match → fallback nil
* [ ] Switching °F ↔ °C on temperature field auto-updates Delta T unit
* [ ] Issue create API payload includes {{unit}} for temperature and calculated fields
* [ ] Issue update API payload includes {{unit}} for temperature and calculated fields
* [ ] Offline sync queue payloads include {{unit}}
* [ ] Non-temperature fields unaffected (no {{unit}} key or null)
* [ ] No infinite re-render loops on unit changes

column1: Z | FE | Integrate DevRev Web SDK (PLuG Widget)
column41:
h2. Overview
Integrate the DevRev Web SDK (PLuG widget) into the React frontend to enable in-app support widget, conversation tracking, and user engagement features. This is the frontend counterpart to the Android (ZP-1008) and iOS (ZP-1007) SDK integrations.

h2. References
* Web SDK Quickstart: https://developer.devrev.ai/sdks/web/quickstart
* User Identity Docs: https://developer.devrev.ai/sdks/web/user-identity
* Android counterpart: ZP-1008
* iOS counterpart: ZP-1007

h2. Implementation Steps

h3. 1. Install DevRev Web SDK
* Install the DevRev PLuG SDK package via npm/yarn
* Alternatively, load the SDK via script tag if preferred
* Verify the package is added to {{package.json}} dependencies

h3. 2. Obtain App ID
* Navigate to https://app.devrev.ai > Settings > Plug settings
* Copy the unique App ID
* Store the App ID in environment configuration files per environment (dev, qa, staging, prod)

h3. 3. Initialize the SDK
* Initialize the DevRev PLuG widget on app startup (e.g., in the root {{App.jsx}} or a dedicated provider component)
* Configure the App ID from environment variables ({{VITE_DEVREV_APP_ID}} or equivalent)
* Ensure the widget loads after the app shell renders but does not block initial page load
* Follow existing patterns for third-party SDK initialization (e.g., Sentry setup)

h3. 4. Configure User Identity
User identification is _NOT automatic_. It must be explicitly configured after user login.

_Recommended Approach: Session Token (server-side)_
* Backend generates a session token using the Application Access Token (AAT) combined with user data
* Frontend receives the session token after login and passes it to the DevRev Web SDK to identify the user

_User Properties to Configure:_
|| Property || Description || Required ||
| {{user_ref}} | Unique user identifier (use user ID from auth context) | Yes (unique) |
| {{email}} | User's email address | Recommended |
| {{display_name}} | User's visible name | Recommended |
| {{phone_numbers}} | E.164 format | Optional |
| {{custom_fields}} | Org-specific attributes (prefix: {{tnt__}}) | Optional |

_Integration Points:_
* After successful login (in the auth flow/context), identify the user with DevRev Web SDK
* On logout, clear the DevRev user session / unidentify the user
* On app startup (page refresh), restore DevRev user identity for already-authenticated users using stored auth context
* Integrate in the same auth flow where Sentry user context is currently set/cleared

h3. 5. Widget Placement & Configuration
* Ensure the PLuG support widget renders in a non-intrusive position (typically bottom-right corner)
* Verify the widget does not overlap with existing UI elements (navigation, modals, FABs, etc.)
* Consider hiding the widget on specific routes if needed (e.g., login page, public pages)
* Ensure the widget is responsive and works on all supported viewport sizes

h3. 6. Create a DevRev Provider/Hook (Optional but Recommended)
* Create a {{DevRevProvider}} context or custom hook (e.g., {{useDevRev}}) to encapsulate SDK initialization and user identity management
* This keeps the integration testable and centralized
* Follow existing provider patterns in the codebase

h3. 7. Testing
* Verify SDK initializes without errors across all environments (dev, qa, staging, prod)
* Verify support widget appears and functions correctly
* Verify user identity is set after login
* Verify user identity is cleared on logout
* Verify conversation history persists for identified users across page refreshes
* Test with different user roles/permissions
* Verify no impact on existing app performance (Lighthouse, bundle size)
* Verify no console errors or warnings from the SDK
* Verify widget does not interfere with existing UI components

h2. Acceptance Criteria
* [ ] DevRev Web SDK installed and configured in the frontend project
* [ ] SDK initializes on app load with correct App ID per environment (dev, qa, staging, prod)
* [ ] User is identified with DevRev after login (user_ref, email, display_name at minimum)
* [ ] User identity is restored for already-authenticated users on page refresh
* [ ] User session is cleared on logout
* [ ] Support widget is accessible, visible, and functional
* [ ] Widget does not overlap or interfere with existing UI elements
* [ ] No regressions in existing functionality
* [ ] No significant impact on bundle size or page load performance

column41:
h2. Overview
Integrate the DevRev Android SDK into the Android app to enable in-app support widget, conversation tracking, and user engagement features.

h2. References
* Android SDK Quickstart: https://developer.devrev.ai/sdks/android/quickstart
* User Identity Docs: https://developer.devrev.ai/sdks/web/user-identity
* iOS counterpart ticket: ZP-1007

h2. Requirements
* Android Studio 2025.1.1 or later
* Android Gradle Plugin 8.2 or later
* Gradle 8.9 or later
* Minimum Android API Level 24 (our app targets minSdk 31, so this is satisfied)

h2. Implementation Steps

h3. 1. Add DevRev SDK via Gradle
* Add {{mavenCentral()}} to the repositories block in root {{build.gradle.kts}} (if not already present)
* Add the dependency to {{app/build.gradle.kts}}:
{code}
implementation("ai.devrev.sdk:devrev-sdk:[VERSION]")
{code}
* Add the version to {{gradle/libs.versions.toml}} for consistency with existing dependency management
* Add ProGuard rules if using code shrinking:
{code}
-keep class ai.devrev.__ { *; }
-keep class com.userexperior._ { _; }
{code}

h3. 2. Obtain App ID
* Navigate to https://app.devrev.ai > Settings > Plug settings
* Copy the unique App ID

h3. 3. Initialize the SDK
* Call {{DevRev.configure(context = this, appId = "APP_ID")}} in {{EgalvanicApplication.onCreate()}} (alongside existing Sentry initialization)
* Store the App ID in {{BuildConfig}} per environment using {{buildConfigField}} in each product flavor (dev, qa, staging, prod) inside {{app/build.gradle.kts}}
* Verify initialization status using {{DevRev.isConfigured}} property
* Follow the existing Sentry initialization pattern for error handling and environment-based configuration

h3. 4. Configure User Identity
User identification is _NOT automatic_. It must be explicitly configured after user login.

_Recommended Approach: Session Token (server-side)_
* Generate a session token on the backend using the Application Access Token (AAT) combined with user data
* Pass the session token to the Android SDK after login to identify the user

_User Properties to Configure:_
|| Property || Description || Required ||
| {{user_ref}} | Unique user identifier (use {{userInfo.sub}}) | Yes (unique) |
| {{email}} | User's email address | Recommended |
| {{display_name}} | User's visible name | Recommended |
| {{phone_numbers}} | E.164 format | Optional |
| {{custom_fields}} | Org-specific attributes (prefix: {{tnt__}}) | Optional |

_Integration Points:_
* After successful login in {{SecureDataStoreManager.saveUserData()}}, identify the user with DevRev SDK (same location where Sentry user context is currently set)
* On logout in {{SecureDataStoreManager.clearUserData()}}, clear the DevRev user session (same location where Sentry user context is currently cleared)
* On app startup, restore DevRev user identity in {{EgalvanicApplication.initializeSentryUserContext()}} (or a new parallel method) for returning users who are already logged in

h3. 5. Hilt Dependency Injection
* Consider creating a {{DevRevModule}} in {{di/}} package to provide the DevRev SDK instance, following the existing Hilt module patterns ({{NetworkModule}}, {{FirebaseModule}}, etc.)
* This keeps the SDK testable and mockable

h3. 6. Testing
* Verify SDK initializes without crashes on all build flavors (dev, qa, staging, prod)
* Verify support widget appears and functions correctly
* Verify user identity is set after login
* Verify conversation history persists for identified users
* Test offline/online transitions
* Verify no impact on existing WorkManager background sync
* Verify ProGuard rules work correctly in release builds

h2. Acceptance Criteria
* [ ] DevRev SDK added as Gradle dependency and builds successfully across all variants
* [ ] SDK initializes on app launch with correct App ID per environment (dev, qa, staging, prod)
* [ ] User is identified with DevRev after login (user_ref, email, display_name at minimum)
* [ ] User identity is restored for already-logged-in users on app startup
* [ ] User session is cleared on logout
* [ ] Support widget is accessible and functional
* [ ] ProGuard rules configured for release builds
* [ ] No regressions in existing functionality

column1: [Android] Sync queue should prioritize photo/attachment uploads before parent entity create/update operations
column41:
h2. Problem

When syncing {{{n}}} records, the sync queue processes items in strict FIFO order ({{createdAt ASC}}) without considering dependency relationships between operations. This causes issues when a parent entity (e.g., Create Asset/Node) is synced before its dependent resources (e.g., Photos, Attachments) have been uploaded.

*Example Scenario:*
# User creates an Asset (Node) and takes a photo of it while offline
# Both operations are added to the sync queue with timestamps close together
# On sync, the "Create Node" request may be processed *before* the "Upload Photo" request
# The backend receives the node creation without the associated photo, causing data inconsistency

*Expected Behavior:*
Photos and attachments associated with an entity should be uploaded *first*, then the parent entity create/update operation should follow. This ensures the backend receives complete data.

h2. Current Implementation

*Sync Queue Entity:* {{SyncQueueItem}} ({{data/local/database/entity/SyncQueueItem.kt}})
* Ordered purely by {{createdAt ASC}} (FIFO) — no priority field exists
* All sync targets (NODE, EDGE, PHOTO, ATTACHMENT, etc.) are treated equally

*Queue Processing:* {{FlushSyncQueueUseCase}} ({{domain/usecase/sync/FlushSyncQueueUseCase.kt}})
* Fetches all items via {{syncQueueItemDao.getAll()}} which uses {{ORDER BY createdAt ASC}}
* Processes sequentially without considering operation dependencies

*DAO Query:*
{code:sql}SELECT * FROM sync_queue_item ORDER BY createdAt ASC
{code}

h2. Proposed Solution

Add a {{priority}} field to {{SyncQueueItem}} and update the queue ordering to process items by priority first, then by {{createdAt}} within the same priority level.

h3. Priority Order (lower number = higher priority, processed first):
|| Priority || Sync Target || Rationale ||
| 0 | PHOTO | Must upload before parent entity so backend can associate |
| 0 | IR_PHOTO | Same as photo |
| 0 | ATTACHMENT | Must upload before parent entity |
| 1 | NODE (Create) | Depends on photos/attachments being uploaded first |
| 1 | EDGE (Create) | Depends on connected nodes existing |
| 2 | NODE (Update) | Lower priority than creates |
| 2 | EDGE (Update) | Lower priority than creates |
| 3 | Other targets | USER_TASK, ISSUE, FORM_INSTANCE, BUILDING, FLOOR, ROOM, MAPPINGS, etc. |
| 4 | DELETE operations | Process deletes last |

h3. Changes Required:
# *{{SyncQueueItem.kt}}* — Add {{priority: Int}} field with default value, add database index
# *Room Migration* — Add migration to add {{priority}} column to {{sync_queue_item}} table
# *{{SyncQueueItemDao.kt}}* — Update {{getAll()}}, {{getNextBatch()}}, and {{getRetryableItems()}} queries to {{ORDER BY priority ASC, createdAt ASC}}
# *Factory methods* ({{SyncQueueItem.forNode()}}, {{forPhoto()}}, etc.) — Set appropriate priority based on target and operation type
# *{{FlushSyncQueueUseCase.kt}}* — No logic changes needed if DAO queries handle ordering

h3. Updated DAO Query:
{code:sql}SELECT * FROM sync_queue_item ORDER BY priority ASC, createdAt ASC
{code}

h2. Acceptance Criteria
* [ ] Photos and attachments are always synced before their parent node/edge create operations
* [ ] CREATE operations are prioritized over UPDATE operations
* [ ] DELETE operations are processed last
* [ ] Within the same priority level, FIFO ordering is maintained
* [ ] Existing sync queue items (from before migration) are assigned correct default priority
* [ ] Sync progress UI ({{Sync {n} records}}) still reflects accurate count and progress
* [ ] Unit tests cover priority ordering logic

h2. Files to Modify
* {{app/src/main/java/com/egalvanic/pz/data/local/database/entity/SyncQueueItem.kt}}
* {{app/src/main/java/com/egalvanic/pz/data/local/database/dao/SyncQueueItemDao.kt}}
* {{app/src/main/java/com/egalvanic/pz/data/local/database/AppDatabase.kt}} (version bump + migration)
* {{app/src/main/java/com/egalvanic/pz/data/local/database/migrations/}} (new migration file)
* {{app/src/main/java/com/egalvanic/pz/domain/usecase/sync/FlushSyncQueueUseCase.kt}} (verify ordering)
* {{app/src/main/java/com/egalvanic/pz/domain/usecase/sync/ExportSyncQueueUseCase.kt}} (include priority in export)

18/Feb/26 7:31 AM;712020:4adb279c-383d-4a86-b512-a5a829380b9f;h2. QA Impacted Areas

h3. 1. Sync Queue Ordering (Critical)

*Test: Photos upload/queue before entity creation on all screens*

||Screen||Action||Verify||
|*Add Asset*|Add 5 photos + set default photo → Save|All 5 photos upload before node creation; no FK error|
|*Asset Detail*|Edit asset, add/update photos → Save|Photos upload before asset save|
|*Issue Details*|Add photos → Save Changes|Photos upload before issue save|
|*Task Details*|Add photos → Save|Photos upload before task save|
|*Add Building*|Add photo → Create|Photo uploads before building save|
|*Add Floor*|Add photo → Create|Photo uploads before floor save|
|*Add Room*|Add photo → Create|Photo uploads before room save|

*Test both online and offline:*

* *Online + empty queue*: Photos upload to S3 directly, then entity API call fires
* *Online + pending queue items*: Photos AND entity both go to sync queue (FIFO preserved)
* *Offline*: Both go to sync queue → come online → flush in correct order

h3. 2. canDirectSync Centralization (High)

*All API-calling operations now use canDirectSync() — queue when pending items exist*

* Create/update/delete *nodes* (assets)
* Create/update *edges* (connections)
* Create/update/delete *tasks*
* Create/update *issues*
* Create *sessions*
* *Photo* upload, update, delete
* *IR photo* metadata sync
* *Attachment* upload
* *Form instance* creation
* *SLD view* save, node/edge mappings
* *Node-session*, *task-session*, *task-node*, *user-task* mappings
* *Location* updates
* Link/unlink child nodes
* Batch complete task nodes

*Regression test:* Perform any of the above while there are pending sync items → verify operation queues instead of calling API directly

h3. 3. Loading Indicator UI (Visual)

*All loading spinners replaced with Material 3 LoadingIndicator (wave animation)*

Screens to visually verify loading states:

* Login, Company Code, Select Site, Create Site
* Home (refresh), Settings (sync)
* Asset list, Asset detail, Add Asset
* Issue list, Issue details
* Task list, Task details
* Session selection, Session detail, Session tabs (files, issues, tasks, details)
* Building/Floor/Room add/edit
* Connections list, Add/Edit connection
* SLD screen, SLD view form, View selection
* Photo walkthrough, Photo diagnostics
* Quick count, Bulk quick count
* Attachments, Camera capture
* Schedule, Sync queue analyzer
* IR photo linking, Issue/Task linking
* Re-authentication, Password change
* *ArcFlash screen*: Circular progress ring (percentage) should remain unchanged

h3. 4. Edge Cases

* Add asset with default photo while offline → go online → flush sync queue → verify photo syncs before node
* Rapid save: tap save multiple times quickly → only one operation should proceed
* Large photo batch (5+): all photos should be processed, none dropped
* Cancel during photo upload → verify no orphaned queue items

----

*Not impacted:* SLD WebView rendering, QR scanning, Firebase push notifications, data download/upsert flow, schedule/work blocks.

column1: Fix ILIKE type mismatch on mutation_inbox.status enum column in search query
column41:
h2. Bug Description

The mutation inbox search/filter query fails with an {{UndefinedFunction}} error when performing an ILIKE operation on the {{mutation_inbox.status}} column. The {{status}} column uses a custom {{mutation_status}} enum type, which does not support the {{ILIKE}} (~~*) operator. The search query attempts to apply ILIKE across multiple columns including {{status}}, but PostgreSQL cannot compare an enum type using pattern matching without an explicit cast.

h2. Error Details

{code}(psycopg2.errors.UndefinedFunction) operator does not exist: mutation_status ~~* unknown
HINT: No operator matches the given name and argument types. You might need to add explicit type casts.
{code}

*SQL:*
{code:sql}SELECT count(*) AS count_1
FROM (
  SELECT mutation_inbox.id, mutation_inbox.mutation_id, mutation_inbox.company_id,
         mutation_inbox.sld_id, mutation_inbox.entity_type, mutation_inbox.entity_id,
         mutation_inbox.op, mutation_inbox.payload, mutation_inbox.schema_version,
         mutation_inbox.client_timestamp, mutation_inbox.received_at, mutation_inbox.user_id,
         mutation_inbox.device_id, mutation_inbox.idempotency_hash, mutation_inbox.status,
         mutation_inbox.attempt_count, mutation_inbox.next_attempt_at, mutation_inbox.error_details,
         mutation_inbox.leased_until, mutation_inbox.leased_by
  FROM mutation_inbox
  WHERE mutation_inbox.company_id = %(company_id_1)s::UUID
    AND mutation_inbox.status = %(status_1)s
    AND (
      CAST(mutation_inbox.mutation_id AS VARCHAR) ILIKE %(param_1)s
      OR mutation_inbox.entity_type ILIKE %(entity_type_1)s
      OR CAST(mutation_inbox.entity_id AS VARCHAR) ILIKE %(param_2)s
      OR mutation_inbox.status ILIKE %(status_2)s   -- ← FAILS HERE
    )
) AS anon_1
{code}

*Parameters:*
* {{company_id_1}}: 73d49148-70fc-4116-86c3-853d4de3f888
* {{status_1}}: dead_letter
* {{param_1}}: %927f6faa-87c6-45a6-ab6c-9b8113a063fe%
* {{entity_type_1}}: %927f6faa-87c6-45a6-ab6c-9b8113a063fe%
* {{param_2}}: %927f6faa-87c6-45a6-ab6c-9b8113a063fe%
* {{status_2}}: %927f6faa-87c6-45a6-ab6c-9b8113a063fe%

*Frontend stack trace:*
* {{APIClient.request}} (src/utils/apiClient.js:246:21)
* {{loadMutations}} (src/pages/AuditLog.jsx:107:24)

h2. Root Cause
The search query applies {{ILIKE}} to all searchable columns including {{mutation_inbox.status}}, but {{status}} is a PostgreSQL enum type ({{mutation_status}}) which does not support pattern matching operators. Other columns like {{mutation_id}} and {{entity_id}} are explicitly cast to {{VARCHAR}} before the {{ILIKE}}, but {{status}} is not.

h2. Suggested Fix
Cast the {{status}} column to {{VARCHAR}}/{{TEXT}} before applying {{ILIKE}}:
{code:sql}CAST(mutation_inbox.status AS TEXT) ILIKE %(status_2)s
{code}
Alternatively, exclude the {{status}} column from the free-text search filter since it is already being filtered by an exact match ({{status = 'dead_letter'}}) in the WHERE clause.

column1: Fix UniqueViolation on ir_photos when uploading IR photos
column41:
h2. Bug Description

IR photo upload fails with a {{UniqueViolation}} error when inserting into the {{ir_photos}} table. The insert fails because a row with the same primary key ({{id}}) already exists, causing the operation to exceed max retries (3) and return a {{TRANSIENT_ERROR}}.

h2. Error Details

{code}(psycopg2.errors.UniqueViolation) duplicate key value violates unique constraint "ir_photos_pk"
DETAIL: Key (id)=(97889f1b-d51b-45a2-8dc7-94f91de822d2) already exists.
{code}

*SQL:*
{code:sql}INSERT INTO ir_photos (id, ir_session_id, visual_photo_key, ir_photo_key, date_created, node_id, sld_id, issue_id, is_deleted)
VALUES (%(id)s::UUID, %(ir_session_id)s::UUID, %(visual_photo_key)s, %(ir_photo_key)s, %(date_created)s, %(node_id)s::UUID, %(sld_id)s::UUID, %(issue_id)s::UUID, %(is_deleted)s)
{code}

*Parameters:*
* {{id}}: 97889f1b-d51b-45a2-8dc7-94f91de822d2
* {{ir_session_id}}: 919fe8f2-f31b-4553-bd80-c52d1ba76152
* {{visual_photo_key}}: FLIR2340
* {{ir_photo_key}}: FLIR2339
* {{node_id}}: 2fa379df-f761-4404-aedf-ee00f03f8440
* {{sld_id}}: 417778ca-0367-4226-9acb-cbb0dd4b1e93
* {{date_created}}: 2026-02-13T15:23:42 UTC
* {{failed_at}}: 2026-02-13T15:24:32 UTC (attempt 4 of 4)

h2. Expected Behavior
IR photo upload should handle the case where a row with the same ID already exists (e.g., due to a retry or soft-deleted row) and succeed without raising a UniqueViolation.

h2. Suggested Fix
Similar to ZP-978 (which fixed UniqueViolation on {{node_terminals}} by handling soft-deleted rows), consider using an upsert (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT) or checking for existing/soft-deleted rows before inserting.

column1: BE Implement customer deactivation with soft-delete and Cognito disable via admin portal
column41:
As a platform admin, I need to deactivate a customer tenant from the admin portal so that I can disable access without permanently losing data.

Per the [Customer Onboarding Technical Guide|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/Product/pages/53837825/Customer+Onboarding+Technical+Guide] (Section 10), deleted companies should never be physically removed.

h4. Company Deactivation
* Set {{companies.active = false}} and {{companies.is_deleted = true}}
* Preserve all associated data (accounts, SLDs, master records) untouched
* Record deactivation timestamp and admin who performed the action

h4. Cognito Disable
* Disable the Cognito User Pool (or disable all users within the pool) to prevent authentication
* Do not delete the User Pool — it must be restorable

h4. Subdomain Release
* Deactivated subdomains are NOT released for reuse (to prevent accidental collision)
* Subdomain validation endpoint must still report deactivated subdomains as unavailable

h4. Reactivation Path
* Provide a reactivation endpoint that reverses deactivation: sets {{active=true}}, {{is_deleted=false}}, and re-enables Cognito
* Reactivation must re-run the health-check (ZP-992) to confirm tenant integrity

h4. API
* {{POST /api/v1/admin/onboarding/\{company_id\}/deactivate}}
* {{POST /api/v1/admin/onboarding/\{company_id\}/reactivate}}
* Both require admin authentication and return updated company status

{color:#de350b}⚠️ _Cross-System Impact:_{color} Deactivation must update the correct database (eGalvanic or BCES-IQ). Cognito disable spans AWS resources. Ensure {{partner_db_middleware.py}} routes correctly.

h4. Cross-System Checklist
* [ ] Changes tested on DEV
* [ ] Changes tested on QA
* [ ] BCES-IQ migration planned (if DB change)
* [ ] Environment variables added to all locations (if new env var)
* [ ] Verified both PROD environments after deployment

📄 Reference: [Cross-System Validation Guide|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENGR/pages/102826000]

h4. Acceptance Criteria
* Deactivation sets {{active=false}} and {{is_deleted=true}} on the company record
* All associated data (accounts, SLDs, master records) remains intact after deactivation
* Cognito User Pool is disabled (not deleted) preventing all user authentication for the tenant
* Deactivated subdomains remain reserved and cannot be claimed by new onboarding
* Deactivation records the timestamp and performing admin's identity
* Reactivation reverses all deactivation changes and re-enables Cognito
* Reactivation triggers a health-check and returns the result
* Both endpoints require admin-level authentication
* Changes verified on BCES-IQ environment

column1: BE Implement onboarding validation and health-check endpoint for customer tenants
column41:
As a platform admin, I need a health-check endpoint that validates a customer's onboarding completeness so that I can confirm a tenant is fully operational or identify what failed.

Per the [Customer Onboarding Technical Guide|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/Product/pages/53837825/Customer+Onboarding+Technical+Guide] (Section 9), the following checks must pass after onboarding.

h4. Validation Checks
* Subdomain resolves correctly (DNS or routing verification)
* Company record exists in the correct database and {{active=true}}
* Cognito User Pool exists and is active
* Cognito App Client exists and is configured
* Alliance partner is assigned and matches expected branding
* Master data tables contain expected default records (non-zero count for all 9 tables)
* Sandbox account exists and Cognito user can authenticate with temporary credentials
* Data isolation: confirm no cross-tenant data leakage (company-scoped query returns only own records)

h4. API
* {{GET /api/v1/admin/onboarding/\{company_id\}/health-check}}
* Response: JSON object with each check as a key, value is {{ \{ status: "pass" | "fail" | "warn", detail: "..." \} }}
* Overall status: {{pass}} (all checks pass), {{partial}} (warnings present), {{fail}} (any critical check fails)

h4. Execution
* Health check must complete within 15 seconds
* Checks run in parallel where possible
* Timeout on individual checks does not block overall response (returns {{fail}} for timed-out check)

{color:#de350b}⚠️ _Cross-System Impact:_{color} Health checks must validate against the correct database (eGalvanic or BCES-IQ) based on alliance partner. Cognito validation spans AWS resources shared across both platforms.

h4. Cross-System Checklist
* [ ] Changes tested on DEV
* [ ] Changes tested on QA
* [ ] BCES-IQ migration planned (if DB change)
* [ ] Environment variables added to all locations (if new env var)
* [ ] Verified both PROD environments after deployment

📄 Reference: [Cross-System Validation Guide|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENGR/pages/102826000]

h4. Acceptance Criteria
* Health-check endpoint returns individual pass/fail/warn status for each validation check
* Overall status correctly aggregates individual results (fail if any critical check fails)
* Health check completes within 15 seconds for a fully provisioned tenant
* Timed-out individual checks return {{fail}} status without blocking the overall response
* Master data validation confirms non-zero record count across all 9 required tables
* Cognito validation confirms User Pool and App Client are active and configured
* Data isolation check confirms company-scoped queries return only the target tenant's records
* Changes verified on BCES-IQ environment

column1: FE Build customer management list view with onboarding status and actions in admin portal
column41:
As a platform admin, I need a list view of all onboarded customers in the Global Admin Portal so that I can monitor onboarding status, view customer details, and take management actions.

h4. List View
* Tabular display of all companies with columns: Company Name, Subdomain, Alliance Partner, Status (Active/Inactive), Cognito Status (Provisioned/Pending/Error), Created Date
* Default sort: newest first
* Pagination: server-side, 25 rows per page

h4. Filters
* Alliance partner (eGalvanic, BCES-IQ)
* Status (Active, Inactive, All)
* Search by company name or subdomain (debounced text input)

h4. Row Actions
* *View Details:* opens a detail panel showing full company record, branding preview, Cognito status, master data status, and sandbox account info
* *Edit Branding:* opens branding fields (logos, colors) for update
* *Deactivate:* triggers soft-delete flow with confirmation dialog (calls deactivation API from ZP-993)
* *Re-run Validation:* triggers health-check endpoint (from ZP-992) and displays results inline

h4. Empty & Error States
* Empty state: "No customers onboarded yet. Start by clicking New Customer."
* Error state: "Failed to load customers. Please retry." with retry button

h4. Acceptance Criteria
* List view displays all onboarded companies with correct column data
* Filters narrow results correctly for alliance partner and status
* Search returns results matching company name or subdomain within 500ms
* Pagination loads subsequent pages without full page reload
* View Details panel displays complete company record including Cognito and branding status
* Deactivate action shows a confirmation dialog before executing
* Re-run Validation displays health-check results inline without page navigation
* Empty and error states render correctly

column1: FE Build multi-step customer onboarding wizard in global admin portal
column41:
As a platform admin, I need a guided multi-step onboarding wizard in the Global Admin Portal so that I can onboard new customers through a clear, validated workflow without technical knowledge of the underlying systems.

h4. Wizard Steps

_Step 1 — Company Information:_
* Company name (required, text input)
* Subdomain (required, text input with real-time availability check via validation endpoint)
* Format hint: "lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only"
* Visual indicator: green checkmark for available, red X for taken or invalid

_Step 2 — Alliance Partner & Branding:_
* Alliance partner selection (required, radio: eGalvanic | BCES-IQ)
* Logo uploads: small logo and large logo (optional, image file inputs)
* Primary color and accent color (optional, color pickers)
* Invoke URL (optional, URL input with format validation)

_Step 3 — Review & Confirm:_
* Summary card showing all entered data
* Editable: clicking any section returns to that step
* "Start Onboarding" button triggers the backend onboarding pipeline

_Step 4 — Provisioning Status:_
* Real-time progress display showing each provisioning step:
** ✅ Company record created
** ✅ Cognito User Pool provisioned
** ✅ Alliance partner assigned
** ✅ Master data seeded
** ✅ Sandbox account created
** ✅ Validation passed
* Error states display failure reason with retry option per step
* On full success: display subdomain URL, sandbox login credentials, and "Go to Customer" link

h4. Navigation
* Back/Next buttons with step validation before advancing
* Progress indicator showing current step (e.g., stepper bar)
* Unsaved changes warning if navigating away

h4. Acceptance Criteria
* Wizard enforces required fields before allowing progression to the next step
* Subdomain availability is validated in real-time as the admin types (debounced, within 300ms of stop-typing)
* Alliance partner selection is required and visually distinct
* Review step displays all entered data accurately and allows edits
* Provisioning status updates in real-time as each backend step completes
* Failed provisioning steps display the error reason and offer a retry action
* Successful onboarding displays the tenant subdomain URL and sandbox credentials
* Wizard state is preserved if the admin navigates away and returns within the same session

column1: BE Create sandbox account provisioning as part of customer onboarding flow
column41:
As a platform admin, I need a sandbox account to be automatically created during customer onboarding so that the onboarding team can immediately verify the new tenant without creating production users.

Per the [Customer Onboarding Technical Guide|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/Product/pages/53837825/Customer+Onboarding+Technical+Guide] (Section 8), each new tenant should have a sandbox account for validation.

h4. Account Creation
* Create a record in the {{accounts}} table linked to the new company via {{accounts.company_id}}
* Account name: {{Sandbox - \{company_name\}}}
* Mark the account with a {{is_sandbox=true}} flag (or equivalent metadata)

h4. Sandbox User
* Create a Cognito user in the company's User Pool with a configurable default email (e.g., {{dharmesh.avaiya@egalvanic.com}} as per current process, or admin-specified)
* Set a temporary password that requires reset on first login

h4. Service Location (SLD)
* Create a default SLD record linked to the sandbox account for testing purposes
* Include placeholder address metadata

{color:#de350b}⚠️ _Cross-System Impact:_{color} Account and SLD records exist in BOTH eGalvanic and BCES-IQ databases. Ensure records are created in the correct database based on alliance partner.

h4. Cross-System Checklist
* [ ] Changes tested on DEV
* [ ] Changes tested on QA
* [ ] BCES-IQ migration planned (if DB change)
* [ ] Environment variables added to all locations (if new env var)
* [ ] Verified both PROD environments after deployment

📄 Reference: [Cross-System Validation Guide|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENGR/pages/102826000]

h4. Acceptance Criteria
* Sandbox account is created in the {{accounts}} table linked to the new company
* Sandbox account is identifiable as non-production (via naming convention or metadata flag)
* A Cognito user is created in the company's User Pool with a temporary password requiring reset
* Default sandbox email is configurable and not hardcoded
* A default SLD record is created and linked to the sandbox account
* Sandbox provisioning failures do not block overall onboarding but are logged and reported to the admin
* Changes verified on BCES-IQ environment

column1: BE Automate alliance partner assignment and master data seeding during onboarding
BE Automate AWS Cognito user pool and app client provisioning during customer onboarding
column41:
As a platform admin, I need Cognito User Pool and App Client creation to happen automatically when onboarding a customer so that I do not need AWS console access to configure tenant authentication.

Currently, onboarding requires manual Cognito configuration per [Customer Onboarding Technical Guide|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/Product/pages/53837825/Customer+Onboarding+Technical+Guide] (Section 4.3–4.4). This story automates that process.

h4. User Pool Creation
* Create a dedicated Cognito User Pool per company using the AWS SDK
* Pool name convention: {{subdomain}}-user-pool
* Configure required attributes: {{email}}, {{name}}
* Apply standard password policy (min 8 chars, mixed case, numbers, special characters)
* Enable email-based verification

h4. App Client Creation
* Create one App Client per User Pool
* Client name convention: {{subdomain}}-app-client
* Enable required auth flows (USER_PASSWORD_AUTH, ALLOW_REFRESH_TOKEN_AUTH)
* Generate client secret if required by alliance partner configuration

h4. Company Record Update
* After successful Cognito provisioning, update the company record with: {{cognito_user_pool_id}}, {{cognito_client_id}}, {{cognito_region}}, and {{client_secret_hash}} (if applicable)

h4. Rollback
* If Cognito provisioning fails, the company record must not retain partial Cognito configuration
* Delete the User Pool if App Client creation fails
* Log the failure for troubleshooting

h4. Configuration
* Cognito region defaults to {{us-east-2}} but must be configurable via environment variable {{COGNITO_DEFAULT_REGION}}

{color:#de350b}⚠️ _Cross-System Impact:_{color} New environment variable {{COGNITO_DEFAULT_REGION}} required. Update {{.env.example}}, GitHub Secrets, and ECS Task Definition. Cognito pools serve both eGalvanic and BCES-IQ tenants.

h4. Cross-System Checklist
* [ ] Changes tested on DEV
* [ ] Changes tested on QA
* [ ] BCES-IQ migration planned (if DB change)
* [ ] Environment variables added to all locations (if new env var)
* [ ] Verified both PROD environments after deployment

📄 Reference: [Cross-System Validation Guide|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENGR/pages/102826000]

h4. Acceptance Criteria
* Cognito User Pool is created automatically with correct naming convention and attribute configuration
* App Client is created within the User Pool with correct auth flows enabled
* Company record is updated with {{cognito_user_pool_id}}, {{cognito_client_id}}, {{cognito_region}} after successful provisioning
* If User Pool creation fails, company record retains no partial Cognito fields
* If App Client creation fails, the orphaned User Pool is deleted and company record retains no partial Cognito fields
* Cognito region is read from {{COGNITO_DEFAULT_REGION}} environment variable with {{us-east-2}} as fallback
* Provisioning completes within 30 seconds end-to-end
* Failure scenarios are logged with AWS error codes and human-readable messages
* Changes verified on BCES-IQ environment

column1: BE Create customer onboarding API with company record and subdomain provisioning
column41:
As a platform admin, I need an API endpoint that creates a new customer company record with a validated subdomain so that I can onboard customers without direct database access.

The API must handle the full company record creation flow currently performed manually per the [Customer Onboarding Technical Guide|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/Product/pages/53837825/Customer+Onboarding+Technical+Guide] (Section 4.1–4.2).

h4. Subdomain Provisioning
* Accept a proposed subdomain value (e.g., {{acme}})
* Validate global uniqueness across both eGalvanic ({{company}}.egalvanic.ai) and BCES-IQ ({{company}}.bces-iq.com) namespaces
* Enforce format rules: lowercase, alphanumeric, no spaces, no special characters except hyphens
* Reject reserved subdomains (e.g., {{admin}}, {{api}}, {{www}}, {{app}}, {{staging}}, {{dev}})

h4. Company Record
* Create a record in the {{companies}} table with fields: {{name}}, {{subdomain}}, {{active=true}}, {{is_deleted=false}}
* Accept optional branding fields: {{small_logo}}, {{large_logo}}, {{primary_color}}, {{accent_color}}, {{invoke_url}}
* Leave Cognito fields ({{cognito_user_pool_id}}, {{cognito_client_id}}, {{cognito_region}}) empty at this stage — they are populated by the Cognito provisioning story
* Return the created company ID for downstream steps

h4. Validation Endpoint
* Provide a {{GET /api/v1/admin/onboarding/validate-subdomain?subdomain=\{value\}}} endpoint for real-time availability checks from the UI

{color:#de350b}⚠️ _Cross-System Impact:_{color} The {{companies}} table exists in BOTH eGalvanic and BCES-IQ databases. The target database is determined by the selected alliance partner. Run migration on BOTH databases.

h4. Cross-System Checklist
* [ ] Changes tested on DEV
* [ ] Changes tested on QA
* [ ] BCES-IQ migration planned (if DB change)
* [ ] Environment variables added to all locations (if new env var)
* [ ] Verified both PROD environments after deployment

📄 Reference: [Cross-System Validation Guide|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENGR/pages/102826000]

h4. Acceptance Criteria
* API creates a company record in the correct database based on alliance partner selection
* Subdomain uniqueness is enforced across both eGalvanic and BCES-IQ namespaces before creation
* Subdomain format validation rejects invalid inputs (uppercase, spaces, special characters, reserved words)
* Branding fields (logos, colors, invoke URL) are stored when provided and nullable when omitted
* Company record is created with {{active=true}} and {{is_deleted=false}}
* Cognito fields remain null at creation time
* Subdomain validation endpoint returns availability status within 200ms
* API returns the created company UUID for use in subsequent onboarding steps
* Duplicate subdomain creation returns a 409 Conflict with a descriptive error message
* Changes verified on BCES-IQ environment

column1: IR Photos not linking; in-session Task Linking concept.

Users should be able to:

* Add IR Photos through the session room asset context menu.
* Link tasks via a context menu item and a modal for linking/unlinking tasks to nodes.
* Use an ephemeral "listening" mode for tasks that auto-link to new or pulled-in nodes.

Currently, the system does not allow users to link tasks to nodes. The expected context menu items and functionalities are missing.

h3. Steps to reproduce

* Open the session room.
* Attempt to add "Add IR Photos" to the asset context menu.
* Look for the "Link Task" context menu item.
* Check for the NodeTaskPickerView modal for linking/unlinking tasks.
* Test the listening mode for tasks to see if they auto-link to new nodes.

h3. Environment

N/A

h3. Workarounds

N/A

h3. Other information

* Ephemeral "listening" mode for tasks is not functioning.
* There is a need for a toggle in the task detail view, task creation, and tasks tab context menu.

column1: FE | User Management - Add edit icon and resend password option in actions column
column41:
h2. Summary
As an admin, I need an actions column in the User Management table with an edit icon and a resend password option, so I can quickly manage users and help them regain access when needed.

h2. Background
The User Management table currently lacks an actions column with quick-access controls. Admins need the ability to edit user details and resend temporary passwords directly from the user listing.

h2. Acceptance Criteria

h3. Actions Column
* [ ] Add an "Actions" column to the User Management data table
* [ ] The column should appear as the last column in the table
* [ ] Column should be non-sortable and have a fixed width

h3. Edit Icon
* [ ] Add an edit (pencil) icon button in the actions column for each user row
* [ ] Clicking the edit icon should open the existing user edit form/modal (or navigate to the user edit page)
* [ ] Edit icon should only be visible/enabled for users with {{users.manage}} permission
* [ ] Add appropriate tooltip (e.g., "Edit User") on hover

h3. Resend Password Option
* [ ] Add a "Resend Password" action — either as an additional icon button or within a dropdown/kebab menu alongside the edit icon
* [ ] Clicking "Resend Password" should show a confirmation dialog (e.g., "Are you sure you want to resend the password to {user_email}?")
* [ ] On confirmation, call the backend API {{POST /users/[user_id]/resend-password}}
* [ ] Show a success toast/notification on successful password resend (e.g., "Temporary password sent to {email}")
* [ ] Show an error toast/notification if the API call fails
* [ ] Resend password option should only be visible/enabled for users with {{users.manage}} permission
* [ ] Add appropriate tooltip on hover

h3. UI/UX
* [ ] Icons should follow the existing design system and icon library
* [ ] Actions should be visually consistent with other action columns across the application
* [ ] Disable actions (with visual indication) for the currently logged-in user's own row
* [ ] Loading state should be shown while the resend password API call is in progress

column1: BE | User Management - Resend password API endpoint
column41:
h2. Summary
As an admin, I need a backend API endpoint to resend a temporary password to a user so that users who missed or lost their initial credentials can regain access without manual intervention.

h2. Background
Currently, when a new user is created via {{POST /users/}}, a temporary password is generated and sent via email (SendGrid template). However, there is no dedicated endpoint to resend the password if the user did not receive or lost the original email. The only option is the forgot-password flow, which requires the user to know their username.

h2. Acceptance Criteria

h3. API Endpoint
* [ ] Create a new {{POST /users/[user_id]/resend-password}} endpoint in {{user_routes.py}}
* [ ] Endpoint should be protected with appropriate permission (e.g., {{users.manage}})
* [ ] Only admins/managers with {{users.manage}} permission should be able to trigger this

h3. Business Logic
* [ ] Generate a new secure temporary password (following existing pattern: base64 + special chars)
* [ ] Update the user's password in AWS Cognito using {{admin_set_user_password()}} with {{Permanent=False}} so the user is forced to change it on first login
* [ ] Send the welcome/credentials email via SendGrid with the new temporary password
* [ ] Return appropriate success/error responses

h3. Validations
* [ ] Verify the target user exists and belongs to the same company as the requesting admin
* [ ] Verify the target user is not deleted ({{is_deleted = False}})
* [ ] Verify the target user is active ({{active = True}})
* [ ] Handle Cognito errors gracefully (user not found in pool, etc.)

h3. Response
* [ ] Return {{200 OK}} with a success message on successful password reset
* [ ] Return {{404}} if user not found
* [ ] Return {{403}} if requesting user lacks permission
* [ ] Return {{400}} for invalid requests

h2. Technical Notes
* Existing password generation logic is in {{user_routes.py}} (POST {{/users/}} handler)
* Cognito user pool is resolved per-company via {{get_cognito_pool_for_company()}}
* Email service uses SendGrid template {{d-82f60f0c80ee4cbebca42918e40b0b61}}
* Consider extracting shared password generation + email sending into a reusable service method

h2. Dependencies
* AWS Cognito admin access
* SendGrid email service

iOS

N/A
