column41:
h3. Summary

Configure Statuspage notifications for subscribers during service outages. Ensure timely alerts for all monitored components.

h3. Context

The Statuspage is currently live and monitoring 10 components. Subscription channels include email, SMS, Slack, webhooks, and Atom/RSS feeds. The backend automation needs verification to trigger notifications automatically for status changes.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* All 10 Statuspage components are connected to real monitoring (no manual-only updates).
* Subscribers on all channels (email, SMS, Slack, webhooks) receive notifications when a component status changes.
* Notifications fire automatically when a real outage occurs (not only when manually created).
* Notification templates are branded and informative.
* End-to-end test documented and passing.
* Runbook created for DevOps: how to manually create/update incidents if needed.

h3. Other information

* Current components being monitored:
** Application, Web App, API Service, Mobile App
** Authentication and Authorization, System Services
** Email, Database, File Storage, Marketing Website
* Subscription channels available:
** Email (with OTP verification)
** SMS/Text (with OTP verification)
** Slack integration (via OAuth)
** Webhooks
** Atom/RSS feeds
* Recommended Approach: Option B (Native Integrations) is preferred if monitoring tools are in place. Option A (Statuspage API via Lambda) offers more control.
* References:

13/Feb/26 8:30 AM;712020:b43bccaf-6e8f-47ef-82f7-5439c6765fdb;we need permission of billing access and it will granted from the root account.

Here is the steps :

*Step 1: Enable IAM User Access to Billing (Root Account Only)*
This step must be performed using the *AWS root account*.

* Log in to the AWS Console using the *root account*.
* Scroll down to:

 *IAM User and Role Access to Billing Information*.

* Click *Edit*.
* Check the option:

 *Activate IAM Access*.

* Click *Update*.

This allows IAM users to access the billing console.

*Step 2: Attach Billing Policy to the IAM User*

* Go to *IAM* → *Users*.
* Select the IAM user you want to grant billing access to.
* Open the *Permissions* tab.
* Click *Add permissions*.
* Choose *Attach policies directly*.

In the search box, type: Billing

* Select the required policy: *Billing : Full billing access*
* Click *Next*.
* Click *Add permissions*.

*Result*
The IAM user will now have access to the *AWS Billing Console* with the permissions granted by the selected policy.

* *Lambda Throttles High* (per function x 6) - Alerts when throttles > 5 per 5 minutes
* *Lambda Duration High* (per function x 6) - Alerts when duration > 60 seconds

Z | iOS | Add Assignment Type Enums (Reporter/Assignee/Watcher) to User-Session and User-Task Mappings
column41:
h2. Problem Statement

The {{mapping_user_task}} and {{mapping_user_session}} tables exist but are not being fully utilized with proper assignment type semantics. Currently:

* {{mapping_user_task}} has a {{mapping_type}} field but uses informal values ("owner"/"assignee") with no enum enforcement
* {{mapping_user_session}} (UserSessionAccess model) has an overly complex structure with unused fields (assigned_by, can_reassign, expires_at) and no {{mapping_type}} field
* Work orders (IR sessions) are not filtered by user assignment on web or mobile — all users with {{sessions.view}} permission see everything
* Mobile "assigned to me" task filtering is not enum-aware (any mapping = assigned, rather than specifically "assignee" type)
* No admin override logic exists for assignment-based visibility

We need a proper assignment type system with Reporter, Assignee, and Watcher roles, applied consistently across backend, web frontend, and iOS mobile, with admin users bypassing assignment filters to see everything.

h2. User Acceptance Tests (UATs)

* [ ] Enum tables {{enum_task_assignment_types}} and {{enum_session_assignment_types}} exist in DB with entries: reporter, assignee, watcher
* [ ] {{mapping_user_session}} table simplified to: id, user_id, session_id, mapping_type, is_deleted (old columns removed)
* [ ] Existing {{mapping_user_task}} data migrated: 'owner' → 'reporter', NULL → 'assignee'
* [ ] Backend API: Can create/update user-session mappings with mapping_type via {{/mapping/user-session/create}} and {{/mapping/user-session/update/<id>}}
* [ ] Backend API: {{get_sld_full_v2}} response includes {{user_session}} mappings in the mappings object
* [ ] Backend API: Non-admin users only see sessions they are assigned to (reporter or assignee) when filtering is active
* [ ] Backend API: Admin users see all sessions regardless of assignment
* [ ] Web: SessionDetail page shows assignment management UI (add/remove reporters/assignees) gated by {{sessions.manage}} permission
* [ ] Web: Sessions list page has "My Work Orders" / "All Work Orders" toggle; non-admins default to "My Work Orders"
* [ ] Mobile: IRSession model has {{owned_by}} and {{assigned_to}} arrays populated from user_session mappings during sync
* [ ] Mobile: Work order list filters by assignee for non-admin users (default view shows assigned sessions only)
* [ ] Mobile: Admin users see all work orders regardless of assignment
* [ ] Mobile: New {{mappingUserSession}} SyncTarget handles offline create/update operations
* [ ] Mobile: Existing task "assigned to me" filter continues to work correctly (only assignee type = assigned)
* [ ] Backward compatible: Mobile handles both 'owner' and 'reporter' mapping types during transition

h2. Execution Plan

h3. Phase 1: Backend Database & Models

*1.1 Alembic Migration:*

* Create {{enum_task_assignment_types}} table (id, name, description) with values: reporter, assignee, watcher
* Create {{enum_session_assignment_types}} table (same structure)
* Restructure {{mapping_user_session}}: drop assigned_by/assigned_at/can_reassign/is_active/expires_at, add mapping_type VARCHAR and is_deleted BOOLEAN
* Migrate data: is_active=false → is_deleted=true; existing rows get mapping_type='assignee'
* Migrate mapping_user_task: 'owner' → 'reporter', NULL → 'assignee'

*1.2 Simplify Model* ({{eg-pz-backend/app/models/UserSessionAccess.py}}):

* Replace UserSessionAccess with MappingUserSession (id, user_id, session_id, mapping_type, is_deleted)

*1.3 Create Enum Models* ({{eg-pz-backend/app/models/EnumAssignmentType.py}}):

* EnumTaskAssignmentType, EnumSessionAssignmentType

*1.4 Update exports* ({{app/models/__init__.py}})

h3. Phase 2: Backend Routes & Controllers

*2.1* Add POST {{/mapping/user-session/create}} and PUT {{/mapping/user-session/update/<id>}} routes
*2.2* Add controller methods with mapping_type validation
*2.3* Refactor old user routes (lines 558-649 in user_routes.py) to use new model
*2.4* Update {{get_sld_full_v2}} to include user_session mappings in response
*2.5* Add session filtering by assignment in session_controller.py (admin bypass)
*2.6* Add GET endpoints for assignment type enums

h3. Phase 3: Frontend (Web)

*3.1* SessionDetail.jsx: Assignment management UI (user chips, autocomplete, role dropdown)
*3.2* Sessions.jsx: "My Work Orders" / "All Work Orders" toggle with admin default
*3.3* mainAPIService.js: Update session assignment functions for new mapping pattern

h3. Phase 4: Mobile (iOS)

*4.1* Add MappingUserSessionDTO to SLDMappingsDTO.swift
*4.2* Add owned_by/assigned_to arrays to IRSession entity
*4.3* Add {{mappingUserSession}} to SyncTarget enum
*4.4* Update SLDSyncService processMappings() for user-session processing
*4.5* Update all sync switch statements (NetworkState, SyncExecutionService, SyncQueueService, SyncDeduplicationService, SyncQueueAnalyzerView)
*4.6* Add APIClient createUserSessionMapping/updateUserSessionMapping methods
*4.7* Add MappingData.userSession() helper
*4.8* Add work order filtering in IRSessionViewModel (admin bypass)

h3. Implementation Order

# Backend migration + models
# Backend routes/controllers
# Mobile DTO + model + sync infrastructure
# Mobile API + filtering UI
# Frontend assignment UI + filtering

column1: Z | Android: | Implement Localization Framework for Internationalization
column41:
h2. User Story



* As a user in a non-English speaking region, I want the app in my language so that I can use it comfortably.
* As a developer, when I add new UI text, I want to use localized string resources so that the app can support multiple languages.
* As a product manager, when I update messaging, I want centralized string management so that changes are consistent.

h2. Context



The app has hardcoded UI strings across multiple Composable files and XML layouts instead of using Android string resources from strings.xml. This makes internationalization impossible and messaging updates difficult.



h2. Examples of Hardcoded Strings



* “Password is required”
* “Session Expired”
* “Invalid credentials”
* “Sign In”
* “Enter your password”

h2. Acceptance Criteria



* All UI strings defined in res/values/strings.xml
* All hardcoded UI strings in Composables replaced with stringResource()
* No hardcoded user-facing text remains in views
* String keys follow consistent naming convention (e.g., “login_password_required”)

h2. Technical Implementation



{noformat}// Add to res/values/strings.xml
<string name="login_password_required">Password is required</string>
<string name="login_invalid_credentials">Invalid credentials</string>
<string name="session_expired">Session Expired</string>
<string name="auth_sign_in">Sign In</string>

// Replace hardcoded strings in Composables
// Before
Text("Password is required")

// After
Text(stringResource(R.string.login_password_required)){noformat}

h2. Implementation Steps

# Audit all Composable files and XML layouts for hardcoded strings
# Add all UI strings to res/values/strings.xml with organized key structure
# Replace hardcoded strings with stringResource() calls in Composables
# Add comments explaining context for translators where needed
# Test that all strings display correctly across the app

h2. Related Issue

* Relates to ZP-905 (iOS: Implement Localization Framework for Internationalization)

column1: Z | iOS| No validation on Reference Temperature and Problem Temperature fields allows unlimited values, causing incorrect Delta T calculation
column41:
h3.  *Description*

While creating an issue, the *Reference Temperature* and *Problem Temperature* fields accept unlimited numeric values without any validation.
Because of this, the *Delta T* value is calculated incorrectly when extreme or unrealistic values are entered.

There should be proper validation to restrict these fields to an acceptable range so that Delta T is calculated accurately.

----

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Open the application
# Navigate to *Create Issue* screen
# Enter an unlimited or very high value in *Reference Temperature* field
# Enter an unlimited or very high value in *Problem Temperature* field
# Save or submit the issue
# Observe the *Delta T* value

----

h3. *Actual Result*

* Reference Temperature and Problem Temperature fields accept unlimited values
* Delta T is calculated incorrectly
* No validation or error message is shown

----

h3.  *Expected Result*

* Reference Temperature and Problem Temperature fields should allow only valid and predefined value ranges
* Proper validation message should be shown if the entered value exceeds the allowed limit
* Delta T should be calculated correctly based on valid input values

column1: Z | Android | Mobile Quick Add feature fails when adding more than 10 assets
column41:
h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Open the Z Platform mobile app on Android
# Navigate to the Quick Add feature
# Begin adding assets one at a time
# Continue adding assets beyond 10 items in a single session

*Expected Behavior*
The Quick Add feature allows users to add any number of assets without degradation in performance or functionality.

*Actual Behavior*
The Quick Add feature glitches when attempting to add more than 10 assets at a time. The app becomes unresponsive or fails to register new asset additions beyond the 10-asset threshold.

*Impact*
Customer-reported issue affecting field users who rely on Quick Add for bulk asset entry. This blocks efficient data collection workflows and forces users to work in smaller batches, reducing productivity.

*Others*
DevRev -  [https://app.devrev.ai/egalvanic/works/TKT-547|https://app.devrev.ai/egalvanic/works/TKT-547]
column211:
11/Feb/26 6:30 AM;712020:4adb279c-383d-4a86-b512-a5a829380b9f;Hello [~accountid:712020:b43bccaf-6e8f-47ef-82f7-5439c6765fdb] Sir, Please find the root cause of the issue and fix the details

  Root Cause: N per-asset reactive queries + no debounce = OOM                                                                                                                           

  The original code in RoomAssetsViewModel.loadStatusIndicatorsForAllAssets() launched 3 coroutines per asset:                                                                           

  assets.forEach { asset ->
      viewModelScope.launch { userTaskDao.getTasksForNode([asset.id|http://asset.id]).collect { ... } }                                                                                                    
      viewModelScope.launch { mappingDao.getTaskNodeMappingsForNodeFlow([asset.id|http://asset.id]).collect { ... } }                                                                                      
      viewModelScope.launch { issueDao.getIssuesForNode([asset.id|http://asset.id]).collect { ... } }
  }

  For 100 assets, that's 300 concurrent Flow collectors, each holding a Room DB cursor open.

  During bulk creation, each DB insert triggered the assetsFlow to re-emit, which called loadStatusIndicatorsForAllAssets() again — without cancelling the previous 300 coroutines. So
  after just 5 inserts, you'd have 1500+ active Flow collectors competing for DB connections and memory, causing the OOM.

  Two compounding problems:

# O(N) queries per load — 3 queries per asset instead of 3 batch queries total
# No debounce — every single DB insert re-triggered the entire load, and no statusIndicatorsJob was tracked/cancelled

  The fix:

* Replaced 3N queries with 3 batch queries (getTasksForNodes, getIssuesForNodes, getTaskNodeCompletionsForNodes)
* Track statusIndicatorsJob and cancel previous before starting new collectors
* Added 300ms debounce on reactive flows so rapid inserts batch into 1-2 updates instead of N
* Added nodeId index on task_node_mapping for efficient batch lookups

column1: Z | DevOps | Sync Terraform State with Current ECS Task Definition Revisions Across All Environments
column41:
h3. User story

* As a DevOps engineer, when Terraform state is misaligned with ECS task definitions, I want to sync the state so that the next {{terraform apply}} does not revert services to stale revisions.

h3. Context

Terraform state files have drifted from the actual deployed ECS task definition revisions across all five environments. This misalignment risks service disruptions during subsequent {{terraform apply}} actions.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* {{terraform plan}} returns no changes to ECS task definition resources in Dev, QA, Staging, Prod, and BCES-IQ.
* State backups exist for all 5 environments prior to modification.
* No service interruptions occur during or after the state sync.
* PR with any Terraform file adjustments is merged.
* Changes are verified on the BCES-IQ environment.

h3. Other information

*Current State Drift:*

{noformat}ECS Task Definition Revisions

Latest revisions:
  Dev:      eg-pz-dev-be-td-ohio:18
  QA:       eg-pz-qa-be-td-ohio:18
  Staging:  eg-pz-staging-be-td-ohio:18
  Prod:     eg-pz-prod-be-td-ohio:19
  BCES-IQ:  bces-iq-prod-be-td-ohio:16

Terraform state should be updated to match so the next apply doesn't revert them.{noformat}

*Scope:*

* Backup existing Terraform state for each environment before any modification.
* For each environment, import or re-sync the {{aws_ecs_task_definition}} resource to match the live revision.
* Validate via {{terraform plan}} that zero unintended changes are produced.
* Commit any Terraform file adjustments and open a PR for review.

*Cross-System Impact:* Apply to BOTH Egalvanic + BCES-IQ resources. The BCES-IQ environment ({{bces-iq-prod-be-td-ohio:15}}) must be handled with the same state-sync procedure and verified independently.

*Cross-System Checklist:*

* Terraform state backup created for all 5 environments
* Changes tested on DEV
* Changes tested on QA
* Staging state synced and {{terraform plan}} clean
* Prod state synced and {{terraform plan}} clean
* BCES-IQ state synced and {{terraform plan}} clean
* Verified both PROD environments after deployment

📄 Reference: [Cross-System Validation Guide|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENGR/pages/102826000]

Z University: Side menu folders not loading — backend only returns pages, not folders
column41:
h3. Impact

The Z University page at {{/z-university}} does not display folder navigation in the sidebar. Users only see the "Welcome to Z University" page. All previously visible folders are missing.

h3. Expected behaviour

The sidebar should show the complete folder/page hierarchy from Confluence. This includes expandable folders with nested pages and articles.

h3. Actual behaviour

The current behaviour shows only the root-level page, "Welcome to Z University." The sidebar does not display any folders.

h3. Steps to reproduce

* Navigate to {{[https://acme.egalvanic.ai/z-university}}.|https://acme.dev.egalvanic.ai/z-university%7D%7D.]
* Observe that the sidebar only shows "Welcome to Z University" and no folders.
* Check the browser Network tab: {{GET /api/z-university/pages}} returns only 1 item with no folders.

h3. Environment

The bug is present in the Z University page environment.

h3. Workarounds

N/A

h3. Other information

* The {{/api/z-university/pages}} endpoint returns HTTP 200 with only 1 item.
* The frontend is functioning correctly, rendering whatever the API returns.
* Proposed fix involves using Confluence v2's {{direct-children}} endpoints to retrieve all content types, including folders. Relevant endpoints include:
** {{GET /wiki/api/v2/pages/{id}/direct-children}}
** {{GET /wiki/api/v2/folders/{id}/direct-children}}
** {{GET /wiki/api/v2/folders/{id}/descendants}}
* Affected files include:
** {{eg-pz-backend/app/services/confluence_service.py}} — {{build_page_hierarchy()}} and {{get_space_pages()}}
** {{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/z_university_routes.py}} — route handler (no changes needed)

iOS: Table cell tap area limited to image, label, and indicator - entire cell should be tappable
column41:
h3. Impact

* Poor user experience due to limited tap areas.
* Accessibility issues with reduced tap target size.
* Inconsistency with iOS Human Interface Guidelines.
* Potential impact on multiple list views in the app.

h3. Expected behaviour

The entire cell should be tappable. Users should be able to tap anywhere within the cell to trigger actions like navigation or selection.

h3. Actual behaviour

Table/list cells in the iOS app only respond to taps on:

* The cell's image icon
* The cell's text label
* The right-side disclosure indicator  
Taps in the empty space around these elements do not trigger any actions.

h3. Steps to reproduce

* Open the app and navigate to any list view (Assets, Connections, Issues, etc.).
* Attempt to tap in the empty space between the label and the trailing indicator.
* Observe that nothing happens.
* Tap directly on the label or icon.
* Observe that the action triggers correctly.

h3. Environment

N/A

h3. Workarounds

N/A

h3. Other information

Affected areas include:

* Asset list views (AssetRow.swift, AssetsTabView.swift)
* Connection lists (ConnectionRow.swift, ConnectionsTabView.swift)
* Issue lists (UnifiedIssueListView.swift)
* Session lists (IRSessionView.swift)
* Task lists (UnifiedTaskListView.swift)
* Entity list views (EntityListView.swift)

*Technical Context:*

* Root Cause: Issues with nested Button or .onTapGesture modifiers, lack of .contentShape(Rectangle()), and use of Spacer() elements.

*Recommended Fix:*

{code:swift}// ❌ INCORRECT - Only label is tappable  
HStack {  
    Image(systemName: "icon")  
    Text("Cell Title")  
        .onTapGesture {  
            // navigate  
        }  
    Spacer()  
    Image(systemName: "chevron.right")  
}

// ✅ CORRECT - Entire cell is tappable  
HStack {  
    Image(systemName: "icon")  
    Text("Cell Title")  
    Spacer()  
    Image(systemName: "chevron.right")  
}  
.contentShape(Rectangle())  
.onTapGesture {  
    // navigate  
}  {code}

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* All table/list cells respond to taps anywhere within the cell bounds.
* Tap target area extends to the full height and width of the cell.
* Visual feedback shows for the entire cell when tapped.
* Solution is implemented consistently across all list views.
* Testing on various device sizes for consistent behavior.
* Accessibility testing confirms improved tap target sizes.

Z | Automation | Implement QA automation for offline-to-online sync validation
column41:
Implement QA automation test coverage for the offline-to-online data synchronization workflow. This validates that records created or modified while the application is in offline mode are correctly synced to the server when connectivity is restored, with no data loss, duplication, or corruption.

The sync validation must cover all entity types that support offline creation and editing, confirming that each record reaches the server with the correct field values, relationships, and attachments intact.

*Scope of Test Case Automation:*

* Validate that offline-created records (jobs, tasks, issues, assets, photos) sync successfully when the device transitions back to online
* Validate that field values, relationships, and attachments are preserved without corruption or truncation after sync
* Validate that duplicate records are not created during the sync process
* Validate sync conflict resolution when the same record has been modified both offline and online
* Validate sync status indicators accurately reflect pending, in-progress, completed, and failed sync states
* Validate that sync failures surface actionable error messages and support retry

----

*Acceptance Criteria*

* Records created offline for each supported entity type (jobs, tasks, issues, assets, photos) are present on the server after sync completes
* All field values, parent-child relationships, and attached media match the offline-created state after sync
* No duplicate records are produced when syncing offline-created data
* Sync conflict scenarios where the same record was modified offline and online are resolved without silent data loss
* Sync status indicators transition correctly through pending, in-progress, completed, and failed states
* Failed sync operations display a specific error message and can be retried without re-creating the record
* The test suite can simulate offline-to-online transitions and validate sync outcomes deterministically

column1: Z | Automation | Implement QA automation for Site Visits/Jobs module test cases
column41:
Implement end-to-end QA automation test coverage for the Site Visits/Jobs module. This covers the core workflows a field user performs when conducting site visits, including job lifecycle management, photo capture, QR-based actions, location handling, task and issue management, and asset operations.

The automation must validate each feature area through deterministic, repeatable test cases that can run as part of the CI/CD pipeline or on-demand regression suite.

*Scope of Test Case Automation:*

* *Create and Activate Jobs* — Validate job creation with required fields, job activation flow, and status transitions
* *IR Photos* — Validate IR photo capture, association to the correct job/asset, and upload persistence
* *Quick QR Action* — Validate QR code scan triggers the correct contextual action and navigates to the expected entity
* *Locations* — Validate location selection, assignment to jobs, and location-based filtering
* *Create and Manage Tasks* — Validate task creation within a job, task assignment, status updates, and completion
* *Issues* — Validate issue creation, severity assignment, linking to jobs/assets, and resolution workflow
* *Assets, Existing and New Asset (Quick Count and Photo Walkthrough)* — Validate selecting existing assets, creating new assets inline, performing quick count operations, and completing photo walkthrough sequences

----

*Acceptance Criteria*

* Automated tests cover job creation with all required fields and activation through status transitions
* IR photo capture, upload, and association to the parent job/asset are validated end-to-end
* Quick QR action triggers the correct navigation and contextual operation for the scanned entity
* Location selection and assignment to a job are validated, including location-based filtering
* Task creation, assignment, status update, and completion within a job context are validated
* Issue creation with severity, linking to a job or asset, and resolution workflow are validated
* Existing asset selection, new asset creation, quick count execution, and photo walkthrough completion are each covered by independent test cases
* All tests produce clear pass/fail results with descriptive failure messages identifying the specific assertion that failed
* Test suite can be executed independently per feature area or as a full Site Visits/Jobs regression run

column1: Z | Automation | Implement S3 bucket policy drift validation test automation across all environments
column41:
Following the spike investigation in ZP-774, this story implements automated test case execution that validates S3 bucket policies across all environments against a predefined expected policy baseline. The goal is to detect infrastructure configuration drift where manual changes in the AWS console diverge from the intended Terraform-managed state.

The validation covers 40 S3 buckets across 5 environments (eg-pz-dev, eg-pz-qa, eg-pz-staging, eg-pz-prod, bces-iq-prod), each containing 8 buckets scoped to functional domains: asset-photos, branding, ir-photos, frontend, onboarding-jobs, attachments, reporting-jobs, and slds.

For each bucket, the test retrieves the current bucket policy via {{aws s3api get-bucket-policy}}, compares it against the expected policy definition (including Statement Sids, Effect, Principal, Action, and Resource), and reports a failure with a detailed reason when a mismatch is detected.

This directly addresses the risk of Terraform plans silently overwriting manual fixes, security configurations becoming inconsistent, and undocumented changes causing production debugging overhead.

----

*Acceptance Criteria*

* The test suite retrieves the live bucket policy for all 40 S3 buckets across eg-pz-dev, eg-pz-qa, eg-pz-staging, eg-pz-prod, and bces-iq-prod environments
* Each bucket policy is compared against a stored expected policy baseline that defines the allowed Statements, Effects, Principals, Actions, and Resources
* When a bucket policy does not match the expected baseline, the test case fails and the failure output includes the bucket name, environment, and a human-readable diff describing the specific mismatch
* When a bucket policy matches the expected baseline, the test case passes
* The expected policy baseline is maintained in a version-controlled configuration file that can be updated when intentional policy changes are made
* The test suite can be executed independently per environment or across all environments in a single run
* The test suite exits with a non-zero exit code when one or more bucket policy validations fail

column1: iOS: Replace print() Statements with Structured Logging
column41:
h3. User Story

* As a developer, when I debug issues, I want structured logs with appropriate levels so that I can filter and understand what's happening.
* As a DevOps engineer, when I analyze crash reports, I want proper logging so that I can identify root causes.

h3. Context

The codebase uses {{print()}} statements throughout for debugging. This provides no log levels, no filtering, and makes it difficult to distinguish between debug output and important errors.

h3. Acceptance Criteria

* ✅ All {{print()}} statements replaced with proper {{slog()}} logging
* ✅ Log levels appropriately set (debug, info, warning, error)
* ✅ Sensitive information (tokens, passwords) not logged
* ✅ Consistent logging format across codebase

h3. Technical Implementation

{code:swift}// Before
print("User logged in: \\(username)")
print("Error: \\(error)")

// After
slog.info("User logged in", metadata: ["username": username])
slog.error("Authentication failed", metadata: ["error": error.localizedDescription]){code}

h3. Log Level Guidelines

* *Debug*: Detailed information for debugging (variable values, flow tracking)
* *Info*: General informational messages (user actions, state changes)
* *Warning*: Unexpected but handled situations (retries, fallbacks)
* *Error*: Error conditions that need attention (API failures, data corruption)

h3. Related Issue

* Relates to ZP-897 (iOS Architecture Refactor)

column1: iOS: Create Shared Utility Library for Common Patterns
iOS: Implement Localization Framework for Internationalization
column41:
h3. User Story

* As a user in a non-English speaking region, I want the app in my language so that I can use it comfortably.
* As a developer, when I add new UI text, I want to use localized strings so that the app can support multiple languages.
* As a product manager, when I update messaging, I want centralized string management so that changes are consistent.

h3. Context

The app has zero {{NSLocalizedString}} usage with 100+ hardcoded UI strings across 30+ files. There is no {{Localizable.strings}} file. This makes internationalization impossible and messaging updates difficult.

h3. Examples of Hardcoded Strings

* "Password is required"
* "Session Expired"
* "Invalid credentials"
* "Sign In"
* "Enter your password"

h3. Acceptance Criteria

* ✅ {{Localizable.strings}} file created with all UI strings
* ✅ All hardcoded UI strings replaced with {{NSLocalizedString()}}
* ✅ No hardcoded user-facing text remains in views
* ✅ String keys follow consistent naming convention (e.g., "login.password_required")

h3. Technical Implementation

{code:swift}// Create Localizable.strings
"login.password_required" = "Password is required";
"login.invalid_credentials" = "Invalid credentials";
"session.expired" = "Session Expired";
"auth.sign_in" = "Sign In";

// Replace hardcoded strings
// Before
Text("Password is required")

// After
Text(NSLocalizedString("login.password_required", comment: "Error when password field is empty")){code}

h3. Implementation Steps

# Audit all View files for hardcoded strings
# Create Localizable.strings with organized key structure
# Replace strings with NSLocalizedString() calls
# Add comments explaining context for translators

column1: iOS: Create Centralized Authorization Provider
column41:
h3. User Story

* As a developer, when I need to make authenticated API calls, I want a single authorization provider so that I don't duplicate Bearer token logic.
* As a maintainer, when authentication schemes change, I want a single place to update so that changes propagate consistently.

h3. Context

There are 14 separate instances of Bearer token authorization header setting across 8 files. This creates maintenance burden, inconsistent retry behavior, and a single point of failure if the auth scheme changes.

h3. Affected Files

* APIClient.swift
* AuthService.swift
* AppDelegate.swift
* AttachmentUploadService.swift
* S3PresignedURLService.swift
* VersionCheckService.swift
* (2 additional files)

h3. Acceptance Criteria

* ✅ Single {{AuthorizationProvider}} protocol/helper consolidates all Bearer token logic
* ✅ All services use centralized authorization provider (no direct Bearer token strings)
* ✅ Consistent token refresh/retry behavior across all services
* ✅ All existing API calls continue to work correctly

h3. Technical Implementation

{code:swift}// Create AuthorizationProvider protocol
protocol AuthorizationProvider {
    func authorizedRequest(from url: URL) async throws -> URLRequest
}

// Implement in APIClient
extension APIClient: AuthorizationProvider {
    func authorizedRequest(from url: URL) async throws -> URLRequest {
        var request = URLRequest(url: url)
        let token = try await AuthService.shared.getAccessToken()
        request.setValue("Bearer \\(token)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
        request.setValue(subdomain, forHTTPHeaderField: "X-Subdomain")
        return request
    }
}

// Use throughout codebase
let request = try await authProvider.authorizedRequest(from: url){code}

h3. Related Issue

* Relates to ZP-897 (iOS Architecture Refactor)

column1: iOS: Optimize Task Completion Calculations from O(n³) to O(n)
column41:
h3. User Story

* As a user, when I view session details with many tasks, I want the task completion statistics to load instantly so that I can quickly assess progress.
* As a developer, when I calculate task completion percentages, I want efficient algorithms so that the app remains responsive.

h3. Context

{{SessionDetailsTabViewModel}} has O(n³) complexity in task completion calculations. This causes significant UI freezes during rendering, especially for sessions with many tasks.

h3. Affected Files

* SessionDetailsTabViewModel.swift

h3. Acceptance Criteria

* ✅ Task completion calculations optimized from O(n³) to O(n) or better
* ✅ No UI freezes during task rendering
* ✅ Task completion percentages remain accurate after optimization

h3. Technical Implementation

* Identify nested loops causing O(n³) complexity
* Use hash maps/dictionaries for O(1) lookups instead of nested iterations
* Consider pre-computing aggregates during data fetch rather than on-demand
* Profile before/after with Instruments to verify improvement

h3. Related Issue

* Relates to ZP-897 (iOS Architecture Refactor)

column1: iOS: Optimize LocationHierarchyViewModel with Memoization
column41:
h3. User Story

* As a user, when I view location hierarchies, I want the UI to load instantly without freezing so that I can navigate efficiently.
* As a developer, when I access the hierarchy property multiple times, I want to avoid redundant O(n²) calculations so that the app performs efficiently.

h3. Context

{{LocationHierarchyViewModel.hierarchy}} is a computed property that recalculates the entire node hierarchy on every access. This causes UI freezes during rendering, especially with large location datasets.

h3. Affected Files

* LocationHierarchyViewModel.swift

h3. Acceptance Criteria

* ✅ LocationHierarchyViewModel implements memoization/caching for hierarchy calculations
* ✅ Hierarchy only recalculates when underlying node data changes
* ✅ No UI freezes when rendering location hierarchy views

h3. Technical Implementation

{code:swift}// Before (recalculates every access)
var hierarchy: [Building] {
    buildNodeHierarchy() // O(n²) computation
}

// After (cached)
private var cachedHierarchy: [Building]?
private var lastNodesHash: Int?

var hierarchy: [Building] {
    let currentHash = nodes.hashValue
    if cachedHierarchy == nil || lastNodesHash != currentHash {
        cachedHierarchy = buildNodeHierarchy()
        lastNodesHash = currentHash
    }
    return cachedHierarchy ?? []
}{code}

h3. Related Issue

* Relates to ZP-897 (iOS Architecture Refactor)

column1: iOS: Fix ViewModel Memory Leaks Causing App Hangs
column41:
h3. User Story

* As a user, when I use the app for extended periods, I want it to remain responsive without hanging so that I can complete my work efficiently.
* As a developer, when I write async code with DispatchQueue, I want to prevent retain cycles so that ViewModels and Views are properly deallocated.

h3. Context

25+ DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter closures are missing {{[weak self]}}, causing retain cycles. This prevents ViewModels and Views from being deallocated, leading to memory buildup and eventual app hangs (ZP-384).

h3. Affected Files

* PresignedPhotoImage.swift
* EntityCreationView.swift
* ReportStore.swift
* NetworkState.swift
* AppStateManager.swift
* SplashScreenView.swift
* AppDelegate.swift

h3. Acceptance Criteria

* ✅ All DispatchQueue closures use {{[weak self]}} where appropriate
* ✅ ViewModels properly deallocate when no longer needed
* ✅ ZP-384 app hang issue resolved (no more 5000ms+ hangs in Sentry)

h3. Technical Implementation

{code:swift}// Before (WRONG)
DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 0.5) {
    self.showAlert = true
}

// After (CORRECT)
DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 0.5) { [weak self] in
    guard let self = self else { return }
    self.showAlert = true
}{code}

h3. Related Issue

* Relates to ZP-897 (iOS Architecture Refactor)
* Fixes ZP-384 (App Hangs)

column1: Separate Agent API Lambda for Staging and Production Environments
column41:
_Current Issue:_
The Agent API (Lambda) is currently shared between staging and production environments, which violates environment isolation best practices.

_Current Setup:_
• Production Environment: https://acme.egalvanic.ai/
• Staging Environment: https://acme.stage.egalvanic.ai/
• _Shared Resource:_ Agent API (Lambda): https://bqitb0ghqa.execute-api.us-east-2.amazonaws.com

* Used by both staging and production for SLD chat sessions
* Located in AWS us-east-2 region

_Problem:_
Sharing the Agent API Lambda between environments creates risks:

* Staging testing can impact production stability
* Production traffic can affect staging performance
* Difficult to test Lambda changes safely
* Security and compliance concerns with mixed environment access

_Required Action:_
Either:

# Create separate Agent API Lambda instances for staging and production, OR
# Remove the Agent API dependency if it's not required

_Recommendation:_
Create dedicated Lambda endpoints:

* Production: Dedicated Agent API Lambda for production environment
* Staging: Dedicated Agent API Lambda for staging environment

This ensures proper environment isolation and allows safe testing without production impact.

FE Asset Issue - Add class-level config and data attributes with core attribute sequencing and option switch
column41:
Add configuration and data attributes at the class level for Asset Issues, introduce column parameters per core attribute, enforce index-based sequencing on core attributes, and introduce an option switch component.

*Scope:*

* *Class-level config & data attributes:* Extend the Asset Issue class to support a config attribute (governing display/behavior rules) and a data attribute (holding runtime values).
* *Columns parameter per core attribute (JSON + FE):* Each core attribute definition gains a columns parameter in its JSON schema, and the frontend renders attributes respecting this column layout.
* *Core attribute sequencing:* The frontend must render core attributes in the order defined by their index property rather than insertion or arbitrary order.
* *Option switch component:* Introduce a toggle/switch UI component as a new input type available for core attribute options within Asset Issues.

This work blocks ZP-828.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* [ ] Asset Issue class exposes a config attribute and a data attribute accessible at the class level
* [ ] Each core attribute includes a columns parameter in its JSON definition
* [ ] The frontend reads and applies the columns parameter when rendering core attributes
* [ ] Core attributes render in ascending index order on the Asset Issue form
* [ ] Changing a core attribute's index value changes its rendered position without requiring a page reload
* [ ] An option switch component is available as an input type for core attribute configuration
* [ ] The option switch component toggles between defined states and persists its value to the data attribute
* [ ] Existing Asset Issue workflows and single-column attribute layouts remain functional (backward compatibility)
* [ ] JSON schema changes pass validation and do not break existing stored Asset Issue data

column1: BE Audit and align Flask models with database schema across Dev, QA, and Prod environments
column41:
Flask ORM models have drifted from the actual database schema across Dev, QA, and Prod environments. Fields present in the database (e.g., {{modified_at}}, among others) are missing from the corresponding Flask model definitions, and potentially vice versa. This drift creates silent data handling failures, prevents proper serialization of database records, and introduces risk when running migrations or deploying across environments.

*Scope of Work:*

* Perform a comprehensive audit of every database table against its corresponding Flask/SQLAlchemy model definition in Dev, QA, and Prod
* Document all discrepancies: missing columns, mismatched types, missing defaults, missing nullable flags, and orphaned model fields with no backing column
* Update Flask models to accurately reflect the production database schema as the source of truth
* Verify that all ORM relationships, foreign keys, and constraints match the actual database constraints
* Ensure serialization layers (e.g., Marshmallow schemas, custom serializers) account for any newly surfaced fields
* Validate changes do not break existing API responses or internal service calls
* Confirm parity across all three environments after remediation

*Known Discrepancies (Non-Exhaustive):*

* {{modified_at}} column exists in database but is absent from Flask model
* Additional discrepancies to be cataloged during audit

column1: Z | Android | Preserve Unsaved Photos from Quick Count and Add Asset in Photo Diagnostics
column41:
h2. Overview

When a user captures photos during Quick Count or Add Asset flows but discards/cancels without saving, the captured photos are currently deleted from disk. Instead, these unsaved photos should be preserved on the filesystem and surfaced in the _Photo Diagnostics_ screen (Settings → Photo Diagnostics) so users can review, recover, or delete them.

h2. Current Behavior

# User opens Quick Count or Add Asset and captures one or more photos
# User presses Back or Cancel → "Discard Changes?" dialog appears
# User taps "Discard"
# {{StagedPhotoCache}} is cleared and {{discardStagedChanges()}} deletes the physical photo files from disk
# Photos are permanently lost — they do not appear anywhere in the app

h2. Expected Behavior

# User opens Quick Count or Add Asset and captures one or more photos
# User presses Back or Cancel → "Discard Changes?" dialog appears
# User taps "Discard"
# The entity changes are discarded, but the captured photo files are *preserved on disk*
# The photos appear in _Photo Diagnostics_ (Settings → Photo Diagnostics) as orphaned/unlinked photos
# Photos are displayed in *descending order* (most recent first) so the latest discarded photos appear at the top
# User can review, keep, save to gallery, or delete orphaned photos from Photo Diagnostics

h2. User Acceptance Tests (UATs)

* [ ] Photos captured during Quick Count that are discarded remain on disk after cancel
* [ ] Photos captured during Add Asset that are discarded remain on disk after cancel
* [ ] Discarded photos appear in Photo Diagnostics screen as orphaned/unlinked photos with "Not Linked" badge
* [ ] Photo Diagnostics displays all photos in descending order by file creation/modification date (most recent first)
* [ ] User can select and delete orphaned photos from Photo Diagnostics
* [ ] User can select and save orphaned photos to device gallery from Photo Diagnostics
* [ ] Tapping an orphaned photo shows the "Orphaned Photo" dialog with option to Delete or Keep
* [ ] Photos that are properly saved (user completes the flow) still behave normally (linked to entity, show as "Synced" or "Pending")
* [ ] No duplicate photos are created — if user captures the same photo and saves, it should not also appear as orphaned

h2. Execution Plan

h3. Phase 1: Modify Discard Behavior

*1.1 Update* {{PhotoManagementViewModel.discardStagedChanges()}}:

* Stop deleting physical photo files from disk when discarding staged changes
* Only clear the in-memory {{StagedPhotoCache}} and staged lists
* Remove the file deletion calls for staged photo additions

*1.2 Update* {{StagedPhotoCache}}:

* Ensure {{remove()}} and {{clear()}} only clean the in-memory cache without deleting files from the filesystem

h3. Phase 2: Photo Diagnostics Sort Order

*2.1 Update* {{PhotoDiagnosticsViewModel}}:

* Sort photo files in descending order by file modification date (most recent first)
* Ensure newly orphaned photos appear at the top of the list

*2.2 Update filesystem scan:*

* When scanning the {{photos}} directory, sort results by {{File.lastModified()}} descending

h3. Phase 3: Testing & Edge Cases

*3.1 Edge case handling:*

* Ensure photos saved successfully during a completed flow are not also listed as orphaned
* Verify storage usage calculation in Photo Diagnostics still accurate after preserving orphaned files
* Confirm bulk delete in Photo Diagnostics properly cleans up orphaned files from disk

h2. Technical Context

||File||Purpose||
|{{presentation/diagnostics/PhotoDiagnosticsViewModel.kt}}|Scans filesystem, identifies orphaned files|
|{{presentation/common/viewmodels/PhotoManagementViewModel.kt}}|Staging/discard logic for photo ops|
|{{StagedPhotoCache.kt}}|In-memory cache for unsaved photos|
|{{presentation/quickcount/QuickCountScreen.kt}}|Quick Count flow|
|Photo storage: {{context.filesDir/photos}}|Local photo directory|

column1: Z | Android | Scheduling
column41:
h2. Overview

Android clone of [ZP-732|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/browse/ZP-732] — implementing the scheduling/calendar feature for the Android mobile app.

h2. Problem Statement

Field technicians need to view their upcoming work schedule directly in the mobile app. The scheduling feature shows work blocks (scheduled time periods) associated with work order sessions, allowing users to see what work is planned, when, and at which site. Users should be able to tap a scheduled work block to navigate to that site.

h2. User Acceptance Tests (UATs)

* [ ] Home/Schedule screen displays a calendar with Day, Week, and Month view modes
* [ ] Calendar shows dot indicators on dates that have scheduled work blocks
* [ ] Day view shows work blocks as time-range blocks on an hourly grid (6 AM - 10 PM)
* [ ] Week view shows day headers with dot indicators and a list of work blocks for the selected day
* [ ] Month view shows a full month grid with dot indicators on dates with work blocks
* [ ] Work blocks display: session name, site name, time range, duration hours, and optional notes
* [ ] Time-based greeting shown (Good Morning/Afternoon/Evening) with user's name
* [ ] Upcoming work blocks grouped into: Today, This Week, Next Week, Later
* [ ] Tapping a work block for a different site prompts "Switch Site" confirmation
* [ ] Tapping a work block for the current site shows "You're already on this site"
* [ ] Schedule fetched from {{/user/schedule}} API endpoint when online
* [ ] Work blocks stored locally in Room database for offline access
* [ ] Work blocks from SLD sync (via {{get_sld_full_v2}} IR session work_blocks) are processed and stored
* [ ] Non-assigned work blocks (from other SLDs) use cached fields (session_name, sld_name, etc.)
* [ ] Schedule screen accessible from main navigation (Home tab or after login before site selection)
* [ ] After-login mode: schedule screen shown with logout option and ability to select a site from work blocks
* [ ] Calendar section is collapsible/expandable

h2. Execution Plan (Android)

h3. Phase 1: Data Layer

*1.1 Room Entity & DAO:*

* Create {{SessionWorkBlockEntity}} Room entity with fields: id, session_id, start_time, end_time, work_length, total_days, notes, created_at, modified_at, is_deleted, duration_hours, cached_session_id, cached_session_name, cached_session_description, cached_sld_id, cached_sld_name
* Create {{SessionWorkBlockDao}} with insert/upsert/query methods (query by date range, by session, non-deleted)
* Add database migration for the new table
* Add foreign key relationship to IRSession entity

*1.2 DTOs:*

column1: Z | Android | Add Assignment Type Enums (Reporter/Assignee/Watcher) to User-Session and User-Task Mappings
column41:
h2. Overview

Android clone of [ZP-714|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/browse/ZP-714] — implementing the mobile (Android) portion of the assignment type system with Reporter, Assignee, and Watcher roles.

h2. Problem Statement

The {{mapping_user_task}} and {{mapping_user_session}} tables now use proper assignment type semantics (reporter, assignee, watcher) on the backend and iOS. The Android app needs to:

* Parse and store {{user_session}} mappings from {{get_sld_full_v2}} sync response
* Populate {{owned_by}} and {{assigned_to}} arrays on IRSession entities from user-session mappings
* Filter work orders (IR sessions) by assignee for non-admin users
* Support the new {{mappingUserSession}} SyncTarget for offline create/update operations
* Handle both legacy ('owner') and new ('reporter') mapping types during transition

h2. User Acceptance Tests (UATs)

* [ ] IRSession Room entity has {{owned_by}} and {{assigned_to}} fields populated from user_session mappings during sync
* [ ] Work order list filters by assignee for non-admin users (default view shows assigned sessions only)
* [ ] Admin users see all work orders regardless of assignment
* [ ] New {{mappingUserSession}} SyncTarget handles offline create/update operations via SyncQueue
* [ ] Existing task "assigned to me" filter continues to work correctly (only assignee type = assigned)
* [ ] Backward compatible: Android handles both 'owner' and 'reporter' mapping types during transition
* [ ] {{MappingUserSession}} DTO and Room entity created with fields: id, user_id, session_id, mapping_type, is_deleted
* [ ] SLD sync service processes user-session mappings from {{get_sld_full_v2}} response mappings object
* [ ] API endpoints for {{create}} and {{update}} user-session mappings integrated via Retrofit

h2. Execution Plan (Android)

h3. Phase 1: Data Layer

*1.1 Room Entity & DAO:*

* Create {{MappingUserSessionEntity}} Room entity (id, user_id, session_id, mapping_type, is_deleted)
* Create {{MappingUserSessionDao}} with insert/update/query methods
* Add database migration to create {{mapping_user_session}} table
* Add {{owned_by}} and {{assigned_to}} text fields (JSON array of user IDs) to {{IRSessionEntity}}

*1.2 DTOs:*

* Create {{MappingUserSessionDTO}} data class for API response parsing
* Update {{SLDMappingsDTO}} to include {{user_session}} list
* Update {{IRSessionDTO}} if needed for work_blocks

*1.3 Repository:*

* Create {{MappingUserSessionRepository}} interface in domain layer
* Create {{MappingUserSessionRepositoryImpl}} in data layer

h3. Phase 2: Sync Infrastructure

*2.1 SyncTarget:*

* Add {{MAPPING_USER_SESSION}} to {{SyncTarget}} enum
* Update {{SyncQueueItem}} handling for the new target
* Add offline create/update support via SyncQueue

*2.2 SLD Sync Processing:*

* Update SLD sync service {{processMappings()}} to handle user-session mappings

h3. Phase 3: API Integration

*3.1 Retrofit Endpoints:*

column1: Re: possible sync issue
column41:
Customer reports a sync issue where asset counts differ between iPad and desktop, with some assets marked as "dead letter" after multiple sync attempts. They seek help to prevent future sync failures, possibly by offline syncing.

The core issue is a *foreign key violation* when inserting a row into the {{nodes}} table. Specifically:

The {{nodes}} row references {{default_photo_id = 31aa8bab-7e73-4a1e-bd0a-d9149612a33d}}, but that UUID doesn't exist in the {{photos}} table. The database enforces the {{fk_nodes_default_photo}} constraint, so the insert fails.

This typically happens because of an *ordering problem* — the code is trying to insert the node before its associated photo has been persisted. A few common causes:

# *The photo insert failed or was rolled back earlier*, but the node insert still proceeded with the now-orphaned photo ID.
# *ORM flush ordering* — SQLAlchemy's autoflush is firing the node INSERT before the photo INSERT has been flushed. The error message itself hints at this: _"consider using a_ {{session.no_autoflush}} _block if this flush is occurring prematurely."_ If the photo and node are being created in the same session, SQLAlchemy may not be inferring the correct dependency order.
# *The photo was created in a different transaction/session* that hasn't committed yet, or was deleted before this insert ran.

*Possible fixes:*

* Ensure the photo is explicitly flushed/committed before the node: {{session.flush()}} after creating the photo, before creating the node.
* Use {{session.no_autoflush}} to prevent premature flushing, then let the final {{commit()}} handle ordering.
* Check your SQLAlchemy {{relationship()}} definitions — if the relationship between {{Node}} and {{Photo}} is properly configured with the right {{foreign_keys}} and cascade settings, SQLAlchemy can usually figure out the correct flush order automatically.
* If the photo is being created asynchronously or in a separate service, make sure it's committed before the node creation is triggered.

*Error:*

  "error_code": "TRANSIENT_ERROR",
  "error_message": "Exceeded max retries (3): This Session's transaction has been rolled back due to a previous exception during flush. To begin a new transaction with this Session, first issue Session.rollback(). Original exception was: (raised as a result of Query-invoked autoflush; consider using a session.no_autoflush block if this flush is occurring prematurely)\n(psycopg2.errors.ForeignKeyViolation) * User can click refresh button to reload application with new version
* User can dismiss notification to continue with current session
* Notification does not interrupt active user workflows or unsaved changes
* Version detection occurs automatically without manual user action
* Notification appears only once per version update

column1: Z | Android | When an existing child asset is linked to WO on mobile, either it doesn't auto-pull in parent locally (Swift Issue, Dev) or it doesn't pull in parent at all (Backend Issue, Prod)
column41:
h2. *Feature Summary*

When a child asset is created or linked to a work order/session, the system will now automatically link its parent node and all ancestor nodes to the session.
This ensures the complete asset hierarchy is always visible in the session’s asset list.

----

h2. *Problem Being Solved*

Previously, when users added a child node to a session, only the selected child was linked.
Parent and ancestor nodes were not included, causing an incomplete hierarchy and breaking the visual structure.

----

h2. *Technical Implementation Details*

h3. *1. Core Logic – Walk Up Parent Hierarchy*

*Pseudocode:*

{noformat}function linkNodeAndAncestorsToSession(node, session):
    nodesToLink = [node]

    // Add immediate parent
    if node.parent exists:
        nodesToLink.append(node.parent)

    // Walk up hierarchy
    currentNode = node.parent
    while currentNode.parent_id != null:
        ancestorNode = findNode(currentNode.parent_id)
        if ancestorNode exists and not deleted:
            nodesToLink.append(ancestorNode)
            currentNode = ancestorNode
        else:
            break

    // Link each node (avoid duplicates)
    for nodeToLink in nodesToLink:
        if not session.nodes.contains(nodeToLink):
            linkSingleNodeToSession(nodeToLink, session)
{noformat}

----

h3. *2. Files / Screens Affected*

||Area / File||Purpose||

column41:
*Steps to Reproduce*

# Log into the Cochran instance
# Navigate to any site's connection configuration
# Review the Source and Target connection mappings
# Compare against expected connection direction

*Expected Behavior*
Source connections should point to the originating node and Target connections should point to the destination node, reflecting the correct directional relationship between connected entities.

*Actual Behavior*
Source and Target connections are inverted across all sites in the Cochran instance. Every site exhibits this flipped mapping, indicating a systematic data issue rather than isolated misconfiguration.

*Impact*
This affects the entire Cochran customer instance and likely causes incorrect data representation in any workflows, reports, or visualizations that rely on connection directionality. Customer testing has been blocked by this issue. A database update is required to flip all affected nodes to restore correct Source/Target relationships.

column1: BE Store SLD view snapshots for user position restoration
column41:
Enable users to explicitly save and restore their SLD (Single Line Diagram) view state, similar to save-state functionality in gaming. Users can manually capture up to 3-5 view positions per SLD, allowing quick restoration of frequently used views without manual navigation.

This feature addresses the friction users experience when repeatedly navigating to specific diagram positions during multi-session work or when switching between different areas of complex diagrams. By allowing users to save meaningful view states on demand, they can bookmark important contexts and resume work instantly.

*Scope:*

* User-initiated save action to capture current view state
* Storage of up to 5 snapshots per user per SLD
* Quick-access restoration UI for saved positions
* Snapshot metadata including timestamp and user-defined label

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* User can explicitly save current view state via dedicated "Save Snapshot" action
* Each user can store up to 5 snapshots per SLD diagram
* User receives feedback when snapshot limit is reached and must delete existing snapshot to save new one
* Snapshot captures viewport coordinates, zoom level, and selected element IDs
* User can assign a custom label/name when saving a snapshot
* User can restore any saved snapshot with single click from snapshot panel
* Snapshots persist across browser sessions
* User can rename or delete individual snapshots
* Snapshot list displays user-defined label, timestamp, and thumbnail preview for each saved position

column1: BE Implement database-level semaphore locking for SLD view concurrent write access
column41:
h3. User story

* As a user, when I attempt to edit an SLD view, I want to acquire write access so that I can make changes without conflicts.
* As a user, when I see that another session holds the lock, I want to know who holds it so that I can decide whether to request a transfer.

h3. Context

Multiple users can currently edit the same SLD view at the same time. This can lead to data conflicts and inconsistent states. A database-level semaphore locking mechanism will be implemented to ensure that only one session can write at a time.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* Only one session can hold write access to a specific SLD view at any time.
* All sessions without write access operate in read-only mode.
* Every mutation attempt validates write access in real-time before execution.
* Attempting a write operation without holding the lock returns an error and does not persist changes.
* Users can see who currently holds the lock, including:
** User name
** Role
** Device type
** Browser
** Timestamp of acquisition
* A single user may have multiple concurrent sessions, and each session is tracked independently.
* Lock holder information is visible to all users viewing the SLD.
* Users can request write access via the existing lock button.
* If another session holds the lock, the requesting user sees who holds it and can request transfer.
* Lock transfer requires explicit confirmation from the current holder or admin override capability.
* Upon acquiring write access, the SLD state automatically synchronizes to reflect the latest persisted data.
* Stale sessions are detected via heartbeat timeout and handled gracefully.
* When a session becomes stale, the lock is automatically released.
* Users are notified when their lock is released due to session timeout.
* Closing the browser or tab releases the lock.
* The {{sld_lock_audit_log}} table captures all lock acquisitions historically with:
** User
** Role
** Device
** Browser
** SLD view ID
** Lock acquired timestamp
** Lock released timestamp
** Release reason
* The {{sld_active_locks}} table maintains current lock state with heartbeat tracking for stale detection.

h3. Other information

h4. Technical Requirements

*New Database Table: sld_lock_audit_log*

||Column||Type||Description||
|id|UUID / BIGINT|Primary key|
|sld_view_id|UUID / FK|Reference to the SLD view|
|user_id|UUID / FK|User who acquired the lock|
|user_name|VARCHAR|Denormalized for historical reference|
|role|VARCHAR|Role active during the session (Tech, Admin, PM, etc.)|
|device_type|VARCHAR|Mobile, Desktop, Tablet|
|browser|VARCHAR|Browser name and version|
|session_id|VARCHAR|Unique session identifier|
|lock_acquired_at|TIMESTAMP|When lock was acquired|
|lock_released_at|TIMESTAMP|When lock was released (nullable if active)|
|release_reason|ENUM|manual_release, transfer, session_timeout, admin_override, browser_closed|
|created_at|TIMESTAMP|Record creation timestamp|

*Active Lock Table: sld_active_locks*

||Column||Type||Description||
|sld_view_id|UUID / PK|One active lock per SLD view|
|user_id|UUID / FK|Current lock holder|
|user_name|VARCHAR|Display name|
|role|VARCHAR|Active role|
|device_type|VARCHAR|Device type|
|browser|VARCHAR|Browser info|
|session_id|VARCHAR|Session identifier|
|acquired_at|TIMESTAMP|Lock acquisition time|
|last_heartbeat|TIMESTAMP|Last activity timestamp for stale detection|

*API Endpoints*

||Endpoint||Method||Description||
|/sld/{id}/lock|POST|Request/acquire write access|
|/sld/{id}/lock|DELETE|Release write access|
|/sld/{id}/lock|GET|Get current lock holder info|
|/sld/{id}/lock/request-transfer|POST|Request lock transfer from current holder|
|/sld/{id}/lock/heartbeat|POST|Keep session alive|

*Client-Side Behavior*

* Reuse existing lock button to toggle lock acquisition/release.
* Lock button displays current holder info when locked by another session.
* Implement heartbeat mechanism (suggested interval: 30 seconds).
* Session fingerprinting to capture device type and browser info.
* Auto-sync SLD state from server when acquiring lock.

*Out of Scope*

* Collaborative real-time editing (multiple simultaneous writers).
* Lock queue/waitlist functionality.
* Scheduled/timed lock reservations.

*Dependencies*

* Database migration for new tables.
* WebSocket or polling mechanism for real-time lock status updates (if not already in place).

column41:
h2. *Feature Summary*

When a child asset is created or linked to a work order/session, the system will now automatically link its parent node and all ancestor nodes to the session.
This ensures the complete asset hierarchy is always visible in the session’s asset list.

----

h2. *Problem Being Solved*

Previously, when users added a child node to a session, only the selected child was linked.
Parent and ancestor nodes were not included, causing an incomplete hierarchy and breaking the visual structure.

----

h2. *Technical Implementation Details*

h3. *1. Core Logic – Walk Up Parent Hierarchy*

*Pseudocode:*

{noformat}function linkNodeAndAncestorsToSession(node, session):
    nodesToLink = [node]

    // Add immediate parent
    if node.parent exists:
        nodesToLink.append(node.parent)

    // Walk up hierarchy
    currentNode = node.parent
    while currentNode.parent_id != null:
        ancestorNode = findNode(currentNode.parent_id)
        if ancestorNode exists and not deleted:
            nodesToLink.append(ancestorNode)
            currentNode = ancestorNode
        else:
            break

    // Link each node (avoid duplicates)
    for nodeToLink in nodesToLink:
        if not session.nodes.contains(nodeToLink):
            linkSingleNodeToSession(nodeToLink, session)
{noformat}

----

|CreateChildNode|Link newly created child + its ancestors|
|LinkExistingNode|Link existing child + ancestor hierarchy|

column1: FE Add client user creation workflow from account management section
column41:
As a platform administrator, I need the ability to create client users directly from the accounts section so that user provisioning is contextually tied to the account and reduces the chance of configuration errors.

This feature introduces an additional pathway for creating client users. The existing user creation workflow remains available and unchanged. The new workflow provides a safer, more streamlined option when the administrator is already working within an account context.

The new workflow enables administrators to:

* Initiate client user creation from within an account's detail view
* Select an appropriate client role (Facility Manager or Client Portal) during creation
* Assign one or more sites under that account as part of the same workflow

Because the account context is already established, the risk of associating a client user with incorrect sites or accounts is minimized.

Additionally, the account detail view should display all existing users who have client role access and are associated with sites under that account, providing a consolidated view of account-level user access regardless of how those users were originally created.

h3. Acceptance Criteria

* A "Create Client User" action is available from the account detail view
* The user creation form displays only client-accessible roles (Facility Manager, Client Portal)
* The site selection field is scoped to sites belonging to the current account
* At least one site must be selected before the user can be created
* The account is automatically associated with the new user without manual selection
* The existing user creation workflow remains functional and unchanged
* The account detail view displays a list of all users with client roles who have access to sites under that account
* Existing client users shown under the account link to their user detail view
* Form validation prevents submission with missing required fields
* Successful user creation displays confirmation and the new user appears in the account's user list

column1: BE RFQ Report generation with asset nameplate data and photos for third-party quoting
column41:
Electrical contractors need to request cost estimates from third-party vendors for specialized work such as circuit breaker maintenance. To facilitate this, the system must generate an RFQ (Request for Quote) Report that can be shared externally.

The report will be generated from an existing quote and will contain a paginated view of all assets included in that quote. Each asset will occupy a single page and display the asset's nameplate data, profile photo, and nameplate photo. This enables third-party vendors to accurately assess the scope and provide pricing without requiring site visits or additional data gathering.

*Scope:*

* Report generation from quote context
* One asset per page layout
* Nameplate data rendering
* Profile photo and nameplate photo inclusion
* PDF export for external distribution

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* User can generate an RFQ Report from any quote containing assets
* Report renders one asset per page
* Each page displays the asset's nameplate data fields
* Each page displays the asset's profile photo when available
* Each page displays the asset's nameplate photo when available
* Report exports as a downloadable PDF
* Assets without photos display a placeholder or omit the photo section gracefully
* Report includes all assets currently associated with the quote

Automatically determine and populate the thermal classification for each anomaly based on the NETA MTS (Maintenance Testing Specifications) table. The system should also auto-populate the corresponding Recommended Corrective Action based on the classification level.

This automation reduces manual entry, ensures consistency with industry standards, and minimizes classification errors.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* Thermal classification is automatically calculated based on temperature differential per NETA MTS guidelines
* Recommended Corrective Action auto-populates based on the determined classification level
* Auto-populated values are displayed in the appropriate report sections
* Users can override auto-populated values if manual adjustment is required
* Classification logic handles edge cases at threshold boundaries correctly

[BE] Fix parent node linking in bulk task creation
column41:
h1. {color:#ff5630}*REALTED COMMIT:* {color}{color:#ff5630}c532781{color}

h2. Steps to Reproduce

# Open a session
# Click Add Tasks → Bulk Add → Class-Specific
# Select a procedure (e.g., circuit breaker → low voltage breaker de-energized testing)
# Complete bulk task creation
# Query database for MappingNodeSession entries for this session

h2. Expected Behavior

Both the child node (circuit breaker) AND all parent nodes in the hierarchy should have MappingNodeSession entries linking them to the session.

h2. Actual Behavior

Only the child node gets linked to the session. Parent nodes are NOT linked, breaking the node hierarchy visibility in the session.

h2. Impact

Parent nodes do not appear in session asset views, making it difficult to understand the asset hierarchy context for tasks created via bulk add.

h2. Technical Documentation

h3. Root Cause

The bulk task creation endpoints in bulk_task_routes.py create MappingNodeSession entries directly without using the MappingController methods that handle parent node traversal.

What exists but is not used:

* get_all_ancestor_ids(node_id) in mapping_controller.py:33-48 - recursively walks up parent hierarchy
* MappingController._ensure_node_session_mappings() - includes all ancestors
* MappingController.bulk_create_node_session_mappings() - includes ancestors and descendants

h3. Files to Modify

app/routes/bulk_task_routes.py - Three endpoints with 6 locations where MappingNodeSession is created directly:

* /bulk/create-tasks-from-procedure (lines ~265-272, ~350-356)
* /bulk/create-tasks-batch (lines ~465-472, ~550-557)
* /bulk/create-unary-tasks (lines ~666-673, ~790-797, ~888-895)

h3. Fix Approach

# Add import for get_all_ancestor_ids at top of bulk_task_routes.py
# Create helper function ensure_node_and_ancestors_linked_to_session(node_id, session_id) to avoid code duplication
# Replace all 6 direct MappingNodeSession creation blocks with calls to this helper

h3. Verification

Query to verify fix: SELECT n.name, n.id, n.parent_id, mns.session_id FROM mapping_node_session mns JOIN nodes n ON mns.node_id = n.id WHERE mns.session_id = YOUR_SESSION_ID AND mns.is_deleted = false;

Verify both the circuit breaker AND its parent nodes appear in results.

column1: [FE] Dashboard components incorrectly coupled to SLD state
column41:
h2. Steps to Reproduce

# Navigate to Arc Flash Readiness dashboard (/arc-flash) or Condition Assessment dashboard (/pm-readiness)
# Note the data loads correctly
# Switch to the SLD view (same facility)
# Interact with the SLD: drag a node, edit a property, add/remove nodes from a view
# Navigate back to the dashboard

h2. Expected Behavior

The dashboard should display its data without re-loading or showing loading spinners. Dashboards should be independent of SLD visualization state changes.

h2. Actual Behavior

The dashboards re-trigger their data loading functions when SLD state changes, causing:

* Unnecessary API calls or re-renders
* Loading spinners appearing during unrelated SLD interactions
* Potential stale data display during transitions

h2. Impact

Poor user experience with unnecessary loading states and potential performance degradation from redundant API calls.

h2. Technical Documentation

h3. Root Cause

Both dashboards have sldNodes and sldEdges in their useEffect dependency arrays:

*NFPA70EDashboard.jsx:187-189:* useEffect depends on [sldId, sldNodes, sldEdges, getCurrentUserId]

*PMReadinessDashboard.jsx:309-311:* useEffect depends on [sldId, sldNodes, getCurrentUserId]

h3. Solution

Remove sldNodes, sldEdges, and getCurrentUserId from the useEffect dependencies. The dashboards should:

# Only re-fetch when sldId changes (user selects a different facility)
# Fetch their own data independently via the API
# Not reactively couple to the SLD visualization state

h3. Files to Modify

*1. eg-pz-frontend/src/components/NFPA70EDashboard.jsx*

* Change useEffect dependency array from [sldId, sldNodes, sldEdges, getCurrentUserId] to [sldId]
* Modify loadOverviewData() to always fetch from API instead of reading from store
* Remove sldNodes and sldEdges from useUIStore destructure

*2. eg-pz-frontend/src/components/PMReadinessDashboard.jsx*

* Change useEffect dependency array from [sldId, sldNodes, getCurrentUserId] to [sldId]
* Modify loadData() to always fetch from API instead of reading from store
* Remove sldNodes from useUIStore destructure

h3. Verification

# Navigate to Arc Flash Readiness dashboard (/arc-flash)
# Verify data loads correctly
# Switch to the SLD view
# Edit a node (e.g., change a property)
# Navigate back to Arc Flash Readiness
# Verify it does not show loading spinner unnecessarily
# Repeat for Condition Assessment dashboard (/pm-readiness)

column41:
h3. Impact

Users are experiencing issues where certain assets do not appear nested under parent nodes in the assets table of front-end work orders. This is due to the session_id being set directly as a foreign key.

h3. Expected behaviour

The system should fetch issues from both the mapping table and the direct foreign key. It should display all relevant assets correctly nested under their respective parent nodes.

h3. Actual behaviour

Currently, the system only fetches issues via the mapping_issue_session. As a result, issues that have their session_id set directly show as "Not linked to any Asset(s)" in the grid.

h3. Steps to reproduce

* Access the {{/ir_session/{id}/assets}} endpoint.
* Set the Issue.session_id directly.
* Observe the assets in the grid.

h3. Environment

N/A

h3. Workarounds

N/A

h3. Other information

The endpoint was only fetching issues via mapping_issue_session before the update. Now it fetches from both sources and deduplicates by id.

column1: Z| BE | Add validation for required `type` field in Photo creation endpoints
column41:
h3. *Problem*

Photos are being created with {{NULL}} values in the *type* field due to missing validation in the photo creation endpoints. This results in issues on the iOS app when switching sides.

----

h3. *Root Cause*

The {{type}} column in the *Photo* model ({{app/models/Photo.py}}) is *nullable by default*, as it does not include a {{nullable=False}} constraint or a default value:

{noformat}type = db.Column(db.String)  # No nullable=False, no default value
{noformat}

----

h3. *Affected Endpoints*

* {{create_photo()}} in {{app/controllers/photo_controller.py}}
* {{create_photo_with_date()}} in {{app/controllers/photo_controller.py}}

Both endpoints use {{type = data.get('type')}}, which becomes {{None}} when the field is not provided.

----

h3. *Acceptance Criteria*

* Validation must be added to the photo creation endpoints to require the *type* field.
* API should return String (as mobile accept the string - non optional).
* Add unit tests to confirm validation behavior.

column1: Release Z-University
Z | Android | Node per-node completion tracking and UI improvements
column41:
h2. Overview

Port iOS changes from commits 9ba368c and 4e049d5 to Android. These commits introduce per-node completion tracking for tasks and several UI improvements.

h2. Features to Implement

h3. 1. Task-Node Per-Node Completion Tracking

* Add {{is_completed}} field to task-node mapping DTO ({{MappingTaskNodeDTO}})
* Add {{nodeCompletions: Map<String, Boolean>}} to UserTask entity to track completion state per node
* Update {{MappingData}} to include {{isCompleted}} field for task-node mappings
* Update API client methods:
** {{createTaskNodeMapping()}} - add optional {{is_completed}} parameter
** {{updateTaskNodeMapping()}} - add optional {{is_completed}} parameter
* Update {{TaskMappingService}} to handle node completion updates
* Update sync service to persist per-node completion state from server

h3. 2. Issue Detail View Updates

* Change status options from ["Open", "In Progress", "Resolved", "Closed"] to ["Open", "Resolved"]
* Update status colors: Open/In Progress should use blue instead of red/orange
* Replace dropdown status picker with toggle button UI (Open/Resolved)
* Add dedicated "Resolution" section with segmented control buttons

h3. 3. Task Detail View Updates

* Move status toggle out of Details card
* Add new "Resolution" section with Pending/Complete segmented control buttons
* Consistent styling with Issue detail view

h3. 4. Location Hierarchy View Updates

* Replace IR photo completion ratio with task completion and issue resolution ratios
* Show indicators at building, floor, and room levels:
** Task completion: circle.fill (red if incomplete, green if all complete)
** Issue resolution: exclamationmark.triangle.fill (blue if unresolved, green if resolved)
* Update view models with new calculation methods:
** {{calculateBuildingTaskCompletionRatio()}}
** {{calculateBuildingIssueResolutionRatio()}}
** {{calculateFloorTaskCompletionRatio()}}
** {{calculateFloorIssueResolutionRatio()}}
** {{calculateRoomTaskCompletionRatio()}}
** {{calculateRoomIssueResolutionRatio()}}

h3. 5. Session Views Updates

* Update SessionRoomDetailView and ViewModel with completion tracking
* Update IRSessionView and ViewModel
* Update SessionDetailsTab and ViewModel
* Update AssetsTab and IssuesTab

h2. iOS Reference

* Commits: 9ba368c, 4e049d5
* PR: #85
* Author: Eric Ehlert

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Per-node completion state syncs correctly with backend
* [ ] Task detail shows Resolution section with Pending/Complete buttons
* [ ] Issue detail shows Resolution section with Open/Resolved buttons
* [ ] Location hierarchy shows task and issue status indicators
* [ ] Offline changes to completion state sync when back online
