column1: Z | Android | Add confirmation dialog to mark linked assets as complete when completing a task
column41:
h2. Overview

When a user marks a task as complete, the app should provide an option to also mark the linked assets (nodes) as complete.

h2. Current Behavior

* User toggles task status from "Open" to "Resolved" via the status toggle button
* Task is marked complete with {{submitted_at}} timestamp
* Linked assets (nodes via {{linkedNodes}} relationship) remain unchanged
* No confirmation dialog is shown

h2. Proposed Behavior

When a user marks a task as complete:

# Show a confirmation dialog asking if the user also wants to mark linked assets as complete
# Dialog should display:
#* Count of linked assets (e.g., "This task has 5 linked assets")
#* Option to mark all linked assets as complete
#* Option to mark only the task as complete (skip assets)
#* Cancel option to abort the operation
# If user selects to mark assets complete, update the status of all {{linkedNodes}}
# This applies to both:
#* Status toggle in TaskDetailScreen
#* Swipe-to-complete action in task list

h2. Technical Context (Android)

*Relevant Files:*

* {{presentation/tasks/TaskDetailScreen.kt}} - Task detail view with status toggle
* {{domain/usecase/task/}} - Task-related use cases
* {{data/local/database/entity/UserTaskEntity.kt}} - Task entity
* {{data/local/database/entity/TaskNodeLinkEntity.kt}} - Task-Node link entity
* {{presentation/tasks/TaskListScreen.kt}} - Task list with swipe actions

*Data Relationships:*

* Tasks are linked to Nodes via {{TaskNodeLinkEntity}} junction table
* Nodes have their own completion/status tracking via {{NodeEntity}}

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Confirmation dialog appears when marking task as complete (if task has linked assets)
* [ ] Dialog shows count of linked assets
* [ ] User can choose to mark assets complete, skip assets, or cancel
* [ ] Both task detail toggle and swipe action trigger this flow
* [ ] Works in both online and offline modes (queues sync operations appropriately)
* [ ] If no linked assets exist, complete task without showing dialog

h2. UX Considerations

* Use {{GenericAlertDialog}} with {{DialogType.Confirmation}} for consistent styling
* Dialog should be clear and not disruptive for quick workflows
* Consider adding a "Don't ask again" preference for power users (future enhancement)
* Ensure swipe action flow remains smooth

h2. Related

Cloned from iOS ticket: ZP-809

column1: QA Validation Ticket: Task & Issue Resolution Feature ("finito")
column41:
New task and issue resolution workflow with per-node task completion tracking, allowing users to mark individual assets as complete within a task.
Test Scenarios

h4. Task Resolution UI

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|1|View pending task|Open an existing pending task|Resolution section shows "Pending" highlighted in blue|
|2|Complete a task|Tap "Complete" button|Button animates to green with checkmark icon, task marked complete|
|3|Revert completed task|Tap "Pending" on a completed task|Button animates to blue, task returns to pending|
|4|Verify persistence|Change status, navigate away, return|Status change persists correctly|

h4. Issue Resolution UI

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|5|View open issue|Open an existing open issue|Resolution section shows "Open" highlighted in blue|
|6|Resolve an issue|Tap "Resolved" button|Button animates to green, issue marked resolved|
|7|Reopen resolved issue|Tap "Open" on resolved issue|Button animates to blue, issue reopened|

h4. Per-Node Task Completion (Linked Assets)

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|8|View linked assets|Open task with multiple linked assets|Each asset displays with empty circle indicator|
|9|Mark single node complete|Complete one asset from session room view|That asset shows green checkmark; task remains pending|
|10|Auto-complete task|Mark ALL linked assets as complete|Task automatically marks as complete|
|11|Unmark node on completed task|Unmark one asset on a completed task|Task reverts to pending; that asset shows empty circle|

h4. Bulk Actions (Session Room)

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|12|Bulk complete tasks|Use "Mark Tasks Complete" action on a node|All tasks linked to that node in the session are marked complete|
|13|Bulk unmark tasks|Use "Unmark Tasks" action on a node|All task completions for that node are removed|
|14|Bulk resolve issues|Use "Resolve Issues" action on a node|All issues linked to that node in the session are resolved|
|15|Bulk reopen issues|Use "Reopen Issues" action on a node|All resolved issues for that node are reopened|

h4. Sync & Offline Behavior

||#||Test Case||Steps||Expected Result||
|16|Online sync|Change task/issue status while online|Changes sync immediately to server|
|17|Offline queue|Change task/issue status while offline|Changes queued locally, sync when back online|
|18|Offline node completion|Mark node complete while offline|Completion status queued and syncs correctly when online|

----

h3. UI/UX Validation

* Resolution buttons use consistent styling (blue for pending/open, green for complete/resolved)
* Checkmark icons display correctly for completed states
* Linked assets list shows node type icon, label, class name, and completion indicator
* Animations are smooth when toggling status

----

h3. Edge Cases

||#||Test Case||Expected Result||
|19|Task with no linked assets|Task can be completed directly without per-node tracking|
|20|Task with single linked asset|Completing that asset auto-completes the task|
|21|Deleted linked asset|Deleted assets excluded from completion calculation|

column1: Improvements to PDF exporting + add DXF support
column41:
h2. Description

Comprehensive improvements to PDF and DXF export functionality for SLD (Single Line Diagram) views. This feature enhances the visual fidelity of exported diagrams, adds DXF format support for CAD software compatibility, and improves performance for large diagrams through async processing.

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* PDF export uses Hershey vector fonts for resolution-independent text rendering
* Line widths and spacing scale proportionally with diagram zoom level
* Long labels truncate with ellipsis when they would overlap other elements
* Group labels move inside enclosure when edges would cross external label
* DXF export produces files that open correctly in AutoCAD and other CAD software
* Async PDF export with S3 polling prevents timeout errors on large diagrams
* Enclosure nodes can be toggled to simplified view for cleaner exports
* Omit text option available in export modal for schematic-only exports

h2. Technical Documentation

h3. Backend Changes

* app/services/pdf_export/dxf_canvas.py - New DXFCanvas adapter for visually-identical DXF export
* app/services/pdf_export_simple.py - Hershey vector fonts, proportional scaling, text truncation, label collision detection
* app/routes/pdf_export_routes.py - Async PDF export with S3 status polling
* app/routes/sld_view_routes.py - DXF export endpoint
* app/models/Node.py, app/models/Mappings.py - is_simplified field for enclosure nodes
* migrations/versions/b7c9d0e1f3a4_add_is_simplified_columns.py - Database migration

h3. Frontend Changes

* src/sld/src/components/ExportModal.jsx - DXF button, restructured layout, collapsible PDF options
* src/sld/src/components/GraphEditor.jsx - Keyboard shortcuts for node operations (T for simplify toggle)
* src/sld/src/components/NodeContextMenu.jsx - Simplify/Expand Enclosure menu option
* src/sld/src/hooks/useDagreLayout.jsx - Handle positioning for simplified groups
* src/sld/src/services/apiService.js - Async PDF polling, DXF export API calls

h3. Key Features

* Hershey vector fonts - Resolution-independent text that scales cleanly at any zoom
* Proportional scaling - All line widths, spacing, and padding scale with diagram size
* Smart label placement - Labels auto-truncate and move to avoid edge collisions
* DXF export - Uses DXFCanvas adapter to produce CAD-compatible files
* Async processing - Large diagrams export via S3 with polling to prevent timeouts
* Simplified enclosures - Toggle enclosure detail level for cleaner high-level views

column1: Allow admin users to soft-delete SLDs from web app
column41:
h2. Description

Admin users need the ability to soft-delete SLDs (Sites) from the admin web interface. The SLD model already has an is_deleted field - this feature adds the backend route/controller and frontend UI to enable deletion.

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* Admin users can delete an SLD from Admin > Sites page
* Delete button appears in row actions next to Edit button
* Confirmation dialog appears before deletion
* Deleted SLD no longer appears in Sites list
* SLD is soft-deleted (is_deleted = true) not hard-deleted

h2. Technical Documentation

h3. Current State

* SLD Model: Already has is_deleted field (line 69-73 in app/models/SLD.py)
* Backend: No delete route or controller method exists
* Frontend: SettingsSites.jsx has Create/Edit but no Delete button

h3. Implementation Plan

*1. Backend: Add Delete Controller Method*

File: eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/sld_controller.py

* Add delete_sld(sld_id) method that queries SLD by ID
* Sets is_deleted = True on the SLD
* Returns standard tuple (success, data/error, status_code)

*2. Backend: Add Delete Route*

File: eg-pz-backend/app/routes/sld_routes.py

* Add route: DELETE /sld/<uuid:sld_id>/delete
* Decorators: @require_auth, @require_permission("slds.manage"), @require_sld_access
* Calls SLDController.delete_sld(sld_id)

*3. Frontend: Add API Service Function*

File: eg-pz-frontend/src/services/mainAPIService.js

* Add deleteSLD(sldId) function
* Calls DELETE /sld/{sldId}/delete

*4. Frontend: Add Delete Button to Settings Sites*

File: eg-pz-frontend/src/components/SettingsSites.jsx

* Add delete button to row actions (next to Edit button)
* Use window.confirm() for confirmation (matching existing patterns)
* Call deleteSLD() on confirmation
* Refresh list on success, show error alert on failure

h3. Files to Modify

* eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/sld_controller.py - Add delete_sld() method
* eg-pz-backend/app/routes/sld_routes.py - Add DELETE route
* eg-pz-frontend/src/services/mainAPIService.js - Add deleteSLD() function
* eg-pz-frontend/src/components/SettingsSites.jsx - Add delete button with confirmation

column1: Z | Android | Serviceability field to add (Physical COM)
column41:
*Problem Statement*

The Android Asset forms currently lack a "Serviceability" field UI to capture the physical condition of maintenance (COM) label. Users need a way to select whether equipment is Serviceable, Limited-Service, or Non-Serviceable when adding or editing assets on the Android app.

Current Behavior:
• No serviceability spinner/dropdown exists in the Add Asset screen
• No serviceability spinner/dropdown exists in the Edit Asset screen
• Users cannot record the physical COM status on mobile

Expected Behavior:
• Serviceability dropdown available in Add Asset screen (optional)
• Serviceability dropdown available in Edit Asset screen
• Field should be clearly labeled with helper text explaining its purpose

----

*Solution*

Add a new optional "Serviceability" dropdown field to the Asset screens with the following options:
• Serviceable
• Limited-Service
• Non-Serviceable

UI Implementation:
• Add Spinner or ExposedDropdownMenu component for serviceability
• Label: "Serviceability" or "Condition of Maintenance"
• Helper text: "Physical COM label status (optional)"
• Position: Place in the asset details section alongside other optional fields

Scope of Changes:
• Add Asset Screen: Include serviceability dropdown
• Edit Asset Screen: Include serviceability dropdown with existing value pre-selected
• API Integration: Send serviceability value in create/update requests

----

*QA Validation Steps*

Test Case 1: Add Asset Screen UI

# Navigate to Add Asset screen
# Verify Serviceability dropdown is present
# Verify dropdown has options: (None), Serviceable, Limited-Service, Non-Serviceable
# Verify field is optional (no required indicator)
# Verify helper text is displayed

Test Case 2: Add Asset without Serviceability

# Fill in required asset fields only
# Leave Serviceability as None/empty
# Submit form
# Verify asset is created successfully

Test Case 3: Add Asset with Serviceability

# Fill in required asset fields
# Select a Serviceability value (e.g., "Limited-Service")
# Submit form
# Verify asset is created with correct serviceability value
# Edit the asset and verify value is pre-selected

Test Case 4: Edit Asset Serviceability

# Open an existing asset for editing
# Verify Serviceability field displays current value (or empty if not set)
# Change the value and save
# Reload and verify the new value persisted

Test Case 5: Clear Serviceability

# Edit an asset that has a serviceability value
# Clear the selection (set to None)
# Save the asset
# Verify the value is cleared

Test Case 6: Offline Sync

# Set serviceability value while offline
# Verify value syncs correctly when back online

Dependencies:
• Requires backend ticket ZP-790 to be completed first

column1: BE: Bulk EMP date recalculation extremely slow and creates duplicate workorders
column41:
h2. Steps to Reproduce

# Navigate to arcpointelectrical.egalvanic.ai
# Open quote "MP 2 - Rev 1"
# Go to Dataset tab
# Select 100+ rows with Arc Flash Audit procedures
# Click "Configure Dates" and change the due date
# Observe the operation taking very long and workorder fragmentation

h2. Expected Behavior

All 100+ Arc Flash Audit lines for Q3 2026 should be grouped into a single workorder named "Arc Flash Audit - Q3 2026". The operation should complete within a few seconds.

h2. Actual Behavior

153 lines went into one workorder, but 14 additional workorders were created with only 1 line each (all named "Arc Flash Audit - Q3 2026"). The operation is extremely slow due to N+1 queries, causing timeouts and retries that result in duplicate workorders.

h2. Impact

Customer cannot efficiently manage EMP dates in bulk. Workorder fragmentation makes scheduling unusable. Database performance degradation affects other users.

h2. Technical Documentation

h3. Root Cause Analysis

File: app/controllers/quote_controller.py, function: bulk_replace_emp_for_nodes_in_quote()

Performance Issues:

* Lines 516-527: N+1 query in empty workorder cleanup - separate COUNT query per workorder
* Lines 367, 476: db.session.flush() inside loops for each new workorder creation
* Lines 161-164: Individual Node.query.get() calls when clearing shortcuts

Workorder Fragmentation Issue:

* workorders_by_key = {} is built fresh per request
* If request times out and retries, each attempt creates new workorders
* No database-level deduplication by workorder name

h3. Implementation Plan

Fix 1: Replace N+1 cleanup query with single GROUP BY

{code:python}workorder_line_counts = db.session.query(
    PlannedWorkorder.id,
    func.count(PlannedWorkorderLine.id).label("line_count")
).outerjoin(
    PlannedWorkorderLine,
    (PlannedWorkorderLine.planned_workorder_id == PlannedWorkorder.id) &
    (PlannedWorkorderLine.is_deleted == False)
).filter(
    PlannedWorkorder.quote_id == quote_id,
    PlannedWorkorder.is_deleted == False
).group_by(PlannedWorkorder.id).all()

empty_wo_ids = [wo_id for wo_id, count in workorder_line_counts if count == 0]
if empty_wo_ids:
    PlannedWorkorder.query.filter(PlannedWorkorder.id.in_(empty_wo_ids)).delete(synchronize_session=False){code}

Fix 2: Batch workorder creation with deferred flush - collect all workorders, assign UUIDs upfront, single bulk_insert_mappings()

Fix 3: Bulk update nodes when clearing shortcuts

{code:python}Node.query.filter(Node.id.in_(node_ids)).update(
    {Node.applied_shortcut: None},
    synchronize_session=False
){code}

Fix 4: Query existing workorders by name for deduplication - add workorders_by_name lookup that persists across retries

h3. Files to Modify

* app/controllers/quote_controller.py (lines 97-540, ~80 lines modified)

column1: Add Rate Modifier to Quote Labor
column41:
h2. Description

Add a rate_modifier field (-100 to 100) to the quote labor system that adjusts the direct labor total by a percentage. This allows customers to apply markup or discount to labor costs without modifying base rates.

*Formula:* {{finalLaborCost = baseLaborCost * (1 + rate_modifier/100)}}

Examples:

* rate_modifier = 0 → no change
* rate_modifier = 10 → 10% markup
* rate_modifier = -15 → 15% discount
* rate_modifier = 100 → doubles the cost

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* Rate Modifier field appears in Time & Materials tab with range -100 to 100
* Setting modifier to 0% leaves labor total unchanged
* Positive values increase labor cost by percentage (e.g., 10% = 1.1x)
* Negative values decrease labor cost by percentage (e.g., -10% = 0.9x)
* Value persists after save and page reload
* Quote calculated_total reflects the rate modifier

h2. Technical Documentation

h3. 1. Database Migration

*Create:* {{eg-pz-backend/migrations/versions/<rev>_add_rate_modifier_to_quote_labor.py}}

{code:python}def upgrade():
    op.add_column('quote_labor', sa.Column(
        'rate_modifier',
        sa.Float(),
        nullable=False,
        server_default='0'
    ))

def downgrade():
    op.drop_column('quote_labor', 'rate_modifier'){code}

h3. 2. Backend Model

*Edit:* {{eg-pz-backend/app/models/QuoteLabor.py}}

column1: User Login Tracking + Weekly Email Report via SendGrid
column41:
h2. Problem Statement

User login activity is not tracked in the database. The {{users}} table has a {{last_login}} column but it's unmapped and never written to. There is no audit trail of login attempts (successful or failed), no visibility into who is actively using the platform, and no mechanism to email stakeholders a usage summary.

We need to record every login attempt in a new {{user_logins}} table, create a configurable email recipient system ({{emails}} + {{mapping_user_email}} tables), and send a weekly login summary email per company via SendGrid, triggered by AWS EventBridge.

----

h2. User Acceptance Tests (UATs)

* [ ] Successful login via {{/auth/login}} creates a row in {{user_logins}} with user_id, email, company_id, IP, user_agent, auth_flow, success=true
* [ ] Failed login creates a row with success=false and failure_reason populated
* [ ] Login tracking never blocks or slows the login response (fire-and-forget)
* [ ] {{emails}} table seeded with a "Weekly Login Summary" row (slug: weekly_login_summary)
* [ ] Admin can subscribe/unsubscribe users to email reports via API routes
* [ ] {{POST /email/trigger-weekly-report}} with correct secret header sends the weekly summary email via SendGrid to all subscribed users
* [ ] Weekly email contains: total logins, unique users, failed attempts, per-user breakdown table
* [ ] {{last_sent_at}} idempotency check prevents duplicate sends on retry
* [ ] IP address correctly captured from {{X-Forwarded-For}} (ALB) not {{request.remote_addr}}

----

h2. Execution Plan

*Backend only.* Implementation order:

# *Models*: {{UserLogin.py}}, {{Email.py}}, {{MappingUserEmail}} in Mappings.py, update {{__init__.py}}
# *Alembic migration*: create 3 tables + seed + indexes
# *Login tracking controller* + modify {{auth_routes.py}} (4 injection points: SRP success, USER_PASSWORD_AUTH success, ClientError, general exception)
# *Email service*: SendGrid wrapper ({{send_email}}, {{send_template_email}})
# *Login report service*: query + HTML render + send
# *Email routes* + register blueprint (9 endpoints)
# *Config updates*: {{requirements.txt}} (sendgrid), env vars (SENDGRID_API_KEY, SENDGRID_FROM_EMAIL, EMAIL_TRIGGER_SECRET)
# *AWS EventBridge rule*: {{cron(0 9 ? * MON *)}} for weekly trigger

h3. Database Tables

*user_logins*: id, user_id, email, company_id, ip_address, user_agent, auth_flow, subdomain, success, failure_reason, created_at, is_deleted

*emails*: id, name, slug, description, template_id, is_active, frequency, company_id, last_sent_at, created_at, modified_at, is_deleted

*mapping_user_email*: id, user_id, email_id, is_deleted, created_at, modified_at

h3. API Routes

||Method||Route||Auth||Description||
|GET|{{/email/types}}|@require_auth|List email types|
|POST|{{/email/types}}|@require_auth|Create email type|
|PUT|{{/email/types/<id>}}|@require_auth|Update email type|
|GET|{{/email/subscriptions}}|@require_auth|List subscribers|
|POST|{{/email/subscriptions}}|@require_auth|Subscribe user|
|PUT|{{/email/subscriptions/<id>}}|@require_auth|Unsubscribe|
|GET|{{/email/login-summary}}|@require_auth|Preview report|
|GET|{{/email/login-history}}|@require_auth|Paginated history|
|POST|{{/email/trigger-weekly-report}}|Secret header|EventBridge trigger|

column1: SLD Improvements
Disable browser popup blocker warning when exporting reports
column41:
h2. Problem

When exporting reports, the browser's popup blocker warning is triggered, requiring users to manually allow popups. This creates a poor user experience and interrupts the report export workflow.

h2. Expected Behavior

Report exports should complete without triggering the browser's popup blocker warning.

h2. Proposed Solution

Modify the report export mechanism to avoid triggering popup blockers. Common approaches include:

# *Direct download* - Use a hidden {{<a>}} element with {{download}} attribute and programmatically click it
# *Blob URL approach* - Create a Blob from the report data and trigger download via {{URL.createObjectURL()}}
# *User-initiated action* - Ensure {{window.open()}} (if needed) is called directly within the user's click event handler, not in an async callback
# *Fetch + download* - Fetch the report data first, then trigger a client-side download

h2. Technical Notes

* Browser popup blockers typically block {{window.open()}} calls that are not directly triggered by user interaction
* If {{window.open()}} is called inside a {{setTimeout}}, {{Promise.then()}}, or async callback, it will likely be blocked
* For file downloads, using the download attribute on anchor tags or Blob URLs is more reliable than {{window.open()}}
* The solution should work across Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge

h2. Reference

Video demonstration: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1oRxKqvRjqYspNuKV0t5sBTmN-uw-SFQq/view

column1: Add DXF file export option for SLD views
column41:
h2. Problem Statement

Users currently can export SLD views as PDFs, but cannot export the DXF files that are created during the PDF generation process. DXF (Drawing Exchange Format) is a CAD file format that engineers need to import SLD diagrams into AutoCAD and other CAD software for further editing and integration with existing drawings.

The backend already creates DXF documents as an intermediate step using the {{ezdxf}} library in {{SLDToPDFConverter}}, but this capability is not exposed to users. Adding DXF export would provide engineers with a standard CAD-compatible format for their electrical one-line diagrams.

h2. User Acceptance Tests (UATs)

* [ ] Users can see a "Export DXF" button in the Export modal alongside existing formats (PDF, SKM XML, JSON)
* [ ] Clicking "Export DXF" triggers an async export job and shows loading state
* [ ] Upon completion, users can download the DXF file
* [ ] DXF file opens correctly in AutoCAD or free DXF viewers
* [ ] DXF export respects view-specific positions when exporting from a view
* [ ] DXF export supports node selection (export subset of nodes)
* [ ] DXF file contains correct electrical symbols (transformers, breakers, motors, etc.)
* [ ] DXF export uses the same ARCH paper size options as PDF

h2. Execution Plan

h3. Architecture Decision

Use the existing {{SLDToPDFConverter}} class for DXF export because:

* It already creates DXF documents with {{ezdxf}} library
* Has full electrical blocks library (transformers, breakers, motors, etc.)
* DXF is its native intermediate format - just needs to be exposed

h3. Implementation Steps

*1. Backend: Add DXF Export Method*
File: {{eg-pz-backend/app/services/pdf_export/json_to_pdf_converter.py}}

Add {{export_to_dxf_bytes()}} method to {{SLDToPDFConverter}}:

{code:python}def export_to_dxf_bytes(self) -> bytes:
    """Export the DXF document to bytes."""
    if not self.node_positions:
        self.convert()

    buffer = io.BytesIO()
    self.doc.write(buffer)
    return buffer.getvalue(){code}

*2. Backend: Add DXF Upload Helper*
File: {{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/pdf_export_routes.py}}

Generalize {{_upload_pdf_to_s3}} to handle both formats with configurable content_type.

*3. Backend: Add DXF Export Routes*
File: {{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/pdf_export_routes.py}}

Add new routes:

* {{POST /sld/export/dxf/start}} - Start async DXF export job
* {{GET /sld/export/dxf/status/<job_id>}} - Get status (can reuse PDF status endpoint)

Add background job function {{_run_dxf_export_job()}} with same data fetching as PDF but using {{SLDToPDFConverter}} to generate DXF.

*4. Frontend: Update ExportModal*
File: {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/ExportModal.jsx}}

Add DXF export option in the "Other Formats" section:

* Add state variables for DXF export ({{exportingDxf}}, {{dxfDownloadUrl}}, etc.)
* Add "Export DXF" button next to SKM XML
* Use same async polling pattern as PDF

h3. Files to Modify

||File||Changes||
|{{eg-pz-backend/app/services/pdf_export/json_to_pdf_converter.py}}|Add {{export_to_dxf_bytes()}} method|
|{{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/pdf_export_routes.py}}|Add DXF routes, generalize upload helper|
|{{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/ExportModal.jsx}}|Add DXF export button and handlers|
|{{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/ExportModal.css}}|Add DXF-specific styling (optional)|

h3. API Specification

*Start DXF Export:*

{noformat}POST /sld/export/dxf/start
Request: { "sld_id": "uuid", "view_id": "uuid", "selected_node_ids": [...], "arch_size": "arch-d" }
Response: { "success": true, "job_id": "uuid" }{noformat}

*Check DXF Export Status:*

{noformat}GET /sld/export/dxf/status/<job_id>
Response: { "success": true, "status": "complete", "download_url": "...", "filename": "...", "node_count": 45, "edge_count": 38 }{noformat}

column1: Z | Automation | SPIKE | Infra Manual Configuration vs Terraform updates
column41:
*Purpose*

Investigate and identify a solution to detect when manual infrastructure configuration changes are made directly in AWS but not reflected in Terraform code. This creates configuration drift that can lead to unexpected behavior, security risks, and deployment issues.

----

*Problem Statement*

Currently, there is no automated way to detect when:
• Someone makes manual changes directly in AWS console or CLI
• Those changes are not captured in Terraform configuration files
• Infrastructure state drifts from the defined IaC (Infrastructure as Code)

This can result in:
• Terraform plans overwriting manual fixes unknowingly
• Security configurations being inconsistent
• Debugging issues caused by undocumented changes
• Loss of manual changes during next Terraform apply

----

*Spike Objectives*

# Research available tools and approaches for detecting Terraform drift:
• terraform plan drift detection
• AWS Config rules
• Third-party tools (Driftctl, Spacelift, env0, etc.)
• CloudTrail monitoring for manual changes
# Evaluate options based on:
• Ease of implementation
• Cost implications
• Integration with existing CI/CD pipelines
• Alerting capabilities
# Propose a recommended approach with:
• Implementation steps
• Estimated effort
• Ongoing maintenance requirements

----

*Expected Deliverables*

• Summary document of research findings
• Comparison matrix of evaluated tools/approaches
• Recommended solution with justification
• High-level implementation plan if solution is approved

column1: Z | FE | Add Checkbox to Include Sub-components in Maintenance Report

*Problem Statement*

Users currently have no control over whether sub-components appear in the Maintenance Report. A UI option is needed to allow users to choose whether to include sub-components.

• Users cannot exclude sub-components from reports

Expected Behavior:

• Default state: unchecked (sub-components excluded)
• When checked, sub-components are included in the report

----

*Solution*

Add Checkbox to ReportGenerationModal.jsx:
• Add new state: includeSubComponents (default: false)
• Add FormControlLabel with Checkbox component
• Label: "Include sub-components"
• Subtitle/helper text: "Show nested components under their parent assets"

Update Report Generation Payload:
• Include include_sub_components parameter in the options object passed to onGenerate
• Map checkbox state to API parameter

UI Placement:
• Add in the "Include Options" section alongside existing checkboxes
• Follows same styling pattern as other checkboxes in the modal

• src/components/ReportGenerationModal.jsx

Dependencies:

column1: Z | BE | Add Option to Exclude Sub-components from Maintenance Report
column41:
*Problem Statement*

Currently, the Maintenance Report includes all sub-components by default, which can make reports unnecessarily long and cluttered. The backend and Lambda need to support filtering sub-components based on a new parameter.

Current Behavior:
• All sub-components are automatically included in the Maintenance Report
• No backend parameter to control sub-component inclusion

Expected Behavior:
• Backend accepts a new parameter to control sub-component visibility
• When parameter is false (default), sub-components are excluded from the report
• When parameter is true, sub-components are included

----

*Solution*

Backend API Changes (reporting_routes.py):
• Accept new parameter: include_sub_components (default: false)
• Pass this parameter to the Step Function input payload

Lambda Changes (project-z-reporting-dataprep-lambda):
• Read include_sub_components from the event/input
• When false: filter out nodes that have a parent_id (sub-components)
• When true: include all nodes as before
• Ensure issues/tasks linked to excluded sub-components are handled appropriately

Files to Modify:
• Backend: app/routes/reporting_routes.py
• Lambda: project-z-reporting-dataprep-lambda/lambda_function.py

----

*QA Validation Steps*

Test Case 1: API Parameter Validation

# Call report generation API without include_sub_components parameter
# Verify it defaults to false
# Call API with include_sub_components: true
# Verify parameter is passed to Step Function

Test Case 2: Lambda Filtering (Sub-components Excluded)

# Trigger report generation with include_sub_components: false
# Verify dataprep Lambda filters out nodes with parent_id
# Verify only top-level assets appear in the generated data

Test Case 3: Lambda Filtering (Sub-components Included)

# Trigger report generation with include_sub_components: true
# Verify all nodes (including sub-components) are included
# Verify hierarchy is preserved

Edge Cases:
• Sub-component with its own issues - issues should be excluded when sub-component is excluded
• Multi-level nesting - all nested levels should be excluded when parameter is false
• Orphan issues (no node_id) - should always be included regardless of parameter

column1: Z | BE | Add Results Summary Page to Maintenance Report
column41:
*Problem Statement*

The Maintenance Report currently lacks a high-level summary that gives users a quick overview of inspection results. Users must manually count assets and issues to understand the scope and findings of the inspection.

Current Behavior:
• Report goes directly into detailed content without an executive summary
• No quick reference for total assets inspected or issue breakdown

Expected Behavior:
• Include a "Results Summary" section at the beginning of the report
• Display total assets evaluated
• Show issue counts grouped by type
• Indicate resolved vs open issues

----

*Solution*

Add a "Results Summary" page to the Maintenance Report that displays:

Summary Statistics:
• Total Items Inspected - Count of all assets/nodes evaluated in the session
• Total Issues Identified - Sum of all issues found during inspection
• Issues Resolved - Count of issues marked as resolved
• Open Issues - Count of issues still requiring attention

Issue Breakdown by Type:
• List each issue type with its count
• Example: "1 Thermal Anomaly, 2 NEC Violation, 1 NFPA 70B Violation"

Summary Statement:
• "In total, the thermographer inspected X items, and in so doing, identified Y issues of the following types: [list]"
• "Of these issues, the thermographer was able to resolve X, leaving a total of Y open issues at your site."

Implementation:
• Add summary data calculation in the dataprep Lambda
• Create a new DOCX template for the Results Summary page
• Insert summary page after cover page, before Issues/Assets sections

----

*QA Validation Steps*

Test Case 1: Verify Summary Accuracy

# Create a session with known number of assets and issues
# Generate a Maintenance Report
# Verify the Results Summary page appears after the cover page
# Confirm "Total Items Inspected" matches actual asset count
# Confirm "Total Issues Identified" matches actual issue count

Test Case 2: Issue Type Breakdown

# Create issues of different types (Thermal Anomaly, NEC Violation, etc.)
# Generate report and verify each issue type is listed with correct count
# Verify counts sum to total issues identified

Test Case 3: Resolved vs Open Issues

# Mark some issues as resolved, leave others open
# Generate report and verify resolved/open counts are accurate
# Verify closing statement shows correct resolved and open counts

Edge Cases:
• Session with 0 issues - verify summary handles gracefully
• Session with only 1 asset - verify singular/plural wording
• Session with all issues resolved - verify messaging is appropriate

column1: Z | Android | Delete button gets stuck and asset is not deleted immediately in SLD view
column41:
h3. *Description*

When a user selects an asset in the SLD view and tries to delete it, the delete button gets stuck and the asset is not removed immediately. The asset gets deleted only after navigating back and reopening the SLD view.

column1: Z | BE | Add Asset Name to Issue Title in Maintenance Report (Front-loaded Option)
column41:
*Problem Statement*

When generating Maintenance Reports with the "Issues Front-loaded" layout option, the issue pages in the Table of Contents (TOC) only display the issue name. This makes it difficult for users to identify which asset an issue relates to without navigating to the actual page.

Current Behavior:
• Issue titles display only: {Issue Name} (e.g., "NEC Violation")

Expected Behavior:
• Issue titles should display: {Issue Name} - {Asset Name} (e.g., "NEC Violation - Panel 240 volt Sub Panel")

----

*Solution*

Update the dataprep Lambda function (project-z-reporting-dataprep-lambda) with the following changes:

SQL Query Update:
• Add LEFT JOIN with nodes table to fetch node_label (asset name) for each issue
• Query now includes: LEFT JOIN nodes n ON n.id = i.node_id
• Selects: n.label as node_label

Manifest Creation Update:
• Modify create_manifest function to format issue labels
• Issue name: issue.get('title') or issue.get('issue_class_name', 'Issue')
• Asset name: issue.get('node_label')
• Combined format: "{issue_name} - {asset_name}"

Files Changed:
• project-z-reporting-dataprep-lambda/lambda_function.py

----

*QA Validation Steps*

Test Case 1: Verify TOC Format

# Navigate to a Session with multiple issues across different assets
# Click "Generate Report" button
# Select "Issues Front-loaded" layout mode
# Generate the report and wait for PDF to be ready
# Open the generated PDF report
# Verify Table of Contents shows issues in format: "{Issue Name} - {Asset Name}"
# Example: "NEC Violation - Panel 240 volt Sub Panel" instead of just "NEC Violation"

Test Case 2: Verify Clickable Links

# Click on issue entries in the TOC
# Confirm they navigate to the correct issue page

Test Case 3: Nested Mode Regression

# Generate a report with "Issues Nested" layout
# Verify that mode still works correctly with the new format

Edge Cases:
• Issue without an associated asset (orphan issue) - should display just the issue name
• Issue with a long asset name - verify text displays properly without truncation issues

column1: Add "Simplify Enclosure" feature for group nodes in SLD views
column41:
h2. Problem Statement

Currently, enclosure/group nodes in SLD views always display as full enclosures showing all child nodes and their internal connections. For complex diagrams, users need a way to "simplify" these enclosures - representing them as their non-enclosure form (based on {{node_class.style}}) while hiding children and redirecting edges to the parent's terminals.

This is purely a UI-layer transformation - the underlying data (edges table, node relationships) remains unchanged. The simplified state is view-specific, allowing the same enclosure to appear simplified in one view and expanded in another.

h2. User Acceptance Tests (UATs)

* [ ] New {{is_simplified}} boolean field exists in both {{nodes}} table and {{mapping_node_sld_view}} table
* [ ] Right-clicking a group node in a specific view shows "Simplify Enclosure" option
* [ ] When simplified, the group node renders using its {{node_class.style}} instead of as an enclosure
* [ ] When simplified, child nodes are hidden from view
* [ ] When simplified, edges targeting child LINE terminals redirect to parent's LINE terminal
* [ ] When simplified, edges sourcing from child LOAD terminals redirect to parent's LOAD terminal
* [ ] Internal edges (between children of same simplified parent) are hidden
* [ ] Edges are hidden if parent lacks the required LINE/LOAD terminal
* [ ] Simplified nodes display with a light green border (similar to purple border for collapsed)
* [ ] Right-clicking a simplified node shows "Expand Enclosure" to restore original state
* [ ] Expanding restores children and original edge connections
* [ ] The {{edges}} table is never modified - redirection is computed at query time

h2. Execution Plan

h3. 1. Database Migration

Add {{is_simplified}} boolean column (default=False) to:

* {{nodes}} table (for "all nodes" view)
* {{mapping_node_sld_view}} table (for specific views)

h3. 2. Backend Model Updates

*Node.py*: Add {{is_simplified}} field and include in {{to_dict()}}
*Mappings.py (MappingNodeSLDView)*: Add {{is_simplified}} field after {{is_collapsed}}

h3. 3. Backend API Endpoint

*Route:* {{PUT /sld-view/<view_id>/nodes/<node_id>/simplified}}
*Controller:* {{SLDViewController.update_node_simplified_state()}}

h3. 4. Edge Redirection Logic (Backend)

In {{get_view_full()}}:

# Build set of simplified parent IDs and child→parent map
# For each edge:
#* Internal edges → HIDE
#* Outgoing from child → redirect to parent's LOAD terminal
#* Incoming to child → redirect to parent's LINE terminal
#* No terminal available → HIDE

h3. 5. Frontend Context Menu

Add "Simplify Enclosure" / "Expand Enclosure" toggle in {{NodeContextMenu.jsx}}

h3. 6. Frontend Handler

{{handleSimplifiedStateChange()}} in {{GraphEditor.jsx}}

h3. 7. Frontend Rendering

Update {{transformNodes()}} in {{App.jsx}} to render simplified groups using {{node_class.style}}

h3. 8. Visual Indicator

Add light green border ({{#4ade80}}) for simplified nodes in {{DynamicNode.jsx}}

h3. Files to Modify

||File||Change||
|{{eg-pz-backend/migrations/versions/xxxx_add_is_simplified.py}}|New migration|
|{{eg-pz-backend/app/models/Node.py}}|Add is_simplified field|
|{{eg-pz-backend/app/models/Mappings.py}}|Add is_simplified to MappingNodeSLDView|
|{{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/sld_view_routes.py}}|Add toggle endpoint|
|{{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py}}|Add controller + edge redirection|
|{{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/services/apiService.js}}|Add API function|
|{{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/NodeContextMenu.jsx}}|Add menu option|
|{{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/GraphEditor.jsx}}|Add handler|
|{{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/App.jsx}}|Update transformNodes()|
|{{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/nodes/DynamicNode.jsx}}|Add green border|

column1: Fix Clickjacking Vulnerability on Root (/) Endpoint
column41:
h3. User story

* As a security auditor, when I assess the root (/) endpoint, I want to ensure it is protected against clickjacking so that users are safe from malicious actions.
* As a developer, when implementing security measures, I want to add the necessary headers to the root (/) endpoint so that it complies with security standards.

h3. Context

A security audit revealed that the root (/) endpoint is vulnerable to clickjacking attacks. This vulnerability allows the page to be embedded in an iframe on malicious websites, potentially deceiving users into taking unintended actions.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* [ ] Root (/) endpoint returns {{X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN}} header.
* [ ] Root (/) endpoint returns {{Content-Security-Policy}} header with {{frame-ancestors 'self'}} directive.
* [ ] Application functionality works correctly after implementation.
* [ ] Headers verified in both staging and production environments.

h3. Other information

* Affected URL: [https://acme.stage.egalvanic.ai/|https://acme.stage.egalvanic.ai/]
* Threat: Clickjacking can trick users into clicking an invisible frame, causing unintended actions.
* QA Validation steps include:
** *Header Verification:* Use browser DevTools to check response headers.
** *Iframe Test:* Create a test HTML file to ensure the iframe fails to load from external origins.
** *Functional Test:* Verify the application homepage and navigation work correctly post-implementation.
** *Cross-browser testing:* Ensure protection works in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge.
* Reference: [OWASP Clickjacking Defense Cheat Sheet|https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Clickjacking_Defense_Cheat_Sheet.html]

column1: Z | Frontend | Secure Password Handling and Clickjacking Defense
column41:
*Problem Statement* A recent security assessment (Cyber Risk Score: _4.0_) identified _58 vulnerabilities_. The following issues require frontend remediation: _Critical – Password in HTTP Traffic_ *Asset:* [https://acme.stage.egalvanic.ai/|https://acme.stage.egalvanic.ai/|smart-link] User passwords may be exposed due to insecure form handling, caching, or transmission outside the authentication flow. _High – Clickjacking (Framable Page)_ *Asset:* [https://acme.stage.egalvanic.ai/|https://acme.stage.egalvanic.ai/|smart-link] Application pages can be embedded within malicious iframes, enabling UI redressing and clickjacking attacks. ---- *Proposed Solution* _Secure Password Handling_ * Ensure all authentication forms submit only to HTTPS endpoints * Prevent password storage in localStorage, sessionStorage, or any client-side cache * Clear password input fields immediately after form submission * Use secure, token-based authentication for all post-login requests * Disable browser autocomplete on password fields where applicable: {{autocomplete="new-password"}} * Ensure no password values are logged to the browser console in any environment _Clickjacking Defense (Defense in Depth)_ * Implement a frame-breaker (framekiller) script as a fallback protection in the application entry point: {noformat}if (window.self !== window.top) { window.top.location = window.self.location; } {noformat} _General Security Best Practices_ * Audit all frontend forms handling sensitive data * Ensure no sensitive data is passed via URL query parameters * Implement secure error handling that does not expose credential information * Review and remove any debug or console logging of sensitive values ---- *Acceptance Criteria* _Password Security_ * All authentication forms submit to HTTPS endpoints only * No password values are stored in localStorage or sessionStorage * Password fields are cleared after form submission * Token-based authentication is used for all post-login API requests * No password values appear in browser console logs * No password values are present in URL parameters (GET requests) _Clickjacking Defense_ * Frame-breaker script is added to the application entry point * Application exits or blocks unauthorized iframe embedding _General Security_ * All forms handling sensitive data are audited * No sensitive data is exposed in URL query strings * Error messages do not reveal credential or authentication details ---- *QA Validation* _Password Handling Testing_ * Open browser developer tools → Application tab * Perform the login flow * Verify localStorage and sessionStorage contain no password values * Confirm password input fields are cleared after submission * Check Network tab to ensure passwords appear only in the login POST body * Review Console tab to confirm no password values are logged _Clickjacking Defense Testing_ * Create a test HTML file on an external domain: {noformat}

Clickjacking Test

{noformat} * Load the page in a browser * Verify the application does not render inside the iframe or redirects to break out _Token Authentication Testing_ * Log in to the application * Inspect subsequent API requests in the Network tab * Verify authentication uses tokens in the Authorization header * Confirm no password values are sent after the initial login request _Console and Debug Testing_ * Open browser console * Perform full login and logout cycle * Search console output for any password or sensitive values * Verify no credentials are logged _Cross-Browser Testing_ * Repeat all tests in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge _Regression Testing_ * Verify login flow functions correctly * Verify “Remember Me” (if applicable) uses tokens rather than passwords * Verify password reset flow works as expected * Verify logout clears session and authentication state properly

column1: Z | Backend | Implement HTTPS Enforcement and Clickjacking Protection Headers
column41:
*Problem Statement*

A recent security assessment (Cyber Risk Score: _4.0_) identified _58 vulnerabilities_. The most critical backend-related issues are outlined below:

_Critical – Password in HTTP Traffic_
*Asset:* [https://acme.stage.egalvanic.ai/|https://acme.stage.egalvanic.ai/|smart-link] 
User passwords are exposed in HTTP traffic outside the authentication flow, creating a risk of interception, reflection, or reuse by malicious actors.

_High – Clickjacking (Framable Page)_
*Asset:* [https://acme.stage.egalvanic.ai/favicon.ico|https://acme.stage.egalvanic.ai/favicon.ico|smart-link] 
Application pages can be embedded within invisible iframes, allowing attackers to trick users into performing unintended actions.

----

*Proposed Solution*

_HTTPS Enforcement & Password Security_

* Enforce HTTPS Strict Transport Security (HSTS) on all API responses
* Add header: {{Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload}}
* Ensure password data is transmitted only during login/registration requests
* Implement secure, pseudo-random session tokens for all post-authentication requests
* Store passwords using PBKDF2 or bcrypt with proper salting
* Remove any password echoing from API responses

_Clickjacking Protection_

* Add {{X-Frame-Options: DENY}} header to all responses
* Implement {{Content-Security-Policy}} with {{frame-ancestors 'self'}} directive
* Apply headers at the web server or reverse proxy level for global coverage

----

*Acceptance Criteria*

_HTTPS & Password Security_

* All API responses include the HSTS header
* Passwords appear only in POST request bodies for login/registration endpoints
* No password data is present in response bodies, URL parameters, cookies, or headers
* Session tokens are used for all authenticated API requests
* All HTTP requests redirect to HTTPS using 301 status codes

_Clickjacking Protection_

* All responses include {{X-Frame-Options: DENY}}
* CSP header includes {{frame-ancestors 'self'}}
* Headers are configured at the server or proxy level

----

*QA Validation*

_Security Header Validation_

* Verify headers using:

{noformat}curl -I https://acme.stage.egalvanic.ai/
{noformat}

* Confirm presence of {{Strict-Transport-Security}}, {{X-Frame-Options}}, and {{Content-Security-Policy}}

_Password Exposure Testing_

* Capture network traffic using Burp Suite or browser developer tools
* Perform login, navigation, and logout flows
* Verify passwords appear only in login POST requests
* Ensure no password data exists in GET parameters, cookies, or response bodies

_Clickjacking Validation_

* Create an external HTML file:

{noformat}<iframe src="https://acme.stage.egalvanic.ai/" width="500" height="500"></iframe>
{noformat}

* Iframe content should fail to load or render blank

_Security Scan & Regression_

* Run OWASP ZAP scan against staging
* Confirm no findings for “Password in HTTP Traffic” or “Clickjacking”
* Verify login, logout, password reset flows, and authenticated API calls work correctly

column1: Enforce procedure uniqueness per node class/subtype/procedure master
column41:
h2. Problem Statement

Currently, there is no constraint preventing duplicate procedures for the same (node_class_id, node_subtype, procedure_master_id) combination. This can lead to data inconsistency where multiple procedures exist for the same equipment type and procedure master, causing confusion about which procedure should be used.

The system needs to enforce that only one active procedure can exist per node class / node subtype / procedure master combination.

h2. User Acceptance Tests (UATs)

* [ ] Creating a procedure with an existing (node_class_id, node_subtype, procedure_master_id) combo returns a 409 Conflict error
* [ ] Creating a procedure with the same node_class but different node_subtype succeeds
* [ ] Creating a procedure with the same combo but different procedure_master succeeds
* [ ] Creating a procedure without a procedure_master_id succeeds (no constraint applied)
* [ ] Soft-deleted procedures do not count toward uniqueness (can recreate after deletion)
* [ ] Error message clearly indicates which existing procedure conflicts
* [ ] Database constraint prevents duplicates even if API validation is bypassed

h2. Execution Plan

h3. 1. Create Alembic Migration

*File*: {{eg-pz-backend/migrations/versions/<timestamp>_add_procedure_uniqueness_constraint.py}}

Create a partial unique index:

{code:sql}CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_procedures_master_class_subtype
ON procedures (
    procedure_master_id,
    COALESCE(node_class_id, '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'::uuid),
    COALESCE(node_subtype, '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'::uuid)
)
WHERE procedure_master_id IS NOT NULL AND is_deleted = false{code}

* Uses COALESCE to handle NULL node_class_id and node_subtype (SQL NULL != NULL)
* Only applies when procedure_master_id is set
* Excludes soft-deleted procedures

h3. 2. Add API Validation

*File*: {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/procedure_controller.py}}

Add helper method {{_check_procedure_uniqueness()}}:

* Query for existing procedure with same (procedure_master_id, node_class_id, node_subtype)
* Handle NULL comparisons with {{IS NULL}} filters
* Only check when procedure_master_id is provided
* Exclude soft-deleted procedures
* On update, exclude the current procedure from check

Update {{create_procedure()}} and {{update_procedure()}}:

* Call uniqueness check before insert/update
* Return 409 Conflict with user-friendly error message if duplicate exists

h3. 3. Update Model Documentation

*File*: {{eg-pz-backend/app/models/Procedure.py}}

Add {{__table_args__}} comment documenting the constraint for developer awareness.

h3. Files to Modify

# {{eg-pz-backend/migrations/versions/}} - New migration file
# {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/procedure_controller.py}} - Add validation
# {{eg-pz-backend/app/models/Procedure.py}} - Document constraint

h3. Pre-Migration Check

Run audit query to find existing duplicates before applying migration:

{code:sql}SELECT procedure_master_id, node_class_id, node_subtype, COUNT(*), array_agg(id)
FROM procedures
WHERE procedure_master_id IS NOT NULL AND is_deleted = false
GROUP BY procedure_master_id, node_class_id, node_subtype
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;{code}

column1: Services (Procedure Groups)
EMP Quote: Add procedure-level date configuration when reassigning shortcuts
column41:
h2. Problem Statement

When modifying the dataset (assigned shortcut for a given asset) in an EMP quote/plan, users cannot set per-procedure scheduling preferences. Currently, the system uses the EMP's start date as the origin time for calculating all planned workorder windows. This limits flexibility for users who need to:

* Align maintenance schedules with actual last-completed dates from previous work
* Set specific desired due dates for individual procedures that differ from the EMP start-based calculation
* Handle multiple assets with different shortcuts while maintaining procedure-level control

Additionally, when multiple assets with different shortcuts are selected, the current UI shows procedures (which differ across shortcuts) rather than the shared ProcedureMasters, making it difficult to configure dates consistently.

h2. User Acceptance Tests (UATs)

* [ ] When reassigning a shortcut in the Dataset tab, a date configuration dialog appears after selecting the new shortcut
* [ ] User can choose between "Set desired first due date" or "Set last completed date" scheduling modes
* [ ] Each procedure in the shortcut has an individual date input field
* [ ] "Copy to all" button copies the first procedure's date to all other procedures
* [ ] When in "last completed" mode, the calculated due date is displayed for each procedure
* [ ] When multiple assets with DIFFERENT shortcuts are selected, ProcedureMasters are shown instead of procedures
* [ ] Date overrides are correctly applied when generating quote lines (verified by checking window_start/window_end in database)
* [ ] For "lastCompleted" mode: window_start = last_completed_date, window_end = last_completed_date + interval
* [ ] For "dueDate" mode: window_end = desired_due_date, window_start = max(emp_start, due_date - interval)
* [ ] Existing shortcut reassignment without date overrides continues to work (backward compatibility)
* [ ] Frontend validation: all procedures must have dates before Apply is enabled
* [ ] Frontend validation: "lastCompleted" dates must be in the past
* [ ] Backend validation: returns 400 if procedure_master_id doesn't match any procedure in shortcut

h2. Execution Plan

h3. Frontend Changes

*1. Create ProcedureDateConfigDialog Component*

* New file: {{eg-pz-frontend/src/components/quote/ProcedureDateConfigDialog.jsx}}
* Radio buttons for scheduling mode
* Date input per procedure (or procedure master if multi-shortcut)
* "Copy to all" button
* Calculated due date display in lastCompleted mode
* Interval badge display (e.g., "Every 12 mo")

*2. Modify DatasetTab.jsx*

* Location: {{eg-pz-frontend/src/components/DatasetTab.jsx}}
* Add state for two-step flow: {{showDateConfig}}, {{pendingShortcut}}, {{procedureMastersForConfig}}
* Modify {{handleReassignShortcut()}} to open date config dialog instead of immediately calling API
* Add {{handleDateConfigApply()}} to receive date overrides and call modified API

*3. Modify mainAPIService.js*

* Add/modify functions to pass date overrides:
** {{bulkReplaceNodeShortcutsWithDates(quoteId, nodeIds, shortcutId, procedureDateOverrides, schedulingMode)}}
** {{replaceNodeShortcutWithDates(quoteId, nodeId, shortcutId, procedureDateOverrides, schedulingMode)}}

h3. Backend Changes

*4. Modify quote_controller.py*

* Location: {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/quote_controller.py}}
* Update both {{bulk_replace_emp_for_nodes_in_quote()}} (line 40) and {{replace_emp_for_node_in_quote()}} (line 1064)
* Accept new optional parameters: {{procedure_date_overrides}} and {{scheduling_mode}}
* Create helper function {{calculate_window_for_procedure()}}
* Apply date overrides when calculating window_start/window_end
* For procedure masters: look up actual procedure_id within each asset's shortcut

h3. API Contract

{code:json}{
  "quote_id": "uuid",
  "node_ids": ["uuid1", "uuid2"],
  "shortcut_id": "uuid",
  "procedure_date_overrides": {
    "procedure_master_id_1": "2024-06-01T00:00:00Z"
  },
  "scheduling_mode": "dueDate" | "lastCompleted"
}{code}

h3. Multi-Shortcut Handling

When multiple assets have different shortcuts:

# Extract all unique ProcedureMasters from selected assets' shortcuts
# Show ProcedureMasters in the date config UI (not procedures)
# Keys in {{procedure_date_overrides}} use {{procedure_master_id}}
# Backend finds the actual {{procedure_id}} for each procedure_master within each asset's shortcut
# If a shortcut lacks a procedure for a given procedure_master, that override is skipped for that asset

h3. Files to Modify

||File||Action||
|{{eg-pz-frontend/src/components/quote/ProcedureDateConfigDialog.jsx}}|CREATE|
|{{eg-pz-frontend/src/components/DatasetTab.jsx}}|MODIFY|
|{{eg-pz-frontend/src/services/mainAPIService.js}}|MODIFY|
|{{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/quote_controller.py}}|MODIFY|

h3. Backward Compatibility

* {{procedure_date_overrides}} and {{scheduling_mode}} are OPTIONAL
* If omitted, existing behavior (using {{quote.emp_start}}) is preserved
* Existing API consumers continue to work without changes

column1: Z | iOS | SLD view position changes are not retained when navigating back to the same view
column41:
h3. *Description*

In the SLD, when the position of elements is changed in any created view and the user navigates back and then reopens the same view, the position changes are not reflected. The updated position is applied only after navigating to the dashboard and refreshing the page.

----

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Open any SLD.
# Open any already created SLD view.
# Change the position of elements in the view.
# Navigate back from the view.
# Open the same SLD view again.
# Observe the element positions.
# Navigate to the dashboard and refresh the page.
# Open the same SLD view again.

----

h3. *Expected Result*

The position changes should be saved and immediately reflected when reopening the same SLD view without requiring a dashboard refresh.

----

h3. *Actual Result*

The position changes are not reflected when reopening the view and are visible only after navigating to the dashboard and refreshing the page.

The ATS connection is *not visible* in the Asset Class view, but it *is visible* when the Junction Board Asset is opened in Edit mode.

h3. User story

* As a project manager, when I am reviewing work block assignments, I want to see the actual scheduling status so that I can ensure all tasks are properly assigned and scheduled.
* As a user, when I am assigning resources, I want to use a searchable Autocomplete feature for person selectors so that I can quickly find and assign the right individuals.
* As a user, when I notice unassigned work blocks, I want to have an "Add Resource" button available so that I can easily assign resources to those blocks.
* As a user, when there are no field technicians assigned, I want to see an "Add Field Technician" button so that I can promptly assign someone to the task.

h3. Context

This issue focuses on improving the scheduling interface by ensuring that work block assignments are properly checked. It aims to enhance user experience with visual indicators and improved resource assignment features.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* Fix scheduling status to check work block assignments, not just their existence.
* Add red indicators for:
** Missing start/due dates
** Missing estimated hours
** Missing field technicians
** Missing work blocks
** Missing resources
* Replace the Select dropdown with a searchable Autocomplete for all person selectors.
* Add a red "Add Resource" button for unassigned work blocks.
* Add a red "Add Field Technician" button when no technicians are assigned.

column1: Z | BE | Implement Response Caching for SLD/View Data
column41:
h2. Problem

Every SLD/view load queries the database from scratch, even for unchanged data. There's no caching layer for frequently accessed data.

*Impact:*

* Same data re-queried on every page load
* Database load scales linearly with user traffic
* No benefit from repeated requests for same view

----

h2. Scope

# Add Redis caching for view data
# Implement cache invalidation on data changes
# Add cache-control headers for browser caching

----

h2. Implementation Details

h3. Option 1: Flask-Caching with Redis

{code:python}# In app/__init__.py
from flask_caching import Cache

cache = Cache(config={
    'CACHE_TYPE': 'redis',
    'CACHE_REDIS_URL': os.environ.get('REDIS_URL', 'redis://localhost:6379/0'),
    'CACHE_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT': 300  # 5 minutes
})

# In sld_view_controller.py
from app import cache

class SLDViewController:
    @staticmethod
    def get_view_full(view_id):
        cache_key = f'view_full:{view_id}'
        
        # Try cache first
        cached = cache.get(cache_key)
        if cached:
            return cached, 200
        
        # ... existing query logic ...
        
        # Cache the result
        cache.set(cache_key, result, timeout=300)
        return result, 200
    
    @staticmethod
    def invalidate_view_cache(view_id):
        cache.delete(f'view_full:{view_id}'){code}

h3. Cache Invalidation Points

{code:python}# In sld_view_controller.py

def batch_update_node_positions(view_id, positions):
    # ... update logic ...
    SLDViewController.invalidate_view_cache(view_id)
    return result

def add_nodes_to_view(view_id, nodes_data):
    # ... add logic ...
    SLDViewController.invalidate_view_cache(view_id)
    return result

def remove_nodes_from_view(view_id, node_ids):
    # ... remove logic ...
    SLDViewController.invalidate_view_cache(view_id)
    return result{code}

h3. HTTP Cache Headers

{code:python}# In sld_view_routes.py
from flask import make_response

@sld_view_routes.route('/sld-view/<uuid:view_id>/full', methods=['GET'])
def get_view_full(view_id):
    result, status = SLDViewController.get_view_full(view_id)
    response = make_response(jsonify(result), status)
    
    # Allow browser caching for 60 seconds
    response.headers['Cache-Control'] = 'public, max-age=60'
    response.headers['ETag'] = hashlib.md5(json.dumps(result).encode()).hexdigest()
    
    return response{code}

----

h2. Cache Strategy

||Data Type||TTL||Invalidation Trigger||
|View Full Data|5 min|Position update, node add/remove|
|SLD Full Data|5 min|Any node/edge change|
|Node Class List|1 hour|Node class CRUD|

----

h2. Files to Modify

||File||Change||
|{{app/__init__.py}}|Add Flask-Caching setup|
|{{app/config.py}}|Add Redis config|
|{{app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py}}|Add caching + invalidation|
|{{app/controllers/sld_controller.py}}|Add caching + invalidation|
|{{requirements.txt}}|Add flask-caching, redis|

----

h2. Environment Variables

{noformat}REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379/0
CACHE_ENABLED=true
CACHE_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT=300{noformat}

----

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Redis caching implemented for view data
* [ ] Cache invalidated on data changes
* [ ] No stale data returned after updates
* [ ] Response time reduced by 80% on cache hits
* [ ] Unit tests for cache behavior

----

h2. Technical Reference

* Parent: ZP-674
* Depends on: ZP-728 (should optimize queries before caching)
* Phase: Long-term (after query optimizations)

column1: Z | BE | Add Database Indexes for SLD Performance
column41:
h2. Problem

The database is missing indexes on commonly queried columns, causing full table scans on large datasets.

*Affected Tables:*

* {{nodes}} - Missing index on {{sld_id}}
* {{edges}} - Missing index on {{source}}, {{target}}, {{sld_id}}
* {{mapping_node_sld_view}} - Missing index on {{sld_view_id}}
* {{photos}} - Missing index on {{entity_id}}, {{sld_id}}

*Impact:*

* Full table scans on every SLD/view load
* Query performance degrades linearly with data size
* Contributes to 502 errors under load

----

h2. Scope

# Add composite indexes for common query patterns
# Add partial indexes with {{WHERE is_deleted = false}} for soft-delete tables
# Analyze query plans to verify index usage

----

h2. Implementation Details

h3. Migration Script

{code:python}from alembic import op
import sqlalchemy as sa

def upgrade():
    # Nodes table
    op.create_index(
        'idx_nodes_sld_id_active',
        'nodes',
        ['sld_id'],
        postgresql_where=sa.text('is_deleted = false')
    )
    op.create_index(
        'idx_nodes_parent_id_active',
        'nodes',
        ['parent_id'],
        postgresql_where=sa.text('is_deleted = false AND parent_id IS NOT NULL')
    )
    
    # Edges table
    op.create_index(
        'idx_edges_sld_source_target',
        'edges',
        ['sld_id', 'source', 'target'],
        postgresql_where=sa.text('is_deleted = false')
    )
    op.create_index(
        'idx_edges_source_active',
        'edges',
        ['source'],
        postgresql_where=sa.text('is_deleted = false')
    )
    op.create_index(
        'idx_edges_target_active',
        'edges',
        ['target'],
        postgresql_where=sa.text('is_deleted = false')
    )
    
    # Mapping table
    op.create_index(
        'idx_mapping_node_sld_view_view_id',
        'mapping_node_sld_view',
        ['sld_view_id'],
        postgresql_where=sa.text('is_deleted = false')
    )
    op.create_index(
        'idx_mapping_node_sld_view_node_id',
        'mapping_node_sld_view',
        ['node_id'],
        postgresql_where=sa.text('is_deleted = false')
    )
    
    # Photos table
    op.create_index(
        'idx_photos_entity_sld',
        'photos',
        ['entity_id', 'sld_id'],
        postgresql_where=sa.text('is_deleted = false')
    )

def downgrade():
    op.drop_index('idx_nodes_sld_id_active')
    op.drop_index('idx_nodes_parent_id_active')
    op.drop_index('idx_edges_sld_source_target')
    op.drop_index('idx_edges_source_active')
    op.drop_index('idx_edges_target_active')
    op.drop_index('idx_mapping_node_sld_view_view_id')
    op.drop_index('idx_mapping_node_sld_view_node_id')
    op.drop_index('idx_photos_entity_sld'){code}

----

h2. Verification Queries

Run these to verify index usage:

{code:sql}-- Check nodes query plan
EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT * FROM nodes WHERE sld_id = '<uuid>' AND is_deleted = false;

-- Check edges query plan
EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT * FROM edges 
WHERE sld_id = '<uuid>' AND is_deleted = false 
AND (source IN ('<uuid1>', '<uuid2>') OR target IN ('<uuid1>', '<uuid2>'));

-- Check mapping query plan
EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT * FROM mapping_node_sld_view 
WHERE sld_view_id = '<uuid>' AND is_deleted = false;{code}

----

h2. Files to Create/Modify

||File||Change||
|{{migrations/versions/xxx_add_performance_indexes.py}}|New migration|

----

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] All specified indexes created
* [ ] EXPLAIN ANALYZE shows index scans instead of seq scans
* [ ] No performance regression on write operations
* [ ] Migration is reversible (downgrade works)

----

h2. Technical Reference

* Parent: ZP-674
* Depends on: None (can be done independently)

column1: Z | BE | Implement Bulk Position Updates
column41:
h2. Problem

The {{batch_update_node_positions()}} method loops through each position and executes individual queries instead of using bulk operations.

*Location:* {{app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py:693-727}}

{code:python}def batch_update_node_positions(view_id, positions):
    for pos in positions:
        mapping = MappingNodeSLDView.query.filter_by(  # Query per position!
            node_id=uuid.UUID(pos['node_id']),
            sld_view_id=view_id,
            is_deleted=False
        ).first()
        if mapping:
            mapping.x = pos['x']
            mapping.y = pos['y']
    db.session.commit()  # Single commit{code}

*Impact:*

* 100 position updates = 100 queries instead of 1-2 bulk queries
* Frequent position updates during drag operations cause database load
* Contributes to 502 errors on busy sites

----

h2. Scope

# Replace loop queries with bulk update operations
# Use SQLAlchemy's {{bulk_update_mappings()}} or {{update().where().values()}}
# Maintain single transaction commit

----

h2. Implementation Details

h3. Option 1: Bulk Update Mappings

{code:python}from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

def batch_update_node_positions(view_id, positions):
    # Fetch all mappings in single query
    node_ids = [uuid.UUID(pos['node_id']) for pos in positions]
    
    mappings = MappingNodeSLDView.query.filter(
        MappingNodeSLDView.sld_view_id == view_id,
        MappingNodeSLDView.node_id.in_(node_ids),
        MappingNodeSLDView.is_deleted == False
    ).all()
    
    # Build lookup for positions
    position_map = {uuid.UUID(p['node_id']): (p['x'], p['y']) for p in positions}
    
    # Update in memory
    updated = 0
    for mapping in mappings:
        if mapping.node_id in position_map:
            mapping.x, mapping.y = position_map[mapping.node_id]
            mapping.modified_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
            updated += 1
    
    db.session.commit()
    return {'success': True, 'updated_count': updated}{code}

h3. Option 2: Raw SQL Bulk Update (Most Efficient)

{code:python}from sqlalchemy import case, literal_column

def batch_update_node_positions(view_id, positions):
    if not positions:
        return {'success': True, 'updated_count': 0}
    
    # Build CASE statement for bulk update
    position_map = {uuid.UUID(p['node_id']): (p['x'], p['y']) for p in positions}
    node_ids = list(position_map.keys())
    
    # Single UPDATE with CASE
    stmt = MappingNodeSLDView.__table__.update().where(
        MappingNodeSLDView.sld_view_id == view_id,
        MappingNodeSLDView.node_id.in_(node_ids),
        MappingNodeSLDView.is_deleted == False
    ).values(
        x=case(
            {nid: pos[0] for nid, pos in position_map.items()},
            value=MappingNodeSLDView.node_id
        ),
        y=case(
            {nid: pos[1] for nid, pos in position_map.items()},
            value=MappingNodeSLDView.node_id
        ),
        modified_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    )
    
    result = db.session.execute(stmt)
    db.session.commit()
    
    return {'success': True, 'updated_count': result.rowcount}{code}

----

h2. Files to Modify

||File||Change||
|{{app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py:693-727}}|Replace loop with bulk update|

----

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Position updates use bulk query instead of loop
* [ ] 100 position updates execute in 1-2 queries instead of 100+
* [ ] No regression in position update functionality
* [ ] Transaction safety maintained
* [ ] Unit tests for bulk update

----

h2. Technical Reference

* Parent: ZP-674
* Related FE ticket: ZP-725 (Incremental Batch Position Updates)

column1: Z | BE | Fix N+1 Query in Node Serialization (Eager Loading)
column41:
h2. Problem

The {{Node.to_dict()}} method triggers a separate database query for each node to fetch its {{NodeClass}} data. This results in O(n) database queries during serialization.

*Location:* {{app/models/Node.py:75-87}}

{code:python}def to_dict(self):
    if self.node_class:
        from .NodeClass import NodeClass
        node_class_obj = NodeClass.query.get(self.node_class)  # Query per node!{code}

*Impact:*

* 500 nodes = 500 additional database queries
* Response time increases linearly with node count
* Primary cause of flickering on large sites

Additionally, {{node_terminals}} relationship uses {{lazy='select'}} which triggers another query per node.

*Location:* {{app/models/Node.py:64-68}}

{code:python}node_terminals = relationship(
    'NodeTerminal',
    lazy='select',  # Triggers separate query per node
){code}

----

h2. Scope

# Add a proper relationship for {{NodeClass}} with eager loading
# Change {{node_terminals}} from {{lazy='select'}} to {{lazy='joined'}} or use {{selectinload}}
# Update controller methods to use {{joinedload}} options
# Batch load NodeClass data in controller before serialization

----

h2. Implementation Details

h3. Option 1: Add Relationship with Eager Loading

{code:python}# In Node.py
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship

class Node(db.Model):
    # Add relationship
    node_class_rel = relationship(
        'NodeClass',
        foreign_keys=[node_class],
        primaryjoin='Node.node_class == NodeClass.id',
        lazy='joined'  # Eager load
    )
    
    # Change node_terminals
    node_terminals = relationship(
        'NodeTerminal',
        lazy='joined',  # Changed from 'select'
    )
    
    def to_dict(self):
        # Use relationship instead of query
        node_class_name = self.node_class_rel.name if self.node_class_rel else None{code}

h3. Option 2: Batch Load in Controller

{code:python}# In sld_view_controller.py
from sqlalchemy.orm import joinedload

def get_view_full(view_id):
    # Use joinedload to eager load relationships
    view_nodes = Node.query.options(
        joinedload(Node.node_class_rel),
        joinedload(Node.node_terminals)
    ).filter(
        Node.id.in_(view_node_ids),
        Node.is_deleted == False
    ).all()
    
    # Or batch load NodeClass separately
    node_class_ids = set(n.node_class for n in view_nodes if n.node_class)
    node_classes = {nc.id: nc for nc in NodeClass.query.filter(NodeClass.id.in_(node_class_ids))}{code}

----

h2. Files to Modify

||File||Change||
|{{app/models/Node.py}}|Add {{node_class_rel}} relationship, change {{node_terminals}} lazy loading|
|{{app/models/Node.py:to_dict()}}|Use relationship instead of query|
|{{app/controllers/sld_controller.py}}|Add joinedload options in get_sld_full(), get_sld_full_v2()|
|{{app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py}}|Add joinedload options in get_view_full()|

----

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* -Node serialization does not trigger individual NodeClass queries-
* -Node terminals are eager loaded with nodes-
* -Query count for 500 nodes reduced from ~520 to ~15-
* -Response time for large SLDs reduced by 50%+-
* -No regression in node data returned-
* -Unit tests for serialization-

----

h2. Technical Reference

* Parent: ZP-674
* Related FE tickets: ZP-723, ZP-724, ZP-725, ZP-726, ZP-727

column41:
h2. Objective

Execute UI automation tests against the dev environment and generate a test report whenever code is merged into the {{release/dev}} branch.

h2. Background

Comprehensive accessibility identifiers have been added across 57 view files in the iOS codebase to support UI automation testing. This task involves validating these automation capabilities in the dev environment.

h2. Acceptance Criteria

# Run the existing UI automation test suite against the dev environment
# Validate that all accessibility identifiers are working correctly
# Generate a comprehensive test report including:
#* Test execution summary (pass/fail counts)
#* Detailed results for each test case
#* Screenshots/logs for any failures
#* Coverage metrics for tested views
# Document any issues or gaps discovered during testing
# Ensure test execution can be triggered after merges to {{release/dev}}

h2. Scope

* Screens with accessibility identifiers:
