column1: Add Assignment Type Enums (Reporter/Assignee/Watcher) to User-Session and User-Task Mappings
column41:
h2. Problem Statement

The {{mapping_user_task}} and {{mapping_user_session}} tables exist but are not being fully utilized with proper assignment type semantics. Currently:

* {{mapping_user_task}} has a {{mapping_type}} field but uses informal values ("owner"/"assignee") with no enum enforcement
* {{mapping_user_session}} (UserSessionAccess model) has an overly complex structure with unused fields (assigned_by, can_reassign, expires_at) and no {{mapping_type}} field
* Work orders (IR sessions) are not filtered by user assignment on web or mobile — all users with {{sessions.view}} permission see everything
* Mobile "assigned to me" task filtering is not enum-aware (any mapping = assigned, rather than specifically "assignee" type)
* No admin override logic exists for assignment-based visibility

We need a proper assignment type system with Reporter, Assignee, and Watcher roles, applied consistently across backend, web frontend, and iOS mobile, with admin users bypassing assignment filters to see everything.

h2. User Acceptance Tests (UATs)

* [ ] Enum tables {{enum_task_assignment_types}} and {{enum_session_assignment_types}} exist in DB with entries: reporter, assignee, watcher
* [ ] {{mapping_user_session}} table simplified to: id, user_id, session_id, mapping_type, is_deleted (old columns removed)
* [ ] Existing {{mapping_user_task}} data migrated: 'owner' → 'reporter', NULL → 'assignee'
* [ ] Backend API: Can create/update user-session mappings with mapping_type via {{/mapping/user-session/create}} and {{/mapping/user-session/update/<id>}}
* [ ] Backend API: {{get_sld_full_v2}} response includes {{user_session}} mappings in the mappings object
* [ ] Backend API: Non-admin users only see sessions they are assigned to (reporter or assignee) when filtering is active
* [ ] Backend API: Admin users see all sessions regardless of assignment
* [ ] Web: SessionDetail page shows assignment management UI (add/remove reporters/assignees) gated by {{sessions.manage}} permission
* [ ] Web: Sessions list page has "My Work Orders" / "All Work Orders" toggle; non-admins default to "My Work Orders"
* [ ] Mobile: IRSession model has {{owned_by}} and {{assigned_to}} arrays populated from user_session mappings during sync
* [ ] Mobile: Work order list filters by assignee for non-admin users (default view shows assigned sessions only)
* [ ] Mobile: Admin users see all work orders regardless of assignment
* [ ] Mobile: New {{mappingUserSession}} SyncTarget handles offline create/update operations
* [ ] Mobile: Existing task "assigned to me" filter continues to work correctly (only assignee type = assigned)
* [ ] Backward compatible: Mobile handles both 'owner' and 'reporter' mapping types during transition

h2. Execution Plan

h3. Phase 1: Backend Database & Models

*1.1 Alembic Migration:*

* Create {{enum_task_assignment_types}} table (id, name, description) with values: reporter, assignee, watcher
* Create {{enum_session_assignment_types}} table (same structure)
* Restructure {{mapping_user_session}}: drop assigned_by/assigned_at/can_reassign/is_active/expires_at, add mapping_type VARCHAR and is_deleted BOOLEAN
* Migrate data: is_active=false → is_deleted=true; existing rows get mapping_type='assignee'
* Migrate mapping_user_task: 'owner' → 'reporter', NULL → 'assignee'

*1.2 Simplify Model* ({{eg-pz-backend/app/models/UserSessionAccess.py}}):

* Replace UserSessionAccess with MappingUserSession (id, user_id, session_id, mapping_type, is_deleted)

*1.3 Create Enum Models* ({{eg-pz-backend/app/models/EnumAssignmentType.py}}):

* EnumTaskAssignmentType, EnumSessionAssignmentType

*1.4 Update exports* ({{app/models/__init__.py}})

h3. Phase 2: Backend Routes & Controllers

*2.1* Add POST {{/mapping/user-session/create}} and PUT {{/mapping/user-session/update/<id>}} routes
*2.2* Add controller methods with mapping_type validation
*2.3* Refactor old user routes (lines 558-649 in user_routes.py) to use new model
*2.4* Update {{get_sld_full_v2}} to include user_session mappings in response
*2.5* Add session filtering by assignment in session_controller.py (admin bypass)
*2.6* Add GET endpoints for assignment type enums

h3. Phase 3: Frontend (Web)

*3.1* SessionDetail.jsx: Assignment management UI (user chips, autocomplete, role dropdown)
*3.2* Sessions.jsx: "My Work Orders" / "All Work Orders" toggle with admin default
*3.3* mainAPIService.js: Update session assignment functions for new mapping pattern

h3. Phase 4: Mobile (iOS)

*4.1* Add MappingUserSessionDTO to SLDMappingsDTO.swift
*4.2* Add owned_by/assigned_to arrays to IRSession entity
*4.3* Add {{mappingUserSession}} to SyncTarget enum
*4.4* Update SLDSyncService processMappings() for user-session processing
*4.5* Update all sync switch statements (NetworkState, SyncExecutionService, SyncQueueService, SyncDeduplicationService, SyncQueueAnalyzerView)
*4.6* Add APIClient createUserSessionMapping/updateUserSessionMapping methods
*4.7* Add MappingData.userSession() helper
*4.8* Add work order filtering in IRSessionViewModel (admin bypass)

h3. Implementation Order

# Backend migration + models
# Backend routes/controllers
# Mobile DTO + model + sync infrastructure
# Mobile API + filtering UI
# Frontend assignment UI + filtering

column1: FE | Normal and Emergency Edges Get Merged After Hard/Browser Refresh in SLD View
column41:
*Description:*
When an edge is created in the ATS class with both *Normal* and *Emergency* connections, it displays correctly after refreshing from the *SLD View*. However, if the page is refreshed using a *hard refresh* or the *browser refresh*, the Normal and Emergency connection lines get merged and do not display correctly.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Create an edge in the *ATS class* with both *Normal* and *Emergency* connections.
# Open the edge in the *SLD View*.
# Refresh the view using the *SLD View refresh option*.
# Observe that Normal and Emergency edges are displayed correctly.
# Now perform a *hard refresh (Ctrl + Shift + R)* or use the *browser refresh*.
# Observe the edge connections again.

*Expected Result:*
Normal and Emergency edges should remain separate and display correctly even after a hard refresh or browser refresh.

h2. Problem Statement

Creating work order plans (quotes) fails with a "failed to create" error. The mutation middleware intercepts quote routes and returns 202 Accepted, but the mutation worker isn't processing them correctly. This prevents users from creating new EMP plans or work order quotes.

The mutation system was designed for mobile sync scenarios, but quotes are typically created from the web interface and don't need async processing.

h2. User Acceptance Tests (UATs)

* [ ] Creating a new work order plan (quote) succeeds without errors
* [ ] Quote appears immediately in the database after creation
* [ ] Updating an existing quote works correctly
* [ ] Quote-task mappings can be created and updated
* [ ] No 202 Accepted responses for quote endpoints - should return actual data

column1: FE | Backward Compatibility Issue: ATS Asset Connections (Normal & Emergency) Not Visible on Web
column41:
h3. *Description*

In backward compatibility testing, when an ATS asset is connected from the mobile app (iOS version 1.16) and the same data is checked on the web application (version 1.11), the connections made under *Normal* and *Emergency* are not displayed in the ATS class on the web. This causes a data sync and visibility issue between mobile and web platforms.

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Open the mobile application on *QA* *iOS version 1.16*.
# Navigate to SLD.
# Connect an *ATS asset* with another asset/class.
# Select *Normal* and/or *Emergency* connection type.
# Save the connection successfully.
# Open the *QA web application version 1.11*.
# Navigate to the same SLD and open the ATS class.
# Check the connected assets under *Normal* and *Emergency* sections.

h3. *Expected Result*

The ATS asset connections made from the mobile app (Normal and Emergency) should be visible correctly in the ATS class on the web application.

h3. *Actual Result*

The ATS asset connections made under Normal and Emergency from the mobile app are *not visible* in the ATS class on the web application.

column41:
h2. Problem Statement

When using "Search & Pull" to add assets into SLD views, the connections to those assets don't appear until the page is refreshed. This creates a confusing user experience where users think the pull operation didn't work correctly.

The root cause is that {{node_terminals}} are not included in the {{pull_and_expand_downstream()}} API response. React Flow needs terminal data to render edge handles (connection points) on nodes. Without terminals, edges cannot be drawn to the newly pulled nodes.

Two code paths exist for loading nodes:

# *Initial view load* ({{get_view_full}}) → uses {{Node.to_dict()}} → includes {{node_terminals}} ✅
# *Search & Pull* ({{pull_and_expand_downstream}}) → manual dict → *missing* {{node_terminals}} ❌

h2. User Acceptance Tests (UATs)

* [ ] Right-click a node in an SLD view and select "Search & Pull"
* [ ] Verify pulled nodes appear with their connections immediately (no refresh required)
* [ ] Verify edge handles appear on the correct sides of the pulled nodes
* [ ] Verify existing Search & Pull functionality continues to work (nodes positioned correctly, downstream traversal works)

h2. Execution Plan

h3. Backend: {{/eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py}}

*Location*: Lines 1170-1183 in {{pull_and_expand_downstream()}}

Add {{node_terminals}} to the {{added_nodes}} response dict:

{code:python}active_terminals = [t for t in node.node_terminals if not t.is_deleted]
added_nodes.append({
    'node_id': str(node.id),
    'label': node.label,
    'type': node.type,
    'width': node.width,
    'height': node.height,
    'node_class': str(node.node_class) if node.node_class else None,
    'node_class_name': node_class_names.get(node.node_class) if node.node_class else None,
    'parent_id': str(node.parent_id) if node.parent_id else None,
    'location': node.location,
    'absolute_x': node.x,
    'absolute_y': node.y,
    'node_terminals': [t.to_dict() for t in active_terminals],
}){code}

h3. Frontend: {{/eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/GraphEditor.jsx}}

*Location*: Lines 649-677 (fallback node creation)

Include {{node_terminals}} in the fallback data object:

{code:javascript}data: {
    label: addedNode.label || 'Unknown',
    node_class: addedNode.node_class,
    node_class_name: addedNode.node_class_name,
    location: addedNode.location,
    classId: addedNode.node_class,
    customClass: customClass,
    node_terminals: addedNode.node_terminals || [],  // Add this line
},{code}

Replace the current edge drawing loop that collects and renders length labels with a simplified version that just draws edges:

column41:
[https://app.devrev.ai/egalvanic/works/TKT-496|https://app.devrev.ai/egalvanic/works/TKT-496|smart-link] 

In production, the SLD default view loads *All Nodes* by default, which overwhelms the browser and causes severe performance degradation.
In development, this default has already been set to {{null}}, avoiding the issue.

Root cause:

* Prod configuration defaults SLD initial state to "All Nodes"
* Dev configuration defaults SLD initial state to {{null}}

Hotfix:

* Align production behavior with development
* Set SLD default view to {{null}} in production so no nodes are auto-loaded on initial render

column1: SLD view-based rendering configuration for assets and connections
column41:
Implement a view-specific configuration system for SLD rendering that allows users to control which properties are displayed.

Users must be able to configure, per SLD view:

* Which asset properties are visible (e.g., name, rating, voltage, class-specific fields)
* Which connection properties are visible (e.g., cable length, conductor type, phases)

These settings must apply consistently to:

* SLD canvas node and edge labels
* SLD PDF exports

The configuration is scoped to the view and must not affect other views of the same system.


h3. Acceptance Criteria

* Users can configure visible asset properties per SLD view
* Users can configure visible connection properties per SLD view
* Node and edge labels update based on active view configuration
* PDF exports exactly reflect the active view configuration
* View-level settings are isolated and do not affect other views
* Hidden properties are not removed or mutated in persisted data

column1: Short-term fix for SLD PDF printing.
column41:
When an SLD is exported or printed to PDF, cable lengths must not be rendered in the output. This change is limited strictly to PDF rendering behavior and does not affect:

* SLD canvas rendering
* Stored connection data
* Future configurability

This is a tactical change to address immediate print requirements.


h3. Acceptance Criteria

* Cable lengths do not appear in SLD PDF exports
* Cable lengths continue to exist in underlying data
* SLD canvas remains unchanged
* No regression in other PDF-rendered properties

column1: SLD Performance Optimization
column41:
h2. Overview

This epic tracks all performance improvements for the SLD (Single Line Diagram) system, covering both frontend GraphEditor optimizations and backend API/database improvements.

----

h2. Problem Statement

h3. Symptoms

* Spike in 502 errors on large sites
* UI flickering during SLD loading
* GraphEditor unusable with 500+ nodes
* Slow view switching and drag operations

h3. Affected Sites

* *Northside Hospital Forsyth* (inglett-stubbs.egalvanic.ai) - Flickering issues
* *Alkegen Firetower* (alkegen.egalvanic.ai) - No issues (fewer assets)

----

h2. Root Cause Analysis

h3. Backend Issues

||Issue||Location||Impact||
|N+1 Query in Node Serialization|{{app/models/Node.py:75-87}}|500 nodes = 500 extra queries|
|Lazy Loading for Node Terminals|{{app/models/Node.py:64-68}}|Additional query per node|
|Loop Queries in Batch Updates|{{app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py:693}}|100 updates = 100 queries|
|Missing Database Indexes|Multiple tables|Full table scans|
|No Response Caching|API layer|Repeated DB queries|

h3. Frontend Issues

||Issue||Impact||
|O(n²) View Transformation|Linear array searches per node|
|Synchronous Blocking Operations|Main thread blocked|
|Unnecessary Re-renders|Complete array replacement|
|Memory Pressure|Excessive object spreading|
|Inefficient Data Structures|Linear searches vs hash lookups|

----

h2. Query Count Analysis

||Endpoint||Current (500 nodes)||After Optimization||
|GET /sld-view/<id>/full|~520 queries|~15 queries|
|PUT /positions (100 updates)|101 queries|2 queries|

----

h2. Implementation Plan

h3. Phase 1 - Backend Foundation (Critical)

* *ZP-728:* Fix N+1 Query in Node Serialization (Eager Loading)
* *ZP-730:* Add Database Indexes for SLD Performance

h3. Phase 2 - Frontend Data Structures

* *ZP-723:* Optimize View Transformation Data Structures
* *ZP-727:* Remove Redundant View Transformation Dependencies

h3. Phase 3 - Non-blocking UI & Memory

* *ZP-724:* Implement Deferred View Transformation Updates
* *ZP-726:* Reduce Unnecessary Re-renders in GraphEditor
* *ZP-729:* Implement Bulk Position Updates (Backend)

h3. Phase 4 - Scalability

* *ZP-725:* Implement Incremental Batch Position Updates
* *ZP-731:* Implement Response Caching (Redis)

----

h2. Implementation Tickets

h3. Backend Optimization

||Ticket||Title||Priority||
|ZP-728|Fix N+1 Query in Node Serialization (Eager Loading)|Critical|
|ZP-729|Implement Bulk Position Updates|High|
|ZP-730|Add Database Indexes for SLD Performance|High|
|ZP-731|Implement Response Caching (Redis)|Medium|

h3. Frontend Optimization

||Ticket||Title||Phase||
|ZP-723|Optimize View Transformation Data Structures|Phase 1|
|ZP-724|Implement Deferred View Transformation Updates|Phase 2|
|ZP-725|Implement Incremental Batch Position Updates|Phase 3|
|ZP-726|Reduce Unnecessary Re-renders in GraphEditor|Phase 2|
|ZP-727|Remove Redundant View Transformation Dependencies|Phase 2|

----

h2. Files Requiring Changes

h3. Backend

||File||Change Required||
|{{app/models/Node.py}}|Add eager loading for node_class_rel|
|{{app/models/Node.py:64}}|Change lazy='select' to lazy='joined'|
|{{app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py:693}}|Implement bulk update|
|{{app/controllers/sld_controller.py:306}}|Add joinedload options|
|{{migrations/}}|Add performance indexes|

h3. Frontend

||File||Change Required||
|GraphEditor component|Map-based lookups, deferred updates|
|View transformation hooks|Batch processing, memoization|

----

h2. Success Criteria

* [ ] SLD usable with 1000+ nodes
* [ ] Maintain 60 FPS during drag operations
* [ ] View switching completes without UI freeze
* [ ] Query count reduced by 90%+ for large SLDs
* [ ] Response time < 500ms for view loads
* [ ] No 502 errors on large sites

----

Z | Android | Distribute Children doesn't sync enclosure width/height to backend in View Mode
column41:
h1. Plan: Fix Parent Enclosure Resize in View Mode (FLOW_ONLY_MODE)

h2. Problem Statement

When "Distribute Children" is executed from FLOW_ONLY_MODE (Swift app embedded SLD in a view), child node position changes are sent correctly, but *parent node width/height resize updates are not synced to the backend*.

* *Working*: Child x/y position updates → {{mapping_node_sld_view}} table ✅
* *NOT Working*: Parent width/height resize updates → {{mapping_node_sld_view}} table ❌

h2. Root Cause Analysis

The {{enclosureSizeUpdated}} message IS being sent from React:

{noformat}📤 MESSAGE EVENT: graphUpdate – {type: "enclosureSizeUpdated", payload: {enclosureId: "...", width: 1000, height: 160}}{noformat}

The iOS handler receives this and calls {{updateNodeViewMappingSize()}} which:

# ✅ Updates local SwiftData mapping (width/height set correctly)
# ❌ Calls API but *doesn't send width/height* to backend

*The bug is at* {{WebAppContainerViewWithView.swift:424-428}}:

{code:swift}let response = try await APIClient.shared.updateNodePositionInView(
    viewId: selectedViewId,
    nodeId: nodeId,
    x: existingMapping.x ?? 0,
    y: existingMapping.y ?? 0
    // MISSING: width and height!
){code}

*The API method only accepts x/y* ({{APIClient.swift:2557}}):

{code:swift}func updateNodePositionInView(viewId: UUID, nodeId: UUID, x: Double, y: Double){code}

*The backend endpoint only processes x/y* ({{sld_view_routes.py:109-121}}):

{code:python}def update_node_position_in_view(view_id, node_id):
    x = data.get('x')
    y = data.get('y')  # No width/height handling{code}

*However, the database model already supports width/height* ({{Mappings.py:431-433}}):

{code:python}width = db.Column(db.Float, nullable=True)
height = db.Column(db.Float, nullable=True){code}

h2. Fix: End-to-End Width/Height Support

h3. 1. Backend Route ({{sld_view_routes.py:109-121}})

Update to accept optional width/height:

{code:python}@sld_view_bp.route('/sld-view/<uuid:view_id>/nodes/<uuid:node_id>/position', methods=['PUT'])
def update_node_position_in_view(view_id, node_id):
    """
    Update a node's position within a specific view.
    Expected body: { x: float, y: float, width?: float, height?: float }
    """
    data = request.get_json()
    x = data.get('x')
    y = data.get('y')
    width = data.get('width')    # NEW
    height = data.get('height')  # NEW
    result, status_code = SLDViewController.update_node_position_in_view(
        view_id, str(node_id), x, y, width, height
    )
    return jsonify(result), status_code{code}

h3. 2. Backend Controller ({{sld_view_controller.py:751-781}})

Update to handle width/height:

{code:python}@staticmethod
def update_node_position_in_view(view_id, node_id, x, y, width=None, height=None):
    # ... existing code ...
    mapping.x = x
    mapping.y = y
    if width is not None:
        mapping.width = width
    if height is not None:
        mapping.height = height
    mapping.modified_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    # ... rest of code ...{code}

h3. 3. iOS APIClient ({{APIClient.swift:2557-2572}})

Update to accept optional width/height:

{code:swift}func updateNodePositionInView(
    viewId: UUID,
    nodeId: UUID,
    x: Double,
    y: Double,
    width: Double? = nil,
    height: Double? = nil
) async throws -> URLResponse {
    var payload: [String: Any] = ["x": x, "y": y]
    if let w = width { payload["width"] = w }
    if let h = height { payload["height"] = h }
    // ... rest of request ...
}{code}

h3. 4. iOS WebAppContainerViewWithView ({{WebAppContainerViewWithView.swift:421-429}})

Update call site to pass width/height:

{code:swift}let response = try await APIClient.shared.updateNodePositionInView(
    viewId: selectedViewId,
    nodeId: nodeId,
    x: existingMapping.x ?? 0,
    y: existingMapping.y ?? 0,
    width: width,    // NEW - pass through from enclosureSizeUpdated
    height: height   // NEW - pass through from enclosureSizeUpdated
){code}

h2. Files to Modify

||Layer||File||Change||
|Backend Route|{{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/sld_view_routes.py:109-121}}|Accept width/height params|
|Backend Controller|{{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py:751-781}}|Set width/height on mapping|
|iOS API|{{eg-pz-mobile-iOS/.../APIClient.swift:2557-2572}}|Add width/height params|
|iOS View|{{eg-pz-mobile-iOS/.../WebAppContainerViewWithView.swift:421-429}}|Pass width/height to API|

h2. Verification Steps

# Run backend locally: {{cd eg-pz-backend && ./run_production_mode.sh}}
# Open iOS app with SLD in view mode
# Select an enclosure and trigger "Distribute Children"
# Check Xcode console for {{[MAPPING DEBUG] updateNodeViewMappingSize called}}
# Verify API call includes width/height in payload
# Query database to confirm {{mapping_node_sld_view}} has updated width/height:
{code:sql}SELECT node_id, x, y, width, height
FROM mapping_node_sld_view
WHERE node_id = '<enclosure-uuid>';{code}

column1: Z | Android | Node Creation Terminal Instantiation - Ensure all entry points create NodeTerminals with stable UUIDs

h1. ZP-612: Node Creation Terminal Instantiation Implementation Plan

h2. Overview

Ensure all node creation entry points (web and mobile) create NodeTerminal instances with stable UUIDs at creation time, enabling offline edge creation and consistent terminal references across platforms.

h2. Key Finding: iOS Sync Infrastructure Ready

* *No new SyncTarget needed*: Terminals sync as part of node sync
* {{APIClient.createNode()}} already includes {{node_terminals}} in payload (line 573)
* {{NodeTerminal.toDTO()}} method exists for serialization
* Full model chain available: {{NodeClass → NodeOrientation → NodeOrientationTerminal}} (blueprints)

h2. Current State Summary

||Component||Status||Notes||
|{{useNodeCreation.jsx}}|✅ Done|Creates terminals via {{createNodeTerminals()}}|
|{{terminalHelpers.js}}|✅ Done|Has {{createNodeTerminals()}} and {{serializeTerminalsForSync()}}|
|Backend {{/node/create}}|✅ Done|Accepts {{node_terminals}} array, falls back to auto-creation|
|{{WebAppContainerViewWithView.swift}}|✅ Done|Creates NodeTerminals from React Flow messages|
|iOS {{NodeTerminal.swift}}|✅ Done|Model with {{toDTO()}} exists|
|iOS {{APIClient.createNode}}|✅ Done|Includes {{node_terminals}} in payload|
|{{AddAssetDialog.jsx}}|❌ Gap|No terminal creation|
|{{QuickCreateChildDialog.jsx}}|❌ Gap|No terminal creation in bulk loop|
|{{AddAssetView.swift}}|❌ Gap|No terminal creation|
|{{QuickCountView.swift}}|❌ Gap|No terminal creation|
|{{PhotoWalkthroughView.swift}}|❌ Gap|No terminal creation|
|{{bulk_upload_controller.py}}|❌ Gap|No terminal creation|

----

h2. Phase 1: Web Frontend Secondary Flows

h3. 1.1 AddAssetDialog.jsx

*File:* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/components/AddAssetDialog.jsx}}

*Changes:*

# Import terminal helpers:
{code:javascript}import { v4 as uuidv4 } from 'uuid';
import { createNodeTerminals, serializeTerminalsForSync } from '../sld/src/utils/terminalHelpers';{code}
# In {{handleSubmit()}} (around line 210), before API call:
{code:javascript}const nodeId = uuidv4();
const nodeTerminals = selectedNodeClass?.node_orientation
  ? createNodeTerminals(nodeId, selectedNodeClass)
  : [];{code}
# Update payload to include node ID and terminals:
{code:javascript}const payload = {
  id: nodeId,  // Frontend-generated UUID
  ...formData,
  sld_id: sldId,
  // ... existing fields
  node_terminals: serializeTerminalsForSync(nodeTerminals),
};{code}

h3. 1.2 QuickCreateChildDialog.jsx

*File:* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/components/unified/QuickCreateChildDialog.jsx}}

*Changes:*

# Import terminal helpers and uuid at top of file
# In the creation loop (lines 201-261), for each OCP:
{code:javascript}const nodeId = uuidv4();
const nodeTerminals = nodeClass?.node_orientation
  ? createNodeTerminals(nodeId, nodeClass)
  : [];

const payload = {
  id: nodeId,
  sld_id: sldId,
  parent_id: parentNodeId,
  label,
  node_class: nodeClass.id,
  // ... existing fields
  node_terminals: serializeTerminalsForSync(nodeTerminals),
};{code}

----

h2. Phase 2: iOS Mobile Flows

h3. 2.1 Create TerminalInstantiationService.swift (New File)

*File:* {{eg-pz-mobile-iOS/Egalvanic PZ/Services/TerminalInstantiationService.swift}}

Centralized service for creating terminals from node class blueprints.

*Note:* No separate SyncOp needed for terminals - they sync with the parent node via {{APIClient.createNode()}} which already includes {{node_terminals}} in the payload.

{code:swift}import SwiftData

class TerminalInstantiationService {
    /// Creates NodeTerminal instances from a node class's orientation blueprints.
    /// Terminals will sync with the node - no separate sync operation needed.
    @MainActor
    static func createTerminalsForNode(
        node: NodeV2,
        nodeClass: NodeClass?,
        modelContext: ModelContext
    ) -> [NodeTerminal] {
        guard let nodeClass = nodeClass,
              let orientation = nodeClass.node_orientation else {
            return []
        }

        let blueprints = orientation.orientation_terminals.filter { !$0.is_deleted }
        var terminals: [NodeTerminal] = []

        for blueprint in blueprints {
            let terminal = NodeTerminal(
                id: UUID(),
                node: node,
                node_orientation_terminal_id: blueprint.id,
                handle_code: blueprint.handle_code,
                label: blueprint.label,
                side: blueprint.side,
                position: blueprint.position,
                offset_percent: blueprint.offset_percent,
                color: blueprint.color,
                show_label: blueprint.show_label
            )
            modelContext.insert(terminal)
            node.node_terminals.append(terminal)
            terminals.append(terminal)
        }

        return terminals
    }
}{code}

h3. 2.2 AddAssetView.swift

*File:* {{eg-pz-mobile-iOS/Egalvanic PZ/Views/Assets/AddAssetView.swift}}

*Changes in* {{performSave()}} (lines 867-933):

After creating {{newNode}} but before calling {{onSave()}}:

{code:swift}// Create terminals from node class orientation
let _ = TerminalInstantiationService.createTerminalsForNode(
    node: newNode,
    nodeClass: nodeClass,
    modelContext: modelContext
){code}

h3. 2.3 QuickCountView.swift

*File:* {{eg-pz-mobile-iOS/Egalvanic PZ/Views/QuickCount/QuickCountView.swift}}

*Changes in* {{createAssets()}} (lines 346-534):

After each {{NodeV2}} creation and insert:

{code:swift}// For parent assets (around line 385)
modelContext.insert(asset)
let _ = TerminalInstantiationService.createTerminalsForNode(
    node: asset,
    nodeClass: entry.nodeClass,
    modelContext: modelContext
)

// For OCP children (around line 441)
modelContext.insert(ocpNode)
let _ = TerminalInstantiationService.createTerminalsForNode(
    node: ocpNode,
    nodeClass: ocpClass,
    modelContext: modelContext
){code}

h3. 2.4 PhotoWalkthroughView.swift

*File:* {{eg-pz-mobile-iOS/Egalvanic PZ/Views/PhotoWalkthrough/PhotoWalkthroughView.swift}}

*Changes in* {{createAllAssets()}} (lines 900-1044):

After each {{NodeV2}} creation:

{code:swift}// For parent nodes (around line 936)
modelContext.insert(parentNode)
let _ = TerminalInstantiationService.createTerminalsForNode(
    node: parentNode,
    nodeClass: parent.nodeClass,
    modelContext: modelContext
)

// For child nodes (around line 991)
modelContext.insert(childNode)
let _ = TerminalInstantiationService.createTerminalsForNode(
    node: childNode,
    nodeClass: child.nodeClass,
    modelContext: modelContext
){code}

----

h2. Phase 3: Backend Bulk Upload

h3. 3.1 bulk_upload_controller.py

*File:* {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/bulk_upload_controller.py}}

*Changes after node creation loop (around line 755):*

{code:python}# After: db.session.add(node)
# Add terminal creation for each node with a node_class
if node_data.get('node_class_id'):
    NodeController._create_terminals_for_node(
        node.id,
        uuid.UUID(node_data['node_class_id'])
    ){code}

Import at top:

{code:python}from app.controllers.node_controller import NodeController{code}

----

h2. Files to Modify

|{{eg-pz-frontend/src/components/AddAssetDialog.jsx}}|Web|Add terminal creation|
|{{eg-pz-frontend/src/components/unified/QuickCreateChildDialog.jsx}}|Web|Add terminal creation in loop|

h3. Web Frontend Testing

column1: Z | Android | SLD Views Phase 1: Load, Display, Back Navigation, and Link Navigation for Android
column41:
h1. SLD Views - Load, Display, Back Navigation, and Link Navigation, Online/Offline CRUD Operations

h2. Goal

Load all view data in the mobile upsert, show a view selection screen in iOS, load selected view in FLOW_ONLY_MODE SLD, add back button to return to view selection, and support SLD link navigation between views. Also, allow nodes and edges to be added, resized, and moved within views.

h2. Critical Constraint: FLOW_ONLY_MODE Isolation

*ALL frontend changes must be wrapped in* {{FLOW_ONLY_MODE}} conditionals. We must not modify how the SLD works in non-FLOW_ONLY_MODE at all. The desktop/web SLD behavior must remain completely unchanged.

----

h2. 1. Backend: Add View Data to {{get_sld_full_v2}}

*File:* {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/sld_controller.py}}

Add to the response:

* {{sld_links}} (top-level array) - cross-view navigation links
* {{mappings.node_sld_view}} - node positions per view
* {{mappings.edge_sld_view}} - edge routing per view

*Changes:*

# Import {{SLDLink}}, {{MappingNodeSLDView}}, {{MappingEdgeSLDView}} from models
# Query these entities after fetching {{sld_views}}
# Add to response dictionary

----

h2. 2. iOS: Add DTOs for View Data

*New/Modified Files:*

* {{SLDViewDTO.swift}} (new) - DTO for SLD views
* {{SLDLinkDTO.swift}} (new) - DTO for cross-view links
* {{SLDMappingsDTO.swift}} (modify) - Add {{node_sld_view}} and {{edge_sld_view}} mapping DTOs
* {{SLDDTO.swift}} (modify) - Add {{sld_views}} and {{sld_links}} arrays

*DTO Structures:*

{code:swift}struct SLDViewDTO: Codable {
    var id: String
    var sld_id: String
    var name: String
    var description: String?
    var view_type: String
    var is_default: Bool
    var is_deleted: Bool
}

struct SLDLinkDTO: Codable {
    var id: String
    var source_sld_view_id: String
    var target_sld_view_id: String
    var source_node_id: String?
    var target_node_id: String?
    // ... position fields
}

struct MappingNodeSLDViewDTO: Codable {
    var id: String
    var node_id: String
    var sld_view_id: String
    var x: Double?

h1. ZP-612: Node Creation Terminal Instantiation Implementation Plan

h2. Overview

Ensure all node creation entry points (web and mobile) create NodeTerminal instances with stable UUIDs at creation time, enabling offline edge creation and consistent terminal references across platforms.

h2. Key Finding: iOS Sync Infrastructure Ready

* *No new SyncTarget needed*: Terminals sync as part of node sync
* {{APIClient.createNode()}} already includes {{node_terminals}} in payload (line 573)
* {{NodeTerminal.toDTO()}} method exists for serialization
* Full model chain available: {{NodeClass → NodeOrientation → NodeOrientationTerminal}} (blueprints)

h2. Current State Summary

||Component||Status||Notes||
|{{useNodeCreation.jsx}}|✅ Done|Creates terminals via {{createNodeTerminals()}}|
|{{terminalHelpers.js}}|✅ Done|Has {{createNodeTerminals()}} and {{serializeTerminalsForSync()}}|
|Backend {{/node/create}}|✅ Done|Accepts {{node_terminals}} array, falls back to auto-creation|
|{{WebAppContainerViewWithView.swift}}|✅ Done|Creates NodeTerminals from React Flow messages|
|iOS {{NodeTerminal.swift}}|✅ Done|Model with {{toDTO()}} exists|
|iOS {{APIClient.createNode}}|✅ Done|Includes {{node_terminals}} in payload|
|{{AddAssetDialog.jsx}}|❌ Gap|No terminal creation|
|{{QuickCreateChildDialog.jsx}}|❌ Gap|No terminal creation in bulk loop|
|{{AddAssetView.swift}}|❌ Gap|No terminal creation|
|{{QuickCountView.swift}}|❌ Gap|No terminal creation|
|{{PhotoWalkthroughView.swift}}|❌ Gap|No terminal creation|
|{{bulk_upload_controller.py}}|❌ Gap|No terminal creation|

----

h2. Phase 1: Web Frontend Secondary Flows

h3. 1.1 AddAssetDialog.jsx

*File:* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/components/AddAssetDialog.jsx}}

*Changes:*

# Import terminal helpers:
{code:javascript}import { v4 as uuidv4 } from 'uuid';
import { createNodeTerminals, serializeTerminalsForSync } from '../sld/src/utils/terminalHelpers';{code}
# In {{handleSubmit()}} (around line 210), before API call:
{code:javascript}const nodeId = uuidv4();
const nodeTerminals = selectedNodeClass?.node_orientation
  ? createNodeTerminals(nodeId, selectedNodeClass)
  : [];{code}
# Update payload to include node ID and terminals:
{code:javascript}const payload = {
  id: nodeId,  // Frontend-generated UUID
  ...formData,
  sld_id: sldId,
  // ... existing fields
  node_terminals: serializeTerminalsForSync(nodeTerminals),
};{code}

h3. 1.2 QuickCreateChildDialog.jsx

*File:* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/components/unified/QuickCreateChildDialog.jsx}}

*Changes:*

# Import terminal helpers and uuid at top of file
# In the creation loop (lines 201-261), for each OCP:
{code:javascript}const nodeId = uuidv4();
const nodeTerminals = nodeClass?.node_orientation
  ? createNodeTerminals(nodeId, nodeClass)
  : [];

const payload = {
  id: nodeId,
  sld_id: sldId,
  parent_id: parentNodeId,
  label,
  node_class: nodeClass.id,
  // ... existing fields
  node_terminals: serializeTerminalsForSync(nodeTerminals),
};{code}

----

h2. Phase 2: iOS Mobile Flows

h3. 2.1 Create TerminalInstantiationService.swift (New File)

*File:* {{eg-pz-mobile-iOS/Egalvanic PZ/Services/TerminalInstantiationService.swift}}

Centralized service for creating terminals from node class blueprints.

*Note:* No separate SyncOp needed for terminals - they sync with the parent node via {{APIClient.createNode()}} which already includes {{node_terminals}} in the payload.

{code:swift}import SwiftData

class TerminalInstantiationService {
    /// Creates NodeTerminal instances from a node class's orientation blueprints.
    /// Terminals will sync with the node - no separate sync operation needed.
    @MainActor
    static func createTerminalsForNode(
        node: NodeV2,
        nodeClass: NodeClass?,
        modelContext: ModelContext
    ) -> [NodeTerminal] {
        guard let nodeClass = nodeClass,
              let orientation = nodeClass.node_orientation else {
            return []
        }

        let blueprints = orientation.orientation_terminals.filter { !$0.is_deleted }
        var terminals: [NodeTerminal] = []

        for blueprint in blueprints {
            let terminal = NodeTerminal(
                id: UUID(),
                node: node,
                node_orientation_terminal_id: blueprint.id,
                handle_code: blueprint.handle_code,
                label: blueprint.label,
                side: blueprint.side,
                position: blueprint.position,
                offset_percent: blueprint.offset_percent,
                color: blueprint.color,
                show_label: blueprint.show_label
            )
            modelContext.insert(terminal)
            node.node_terminals.append(terminal)
            terminals.append(terminal)
        }

        return terminals
    }
}{code}

h3. 2.2 AddAssetView.swift

*File:* {{eg-pz-mobile-iOS/Egalvanic PZ/Views/Assets/AddAssetView.swift}}

*Changes in* {{performSave()}} (lines 867-933):

{code:swift}// Create terminals from node class orientation

    node: newNode,
    nodeClass: nodeClass,
    modelContext: modelContext
){code}

    node: asset,

    modelContext: modelContext

    node: ocpNode,

    modelContext: modelContext

h1. Plan: Add "Create Link" to Child Asset Context Menu

h2. Problem

The "Create Link" option is missing from the node context menu for child assets (nodes inside enclosures). This is because of an explicit {{!node.parentId}} condition that hides the menu item for any node with a parent.

h2. Root Cause

*File:* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/NodeContextMenu.jsx:168}}

{code:javascript}const showCreateLink = activeViewId && activeViewId !== 'all' && onCreateLink && !node.parentId;{code}

The {{!node.parentId}} condition filters out child nodes. Child assets have a {{parentId}} when they are placed inside an enclosure.

h2. Fix

Remove the {{!node.parentId}} condition from line 168:

{code:javascript}// Before
const showCreateLink = activeViewId && activeViewId !== 'all' && onCreateLink && !node.parentId;

// After
const showCreateLink = activeViewId && activeViewId !== 'all' && onCreateLink;{code}

# {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/NodeContextMenu.jsx}} (line 168)

column1: Z | Android | Fix SLD Links - Display, Navigation, Creation, and Offline Sync
Z | Android | Fix node jitter when adding/removing nodes from enclosures in iOS SLD
column41:
h1. Plan: Fix Node Positions Reset When Adding/Removing from Enclosures in View Mode

h2. Problem Summary

When nodes are *added to or removed from* enclosures (boxes) in the iOS SLD while in a *specific view*, all node positions reset to their base {{node.x/y}} values instead of using the view-specific positions from {{mapping_node_sld_view}}.

*Symptoms:*

* Node jumps to wrong position
* Node may disappear briefly
* Viewport shifts unexpectedly
* Works fine in web app, broken in mobile

*Key insight:* The database has correct positions. The issue is in how the DTO is created when sending data back to React.

h2. Root Cause

*Location:* {{eg-pz-mobile-iOS/Egalvanic PZ/Views/SLD/WebAppContainerViewWithView.swift:803-811}}

{code:swift}private func updateWebViewGraph() {
    let dto = SLDService.shared.createDTO(
        forSLDId: sld.id,
        nodes: nodesInView,
        edges: edgesInView,
        customName: "updated-bridge-view"
    )
    WebViewBridge.updateGraph(with: dto, animated: true)
}{code}

*The problem:* This calls {{createDTO}} *without* the {{nodePositionOverrides}} parameter!

There are TWO versions of {{createDTO}} in {{SLDService+DTO.swift}}:

# *Line 200-268*: {{createDTO(forSLDId:nodes:edges:customName:)}} - Uses {{node.x}} and {{node.y}} directly (BASE positions)
# *Line 281+*: {{createDTO(forSLDId:nodes:edges:customName:nodePositionOverrides:edgeRoutingOverrides:)}} - Takes view-specific position overrides from mappings

{{WebAppContainerViewWithView.updateWebViewGraph()}} is calling version #1, which sends base positions to React instead of the view-specific positions.

----

h2. Solution: Use Existing Override Properties in createDTO

The file already has computed properties that build the overrides (lines 60-77):

* {{nodePositionOverrides}} - builds from {{nodeViewMappings}}
* {{edgeRoutingOverrides}} - builds from {{edgeViewMappings}}

Simply pass them to the {{createDTO}} call.

h3. Implementation

Change {{updateWebViewGraph()}} from:

{code:swift}private func updateWebViewGraph() {
    let dto = SLDService.shared.createDTO(
        forSLDId: sld.id,
        nodes: nodesInView,
        edges: edgesInView,
        customName: "updated-bridge-view"
    )
    WebViewBridge.updateGraph(with: dto, animated: true)
}{code}

To:

{code:swift}private func updateWebViewGraph() {
    let dto = SLDService.shared.createDTO(
        forSLDId: sld.id,
        nodes: nodesInView,
        edges: edgesInView,
        customName: "updated-bridge-view",
        nodePositionOverrides: nodePositionOverrides,
        edgeRoutingOverrides: edgeRoutingOverrides
    )
    WebViewBridge.updateGraph(with: dto, animated: true)
}{code}

*That's it - a 2-line change.*

----

h2. Files to Modify

# {{eg-pz-mobile-iOS/Egalvanic PZ/Views/SLD/WebAppContainerViewWithView.swift}}
#* Lines 803-811: Add {{nodePositionOverrides}} and {{edgeRoutingOverrides}} parameters to {{createDTO}} call

----

h2. Verification Plan

# *Manual Testing in iOS Simulator (in a specific view, not "all"):*
#* *Remove* a node from an enclosure → all nodes should remain in their view-specific positions
#* *Add* a node to an enclosure → all nodes should remain in their view-specific positions
#* No viewport shift or position resets in either case
# *Compare with Web:*
#* Same operations in web app should produce identical results
# *Verify Persistence:*
#* Add/remove node from enclosure, close and reopen the SLD
#* Positions should be correct on reload

column1: Child nodes positioned at (0,0) when adding parent to view from all nodes
column41:
h1. Plan: Fix Child Node Positions When Adding to View from All Nodes

h2. Problem

When multi-adding nodes to a view from the "all nodes view", parent nodes keep their original x,y positions from the main nodes table, but *child nodes do not*. Child nodes are being placed at position (0, 0) instead of their correct absolute positions.

h2. Root Cause

In {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py}} (lines 581-585), when a parent node (enclosure) is added to a view, its children are auto-included. However, the code explicitly sets their position to {{x=0, y=0}}:

{code:python}elif node_id in auto_included_children:
    # Child nodes store x=0, y=0 in the mapping table
    # Frontend calculates actual position using:
    # parent's view position + child's relative position (from nodes table)
    x, y = 0, 0{code}

The comment suggests the frontend should calculate the position dynamically, but the frontend ({{GraphEditor.jsx}} lines 3511-3513) simply uses the mapping values directly:

{code:javascript}position: mapping && mapping.x != null && mapping.y != null
  ? { x: mapping.x, y: mapping.y }
  : { ...baseNode.position }{code}

Since {{0}} is not null, child nodes get positioned at (0, 0).

h2. Solution

Modify the backend to use {{_get_absolute_position(node)}} for auto-included children, consistent with how explicitly selected nodes are handled.

h2. Files to Modify

h3. {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py}}

*Line 581-585* - Change from:

{code:python}elif node_id in auto_included_children:
    # Child nodes store x=0, y=0 in the mapping table
    # Frontend calculates actual position using:
    # parent's view position + child's relative position (from nodes table)
    x, y = 0, 0{code}

To:

{code:python}elif node_id in auto_included_children:
    # Use absolute position for auto-included children (same as explicit nodes)
    x, y = SLDViewController._get_absolute_position(node){code}

h2. Verification

# Start the backend locally: {{cd eg-pz-backend && ./run_production_mode.sh}}
# Start the frontend: {{cd eg-pz-frontend && npm run dev}}
# Navigate to an SLD with parent nodes (enclosures) containing child nodes
# Go to the "all nodes" view
# Multi-select a parent node (enclosure) - the children will be auto-included
# Add to a new or existing view
# Verify that both the parent AND child nodes appear at their correct absolute positions (same as they appear in the all nodes view)

column1: Distribute Children doesn't sync enclosure width/height to backend in View Mode
column41:
h1. Plan: Fix Parent Enclosure Resize in View Mode (FLOW_ONLY_MODE)

h2. Problem Statement

When "Distribute Children" is executed from FLOW_ONLY_MODE (Swift app embedded SLD in a view), child node position changes are sent correctly, but *parent node width/height resize updates are not synced to the backend*.

* *Working*: Child x/y position updates → {{mapping_node_sld_view}} table ✅
* *NOT Working*: Parent width/height resize updates → {{mapping_node_sld_view}} table ❌

h2. Root Cause Analysis

The {{enclosureSizeUpdated}} message IS being sent from React:

{noformat}📤 MESSAGE EVENT: graphUpdate – {type: "enclosureSizeUpdated", payload: {enclosureId: "...", width: 1000, height: 160}}{noformat}

The iOS handler receives this and calls {{updateNodeViewMappingSize()}} which:

# ✅ Updates local SwiftData mapping (width/height set correctly)
# ❌ Calls API but *doesn't send width/height* to backend

*The bug is at* {{WebAppContainerViewWithView.swift:424-428}}:

{code:swift}let response = try await APIClient.shared.updateNodePositionInView(
    viewId: selectedViewId,
    nodeId: nodeId,
    x: existingMapping.x ?? 0,
    y: existingMapping.y ?? 0
    // MISSING: width and height!
){code}

*The API method only accepts x/y* ({{APIClient.swift:2557}}):

{code:swift}func updateNodePositionInView(viewId: UUID, nodeId: UUID, x: Double, y: Double){code}

*The backend endpoint only processes x/y* ({{sld_view_routes.py:109-121}}):

{code:python}def update_node_position_in_view(view_id, node_id):
    x = data.get('x')
    y = data.get('y')  # No width/height handling{code}

*However, the database model already supports width/height* ({{Mappings.py:431-433}}):

{code:python}width = db.Column(db.Float, nullable=True)
height = db.Column(db.Float, nullable=True){code}

h2. Fix: End-to-End Width/Height Support

h3. 1. Backend Route ({{sld_view_routes.py:109-121}})

Update to accept optional width/height:

{code:python}@sld_view_bp.route('/sld-view/<uuid:view_id>/nodes/<uuid:node_id>/position', methods=['PUT'])
def update_node_position_in_view(view_id, node_id):
    """
    Update a node's position within a specific view.
    Expected body: { x: float, y: float, width?: float, height?: float }
    """
    data = request.get_json()
    x = data.get('x')
    y = data.get('y')
    width = data.get('width')    # NEW
    height = data.get('height')  # NEW
    result, status_code = SLDViewController.update_node_position_in_view(
        view_id, str(node_id), x, y, width, height
    )
    return jsonify(result), status_code{code}

h3. 2. Backend Controller ({{sld_view_controller.py:751-781}})

Update to handle width/height:

{code:python}@staticmethod
def update_node_position_in_view(view_id, node_id, x, y, width=None, height=None):
    # ... existing code ...
    mapping.x = x
    mapping.y = y
    if width is not None:
        mapping.width = width
    if height is not None:
        mapping.height = height
    mapping.modified_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    # ... rest of code ...{code}

h3. 3. iOS APIClient ({{APIClient.swift:2557-2572}})

Update to accept optional width/height:

{code:swift}func updateNodePositionInView(
    viewId: UUID,
    nodeId: UUID,
    x: Double,
    y: Double,
    width: Double? = nil,
    height: Double? = nil
) async throws -> URLResponse {
    var payload: [String: Any] = ["x": x, "y": y]
    if let w = width { payload["width"] = w }
    if let h = height { payload["height"] = h }
    // ... rest of request ...
}{code}

h3. 4. iOS WebAppContainerViewWithView ({{WebAppContainerViewWithView.swift:421-429}})

Update call site to pass width/height:

{code:swift}let response = try await APIClient.shared.updateNodePositionInView(
    viewId: selectedViewId,
    nodeId: nodeId,
    x: existingMapping.x ?? 0,
    y: existingMapping.y ?? 0,
    width: width,    // NEW - pass through from enclosureSizeUpdated
    height: height   // NEW - pass through from enclosureSizeUpdated
){code}

h2. Files to Modify

||Layer||File||Change||
|Backend Route|{{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/sld_view_routes.py:109-121}}|Accept width/height params|
|Backend Controller|{{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py:751-781}}|Set width/height on mapping|
|iOS API|{{eg-pz-mobile-iOS/.../APIClient.swift:2557-2572}}|Add width/height params|
|iOS View|{{eg-pz-mobile-iOS/.../WebAppContainerViewWithView.swift:421-429}}|Pass width/height to API|

column1: SLD Bottom Toolbar: Add "More" menu for small screens and reposition Lock button
column41:
h1. SLD Bottom Toolbar: Small Screen "More" Menu & Lock Button Repositioning

h2. Requirements

h3. 1. Small Screen "More" Menu (FLOW_ONLY_MODE only)

On small screens (< 640px) when in FLOW_ONLY_MODE, group these 4 toggles into a "More" dropdown menu:

* Status Badges toggle
* Selection Highlight toggle
* Lineage Trace toggle
* Edge Labels toggle

h3. 2. Lock Button Repositioning (All Cases)

Move the Lock button to the right-most position in the toolbar in ALL cases (not just small screen).

h2. Current Button Order

# Filter button
# Search button
# Divider
# Status Badges toggle
# Selection Highlight toggle
# Lineage Trace toggle
# Edge Labels toggle
# Lock Editing toggle ← *Move to end*
# MiniMap toggle (conditional)
# Fit View button (conditional)

h2. Implementation Plan

h3. File: {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/BottomControls.jsx}}

h4. Step 1: Add State for "More" Menu

{code:javascript}const [moreMenuOpen, setMoreMenuOpen] = React.useState(false);
const moreButtonRef = React.useRef(null);{code}

h4. Step 2: Detect Small Screen

Use CSS media query or window width detection:

{code:javascript}const [isSmallScreen, setIsSmallScreen] = React.useState(window.innerWidth < 640);

React.useEffect(() => {
  const handleResize = () => setIsSmallScreen(window.innerWidth < 640);
  window.addEventListener('resize', handleResize);
  return () => window.removeEventListener('resize', handleResize);
}, []);{code}

h4. Step 3: Create "More" Button Component

When {{isSmallScreen && flowOnlyMode}}, show a "More" button with a dropdown icon that opens a popup menu.

h4. Step 4: Create More Menu Dropdown

Position a dropdown menu below the "More" button containing the 4 toggles. Use CSS positioning similar to existing context menus.

Options for dropdown implementation:

* *Option A*: Custom CSS-based dropdown (matches existing SLD patterns)
* *Option B*: Material-UI Menu/Popover (cleaner, but adds dependency to SLD module)

*Recommendation*: Use custom CSS dropdown to match existing SLD patterns.

h4. Step 5: Reorder JSX Elements

Move the Lock toggle to appear after MiniMap and Fit View (rightmost position).

New order:

# Filter button
# Search button
# Divider
# *If small screen + flowOnlyMode*: "More" button (contains 4 toggles)
# *If NOT (small screen + flowOnlyMode)*: Show 4 toggles individually
# MiniMap toggle (conditional)
# Fit View button (conditional)
# *Lock Editing toggle* ← Moved to end

h3. File: {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/BottomControls.css}}

Add styles for:

* {{.more-menu-button}} - Button with 3-dot icon or "More" text
* {{.more-menu-dropdown}} - Dropdown container
* {{.more-menu-dropdown.open}} - Visible state
* {{.more-menu-item}} - Individual toggle items in dropdown

Match existing styling patterns from {{.control-button}} and {{.control-toggle}}.

h2. Key Design Decisions

# *Only in FLOW_ONLY_MODE*: The "More" menu only appears when {{flowOnlyMode={true}}} AND screen is small
# *Regular mode behavior unchanged*: On large screens or non-flow-only mode, all buttons show individually
# *Lock always rightmost*: In ALL cases, Lock button is the last toggle/button
# *Close on outside click*: Clicking outside the dropdown closes it (like existing context menus)
# *Close on selection*: Clicking a toggle inside the dropdown keeps it open (toggles are independent)

h2. Responsive Breakpoint

Use the existing 640px breakpoint already defined in BottomControls.css:

{code:css}@media (max-width: 640px) {
  /* Small screen styles */
}{code}

h2. Verification

h3. Test Cases

# *Large screen (> 640px)*:
#* All buttons visible individually
#* Lock button is rightmost
#* Works in both flowOnlyMode and regular mode
# *Small screen (< 640px) + flowOnlyMode*:
#* "More" button visible
#* Status Badges, Selection Highlight, Lineage Trace, Edge Labels hidden
#* Clicking "More" shows dropdown with 4 toggles
#* Lock button is rightmost
# *Small screen (< 640px) + NOT flowOnlyMode*:
#* All buttons visible individually (no "More" menu)
#* Lock button is rightmost
# *Dropdown behavior*:
#* Clicking "More" opens dropdown
#* Clicking outside closes dropdown
#* Toggles work correctly from dropdown
#* Active states visible in dropdown

h3. Manual Testing Steps

# Open SLD in FLOW_ONLY_MODE on small screen (< 640px)
# Verify "More" button appears
# Click "More" - dropdown should open
# Toggle each of the 4 options - verify they work
# Click outside - dropdown should close
# Verify Lock button is rightmost in all scenarios
# Test on large screen - all buttons individual, Lock rightmost

column41:
h1. Plan: Add "Create Link" to Child Asset Context Menu

h2. Problem

The "Create Link" option is missing from the node context menu for child assets (nodes inside enclosures). This is because of an explicit {{!node.parentId}} condition that hides the menu item for any node with a parent.

h2. Root Cause

*File:* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/NodeContextMenu.jsx:168}}

{code:javascript}const showCreateLink = activeViewId && activeViewId !== 'all' && onCreateLink && !node.parentId;{code}

The {{!node.parentId}} condition filters out child nodes. Child assets have a {{parentId}} when they are placed inside an enclosure.

h2. Fix

Remove the {{!node.parentId}} condition from line 168:

{code:javascript}// Before
const showCreateLink = activeViewId && activeViewId !== 'all' && onCreateLink && !node.parentId;

// After
const showCreateLink = activeViewId && activeViewId !== 'all' && onCreateLink;{code}

h2. Files to Modify

# {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/NodeContextMenu.jsx}} (line 168)

h2. Verification

column1: Z | iOS | Fix SLD Links - Display, Navigation, Creation, and Offline Sync
Fix node jitter when adding/removing nodes from enclosures in iOS SLD
column41:
h1. Plan: Fix Node Positions Reset When Adding/Removing from Enclosures in View Mode

h2. Problem Summary

When nodes are *added to or removed from* enclosures (boxes) in the iOS SLD while in a *specific view*, all node positions reset to their base {{node.x/y}} values instead of using the view-specific positions from {{mapping_node_sld_view}}.

*Symptoms:*

* Node jumps to wrong position
* Node may disappear briefly
* Viewport shifts unexpectedly
* Works fine in web app, broken in mobile

*Key insight:* The database has correct positions. The issue is in how the DTO is created when sending data back to React.

h2. Root Cause

*Location:* {{eg-pz-mobile-iOS/Egalvanic PZ/Views/SLD/WebAppContainerViewWithView.swift:803-811}}

{code:swift}private func updateWebViewGraph() {
    let dto = SLDService.shared.createDTO(
        forSLDId: sld.id,
        nodes: nodesInView,
        edges: edgesInView,
        customName: "updated-bridge-view"
    )
    WebViewBridge.updateGraph(with: dto, animated: true)
}{code}

*The problem:* This calls {{createDTO}} *without* the {{nodePositionOverrides}} parameter!

There are TWO versions of {{createDTO}} in {{SLDService+DTO.swift}}:

# *Line 200-268*: {{createDTO(forSLDId:nodes:edges:customName:)}} - Uses {{node.x}} and {{node.y}} directly (BASE positions)
# *Line 281+*: {{createDTO(forSLDId:nodes:edges:customName:nodePositionOverrides:edgeRoutingOverrides:)}} - Takes view-specific position overrides from mappings

{{WebAppContainerViewWithView.updateWebViewGraph()}} is calling version #1, which sends base positions to React instead of the view-specific positions.

----

h2. Solution: Use Existing Override Properties in createDTO

The file already has computed properties that build the overrides (lines 60-77):

* {{nodePositionOverrides}} - builds from {{nodeViewMappings}}
* {{edgeRoutingOverrides}} - builds from {{edgeViewMappings}}

Simply pass them to the {{createDTO}} call.

h3. Implementation

Change {{updateWebViewGraph()}} from:

{code:swift}private func updateWebViewGraph() {
    let dto = SLDService.shared.createDTO(
        forSLDId: sld.id,
        nodes: nodesInView,
        edges: edgesInView,
        customName: "updated-bridge-view"
    )
    WebViewBridge.updateGraph(with: dto, animated: true)
}{code}

To:

{code:swift}private func updateWebViewGraph() {
    let dto = SLDService.shared.createDTO(
        forSLDId: sld.id,
        nodes: nodesInView,
        edges: edgesInView,
        customName: "updated-bridge-view",
        nodePositionOverrides: nodePositionOverrides,
        edgeRoutingOverrides: edgeRoutingOverrides
    )
    WebViewBridge.updateGraph(with: dto, animated: true)
}{code}

*That's it - a 2-line change.*

----

h2. Files to Modify

# {{eg-pz-mobile-iOS/Egalvanic PZ/Views/SLD/WebAppContainerViewWithView.swift}}
#* Lines 803-811: Add {{nodePositionOverrides}} and {{edgeRoutingOverrides}} parameters to {{createDTO}} call

----

h2. Verification Plan

# *Manual Testing in iOS Simulator (in a specific view, not "all"):*
#* *Remove* a node from an enclosure → all nodes should remain in their view-specific positions
#* *Add* a node to an enclosure → all nodes should remain in their view-specific positions
#* No viewport shift or position resets in either case
# *Compare with Web:*
#* Same operations in web app should produce identical results
# *Verify Persistence:*
#* Add/remove node from enclosure, close and reopen the SLD
#* Positions should be correct on reload

column1: SLD Views Phase 1: Load, Display, Back Navigation, and Link Navigation for iOS
column41:
h1. SLD Views - Load, Display, Back Navigation, and Link Navigation, Online/Offline CRUD Operations

h2. Goal

Load all view data in the mobile upsert, show a view selection screen in iOS, load selected view in FLOW_ONLY_MODE SLD, add back button to return to view selection, and support SLD link navigation between views. Also, allow nodes and edges to be added, resized, and moved within views.

h2. Critical Constraint: FLOW_ONLY_MODE Isolation

*ALL frontend changes must be wrapped in* {{FLOW_ONLY_MODE}} conditionals. We must not modify how the SLD works in non-FLOW_ONLY_MODE at all. The desktop/web SLD behavior must remain completely unchanged.

----

h2. 1. Backend: Add View Data to {{get_sld_full_v2}}

*File:* {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/sld_controller.py}}

Add to the response:

* {{sld_links}} (top-level array) - cross-view navigation links
* {{mappings.node_sld_view}} - node positions per view
* {{mappings.edge_sld_view}} - edge routing per view

*Changes:*

# Import {{SLDLink}}, {{MappingNodeSLDView}}, {{MappingEdgeSLDView}} from models
# Query these entities after fetching {{sld_views}}
# Add to response dictionary

----

h2. 2. iOS: Add DTOs for View Data

*New/Modified Files:*

* {{SLDViewDTO.swift}} (new) - DTO for SLD views
* {{SLDLinkDTO.swift}} (new) - DTO for cross-view links
* {{SLDMappingsDTO.swift}} (modify) - Add {{node_sld_view}} and {{edge_sld_view}} mapping DTOs
* {{SLDDTO.swift}} (modify) - Add {{sld_views}} and {{sld_links}} arrays

*DTO Structures:*

{code:swift}struct SLDViewDTO: Codable {
    var id: String
    var sld_id: String
    var name: String
    var description: String?
    var view_type: String
    var is_default: Bool
    var is_deleted: Bool
}

struct SLDLinkDTO: Codable {
    var id: String
    var source_sld_view_id: String
    var target_sld_view_id: String
    var source_node_id: String?
    var target_node_id: String?
    // ... position fields
}

struct MappingNodeSLDViewDTO: Codable {
    var id: String
    var node_id: String
    var sld_view_id: String
    var x: Double?
    var y: Double?
    var width: Double?
    var height: Double?
    var is_collapsed: Bool
    var is_deleted: Bool
}

struct MappingEdgeSLDViewDTO: Codable {
    var id: String
    var edge_id: String
    var sld_view_id: String
    var points: [EdgePoint]?
    var algorithm: String?
    var is_deleted: Bool
}{code}

----

h2. 3. iOS: Add SwiftData Models

*New Files:*

* {{SLDViewV2.swift}} - SwiftData model for views
* {{SLDLinkV2.swift}} - SwiftData model for links
* {{MappingNodeSLDView.swift}} - SwiftData model for node-view mappings
* {{MappingEdgeSLDView.swift}} - SwiftData model for edge-view mappings

----

h2. 4. iOS: Update SLDSyncService

*File:* {{SLDSyncService.swift}}

# Add to {{MappingDTOs}} struct:
#* {{nodeSLDView: [MappingNodeSLDViewDTO]}}
#* {{edgeSLDView: [MappingEdgeSLDViewDTO]}}
# Add to {{PendingDTOs}} struct:
#* {{sldViews: [(viewDTO: SLDViewDTO, sldId: UUID)]}}
#* {{sldLinks: [(linkDTO: SLDLinkDTO, sldId: UUID)]}}
# Add extraction in {{processCoreData()}}:
#* Extract {{sld_views}} from SLDDTO
#* Extract {{sld_links}} from SLDDTO
#* Extract mappings from {{sldDTO.mappings}}
# Add processing methods:
#* {{processSLDViews()}} - upsert views
#* {{processSLDLinks()}} - upsert links
#* {{processNodeSLDViewMappings()}} - upsert node-view mappings
#* {{processEdgeSLDViewMappings()}} - upsert edge-view mappings
# Call new methods in {{upsertSwiftDataObjects()}} in correct dependency order

----

h2. 5. iOS: View Selection Screen

*New File:* {{SLDViewSelectorView.swift}}

* Display list of views from SwiftData query
* Each row shows view name, type badge, node count
* Tapping a row selects that view and navigates to SLD
* No "All Nodes" option on mobile

*Modify:* {{MainTabView.swift}}

* Replace direct {{WebAppContainerView}} with new navigation flow:
*# Show {{SLDViewSelectorView}} first
*# On view selection, navigate to {{WebAppContainerView}} with selected view
*# Pass selected view ID to WebView

----

h2. 6. iOS: Pass View Data to WebView

*Modify:* {{WebViewBridge.swift}}

# Accept optional {{selectedView: SLDViewV2?}} parameter
# Filter nodes/edges based on {{MappingNodeSLDView}} for selected view
# Use view-specific positions from mappings
# Include {{activeViewId}} in initial data payload

*Modify:* {{WebAppContainerView.swift}}

# Accept {{selectedView}} parameter
# Query nodes that are in the selected view (via mappings)
# Pass to WebViewBridge

----

h2. 7. iOS: Handle Back Event from WebView

*Modify:* {{WebViewBridge.swift}}

# Add new message handler: {{viewBack}}
# Add callback: {{onViewBack: () -> Void}}

*Modify:* {{WebAppContainerView.swift}} / Navigation

# When {{viewBack}} message received, dismiss/navigate back to view selector
# Use {{@Environment(\.dismiss)}} or navigation state

----

h2. 7b. iOS: Handle SLD Link Navigation

*Modify:* {{WebViewBridge.swift}}

# Add new message handler: {{navigateToView}}
# Add callback: {{onNavigateToView: (UUID) -> Void}} - receives target view ID

column1: Add MiniMap Toggle Button to SLD Bottom Left Bar
column41:
h1. Plan: Add MiniMap Toggle Button to SLD Bottom Left Bar

h2. Summary

Add a toggle button in the bottom-left corner of the SLD view (when {{flowOnlyMode=false}}) that shows/hides the ReactFlow MiniMap above the button.

h2. File to Modify

* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/GraphEditor.jsx}}

h2. Implementation Steps

h3. 1. Import MiniMap from ReactFlow

Add {{MiniMap}} to the existing reactflow imports at line 2-9:

{code:javascript}import ReactFlow, {
  Controls,
  Background,
  useNodesState,
  useEdgesState,
  addEdge,
  Panel,
  MiniMap,  // Add this
} from "reactflow";{code}

h3. 2. Add State for MiniMap Visibility

Add state around line 348 (near other UI state):

{code:javascript}const [showMiniMap, setShowMiniMap] = useState(false);{code}

h3. 3. Add Bottom-Left Panel with Toggle Button and MiniMap

Inside the ReactFlow component (after the existing top Panel, around line 1266), add:

{noformat}{/* Bottom-left panel with minimap toggle - only when not in flow-only mode */}
{!flowOnlyMode && (
  <Panel position="bottom-left" style={{ display: 'flex', flexDirection: 'column', alignItems: 'flex-start', gap: '8px' }}>
    {showMiniMap && (
      <MiniMap
        nodeColor={(node) => {
          if (node.type === 'group') return '#6366f1';
          return '#94a3b8';
        }}
        maskColor="rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8)"
        style={{
          backgroundColor: '#1e293b',
          border: '1px solid #334155',
          borderRadius: '8px',
        }}
        zoomable
        pannable
      />
    )}
    <button
      onClick={() => setShowMiniMap(!showMiniMap)}
      style={{
        padding: '8px 12px',
        backgroundColor: showMiniMap ? '#3b82f6' : '#334155',
        color: '#fff',
        border: 'none',
        borderRadius: '6px',
        cursor: 'pointer',
        fontSize: '12px',
        fontWeight: '500',
        display: 'flex',
        alignItems: 'center',
        gap: '6px',
      }}
      title={showMiniMap ? 'Hide MiniMap' : 'Show MiniMap'}
    >
      <svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" strokeWidth="2">
        <rect x="3" y="3" width="18" height="18" rx="2" />
        <rect x="7" y="7" width="3" height="3" />
        <rect x="14" y="7" width="3" height="3" />
        <rect x="7" y="14" width="3" height="3" />
      </svg>
      {showMiniMap ? 'Hide Map' : 'Map'}
    </button>
  </Panel>
)}{noformat}

h2. Visual Layout

{noformat}┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  [Top Toolbar - EditorToolbar]          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                         │
│         ReactFlow Canvas                │
│                                         │
│                                         │
│  ┌──────────┐                           │
│  │ MiniMap  │  (when visible)           │
│  │          │                           │
│  └──────────┘                           │
│  [Map]        ← Toggle button           │
└─────────────────────────────────────────┘{noformat}

h2. Verification

# Navigate to an SLD view
# Verify the "Map" button appears in the bottom-left corner
# Click the button to toggle the MiniMap on/off
# Verify the MiniMap displays correctly above the button when enabled
# Verify button changes appearance when MiniMap is shown (blue vs gray)

column41:
h1. Company-Specific Procedure Masters Implementation Plan

h2. Summary

Enable users to create and manage company-specific procedure masters. Currently, procedure masters are global-only with no company isolation. This plan adds {{company_id}} and {{is_global}} columns to {{procedure_masters}}, backend CRUD endpoints, and frontend UI for creating/editing procedure masters.

h2. Current State

* *ProcedureMaster model*: No {{company_id}} or {{is_global}} columns (confirmed via dev DB check)
* *Procedure model*: Already has {{company_id}} + {{is_global}} pattern (to follow)
* *Frontend*: {{eg-pz-frontend-ZP-580/}} (= cicd/dev branch) has {{ProcedureEditor.jsx}} and {{ProcedureCreator.jsx}} with procedure master dropdowns but no "Create New" option
* *Backend*: Only has GET routes for procedure masters via {{LookupController}}; no create/update routes exist

h2. User Preferences

* *Create flow*: Inline text field in dropdown (not modal)
* *Deletion*: Soft-hide only (no hard delete)

h2. Implementation Steps

h3. 1. Database Migration

*File*: {{eg-pz-backend/migrations/versions/xxx_add_company_id_to_procedure_masters.py}}

Add columns to {{procedure_masters}} table:

* {{company_id}} (UUID, FK to companies, nullable) - NULL = global
* {{is_global}} (Boolean, default False)

{code:sql}ALTER TABLE procedure_masters ADD COLUMN company_id UUID REFERENCES companies(id);
ALTER TABLE procedure_masters ADD COLUMN is_global BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false;
-- Mark existing records as global
UPDATE procedure_masters SET is_global = true WHERE company_id IS NULL;{code}

h3. 2. Update ProcedureMaster Model

*File*: {{eg-pz-backend/app/models/ProcedureMaster.py}}

Add:

* {{company_id}} column with FK to companies
* {{is_global}} column (Boolean, default False)
* Update {{to_dict()}} to include new fields

h3. 3. Create ProcedureMaster Controller

*File*: {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/procedure_master_controller.py}} (new)

Methods:

* {{create_procedure_master(data, user_id)}} - Creates company-specific procedure master (is_global=False, company_id from user)
* {{update_procedure_master(id, data, user_id)}} - Updates name only for non-global masters owned by user's company
* {{hide_procedure_master(id, user_id)}} - Soft-hide (set is_deleted=True) for non-global masters only

h3. 4. Create ProcedureMaster Routes

*File*: {{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/procedure_master_routes.py}} (new)

Routes:

* {{POST /procedure-master/create}} - Create new company-specific procedure master
* {{PUT /procedure-master/update/<id>}} - Update name (only non-global, same company)
* {{DELETE /procedure-master/hide/<id>}} - Soft-hide (only non-global, same company)
* All routes require {{procedures.manage}} permission

h3. 5. Update LookupController

*File*: {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/lookup_controller.py}}

Modify {{get_all_procedure_masters()}} and {{get_unary_procedure_masters()}}:

* Accept {{user_id}} parameter
* Return procedure masters where {{is_global=True}} OR {{company_id=user.company_id}}
* Include {{is_global}} in response for UI display

h3. 6. Update Lookup Routes

*File*: {{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/lookup_routes.py}}

Update {{/lookup/procedure-masters}} and {{/lookup/unary-procedure-masters}}:

* Pass {{user_id}} from {{request.cognito_user.get('sub')}} to controller

h3. 7. Update Frontend ProcedureEditor Component

*File*: {{eg-pz-frontend-ZP-580/src/components/ProcedureEditor.jsx}}

Modify procedure master selector (lines 501-518):

* Add state for {{creatingNewMaster}} and {{newMasterName}}
* Add "Create New" as first MenuItem in Select
* When "Create New" selected, replace Select with inline TextField + Save/Cancel buttons
* On save: call API, then auto-select the new procedure master
* Show edit icon (pencil) next to non-global procedure masters
* Clicking edit icon → inline edit mode for name
* Show globe icon for global procedure masters (read-only)

h3. 8. Update Frontend ProcedureCreator Component

*File*: {{eg-pz-frontend-ZP-580/src/components/ProcedureCreator.jsx}}

Same inline pattern as ProcedureEditor:

* Add "Create New" option to procedure master selector (lines 461-482)
* Inline text field when creating new
* Show {{is_global}} indicator (globe icon) for global procedure masters

h3. 9. Add API Service Methods

*File*: {{eg-pz-frontend-ZP-580/src/services/mainAPIService.js}}

Add:

* {{createProcedureMaster(data)}} - POST to {{/procedure-master/create}}
* {{updateProcedureMaster(id, data)}} - PUT to {{/procedure-master/update/:id}}

h2. Critical Files to Modify

||File||Change||
|{{eg-pz-backend/app/models/ProcedureMaster.py}}|Add company_id, is_global columns|
|{{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/lookup_controller.py}}|Filter by company + global|
|{{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/lookup_routes.py}}|Pass user_id to controller|
|{{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/procedure_master_controller.py}}|New file - CRUD logic|
|{{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/procedure_master_routes.py}}|New file - CRUD routes|
|{{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/__init__.py}}|Register new blueprint|
|{{eg-pz-frontend-ZP-580/src/components/ProcedureEditor.jsx}}|Add "Create New" + edit|
|{{eg-pz-frontend-ZP-580/src/components/ProcedureCreator.jsx}}|Add "Create New"|
|{{eg-pz-frontend-ZP-580/src/services/mainAPIService.js}}|Add API methods|

h2. Verification

#* Test {{/lookup/procedure-masters}} returns global + company-specific
#* Test {{POST /procedure-master/create}} creates company-specific
#* Test {{PUT /procedure-master/update/:id}} only allows non-global edits

column1: Prevent soft-deletion of locations with active children
column41:
h1. Plan: Prevent Soft-Deletion of Locations with Active Children

h2. Problem

Buildings, floors, and rooms can be soft-deleted even when they contain active (non-deleted) child entities. This causes orphaned data where nodes reference rooms that appear deleted, breaking the location hierarchy API.

h2. Root Cause

The {{update_building()}}, {{update_floor()}}, and {{update_room()}} methods in {{location_controller.py}} accept {{is_deleted}} in the request payload and set it directly without any validation.

h2. Solution

Add validation checks before allowing soft-deletion:

h3. File: {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/location_controller.py}}

h4. 1. {{update_building()}} (around line 77)

Before setting {{is_deleted = True}}, check for:

* Active floors in the building
* (Optionally) Active nodes transitively through floors→rooms

{code:python}if 'is_deleted' in data and data['is_deleted'] and not building.is_deleted:
    # Check for active floors
    active_floors = Floor.query.filter_by(building_id=building_id, is_deleted=False).count()
    if active_floors > 0:
        return {'success': False, 'error': f'Cannot delete building with {active_floors} active floor(s). Delete floors first.'}, 400{code}

h4. 2. {{update_floor()}} (around line 167)

Before setting {{is_deleted = True}}, check for:

* Active rooms on the floor

{code:python}if 'is_deleted' in data and data['is_deleted'] and not floor.is_deleted:
    # Check for active rooms
    active_rooms = Room.query.filter_by(floor_id=floor_id, is_deleted=False).count()
    if active_rooms > 0:
        return {'success': False, 'error': f'Cannot delete floor with {active_rooms} active room(s). Delete rooms first.'}, 400{code}

h4. 3. {{update_room()}} (around line 260)

Before setting {{is_deleted = True}}, check for:

* Active nodes in the room

{code:python}if 'is_deleted' in data and data['is_deleted'] and not room.is_deleted:
    # Check for active nodes
    active_nodes = Node.query.filter_by(room_id=room_id, is_deleted=False).count()
    if active_nodes > 0:
        return {'success': False, 'error': f'Cannot delete room with {active_nodes} active node(s). Remove or reassign nodes first.'}, 400{code}

h2. Verification

# Start local backend: {{cd eg-pz-backend && ./run_production_mode.sh}}
# Test each validation:
#* Try to delete a room that has nodes → should fail with 400
#* Try to delete a floor that has rooms → should fail with 400
#* Try to delete a building that has floors → should fail with 400
# Test happy path:
#* Delete a room with no nodes → should succeed
#* Delete a floor with no rooms → should succeed
#* Delete a building with no floors → should succeed

h2. Notes

* The validation uses {{and not entity.is_deleted}} to avoid re-validating already-deleted entities (allows undelete operations)
* Error messages guide the user on what to do (delete children first or reassign nodes)

column1: Z | FE | Site Access Complexity Level for Sites
column41:
h2. Overview

Implement a Site Access Complexity level feature for Sites that acts as a time multiplier for site-related operations.

h2. Business Context

Different sites have varying levels of access complexity (security clearances, remote locations, restricted access times, etc.). This complexity factor should be reflected in time estimates and scheduling.

h2. Requirements

* Add a complexity level field to Sites
* Complexity level acts as a multiplier for time calculations
* Example: A complexity of 1.5 means normal time × 1.5 for that site

h2. Example

||Site||Normal Time||Complexity||Adjusted Time||
|Site A|2 hours|1.0|2 hours|
|Site B|2 hours|1.5|3 hours|
|Site C|2 hours|2.0|4 hours|

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* Sites have a configurable complexity level field
* Default complexity level is 1.0 (no change)
* Time estimates are automatically adjusted based on site complexity
* Complexity multiplier is visible when viewing site details
* Reports and scheduling reflect complexity-adjusted times

* Apply multiplier in scheduling/planning views
* Ensure consistency across all time-related calculations

h4. 4. Reporting & Analytics

* Add filtering/grouping by complexity level
* Update quote summaries to show complexity impact

h3. Technical Implementation

* Coordinate with backend on API contract
* Update TypeScript types/interfaces
* Create helper functions for complexity-adjusted time calculations
* Ensure proper validation of complexity values
* Maintain responsive and accessible UI

h3. Additional Acceptance Criteria

* API integration properly sends/receives complexity data
* Form validation ensures valid complexity values
* User can update complexity and see immediate impact
* UI is responsive and accessible across all devices
