column1: FE - Add Sites tab to account detail page
column41:
Add a new "Sites" tab to the account detail page that displays all sites associated with the selected account and allows users to create and edit sites directly from the account context.

The tab should:

* Display a list/table of all sites linked to the current account
* Include relevant site information (name, address, status, etc.)
* Support navigation to individual site detail pages
* Provide the ability to create a new site under the current account
* Provide the ability to edit existing sites
* Follow existing tab patterns and styling on the account detail page

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* Sites tab is visible on the account detail page
* Sites tab displays all sites associated with the account
* Site list shows relevant columns (name, address, status)
* Clicking a site navigates to the site detail page
* Tab displays empty state when no sites are associated
* Tab styling is consistent with other tabs on the account detail page
* Data loads with appropriate loading indicator
* "Add Site" button is available within the Sites tab
* Clicking "Add Site" opens site creation form/modal
* New site is automatically associated with the current account
* Site list refreshes after successful site creation
* Edit action is available for each site in the list
* Clicking edit opens site edit form/modal with pre-populated data
* Site list refreshes after successful site update
* Appropriate validation and error handling on site creation and editing

column1: Add Icon and Orientation Selection to Node Class Creation/Modification
column41:
h2. Refactoring Plan

h1. Plan: Redesign AssetClassCreator/Editor with Database SVGs and Minimal UI

h2. Summary

Redesign the asset class creation/editing screens to be more minimal:

* Load SVG icons from database instead of bundled files
* Filter orientations to essential subset
* Simplify UI with modal icon picker, dropdown orientation, and streamlined layout

h2. Changes

h3. 1. Update IconSelector to Use Database SVGs

*File:* {{src/components/IconSelector.jsx}}

* Remove all bundled SVG imports and {{iconUrlMap}}
* Render icons using {{dangerouslySetInnerHTML}} with the {{svg}} field from API
* Each icon in the API response has: {{{id, name, svg, company_id, is_deleted}}}

h3. 2. Create IconPickerModal Component

*File:* {{src/components/IconPickerModal.jsx}} (NEW)

* Modal dialog that opens when user clicks icon selector button
* Grid of icons inside modal (reuse IconSelector or embed similar grid)
* Search/filter by name (optional, nice-to-have)
* Click icon to select and close modal
* Props: {{open}}, {{onClose}}, {{onSelect(iconId, iconName, iconSvg)}}, {{icons}}, {{selectedId}}

h3. 3. Filter Orientations in Backend Route

*File:* {{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/class_routes.py}}

Update {{get_node_orientations()}} to filter:

{code:python}ALLOWED_ORIENTATIONS = ['vertical', 'horizontal', 'horizontal-square', 'double-target', 'source', 'endpoint']
orientations = NodeOrientation.query.filter(
    NodeOrientation.is_deleted == False,
    NodeOrientation.code.in_(ALLOWED_ORIENTATIONS)
).all(){code}

h3. 4. Redesign AssetClassCreator Layout

*File:* {{src/components/AssetClassCreator.jsx}}

New layout structure:

{noformat}┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                         │
│  ┌──────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │              │  │ Name: [________________]        │ │
│  │   PREVIEW    │  │                                 │ │
│  │   (live)     │  │ Icon: [Button: Select Icon]    │ │
│  │              │  │                                 │ │
│  │              │  │ Orientation: [Dropdown ▼]      │ │
│  └──────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                         │
│  Classification:                                        │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│  │ Box OK: [YES|NO]  OCP OK: [YES|NO]  Needs Source: [YES|NO] │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
│                                                         │
│  Copy Definition From: [Dropdown ▼]                     │
│                                                         │
│  Core Attributes:                                       │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│  │ [+ Add Attribute]                                   ││
│  │ Attribute cards...                                  ││
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
│                                                         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘{noformat}

Changes:

* Remove tabbed sections - single scrollable form
* Preview on left, basic info on right (side by side)
* Classification toggles as horizontal ToggleButtonGroup (YES|NO style)
* Icon selection via button that opens IconPickerModal
* Orientation via MUI Select dropdown
* "Copy Definition From" moved below classification
* Core Attributes at bottom

h3. 5. Redesign AssetClassEditor Layout

*File:* {{src/components/AssetClassEditor.jsx}}

Same layout changes as Creator, plus:

* Orientation dropdown is disabled (read-only) with helper text
* Shortcuts and Subtypes sections remain at bottom (can be collapsible)

h3. 6. Update NodeClassPreview to Accept SVG Content

*File:* {{src/components/NodeClassPreview.jsx}}

* Already supports {{iconSvg}} prop for database SVGs
* Ensure it prioritizes {{iconSvg}} over {{iconName}} lookup

h3. 7. Remove Unused Components

After changes, can deprecate:

* {{OrientationSelector.jsx}} - replaced by dropdown
* Bundled icon imports in {{IconSelector.jsx}}

h2. Files to Modify

# {{src/components/IconSelector.jsx}} - Use database SVGs
# {{src/components/IconPickerModal.jsx}} - NEW: Modal wrapper
# {{src/components/AssetClassCreator.jsx}} - New minimal layout
# {{src/components/AssetClassEditor.jsx}} - New minimal layout
# {{src/components/NodeClassPreview.jsx}} - Minor updates if needed
# {{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/class_routes.py}} - Filter orientations
# {{src/components/OrientationSelector.jsx}} - DELETE (no longer needed)

h2. Verification

# Open AssetClassCreator modal
# Verify preview shows on left, form fields on right
# Click icon button - modal opens with all icons using database SVGs
# Select icon - preview updates, modal closes
# Orientation dropdown shows only 6 options (no group/dual/junctionBox)
# Classification toggles are horizontal YES|NO switches
# Core Attributes section at bottom works correctly
# "Copy Definition From" copies icon, orientation, toggles, and attributes
# AssetClassEditor shows same layout with orientation disabled
# Create and edit node classes successfully via API

----

column211:
08/Jan/26 2:02 PM;623b9df4866b810069e4062a;h2. Original Implementation Plan (moved for refactoring)

h1. Plan: Add Icon and Orientation Selection to Node Class Creation/Modification

h2. Overview

Add required icon selection to node class creation and editing forms. When an icon is selected:

* Set {{node_class.style}} = {{node_icon.name}}
* Set {{node_class.icon_id}} = {{node_icon.id}}

Additionally, add orientation selection to node class *creation only* (not edit):

* Set {{node_class.node_orientation_id}} = selected orientation's ID
* *Deprecate* {{node_class.orientation}} string field - derive orientation from {{node_orientation.code}} instead

h2. Current State

* *Backend*: NodeIcon model exists, NodeClass has {{style}} and {{icon_id}} fields, but NO route to fetch icons
* *Frontend*: Icon mapping exists in AssetClassCard.jsx (14 icons + default), but NO icon selector in forms
* *Database*: 18 icons exist in {{node_icons}} table (including {{default}} for fallback)
* *Orientations (DEV/QA only)*: 9 orientation types seeded in {{node_orientations}} table
* Prod and BCES-IQ don't have orientation system yet (intentional)
* *Redundancy*: {{node_class.orientation}} (string) duplicates {{node_orientation.code}} - we will use {{node_orientation_id}} exclusively
* *Frontend uses hardcoded logic* for handle rendering based on orientation string - needs to derive from {{node_orientation.code}}

----

h2. Implementation Plan

h3. 1. Backend: Add GET Routes for Icons and Orientations

h3. 2. Frontend: Add API Service Functions

h3. 3. Frontend: Create IconSelector Component

h3. 4. Frontend: Create OrientationSelector Component

h3. 5. Frontend: Update AssetClassCreator.jsx

h3. 6. Frontend: Update AssetClassEditor.jsx

h3. 7. Backend: Update Create/Update Routes

h3. 8. Backend: Update NodeClass.to_dict() to Derive Orientation

h3. 9. Frontend: Update DynamicNode to Use node_orientation.code

h3. 10. Frontend: Add Node Class Preview Component

column1: Z | FE | Preserve All Tab filter and scroll position after page refresh (Work Orders)
column41:
h2. Description

The All Tab in the work order view resets filter selections and scroll position after a page refresh. This disrupts user workflow and requires users to re-apply filters and scroll back to their previous position.

h2. User Story

As a user viewing work orders in the All Tab, I want my filter selections and scroll position to persist after a page refresh so that I can continue working from where I left off.

h2. Current Behavior

* Filters reset to default after page refresh
* Scroll position resets to top after page refresh

h2. Expected Behavior

* Filter selections persist after page refresh
* Scroll position is restored after page refresh

h2. Requirements

* Store filter state in session storage or URL parameters
* Store scroll position before refresh
* Restore filter and scroll state on page load

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* Filter selections persist after page refresh in the All Tab
* Scroll position is restored to previous location after refresh
* State is properly cleared on logout or explicit reset
* Works consistently across different browsers

Enable bulk adding of assets to a task by filtering and selecting assets based on location hierarchy (building, floor, room).

h2. User Story

As a user creating tasks, I want to bulk add assets by selecting a location (building/floor/room) so that I can quickly add all assets in that location to a task.

h2. Requirements

* Add location-based filter in bulk add assets view
* Support filtering by Building, Floor, and Room
* Allow cascading selection (Building → Floor → Room)
* Select all assets matching the location criteria
* Add selected assets to the task in bulk

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* User can filter assets by Building
* User can filter assets by Floor (after selecting Building)
* User can filter assets by Room (after selecting Floor)
* User can select all visible/filtered assets at once
* Selected assets are successfully added to the task
* Proper validation and error handling for bulk operations

column1: Z | FE | Preserve filter state for bulk add task view (Work Orders)
column41:
h2. Description

Preserve the filter state when navigating within the bulk add task view. Currently, filter selections are lost when users navigate away or perform actions.

h2. User Story

As a user working in the bulk add task view, I want my filter selections to be preserved so that I don't have to re-apply filters after each action.

h2. Requirements

column1: Z | BE | Site Access Complexity Level for Sites
column41:
h2. Overview

Implement a Site Access Complexity level feature for Sites that acts as a time multiplier for site-related operations.

h2. Business Context

Different sites have varying levels of access complexity (security clearances, remote locations, restricted access times, etc.). This complexity factor should be reflected in time estimates and scheduling.

h2. Requirements

* Add a complexity level field to Sites
* Complexity level acts as a multiplier for time calculations
* Example: A complexity of 1.5 means normal time × 1.5 for that site

h2. Example

||Site||Normal Time||Complexity||Adjusted Time||
|Site A|2 hours|1.0|2 hours|
|Site B|2 hours|1.5|3 hours|
|Site C|2 hours|2.0|4 hours|

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* Sites have a configurable complexity level field
* Default complexity level is 1.0 (no change)
* Time estimates are automatically adjusted based on site complexity
* Complexity multiplier is visible when viewing site details
* Reports and scheduling reflect complexity-adjusted times

column1: iOS | Download and Load Updated SLD Viewer Without App Release
column41:
*Description*

* Integrate API call to fetch {{sld_viewer_version}} and {{sld_viewer_download_url}} on login.
* Compare API version with locally stored viewer version.
* Download viewer ZIP silently when a newer version is available.
* Extract and store viewer assets in local device storage.
* Use bundled or last downloaded viewer accordingly.
* Store and manage viewer version metadata locally.
* Support backend rollback by loading older version if required.
* Handle fallback scenarios:
** API failure
** Empty API fields
** No internet

*Acceptance Criteria*

* API is called successfully on app login.
* App correctly detects newer/older/same viewer versions.
* Version comparison logic works as expected.
* Load viewer from:
** Latest downloaded version (if available)
** Bundled viewer otherwise
** Download runs in background without blocking UI.
** Viewer ZIP is extracted successfully.
** Download failures do not crash the app.
** Correct viewer version is loaded consistently.
** Offline access works after first successful download.
** Local version is updated after successful download.
** App loads downgraded version when backend points to older version.

More detailed information: [https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENGR/pages/68943873/Dynamic+SLD|https://egalvanic.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENGR/pages/68943873/Dynamic+SLD|smart-link]

* Maintain backward compatibility where possible.
* Ensure viewer works without network access once downloaded.

* Validate viewer behavior when backend points to an older version.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* ZIP runs independently without external dependencies.
* Viewer works when loaded from local storage.
* Each release has a unique version.
* Version matches backend configuration.
* Viewer renders fully in offline mode.
* No broken assets or console errors.
* Older versions load correctly.

column1: Z | iOS | Main Asset’s Child Asset Is Available for Connection on Connection Screen
column41:
h3. *Description*

When a child asset is already associated with a main asset, it should not be available for connection again with the same main asset.
Currently, even though the child asset correctly does not appear on the Line Side or Load Side on the Asset Details screen (which is expected behavior), the same child asset is still selectable on the Connection screen. This allows the main asset to be connected again with its own child asset, which should not be allowed.

----

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Create or open a *Main Asset*.
# Add a *Child Asset* to the Main Asset.
# Navigate to the *Asset Details* screen of the Main Asset.
# Verify that the child asset does *not* appear on the Line Side or Load Side (accepted behavior).
# Now open the *Connection screen*.
# Try to create a connection for the Main Asset.
# Observe that the Main Asset’s own child asset is still available for selection and can be connected.

----

h3. *Expected Result*

The Main Asset’s child asset should *not* be available for selection on the Connection screen, and the system should prevent connecting a main asset with its own child asset.

----

h3. *Actual Result*

The Main Asset’s child asset is visible and selectable on the Connection screen, allowing an invalid connection.

column1: View-Specific Edge Breakpoints
column41:
h2. Overview

Add view-specific edge breakpoints so edges can have different routing per view.

----

h2. 7.1 Database Migration

*File:* {{eg-pz-backend/migrations/versions/}} (new or same migration as Part 1)

Create new {{mapping_edge_sld_view}} table:

{code:sql}CREATE TABLE mapping_edge_sld_view (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    edge_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES edges(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    sld_view_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES sld_views(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    points JSONB NULL,
    algorithm VARCHAR(50) NULL,
    is_deleted BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    UNIQUE(edge_id, sld_view_id)
);{code}

h2. 7.2 Add Model

*File:* {{eg-pz-backend/app/models/Mappings.py}}

Add {{MappingEdgeSLDView}} class:

{code:python}class MappingEdgeSLDView(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = 'mapping_edge_sld_view'
    
    id = db.Column(UUID(as_uuid=True), primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4)
    edge_id = db.Column(UUID(as_uuid=True), db.ForeignKey('edges.id', ondelete='CASCADE'), nullable=False)
    sld_view_id = db.Column(UUID(as_uuid=True), db.ForeignKey('sld_views.id', ondelete='CASCADE'), nullable=False)
    points = db.Column(JSONB, nullable=True)
    algorithm = db.Column(db.String(50), nullable=True)
    is_deleted = db.Column(db.Boolean, default=False){code}

h2. 7.3 Update Backend Controllers

*File:* {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py}}

In {{get_view_full()}}:

* Include view-specific edge points if available
* Fall back to edges.points if no view-specific points

*File:* {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/edge_controller.py}}

Add {{update_edge_points()}} function:

* Accept view_id parameter
* If view_id: update/create mapping_edge_sld_view entry
* If no view_id: update edges.points directly

h2. 7.4 Add API Endpoints

*File:* {{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/sld_view_routes.py}}

Add endpoint: {{PUT /sld-view/<view_id>/edges/<edge_id>/points}}

h2. 7.5 Update Frontend

*File:* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/edges/EditableEdge.jsx}}

Save view-specific points when in a view context.

*File:* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/edges/DynamicEdge.jsx}}

Load view-specific points when available.

----

h2. Files to Modify

h3. Backend

# {{eg-pz-backend/migrations/versions/xxx_view_specific_features.py}}
# {{eg-pz-backend/app/models/Mappings.py}}
# {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py}}
# {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/edge_controller.py}}
# {{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/sld_view_routes.py}}

h3. Frontend

# {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/edges/EditableEdge.jsx}}
# {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/edges/DynamicEdge.jsx}}

----

h2. Edge Cases

# Null view edge points - fall back to edges.points
# Edge not in mapping_edge_sld_view - use edges table values

column1: Hotfix: Add Confirmation Dialog for Adding Node to Group
column41:
h2. Overview

Show a confirmation dialog when dragging a node into a group to prevent accidental grouping.

----

h2. 6.1 Frontend Implementation

*File:* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/hooks/useDragAndDrop.jsx}}

When detecting a drop onto a group node:

# Pause the operation
# Show confirmation dialog
# On confirm: proceed with adding to group
# On cancel: revert node position to original

h2. 6.2 Dialog Component

*File:* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/GraphEditor.jsx}}

Add dialog state and rendering:

{noformat}const [groupConfirmDialog, setGroupConfirmDialog] = useState({
  open: false,
  nodeId: null,
  groupId: null,
  originalPosition: null
});

// Dialog JSX
<Dialog open={groupConfirmDialog.open}>
  <DialogTitle>Add to Group?</DialogTitle>
  <DialogContent>
    Are you sure you want to add this node to the group?
  </DialogContent>
  <DialogActions>
    <Button onClick={handleCancelGroupAdd}>Cancel</Button>
    <Button onClick={handleConfirmGroupAdd}>Confirm</Button>
  </DialogActions>
</Dialog>{noformat}

----

h2. Files to Modify

h3. Frontend

# {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/hooks/useDragAndDrop.jsx}}
# {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/GraphEditor.jsx}}

----

h2. Edge Cases

# User cancels add-to-group - revert node position
# User confirms - proceed with grouping and distribute logic

column1: Hotfix: Prevent Direct Parent Switching for Nodes
column41:
h2. Overview

*Bug/Rule*: A node with non-null {{parent_id}} can only change to {{null}}. Cannot switch directly to a different parent.

This prevents confusing behavior where dragging a node from one group directly to another bypasses proper removal logic.

----

h2. 5.1 Backend Validation

*File:* {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/node_controller.py}}

In {{update_node()}}:

* Check if node already has a {{parent_id}}
* If new {{parent_id}} is provided and is different from current (and not null)
* Return 400 error with message

{code:python}if node.parent_id and new_parent_id and node.parent_id != new_parent_id:
    return False, "Cannot switch parents directly. Remove from current group first.", 400{code}

h2. 5.2 Frontend Handling

*File:* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/hooks/useDragAndDrop.jsx}}

Show error message: "Remove from current group first"

----

h2. Files to Modify

h3. Backend

# {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/node_controller.py}}

h3. Frontend

# {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/hooks/useDragAndDrop.jsx}}

----

h2. Edge Cases

# User tries to switch parents - block with error
# User drags to null parent (removal) - allowed
# User drags unparented node into group - allowed

column1: Distribute Children on Add/Remove from Group

*File:* {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/node_controller.py}}

Add {{_distribute_children()}} function:

* Preserves left-to-right order of existing children
* New children go to end
* Repositions all children evenly within parent bounds
* Respects padding from parent boundaries

h2. 4.2 Call Distribute on Child Add/Remove

Trigger {{_distribute_children()}} on {{parent_id}} changes in {{update_node()}}:

* When a node's parent_id is set (added to group)
* When a node's parent_id is cleared (removed from group)

column1: Fix VFD selection outline and Straighten Downstream viewport snap
column41:
h1. Plan: Fix VFD Selection Outline and Straighten Downstream Viewport

h2. Summary

Two small fixes:

# VFD nodes show a weird outline when selected (partial fix from ZP-490 didn't cover selected state)
# "Straighten Downstream" snaps the viewport to fit all nodes, but should leave viewport unchanged

----

h2. Issue 1: VFD Selection Outline

h3. Root Cause

ZP-490 added {{vfd: DynamicNode}} to the {{nodeTypes}} map, but did NOT add {{.react-flow__node-vfd}} to the CSS selector list that removes ReactFlow's default wrapper styling.

In {{App.css}} (lines 306-321), these node types are listed to receive transparent background and no border/padding:

* electricalPanel, disconnectSwitch, transformer, circuitBreaker, mcc, ats, ups, fuse, relay, switch

*VFD is missing from this list*, so VFD nodes still have ReactFlow's default wrapper styling, which becomes visible as a "second box" when selected (the default {{.react-flow__node.selected}} box-shadow at line 211).

h3. Fix

Add {{.react-flow__node-vfd}} to the selector list in {{App.css}}.

*File:* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/App.css}}
*Location:* Lines 306-315

*Change:*

{code:css}.react-flow__node-electricalPanel,
.react-flow__node-disconnectSwitch,
.react-flow__node-transformer,
.react-flow__node-circuitBreaker,
.react-flow__node-mcc,
.react-flow__node-ats,
.react-flow__node-ups,
.react-flow__node-vfd,         /* ADD THIS LINE */
.react-flow__node-fuse,
.react-flow__node-relay,
.react-flow__node-switch {{code}

----

h2. Issue 2: Straighten Downstream Viewport Snap

h3. Root Cause

The {{straightenDownstream}} function in {{useDagreLayout.jsx}} calls {{reactFlowInstance.fitView()}} after repositioning nodes (lines 862-867). This snaps the viewport to show all nodes, overriding the user's current view position.

h3. Fix

Remove or comment out the {{fitView}} call.

*File:* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/hooks/useDagreLayout.jsx}}
*Location:* Lines 862-867

*Remove this code block:*

{code:javascript}// Fit view to show all nodes
if (reactFlowInstance) {
  setTimeout(() => {
    reactFlowInstance.fitView({ padding: 0.1, maxZoom: 1 });
  }, 50);
}{code}

----

h2. Files to Modify

# {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/App.css}} - Add VFD to node type selector list
# {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/hooks/useDagreLayout.jsx}} - Remove fitView call

h2. Testing

# Load an SLD with VFD nodes, select a VFD - verify no "double outline" artifact
# Execute "Straighten Downstream" on any node - verify viewport stays in place

column1: Fix auto-distribute children on pulled group nodes (ZP-492 regression)
column211:
06/Jan/26 6:37 PM;623b9df4866b810069e4062a;h2. Original Implementation Plan (moved for refactoring)

h1. Plan: Fix Auto-Distribute Children on Pulled Group Nodes (ZP-492 Revisited)

h2. Problem Summary

Child assets are still poorly positioned after being added via:

# Manage Assets
# Search & Pull
# Manage Assets → Lineage Pull

The original implementation (commit 63ff9e8) had several issues that prevent auto-distribute from working correctly.

h2. Root Causes Identified

h3. Issue 1: Manage Assets uses stale {{allAssetsLightweight}} for group detection

*Location*: {{GraphEditor.jsx:3998-4001}}

{code:javascript}const groupIds = addedNodeIds.filter(id => {
  const asset = allAssetsLightweight.find(a => a.id === id);
  return asset?.type === 'group';  // allAssetsLightweight may be stale!
});{code}

The {{allAssetsLightweight}} store data is captured in a closure and may not reflect freshly-added nodes.

h3. Issue 2: View toggle causes timing chaos

*Location*: {{GraphEditor.jsx:3991-4005}}
The view is toggled off ({{setActiveViewId(null)}}) then back on, which:

* Triggers a complete refetch of view data
* Reconstructs all nodes from scratch
* By the time distribute runs (300ms+ later), node state is different

h3. Issue 3: ManageAssetsModal doesn't pass node type information

*Location*: {{ManageAssetsModal.jsx:222, 328}}
Only node IDs are passed to {{onAssetsChanged}}, not type information, forcing reliance on potentially stale store data.

h3. Issue 4: Stale closure of {{handleDistributeNodes}}

*Location*: {{GraphEditor.jsx:699}}
After ZP-493 fix removed {{handleDistributeNodes}} from dependencies, the function captured in callbacks may reference stale {{nodes}} state.

h2. Solution: Two-Step Operation

Per user requirement: "This should be a two step operation: first, pull all assets into the view, next, run Distribute Children on all groups at once."

h3. Step 1: Modify ManageAssetsModal to pass asset type info

*File*: {{src/sld/src/components/ManageAssetsModal.jsx}}

Change {{handleAddToView}} (line 222) and {{handleConfirmLineage}} (line 328) to pass full asset objects or at least type info.

h3. Step 2: Fix onAssetsChanged in GraphEditor.jsx to use passed type info

h3. Step 3: Add a stable ref for handleDistributeNodes

h3. Step 4: Increase timeout to ensure nodes are in state

h2. Files to Modify

||File||Changes||
|{{src/sld/src/components/ManageAssetsModal.jsx}}|Pass type info in {{onAssetsChanged}} calls (lines 222, 328)|
|{{src/sld/src/components/GraphEditor.jsx}}|Add {{handleDistributeNodesRef}} ref, Update {{onAssetsChanged}} to use passed type info, Update all setTimeout calls to use ref, Increase timeouts to 300ms|

----

Moved to comments on 2026-01-06 to make room for refactoring plan.

column1: Fix TDZ error in GraphEditor.jsx causing app crash (ZP-492 regression)
column41:
h1. Fix: TDZ Error in GraphEditor.jsx (ZP-492)

h2. Problem

Commit {{63ff9e8}} (ZP-492) added {{handleDistributeNodes}} to useCallback dependency arrays before it's defined, causing:

{noformat}ReferenceError: Cannot access 'Ki' before initialization{noformat}

h2. Root Cause

* {{handleDistributeNodes}} comes from {{useSelectionHandlers}} hook at line ~1660
* It was added to dependency arrays at lines ~695 and ~934 (before it exists)
* React evaluates dependency arrays immediately, triggering TDZ error

h2. Fix

Remove {{handleDistributeNodes}} from both dependency arrays in {{src/sld/src/components/GraphEditor.jsx}}:

# *Line ~695* (handleSearchAndPullDownstream):
#* Remove {{handleDistributeNodes}} from dependency array
# *Line ~934* (handleSearchAndPullUpstream):
#* Remove {{handleDistributeNodes}} from dependency array

h2. Why This Is Safe

* {{handleDistributeNodes}} is only called inside {{setTimeout()}} callbacks
* setTimeout callbacks capture the function by closure at execution time
* No need for it in the dependency array

h2. Files to Modify

* {{src/sld/src/components/GraphEditor.jsx}}

h2. Branch

* Apply fix to {{cicd/dev}} branch

column1: VFD nodes render with visible "second box" behind them - missing nodeTypes entry
column41:
h1. Fix VFD Node Rendering - Missing nodeTypes Entry

h2. Problem

VFD nodes on SLDs render with a visible "second box" behind them in the EG Dev environment.

h2. Root Cause

The {{vfd}} node type is *missing from the* {{nodeTypes}} map in {{GraphEditor.jsx}} (lines 110-129).

When a node has {{type: "vfd"}} but there's no corresponding entry in the {{nodeTypes}} map, ReactFlow falls back to its default node rendering (a simple rectangular box). This default box renders behind the {{DynamicNode}} component, creating the visual artifact.

*Current nodeTypes map includes:*

* electricalPanel, disconnectSwitch, transformer, circuitBreaker, mcc, ats, ups, fuse, relay, switch, generator, utility, motor, default

*Missing:* {{vfd}}

h2. Database Context

VFD node class configuration in {{egpzdev}}:

* {{style: "vfd"}}
* {{width: 600, height: 160}}
* {{box: true}} (can be converted to enclosure)
* Has custom icon SVG stored in {{node_icons}} table

h2. Solution

Add {{vfd: DynamicNode}} to the {{nodeTypes}} map in {{GraphEditor.jsx}}.

h2. File to Modify

{{/Users/ericehlert/projects/eg-pz/eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/GraphEditor.jsx}}

h2. Implementation

*Location:* Lines 110-129, inside the {{nodeTypes}} constant

*Change:*

{code:javascript}const nodeTypes = {
  group: EnclosureNode,
  junctionBox: JunctionBoxNode,
  reference: ReferenceNode,
  // All other types use DynamicNode for database-driven rendering
  electricalPanel: DynamicNode,
  disconnectSwitch: DynamicNode,
  transformer: DynamicNode,
  circuitBreaker: DynamicNode,
  mcc: DynamicNode,
  ats: DynamicNode,
  ups: DynamicNode,
  vfd: DynamicNode,        // <-- ADD THIS LINE
  fuse: DynamicNode,
  relay: DynamicNode,
  switch: DynamicNode,
  generator: DynamicNode,
  utility: DynamicNode,
  motor: DynamicNode,
  default: DynamicNode,
};{code}

h2. Testing

# Verify VFD nodes render correctly without the dark box artifact
# Verify VFD icon displays properly in the symbol area
# Verify node selection, dragging, and connections work normally

h2. Risk Assessment

*Low risk* - This is an additive change that simply registers an existing component for a specific node type. No behavior changes to other node types.

column1: Add "Add Lineage to View" option in Manage Assets modal
column41:
h1. Add Lineage to View Feature

h2. Overview

Add an "Add Lineage to View" option to the Manage Assets modal that finds the entire *downstream* lineage of selected nodes (using the same DFS algorithm as the lineage highlighter) and pulls all those nodes into the current view in a single batch operation.

h2. User Flow

# User opens "Manage Assets" modal for a view
# User selects one or more nodes (not in view)
# User clicks "Add Lineage to View" button (new)
# System shows preview dialog with nodes grouped by type/location
# User confirms → nodes are batch-added to view with mappings
# Modal closes and view refreshes

----

h2. Implementation Plan

h3. 1. Backend: New Endpoint for Full Downstream Lineage Preview

*File:* {{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/sld_view_routes.py}}

Add new route:

{noformat}POST /sld-view/<view_id>/preview-downstream-lineage
Body: { node_ids: [uuid, ...] }{noformat}

*File:* {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py}}

Add {{preview_downstream_lineage()}} method that:

* Takes a list of starting node IDs
* For each node, recursively traverses downstream via edges (DFS)
* Uses same enclosure/parent-child logic as frontend {{useLineageTrace}}
* Returns nodes NOT already in view, grouped by type and location
* Does NOT create mappings yet (preview only)

Response format:

{code:json}{
  "success": true,
  "nodes_to_add": [
    { "id": "uuid", "label": "...", "type": "...", "location": "...", "node_class_name": "..." }
  ],
  "total_count": 12,
  "by_type": { "Transformer": 3, "Panel": 5, "Breaker": 4 },
  "by_location": { "Substation A": 7, "Substation B": 5 }
}{code}

h3. 2. Backend: Batch Add Nodes Endpoint (reuse existing)

The existing {{POST /sld-view/<view_id>/nodes/add}} endpoint already supports batch adding nodes with {{{ nodes: [{node_id, x, y}, ...] }}}. We'll reuse this.

h3. 3. Frontend: UI Store Updates

*File:* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/store/ui.js}}

Add new store method:

{code:javascript}previewDownstreamLineage: async (viewId, nodeIds) => {
  const response = await apiClient.post(`/sld-view/${viewId}/preview-downstream-lineage`, { node_ids: nodeIds });
  return response.data;
}{code}

h3. 4. Frontend: ManageAssetsModal Updates

*File:* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/ManageAssetsModal.jsx}}

Changes:

# Add state for lineage preview modal ({{showLineagePreview}}, {{lineagePreviewData}})
# Add "Add Lineage to View" button next to "Add to View" (visible when selection has nodes not in view)
# On click, call {{previewDownstreamLineage}} API
# Show preview confirmation dialog with grouped results
# On confirm, call existing {{addNodesToView}} with all node IDs
# Close modal and trigger refresh

h3. 5. Frontend: New LineagePreviewDialog Component

*File:* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/LineagePreviewDialog.jsx}} (new)

A confirmation dialog showing:

* Total count summary
* Expandable sections grouped by type
* Expandable sections grouped by location
* Cancel / Confirm buttons

----

h2. Files to Modify

h3. Backend

# {{eg-pz-backend/app/routes/sld_view_routes.py}} - Add new route
# {{eg-pz-backend/app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py}} - Add {{preview_downstream_lineage()}} method

h3. Frontend

# {{eg-pz-frontend/src/store/ui.js}} - Add {{previewDownstreamLineage}} store method
# {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/ManageAssetsModal.jsx}} - Add button and dialog integration
# {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/ManageAssetsModal.css}} - Styles for new button
# {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/LineagePreviewDialog.jsx}} (new) - Preview confirmation dialog
# {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/LineagePreviewDialog.css}} (new) - Dialog styles

----

h2. Backend Algorithm Details

The downstream lineage traversal will mirror the frontend {{useLineageTrace.jsx}} logic:

{code:python}def get_downstream_lineage(start_node_ids, sld_id, existing_view_node_ids):
    """
    DFS traversal to find all downstream nodes.
    Handles enclosure/parent-child relationships like the frontend.
    """
    visited = set()
    result = set()

    # Build edge maps
    edges = Edge.query.filter_by(sld_id=sld_id, is_deleted=False).all()
    edges_by_source = defaultdict(list)  # source_id -> [edges]
    for edge in edges:
        edges_by_source[edge.source].append(edge)

    # Build parent-child maps
    nodes = Node.query.filter_by(sld_id=sld_id, is_deleted=False).all()
    children_by_parent = defaultdict(list)
    node_map = {}
    for node in nodes:
        node_map[node.id] = node
        if node.parent_id:
            children_by_parent[node.parent_id].append(node.id)

    def trace_downstream(node_id):
        if node_id in visited:
            return
        visited.add(node_id)

        # Get enclosure unit (node + parent + children/siblings)
        enclosure_unit = get_enclosure_unit(node_id, node_map, children_by_parent)

        # Find all outgoing edges from enclosure unit
        for unit_node_id in enclosure_unit:
            for edge in edges_by_source.get(unit_node_id, []):
                target_id = edge.target
                if target_id not in visited:
                    # Add target and its lineage-relevant enclosure
                    enclosure_nodes = get_lineage_enclosure_nodes(target_id, ...)
                    for enc_id in enclosure_nodes:
                        if enc_id not in existing_view_node_ids:
                            result.add(enc_id)
                    # Recurse
                    trace_downstream(target_id)

    for start_id in start_node_ids:
        trace_downstream(start_id)

    return result{code}

----

h2. Parent/Child Constraint Handling

When adding lineage nodes to view, we must ensure parent nodes are included when adding children. The algorithm naturally handles this by:

# Including the full "lineage enclosure" (parent + lineside siblings) for each downstream node
# The existing {{addNodesToView}} endpoint auto-includes children when adding parent enclosures

----

h2. Edge Cases

* *Circular references*: The {{visited}} set prevents infinite loops
* *Already in view*: Filter out nodes already in the view
* *Multiple selected nodes*: Union of all downstream lineages
* *Empty result*: Show message "No additional downstream assets found"

column1: Add Distribute Children and Straighten Downstream to node context menu with enhanced functionality
column41:
h2. Overview

Add "Distribute Children" and "Straighten Downstream" options to the node right-click context menu. Enhance Straighten Downstream to handle simple node-to-node connections (not just enclosures), and update Distribute Children with new sizing and placement logic.

h2. Requirements

* Add "Distribute Children" to context menu (only visible for enclosures with children)
* Add "Straighten Downstream" to context menu (visible for enclosures with children AND regular nodes with downstream connections)
* Straighten Downstream should handle simple node-to-node connections by snapping target directly below source
* Remove staging/accept/discard UI from Straighten Downstream - execute immediately
* Update Distribute Children placement logic:
** Inbound connections → top row
** Both inbound+outbound → top row
** Outbound only → bottom row
** No connections → bottom row
* Update Distribute Children sizing: parent width = 500px × max(top_count, bottom_count), height = 160px

h2. Technical Approach

h3. Files to Modify

* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/NodeContextMenu.jsx}} - Add new menu items with visibility logic
* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/components/GraphEditor.jsx}} - Pass handlers and compute hasChildren for context menu
* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/hooks/useDagreLayout.jsx}} - Modify straightenDownstream to handle simple nodes, remove staging
* {{eg-pz-frontend/src/sld/src/hooks/useSelectionHandlers.jsx}} - Modify handleDistributeNodes with new sizing/placement logic

h3. Implementation Steps

# Add new props to NodeContextMenu: hasChildren, onDistributeChildren, onStraightenDownstream
# Add visibility logic for new menu items
# Update GraphEditor to compute hasChildren on right-click and pass to context menu
# Modify straightenDownstream to detect node type and handle simple node-to-node alignment
# Make straightenDownstream async and auto-save positions immediately (no staging)
# Update handleDistributeNodes row assignment logic (inbound → top, else → bottom)
# Add parent resizing logic (width = 500 × max row count, height = 160)
# Update child positioning to evenly distribute within new width

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Right-click on enclosure with children shows "Distribute Children" option
* [ ] Right-click on enclosure without children does not show "Distribute Children"
* [ ] Right-click on regular node shows "Straighten Downstream" option
* [ ] Right-click on child node (inside enclosure) does not show "Straighten Downstream"
* [ ] Straighten Downstream on simple node snaps target node below source at same X
* [ ] Straighten Downstream executes immediately without Save/Discard prompt
* [ ] Distribute Children places inbound-connected nodes in top row
* [ ] Distribute Children places outbound-only and unconnected nodes in bottom row
* [ ] Distribute Children resizes parent to width = 500 × max(top, bottom), height = 160
* [ ] Children are evenly spaced within their respective rows

h2. Notes

* Regular node height is 80px, gap of 100px used for straighten downstream spacing
* Child nodes are 240px wide, so 500px per child provides 260px spacing
* Both functions should respect FLOW_ONLY_MODE and view mode for saving positions

column1: Add "Search & Pull Upstream" feature to SLD views
column41:
h2. Overview

Add a "Search & Pull Upstream" feature to complement the existing "Search & Pull" (downstream) feature. This finds nodes that are sources of edges pointing TO a given node and pulls them into the current view, positioning them above the source node.

h2. Requirements

* Same UI locations as downstream (ManageAssetsModal + node context menu)
* Show both upstream and downstream count badges
* Position upstream nodes ABOVE the source node (negative Y offset)

h2. Technical Approach

h3. Files to Modify

*Backend:*

* {{app/controllers/sld_controller.py}} - Add {{upstream_outside_view_count}} computation to {{get_all_nodes_summary}}
* {{app/controllers/sld_view_controller.py}} - Add {{pull_and_expand_upstream}} method (mirror of downstream)
* {{app/routes/sld_view_routes.py}} - Add POST {{/sld-view/<view_id>/pull-and-expand-upstream}} route

*Frontend:*

* {{src/store/ui.js}} - Add {{pullAndExpandUpstream}} store function
* {{src/sld/src/components/ManageAssetsModal.jsx}} - Add upstream badge display
* {{src/sld/src/components/ManageAssetsModal.css}} - Add upstream badge styling
* {{src/sld/src/components/GraphEditor.jsx}} - Add {{handleSearchAndPullUpstream}} handler + pass props
* {{src/sld/src/components/NodeContextMenu.jsx}} - Add upstream menu item with Upload icon

h3. Implementation Steps

# Backend: Add {{upstream_map}} and {{upstream_outside_view}} computation to {{get_all_nodes_summary}}
# Backend: Add {{pull_and_expand_upstream}} method (copy downstream, reverse edge direction)
# Backend: Add route for new endpoint
# Frontend: Add {{pullAndExpandUpstream}} store function
# Frontend: Add upstream badge to ManageAssetsModal
# Frontend: Add {{handleSearchAndPullUpstream}} handler to GraphEditor (VERTICAL_OFFSET = -200)
# Frontend: Add menu item to NodeContextMenu

h3. Key Logic Difference

* *Downstream:* {{Edge.source = node_id}} → get {{target}} nodes
* *Upstream:* {{Edge.target = node_id}} → get {{source}} nodes

Same enclosure handling applies (pull parent + all siblings together).

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* [ ] Upstream badge shows count in ManageAssetsModal
* [ ] "Search & Pull Upstream" appears in node context menu
* [ ] Clicking pulls upstream nodes into view
* [ ] Upstream nodes positioned ABOVE source node
* [ ] Enclosure handling works (parent + siblings pulled together)
* [ ] Badge count updates after pull operation

h2. Notes

* Edge cases: enclosure nodes, already-in-view nodes, child nodes (don't show option)
* No upstream nodes returns empty response with {{added_count: 0}}

column1: Overcurrent Protection – Breaker List for 3rd-Party Quoting
column41:
Create a report specifically for overcurrent protection workflows that generates a structured list of breakers to be sent to third-party vendors for breaker testing quotes.

The report must:

* Enumerate all relevant breakers associated with a site / facility / scope.
* Include breaker nameplate (if data present) required for testing quotes.
* Attach or embed breaker images where available.
* Be exportable/shareable as a standalone document (Work/PDF).

This report is not an internal analysis tool; it is an external-facing artifact intended to reduce back-and-forth with third-party testing vendors.



*Acceptance Criteria*

* Report lists all breakers included in the selected scope
* Each breaker entry includes identifying metadata (name/ID, rating, location if available)
* Breaker images are included per breaker when available
* Report can be generated on demand and exported as a PDF
* Output format is suitable for sending directly to third-party breaker testing vendors

column1: Web - User must be able to bulk-add tasks by procedure master
column41:
{color:#ff5630}<<BEGIN AI PROMPT>>{color}
*Backend Enhancement: Node Subtype Enforcement in Procedure Matching*

Update procedure-to-node matching logic across multiple bulk operations to enforce node subtype designations. Currently, these functions match procedures by node_class_id but don't properly prioritize subtype-specific procedures or filter class-level fallbacks.

  *Affected Locations:*

# bulk_task_routes.py → create_unary_tasks() (lines 710-815): Creates tasks from procedure masters for multiple nodes. Current logic matches only on node_class_id and falls back to any procedure for the master if no class match exists.
# quote_controller.py → _process_bulk_add_procedures() (lines 2359-2408, unary mode): Bulk adds quote lines to workorders. Current logic builds a class-level fallback (procedures_by_class) that includes procedures with subtype restrictions, which could incorrectly match a node without a subtype to a subtype-specific procedure.

  *Required Code Changes:*

  The new matching logic should use a priority-based approach:

# Priority 1 (Exact match): procedure_master_id + node_class_id + node_subtype — only if the node has a subtype
# Priority 2 (Class-level): procedure_master_id + node_class_id where procedure.node_subtype IS NULL — procedures with no subtype restriction
# Priority 3 (Any class match): If the node has no subtype, allow matching any procedure for that class (subtype-specific or not)
# No match: If no procedure exists for the node's class, skip the node entirely — no task created (for individual task mode), node not linked (for grouped task mode), or no quote line created

  This ensures subtype-specific procedures take priority when applicable, while still allowing nodes without subtypes to match class-level or any available procedures for their class. Nodes without a matching procedure for their class are skipped rather than falling back to unrelated generic procedures.

  *Required Data Migration:*

  Set the following procedure masters to unary=true, group_tasks=false (creates individual tasks per node with nearest class/subtype match):

* Visual Inspection
* Clean, Tighten, Torque
* Fluid Sample Analysis
* Battery Performance Testing
* Cleaning
* Lubrication
* Mechanical Servicing

{color:#ff5630}<<END AI PROMPT>>{color}



*ACCPETANCE CRITERIA:*

* The above procedure masters appear, in addition to Arc Flash Audit and Infrared Thermography, when bulk adding tasks to work orders or procedures to standard quotes (see screenshots)
* The result of the operation is a single task / quote line that is linked to the appropriate node subtype procedure and form
** Case 1: Exact match exists between node and procedure on class, subtype, and procedure master → this procedure is what gets linked to each task or line
** Case 2: Node has no subtype, and no procedure exists that share class + procedure master + null subtype → the task or line is linked to any procedure that share class + procedure master (regardless of subtype)
** Case 3: Node has null subtype, and a procedure exists that shares class + procedure master + null subtype → the task or line is linked to that null subtype procedure
** Case 4: Node has subtype, and a procedure exists that shares class + procedure master + null subtype, but not that node’s specific subtype → the task or line is linked to that null subtype procedure
** Case 5: Node has subtype, and all procedures that exist for that class / procedure master have a different node subtype → this procedure is skipped
** Case 6: Node has no subtype, and no procedures exist for that class / procedure master → this procedure is skipped

column1: Android | Error displayed when clicking Junction Box to connect after it is already connected to another asset
column41:
h3. *Bug Description*

When a Junction Box is already connected to another asset class, clicking on the same Junction Box again to create another connection shows a *“Failed to connect”* error. The system does not clearly indicate that the Junction Box is already connected, which causes confusion for the user.

----

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Open the SLD (Single Line Diagram) screen.
# Add a *Junction Box* asset.
# Connect the Junction Box to any other asset class (e.g., Relay, Panel, etc.).
# After the connection is successfully created, click on the same Junction Box again.
# Try to connect it to another asset.

----

h3. *Actual Result*

* A *“Failed to connect”* error message is displayed.

----

h3. *Expected Result*

* The system should either:
** Prevent the user from attempting a second connection with a clear validation message, *or*
** Allow the connection if multiple connections are supported.
* A proper and understandable message should be shown instead of a generic error.
!1000197233.mp4|width=643,alt="1000197233.mp4"!

column1: iOS | SLD Connection Between Junction Box and Relay Is Removed After Dashboard Refresh
column41:
h3. *Description*

In the SLD, when a Junction Box asset class is connected to a Relay asset class, the connection is created successfully.
However, after navigating to the Dashboard and clicking the Refresh button (which refreshes the app), the previously created connection between the Junction Box and Relay is removed when returning to the SLD.
This indicates that the SLD connection is not being persisted after an application refresh.

----

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Open the *SLD*.
# Add a *Junction Box* asset class.
# Add a *Relay* asset class.
# Connect the Junction Box to the Relay.
# Navigate to the *Dashboard*.
# Click on the *Refresh* button (app refresh).
# Go back to the *SLD*.
# Check the connection between the Junction Box and Relay.

----

h3. *Expected Result*

* The connection between the Junction Box and Relay should remain intact after the dashboard/app refresh.
* SLD connections should be persisted correctly.

----

h3. *Actual Result*

* The connection between the Junction Box and Relay is removed after the dashboard refresh.
* The SLD does not retain the previously saved connection.
!Video_20260101_140713_817.mp4|width=592,alt="Video_20260101_140713_817.mp4"!

column1: Android | SLD Node Is Not Created When Connecting Junction Box to Relay Asset
column41:
h3. *Description*

In the SLD, when a Junction Box asset class is connected to a Relay asset class, the node is not created or connected properly.
When attempting to connect the same assets again, the system throws an error stating that the node already exists. This indicates that the node is created internally but is not visible or properly linked in the SLD UI.

----

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Open the SLD module.
# Add a *Junction Box* asset class.
# Add a *Relay* asset class.
# Try to connect the Junction Box to the Relay.
# Observe that the node connection is not visible or not created.
# Try to connect the same Junction Box and Relay again.
# An error appears stating that the node is already created.

----

h3. *Expected Result*

* The node should be created and visibly connected in the SLD on the first attempt.
* Reconnecting the same assets should not show a “node already created” error.

----

h3. *Actual Result*

* The node is not visible or connected in the SLD.
* On retry, the system shows an error that the node already exists.
!1000196975.mp4|width=643,alt="1000196975.mp4"!

column1: Apple Store Organisation Account
column41:
h3. Current Situation

The iOS app is currently published using Eric's personal Apple Developer account. This creates several risks and limitations for the organization.

h3. Problem Statement

*Risks with Personal Account:*

* Single point of failure - if Eric's account has issues, app distribution is blocked
* Limited team access - difficult for other developers to manage certificates, provisioning profiles
* Professional appearance - organization account shows company name in App Store
* Legal/IP concerns - app technically owned by individual, not company

h3. Goal

Migrate the iOS app from Eric's personal Apple Developer account to an eGalvanic organization account (Apple Developer Program for Organizations).

h3. Requirements

*Prerequisites:*

* Apple Developer Program enrollment as an Organization ($99/year)
* D-U-N-S Number for eGalvanic (if not already obtained)
* Legal entity verification documents
* Organization email addresses for team members

*Migration Scope:*

* Transfer app ownership to organization account
* Migrate certificates and provisioning profiles
* Update App Store Connect access for team members
* Configure proper team roles and permissions
* Update CI/CD pipeline with new signing credentials
* Verify TestFlight distribution still works
* Ensure push notification certificates are migrated

h3. Acceptance Criteria

* ✅ Apple Developer Program organization account created for eGalvanic
* ✅ iOS app successfully transferred to organization account
* ✅ All certificates and provisioning profiles migrated and working
* ✅ Team members have appropriate access levels in App Store Connect
* ✅ CI/CD pipeline updated with new signing credentials
* ✅ App can be built, signed, and deployed from organization account
* ✅ TestFlight builds can be distributed successfully
* ✅ Push notifications continue to work
* ✅ No disruption to existing users (app updates work seamlessly)
* ✅ Documentation updated with new account details

h3. Implementation Steps

*Phase 1: Setup Organization Account*

# Enroll in Apple Developer Program as Organization
# Complete legal entity verification
# Pay annual fee ($99)
# Set up organization admin account

*Phase 2: Prepare for Migration*

# Document current certificates, provisioning profiles, app IDs
# Export/backup current signing credentials
# Note all app services in use (push notifications, iCloud, etc.)
# Create migration plan with rollback strategy

*Phase 3: Execute Migration*

# Create new App ID in organization account
# Transfer app from personal to organization account (via Apple Support)
# Generate new certificates and provisioning profiles
# Update Xcode project with new team/signing settings
# Rebuild and test app with new credentials

*Phase 4: Update Infrastructure*

# Update CI/CD with new signing credentials
# Configure fastlane/automation tools with new account
# Test build and deployment pipeline
# Update documentation

*Phase 5: Team Access*

# Invite team members to App Store Connect
# Assign appropriate roles (Admin, Developer, Marketing, etc.)
# Configure two-factor authentication for all users

*Phase 6: Verification*

# Submit test build to TestFlight
# Verify app can be updated from organization account
# Test push notifications
# Verify all app services functioning
# Deploy to production

h3. Important Notes

* *App Transfer Process:* Apple requires manual approval for app transfers. Submit request via App Store Connect.
* *User Impact:* If done correctly, existing users won't notice any change. App updates work seamlessly.
* *Bundle ID:* Bundle identifier remains the same during transfer.
* *Ratings/Reviews:* All existing ratings and reviews are preserved.
* *Timing:* Plan migration during low-activity period to minimize risk.
* *Communication:* Notify team before migration begins.

h3. Resources

* [Apple Developer Program - Organizations|https://developer.apple.com/programs/enroll/]
* [Transferring Apps|https://developer.apple.com/help/app-store-connect/manage-your-apps/transfer-an-app]
* [Managing Team Members|https://developer.apple.com/help/app-store-connect/manage-your-team/]

h3. Risks & Mitigation

||Risk||Mitigation||
|App transfer rejected by Apple|Ensure all legal documentation is accurate|
|CI/CD pipeline breaks|Test in staging environment first, have rollback plan|
|Push notifications stop working|Migrate and test certificates before production deployment|
|Team members lose access temporarily|Schedule migration during non-critical period|
|Build/signing issues|Keep backup of old certificates until fully verified|

column1: FE | Connection Not Deleted When Load-Side Line Is Removed from Asset on SLD Page
column41:
*Description:*
On the SLD (Single Line Diagram) page, when a connection is created and then an asset is edited directly from the SLD page, deleting the line from the load side does not remove the associated connection. The connection still remains even after the load-side line is deleted.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open the SLD page.
# Create a connection between assets.
# From the SLD page, edit any connected asset.
# Delete the line from the load side of the asset.

column1: FE: Support multi-handle edge selection on node connection
column41:
When connecting edges to or from multi-handle nodes via double click, prompt the user to select which handle the new edge should attach to.
The selected handle must be persisted in the Edges table.

Acceptance Criteria:

* Double-clicking to connect an edge to a multi-handle node opens a handle-selection dialog.
* The dialog lists all valid handles for the node (e.g., “N” for ATS).
* Users must select a handle before the edge is created.
* Selected handle information is stored in the Edges table.
* Existing edges without handle metadata continue to render and function correctly.

column1: FE: Refactor SLD nodes to dynamic node component
column41:
Refactor SLD node rendering to use a single dynamic React component instead of multiple hardcoded node components (e.g., ATSNode.jsx, TransformerNode.jsx).
The dynamic node component renders icons, handles, and behavior based on node class metadata rather than node-specific React files.

Acceptance Criteria:

* All SLD nodes are rendered using a single dynamic node component.
* Node icon selection is driven by node class properties.
* Handle count, type, and placement are derived from node metadata.
* Existing node types (ATS, Transformer, Tie Breaker, etc.) render correctly.
* No regressions in existing SLD interactions or performance.

column1: FE: Search & Pull downstream assets into SLD view
column41:
Add a “Search & Pull” action to the SLD right-click menu that allows users to discover and pull in downstream assets that exist in the system but are not currently visible in the active SLD view.
This enables incremental expansion of the diagram starting from a known asset (e.g., utility or generator) without loading the full network.

Acceptance Criteria:

* Right-clicking an asset includes a “Search & Pull downstream assets” option.
* The system identifies downstream assets not currently present in the active view.
* Selected downstream assets are added to the SLD view without reloading the page.
* Newly pulled assets preserve correct parent-child and edge relationships.
* Search & Pull respects existing view filters and does not duplicate assets already shown.

column1: FE: Export selected SLD assets to PDF
column41:
Enable users to export selected assets from the SLD into a PDF for reporting and sharing.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* Users can select one or more assets on the SLD.
* Users can initiate an export action for selected assets.
* The system generates a PDF containing only the selected assets.
* Users can choose the output page size before export.
* Exported PDFs accurately reflect the SLD layout and selections.

column1: FE: Collapse and clean expand downstream assets
column41:
Implement logic to collapse all downstream assets from a selected node and progressively re-expand them one level at a time to help users clean up complex diagrams.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* Users can collapse all downstream assets from a selected asset.
* Collapsed assets are visually hidden from the SLD.
* Users can expand downstream assets one hierarchy level at a time.
* Expanded assets maintain correct parent-child relationships.
* Repeated expand actions progressively reveal deeper levels.

column1: FE: Enhanced right-click navigation on SLD assets
column41:
Add an enhanced contextual right-click menu to SLD assets to improve navigation and bulk selection actions.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* Right-clicking an asset opens a contextual menu.
* Menu includes options to:
** Collapse all downstream assets.
** Expand clean (expand one level at a time after collapse).
** Select full lineage (upstream and downstream).
** Select downstream assets only.
** Select upstream assets only.
** Delete the selected asset.
* Actions apply only to the selected asset context.
* Menu actions update the SLD without requiring a page refresh.

column1: FE: Auto-generate SLD views by location
column41:
Automatically generate SLD views based on asset location metadata to simplify navigation and filtering for users.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* The system can generate views based on defined asset locations.
* Each generated view includes only assets from its associated location.
* Auto-generated views are clearly labeled and identifiable.
* Users can apply auto-generated views like custom views.
* Users can delete auto-generated views if desired.
column211: 30/Dec/25 7:48 AM;712020:506eab12-6be8-4af7-8629-0824e8bb2fb2;The ticket has been verified against Acceptance Criteria on DEV env!

column1: FE: Create and manage SLD views
column41:
Introduce Views for the SLD to allow users to filter and display a subset of assets.

Views allow users to save and reuse filtered SLD configurations.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* Users can create a new custom view.
* Users can edit an existing view.
* Users can delete a view.
* Applying a view filters the SLD to only show assets included in that view.
* Views persist across user sessions.

column1: FE: Trace and highlight asset lineage on SLD
column41:
Add the ability to trace asset lineage on the Single Line Diagram (SLD), allowing users to clearly understand upstream and downstream relationships.

Functionality includes:

* Tracing upstream assets from a selected asset.
* Tracing downstream assets from a selected asset.
* Visually highlighting the selected asset and its lineage path on the SLD.


*Acceptance Criteria*

* Users can select an asset and view its upstream lineage.
* Users can select an asset and view its downstream lineage.
* Lineage paths are visually distinct from non-related assets.
* Highlighting is cleared or updated when a new asset is selected.
* Lineage tracing performs reliably on large SLDs.

column1: SLD Improvements
column41:
This epic covers a set of enhancements to the Single Line Diagram (SLD) to improve asset visibility, navigation, filtering, and export.

*Asset Lineage & Highlighting*

* Enable tracing of asset lineage, including upstream and downstream paths.
* Allow users to highlight a selected asset and its related lineage on the SLD.

*Views Management*

* Introduce the concept of Views to allow users to filter and display selected assets on the SLD.
* Users should be able to:
** Create new custom views.
** Auto-generate views based on locations.
** Edit existing views.
** Delete views.

*Enhanced Right-Click Navigation*

* Add contextual right-click menu options on SLD assets:
** Collapse all downstream assets.
** Expand clean (after collapse, expand assets one level at a time to progressively clean downstream visibility).
** Select full lineage (upstream + downstream).
** Select downstream assets only.
** Select upstream assets only.
** Delete an asset.

*SLD Printing / Export*

* Allow users to select one or more assets on the SLD.
* Enable export of the selected assets to a PDF.
* Support exporting based on a user-selected page size.

#* *≥600*

Introduce an automated reminder system to track test equipment calibration dates and notify users before and after calibration is due. This is to ensure that only in-calibration equipment is used during site visits / jobs and audits.

Acceptance Criteria

* Test equipment has calibration fields: last_calibration_date, calibration_due_date, calibration_interval_months, calibration_provider, calibration_certificate_number.
* When last_calibration_date and calibration_interval_months are set, calibration_due_date is auto-calculated.
* Calibration status (OK, Due Soon, Overdue) is calculated correctly based on current date and calibration_due_date and is available via API.
* Unit tests cover status calculation, reminder generation

column1: iOS | App freezes for several seconds on tapping Arc Flash
column41:
*Issue Type:* Bug
*Priority:* High
*Component:* Mobile – iOS

h3. *Description*

When the user taps on *Arc Flash*, the iOS application becomes unresponsive and freezes for *5 seconds or more*. Additionally, if the user taps multiple buttons repeatedly (including Arc Flash or other actions) while the app is frozen or lagging, the application *hangs*

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Launch the iOS application.
# Log in with valid credentials.
# Navigate to a screen where *Arc Flash* option is available.
# Tap on *Arc Flash*.
# Observe that the application freezes for *5 or more seconds*.
# While the app is frozen

h3. *Expected Result*

* App should respond immediately when *Arc Flash* is tapped.
* No UI freeze or lag should occur.
* Repeated button interactions should be handled gracefully.
* Application should *not hang or crash* under any condition.

h3. *Actual Result*

* App freezes for *5+ seconds* after tapping *Arc Flash*.
* UI becomes unresponsive during this time.
* Repeated button taps cause the app to *hang*.
* App *crashes  (≈1 out of 10 attempts)*.

column1: Android | Upload ProGuard Mapping File to Sentry
column41:
When ProGuard/R8 obfuscation is enabled in release builds, crash logs in Sentry display obfuscated class names, method names, and line numbers (e.g., {{a.b.c.d()}} instead of {{com.example.UserRepository.fetchData()}}). This makes debugging production crashes extremely difficult.
To restore readable stack traces, Sentry needs the {{mapping.txt}} file generated during each release build.

Objective
Automatically upload the ProGuard {{mapping.txt}} file to Sentry as part of the release build process so that all crash reports display deobfuscated, human-readable stack traces.

Requirements

* *Integrate Sentry Gradle Plugin* to handle automatic mapping file uploads
* *Configure authentication* with Sentry using auth token
* *Ensure mapping files are uploaded* for every release build (both local and CI/CD)
* *Associate mappings with correct version* using {{versionName}} and {{versionCode}}

Implementation Steps

# Add Sentry Gradle Plugin
In your project-level {{build.gradle}} or {{build.gradle.kts}}:

{noformat}plugins {
    id("io.sentry.android.gradle") version "4.14.1" // Use latest version
}
{noformat}

# Configure Sentry Plugin
In your app-level {{build.gradle.kts}}:

{noformat}sentry {
    // Enables automatic upload of ProGuard mappings
    autoUploadProguardMapping.set(true)
    
    // Organization and project slugs from Sentry
    org.set("your-org-slug")
    projectName.set("your-project-slug")
    
    // Auth token (use environment variable, never hardcode)
    authToken.set(System.getenv("SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN"))
    
    // Include source context for better debugging (optional)
    includeSourceContext.set(true)
}
{noformat}

# Generate Sentry Auth Token

* Go to Sentry → Settings → Auth Tokens
* Create a new token with scopes: {{project:releases}}, {{project:write}}, {{org:read}}
* Store token securely as environment variable

# Configure CI/CD (GitHub Actions example)
Add the auth token as a repository secret and include in workflow:

{noformat}env:
  SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN }}
{noformat}

# Verify ProGuard is Generating Mapping File
Ensure your release build type has:

{noformat}buildTypes {
    release {
        isMinifyEnabled = true
        isShrinkResources = true
        proguardFiles(
            getDefaultProguardFile("proguard-android-optimize.txt"),
            "proguard-rules.pro"
        )
    }
}
{noformat}

Acceptance Criteria

* -Sentry Gradle plugin is integrated and configured-
* -Auth token is securely stored as environment variable (not committed to repo)-
* -Running {{./gradlew assembleRelease}} automatically uploads mapping file to Sentry-
* -CI/CD pipeline successfully uploads mappings on release builds-
* -Crash reports in Sentry dashboard show deobfuscated stack traces with:-
* -Readable class names-
* -Readable method names-
* -Correct line numbers-
* -Each app version has its corresponding mapping file in Sentry-

Verification

* Build a release APK: {{./gradlew assembleRelease}}
* Check build logs for Sentry upload confirmation
* In Sentry dashboard, go to *Settings → ProGuard* to verify mapping file was uploaded
* Trigger a test crash and confirm the stack trace is readable

References

* [Sentry Android ProGuard Documentation|https://docs.sentry.io/platforms/android/configuration/gradle/]
* [Sentry Gradle Plugin GitHub|https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-android-gradle-plugin]

Notes

* Mapping files are unique per build; ensure version codes increment for each release
* Consider enabling {{includeSourceContext}} for even better debugging context
* For manual uploads (if needed): {{sentry-cli upload-proguard mapping.txt}}

column1: Android | Attachment Uploaded Offline During Site Visit Does Not Appear in Asset After Sync
column41:
*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open the mobile application.
# Turn off the internet connection (offline mode).
# Go to *Site Visit*.
# Upload an attachment.
# Select *Task* and *Asset* while uploading the attachment.
# Save the attachment.
# Turn the internet connection back on.
# Sync the offline data.
# Open the web application.
# Navigate to the same asset.
# Check the *Attachments* section.

*Expected Result:*
The attachment uploaded during the site visit in offline mode should appear in both:

* Site Visit attachments
* Asset attachments after sync

*Actual Result:*
The attachment appears correctly in the Site Visit, but it does *not* appear in the Asset’s Attachments section on the web after sync.
This issue occurs *only when the attachment is uploaded in offline mode*.
In online mode, the attachment works as expected.

23/Dec/25 3:35 AM;712020:19109706-a11c-4efd-abee-5ca72d900e45;[~accountid:712020:506eab12-6be8-4af7-8629-0824e8bb2fb2] 

*Current Behavior:* When a user updates an issue, the UI briefly displays old/stale data instead of the newly updated data.

*Root Cause:* The mutation endpoint responds immediately with a success status before the actual database update is completed. The update processing happens asynchronously in the background. If the UI refreshes or fetches data right after receiving the mutation response, it retrieves the old data because the background update hasn't finished yet. So if you will try after 1 or 2 minutes it should be display the updated records.

column1: SLDs – Search returns same results on repeated searches in SLD view
column41:
On the *SLD (Single Line Diagram)* screen, the *Search* functionality does not refresh results when a new search is performed.
After the first successful search, performing another search shows the *same previous results*, even when a different keyword is entered.

h3. *Pre-requisites*

* User is logged into the web application
* User has access to a site with SLD data

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Log in to the web application.
# Navigate to any site that has SLDs configured.
# Open the *SLDs (Single Line Diagram)* screen.
# Click on the *Search* button available at the *bottom-right* of the screen.
# Enter a valid keyword and perform the search.
# Verify that the correct search results are displayed.
# Clear or change the search keyword.
# Perform the search again with a *different keyword*.
# Observe the search results.

h3. *Expected Result*

* Search results should update based on the *latest search keyword*.
* Each search action should return fresh and relevant results.
* Previous search results should not persist after a new search is executed.

h3. *Actual Result*

* Search results remain the *same as the first search*.
* New search keywords do not update or refresh the results.
* Search appears to be stuck with the initial result set.

h3. *Priority*

Medium

h3. *Environment*

* Platform: Web
* Module: SLDs

column1: SLDs – Filters are not working on SLD view
column41:
Filters on the *SLD (Single Line Diagram)* screen are not functioning as expected.
When a user applies any available filter on the SLD view, the diagram does not update based on the selected filter criteria. This impacts usability and makes it difficult to analyze or isolate specific assets/nodes on the SLD.

h3. *Pre-requisites*

* User is logged into the web application
* User has access to a site with SLD data available

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Log in to the web application.
# Select any site that has an SLD configured.
# Navigate to the *SLD (Single Line Diagram)* view.
# Open the *Filter* option available on the SLD screen.
# Apply any filter.
# Observe the SLD after applying the filter.

h3. *Acceptance Criteria*

* SLD should update based on the selected filter.
* Only assets/nodes matching the filter criteria should be visible on the SLD.
* Non-matching elements should be hidden or excluded.

h3. *Priority*

Medium 

h3. *Environment*

* Platform: Web
* Module: SLD
* Browser: Chrome / Firefox (reproducible across browsers)

column1: iOS: OCP Not Visible After Changing Asset Class from MCC to MCC Bucket (Visible on Web)
column41:
*Module:* Assets
*Platform:* iOS and web

h3. *Description*

When the asset class is changed from *MCC* to *MCC Bucket*, the *OCP is not visible in the iOS app*. However, the same OCP is *visible correctly in the web application* for the same asset.

h3. *Pre-requisites*

* User is logged in to the iOS application
* Asset exists with Asset Class = MCC
* OCP is already associated with the asset

h3. *Steps to Reproduce*

# Log in to the iOS application.
# Open an existing asset with Asset Class set to *MCC* with OCP created or create a MCC class with OCP. 
# Edit the asset.
# Change the Asset Class from *MCC* to *MCC Bucket*.
# Save the asset.
# Navigate to the OCP section of the same asset in the iOS app.
# Open the same asset in the *web application* and check the OCP.

h3. *Expected Result*

* OCP should be visible in the iOS application after changing the asset class to *MCC Bucket*, consistent with the web application.

h3. *Actual Result*

* OCP is *not visible in the iOS application* after changing the asset class to *MCC Bucket*.
* OCP is *visible correctly in the web application* for the same asset.
