03/Mar/26 2:00 AM;712020:9881bb6e-cbf7-47a6-bf7a-838a383b2866;[~accountid:712020:506eab12-6be8-4af7-8629-0824e8bb2fb2] 

h3. Changes Made

# *P2: Await onSyncLog* — Changed {{onSyncLog}} callback from fire-and-forget {{Task {}}} to awaited, eliminating non-deterministic {{context.save()}} timing during sync chunk processing
# *P0: SessionRoomAssetRow snapshots* — Added init-time snapshots for {{node_class}}, {{node_tasks}}, {{issues}}, {{ir_photos}} relationships to prevent EXC_BAD_ACCESS during sync-triggered re-renders
# *P0: AssetsTabView let bindings* — Captured {{node_class?.style}}, {{node_class?.name}}, {{room?.name}} into local snapshots in row builder functions

h3. Files Modified

* {{SyncExecutionService.swift}} — async callback type + await at 4 call sites
* {{NetworkState.swift}} — removed fire-and-forget Task wrapper on onSyncLog
* {{SessionRoomDetailView.swift}} — SessionRoomAssetRow init with 8 relationship snapshots
* {{AssetsTabView.swift}} — local let bindings in nodeRow() and multiSelectNodeRow()

----

h3. Preconditions (All Tests)

* App installed with the new build
* User logged in with valid session
* At least one SLD/site with *10+ nodes* (some with node classes, rooms, tasks, issues, IR photos)
* Device has internet connectivity
* Some *pending offline changes* in sync queue (or ability to create them by going offline → making edits → coming back online)

----

h2. Section 1: Sync Functionality (P2 — onSyncLog await)

h3. Test 1.1: Basic Sync Flush

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Go offline, make 5-10 node edits (change labels, move nodes, etc.)|Changes saved locally, sync queue count increases|
|2|Come back online|Sync queue count visible|
|3|Tap Sync button|Sync starts, progress bar updates smoothly|
|4|Wait for sync to complete|All operations succeed, sync queue count drops to 0|
|5|Pull-to-refresh or re-fetch data|All changes reflected from server|

*Pass criteria:* Sync completes without errors, no crash, all data synced correctly.

h3. Test 1.2: Large Sync (Chunk Processing)

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Go offline| |
|2|Make *20+ changes* across nodes, edges, photos|Queue count shows 20+ items|
|3|Come back online and tap Sync|Sync processes in chunks|
|4|Wait for completion|All operations succeed|
|5|Check sync log/history if available|All operations logged with correct timestamps|

*Pass criteria:* Large sync completes fully, no missing operations, no crash.

h3. Test 1.3: Sync with Network Interruption

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Create 10+ pending changes| |
|2|Start sync|Progress begins|
|3|Toggle airplane mode mid-sync|Sync detects network loss, shows appropriate error|
|4|Re-enable network|Remaining items stay in queue|
|5|Tap Sync again|Remaining items sync successfully|

*Pass criteria:* No crash on network loss. Remaining items preserved and sync-able.

h3. Test 1.4: Sync with No Pending Changes

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Ensure sync queue is empty (count = 0)| |
|2|Tap Sync|Sync starts and completes immediately with no errors|

*Pass criteria:* No crash, no error messages.

----

h2. Section 2: Session Room Asset List (P0 — SessionRoomAssetRow)

h3. Test 2.1: Asset Row Display — Basic

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Open a session with multiple rooms| |
|2|Navigate to a room with 5+ assets|Asset rows display correctly|
|3|Verify each row shows: node class icon (correct style), node label, node class name|All correct|
|4|Scroll through the list|All rows render correctly, no blank rows|

h3. Test 2.2: Asset Row — Expand/Collapse

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Find an asset with child nodes (expandable)| |
|2|Tap the chevron to expand|Children appear, chevron rotates|
|3|Verify children show correct class icons and labels|Data correct|
|4|Tap again to collapse|Children hide|
|5|Repeat rapidly 5+ times|No crash, smooth animation|

h3. Test 2.3: Asset Row — Task Indicators

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Find a node with linked tasks| |
|2|Verify task completion indicator shows correct state|Matches actual task state|
|3|Tap into the node → complete a task|Task marked complete|
|4|Navigate back to room asset list|Task indicator updated to reflect completion|
|5|Tap back in → uncomplete the task|Task unmarked|
|6|Navigate back|Indicator reverts|

h3. Test 2.4: Asset Row — Issue Indicators

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Find a node with linked issues| |
|2|Verify issue indicator shows correct state|Matches actual issue state|
|3|Tap into node → resolve an issue| |
|4|Navigate back|Issue indicator updated|
|5|Tap back in → reopen the issue| |
|6|Navigate back|Indicator reverts|

h3. Test 2.5: Asset Row — IR Photo Count Overlay

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Enable IR overlay filter on asset list| |
|2|Verify IR photo counts display correctly per node|Counts match actual IR photos|
|3|Tap into a node → add a new IR photo pair| |
|4|Navigate back|IR count incremented|

h3. Test 2.6: Asset Row — Arc Flash Overlay

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Enable Arc Flash overlay filter| |
|2|Verify green checkmark for complete nodes, red X for incomplete|Matches actual AF completion|
|3|Tap into an incomplete node → fill in required AF attributes| |
|4|Navigate back|Status changes to green checkmark|

h3. Test 2.7: Asset Row — COM Overlay

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Enable COM overlay filter| |
|2|Verify checkmarks for nodes with class + subtype + COM values|Matches actual data|

h3. Test 2.8: Asset Row — Context Menu

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Long-press an asset row|Context menu appears|
|2|Tap "Add IR Photos"|Navigates to IR photos mode|
|3|Go back, long-press again|Context menu appears|
|4|Tap "Collect AF Data"|Navigates to AF data mode|

h3. Test 2.9: Node Class Change Reflects in List

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Note a node's class icon and name in the list| |
|2|Tap into the node → change its node class| |
|3|Save and navigate back|Row shows new class icon and new class name|

----

h2. Section 3: Assets Tab View (P0 — AssetsTabView)

h3. Test 3.1: Asset List Display

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Navigate to the Assets tab (SLD-level view)|All nodes listed|
|2|Verify each row shows correct node class icon, label, class name, room name|All correct|

h3. Test 3.2: Asset Search

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Type a node's label in search|Node appears in filtered list|
|2|Clear search, type a node class name|Nodes of that class appear|
|3|Clear search, type a room name|Nodes in that room appear|
|4|Type gibberish|No results shown|
|5|Clear search|Full list restored|

h3. Test 3.3: Multi-Select Mode

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Enter multi-select mode| |
|2|Verify rows show selection circles, correct class icon and class name|Correct|
|3|Select/deselect several nodes|Selection toggles correctly|

h3. Test 3.4: Grouping Options

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Switch grouping to "By Location"|Nodes grouped by room|
|2|Verify room names NOT shown in rows (grouped already)|Room column hidden|
|3|Switch to "No Grouping"|Flat list restored|

----

h2. Section 4: THE CRASH SCENARIO (Critical Path)

h3. Test 4.1: Sync While Viewing Session Room Assets ⚠️

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Go offline| |
|2|Make *15-20 node edits* (label changes, class changes, attribute edits)|Queue fills up|
|3|Come back online| |
|4|Navigate to a *session room detail* with multiple assets visible| |
|5|*While viewing the asset list*, tap Sync|Sync runs while assets are displayed|
|6|*Do NOT navigate away* — stay on the asset list during sync|App should NOT crash|
|7|Wait for sync to complete|All operations succeed, asset list still displayed|
|8|Verify asset data is still correct|All rows display correctly|

*Pass criteria:* No crash. This is the exact scenario that caused the original EXC_BAD_ACCESS.

h3. Test 4.2: Sync While Viewing Assets Tab ⚠️

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Go offline, make 15-20 edits| |
|2|Come back online| |
|3|Navigate to the *Assets Tab* (AssetsTabView)| |
|4|*While viewing the asset list*, tap Sync| |
|5|Stay on the assets tab during entire sync|App should NOT crash|
|6|After sync, verify all rows still display correctly| |

h3. Test 4.3: Sync While Scrolling Asset List ⚠️

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Create 15-20 pending changes| |
|2|Navigate to session room with many assets| |
|3|Tap Sync| |
|4|*Actively scroll* up and down through the asset list during sync|App should NOT crash|
|5|Continue scrolling until sync completes| |

h3. Test 4.4: Sync While Expanding/Collapsing Assets

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Create pending changes| |
|2|Navigate to session room with expandable assets| |
|3|Tap Sync| |
|4|*Rapidly expand/collapse* asset rows during sync|App should NOT crash|

h3. Test 4.5: Sync While on Location Hierarchy

||Step||Action||Expected Result||
|1|Create pending changes| |
|2|Navigate to *Location Hierarchy* view (uses same SessionRoomAssetRow)| |
|3|Tap Sync while viewing the hierarchy| |
|4|Stay on screen until sync completes|App should NOT crash|

----

h2. Section 5: Edge Cases

h3. Test 5.1: Node with No Class (Unclassified)

Verify row shows default icon and node type as fallback text.

h3. Test 5.2: Node with No Room

In AssetsTabView, verify "No location" shown in red.

h3. Test 5.3: Empty Session (No Assets)

Navigate to empty room — no crash, appropriate empty message.

h3. Test 5.4: Rapid Navigation During Sync

Start sync with 20+ items, rapidly navigate between rooms/detail/back during sync — no crash.

----

h2. Test Priority Matrix

||Priority||Tests||Time Estimate||
|*Must Pass*|4.1, 4.2, 4.3 (crash scenarios)|~15 min|
|*Must Pass*|1.1, 1.2 (sync basic functionality)|~10 min|
|*Must Pass*|2.1, 2.3, 2.4 (row display + indicators)|~10 min|
|*Should Pass*|2.2, 2.5, 2.6, 2.9, 3.1, 3.2|~15 min|
|*Good to Have*|4.4, 4.5, 5.1-5.4, 1.3, 1.4|~15 min|
| |*Total*|*~65 min*|

If time is limited, run just the *"Must Pass"* tests (~35 min). The crash scenarios (Section 4) are the most critical.

h2. Manual QA Test Plan: SwiftData Crash Fix (Z-PLATFORM-5S)

h3. Changes Summary

5 changes across 2 files to eliminate unsafe SwiftData to-many relationship access during SwiftUI body evaluation in {{EditNodeDetailView}}:

# Added {{originalIRPhotos}} snapshot property (same pattern as existing {{originalPhotos}})
# Fixed {{profilePhoto}} to use {{visiblePhotos}} instead of live {{node.photos}}/{{node.defaultPhoto}}
# Fixed {{getExistingIRPhotos()}} to read from snapshot instead of {{node.ir_photos}}
# Fixed {{IRPhotosCard}} to receive pre-computed {{[IRPhoto]}} instead of holding a live {{NodeV2}} reference
# Fixed {{NodeCoreAttributesSection.hasPhotos}} to receive a Bool parameter from {{visiblePhotos}} instead of accessing {{node.photos}}

h3. Prerequisites

* Device or simulator with the app installed
* A node with existing photos, IR photos, and core attributes
* A node with NO photos (for empty state testing)
* An active IR session configured
* Network connection (for sync and extraction tests)

----

h3. Test 1: Crash Scenario (The Bug Fix) — PRIORITY 1

||Step||Action||Expected||
|1|Open a node with photos + IR photos in EditNodeDetailView|View loads normally, profile photo shows, IR photos section shows|
|2|Press Home button (background the app)|App goes to background|
|3|Wait 5-10 seconds (let VersionCheckService → flushQueue → refreshFromServer run)|—|
|4|Return to the app|*No crash.* View displays correctly. Profile photo still shows. IR photos still listed|
|5|Repeat steps 2-4 several times|No crash on any attempt|
|6|After returning, tap Save|Save completes normally|

----

h3. Test 2: Profile Photo Display — PRIORITY 3

||Step||Action||Expected||
|1|Open a node with multiple photos, one set as default|Profile header shows the default photo|
|2|Open a node with photos but no explicit default|Profile header shows the first node_profile type photo, or first available photo|
|3|Open a node with zero photos|Profile header shows placeholder/no photo|
|4|Add a new photo (stage it)|Profile photo updates if it's the first photo (auto-default)|
|5|Delete the current default photo (stage deletion)|Profile photo falls back to next available photo|
|6|Set a different photo as default|Profile photo updates immediately to the new selection|
|7|Save all changes|Profile photo persists correctly after save|

----

h3. Test 3: Photo Addition / Deletion Flow — PRIORITY 3

||Step||Action||Expected||
|1|Open a node with no photos|Photo section shows empty state|
|2|Add a photo|Photo appears in the photo section. Profile header updates|
|3|Add a second photo|Both photos visible|
|4|Delete the first photo|Only second photo remains. Profile photo updates|
|5|Save|Changes persist. Reopen node — photos correct|
|6|Open a node with existing photos|All existing photos display|
|7|Delete an existing photo, add a new one|Deleted photo gone, new photo shows. Save works|

----

h3. Test 4: IR Photos Display — PRIORITY 4

||Step||Action||Expected||
|1|Open a node WITH existing IR photos (and active IR session)|IR Photos section shows. Existing IR photos listed with correct keys and dates|
|2|Open a node with NO IR photos (but active IR session)|IR Photos section shows with input fields, no existing photos listed|
|3|Open a node with existing IR photos but NO active session|IR Photos section shows existing photos (read-only), no input fields|
|4|Open a node with no IR photos and no active session|IR Photos section hidden entirely|

----

h3. Test 5: IR Photo Staging — PRIORITY 4

||Step||Action||Expected||
|1|Open node with active IR session|IR input fields visible|
|2|Enter IR photo key only (FLIR-SEP session)|"Add" button stays disabled (need both keys)|
|3|Enter both visual + IR photo keys|"Add" button enables|
|4|Tap Add|Staged IR photo appears in "New IR Photos" section. Input fields reset to next keys|
|5|Add a second IR photo pair|Both staged photos listed|
|6|Delete a staged IR photo (tap X)|Staged photo removed from list|
|7|Save|All staged IR photos persist. Reopen node — they show as existing|
|8|For FLIR-IND session: enter only IR key|"Add" button enables (visual not required). Visual field shows disabled state|

----

h3. Test 6: AI Extraction Button (Core Attributes) — PRIORITY 5

||Step||Action||Expected||
|1|Open a node with NO photos (online mode)|Extraction button (sparkles icon) is *disabled/grayed out*|
|2|Add a photo (stage it, don't save yet)|Extraction button *enables immediately* (blue)|
|3|Delete the only photo (stage deletion)|Extraction button *disables immediately*|
|4|Open a node WITH existing photos|Extraction button is enabled|
|5|Tap extraction button|Overwrite confirmation dialog appears|
|6|Choose "Keep existing" or "Overwrite"|Extraction runs, spinner shows, attributes populate on success|
|7|Go offline|Extraction button hidden entirely (correct existing behavior)|

----

h3. Test 7: Save Integrity — PRIORITY 2

||Step||Action||Expected||
|1|Open a node, modify: label, room, COM, class, subtype, QR code|All draft values shown in UI|
|2|Add a photo, add an IR photo pair, change default photo|Staged changes reflected in UI|
|3|Tap Save|Save completes, view dismisses|
|4|Reopen the same node|All changes persisted: label, room, COM, class, subtype, QR code, photos, IR photos, default photo|
|5|Verify on server (if online)|Data synced correctly|

----

h3. Test 8: Discard Changes

||Step||Action||Expected||
|1|Open node, add a photo, add IR photo, change label|"Save Changes" button appears|
|2|Tap Cancel (back)|"Discard changes?" alert appears|
|3|Tap Discard|View dismisses. No changes saved|
|4|Reopen node|Original state — no photo additions, no label change|

----

h3. Test 9: Focus Modes

||Step||Action||Expected||
|1|Open node in .all mode|All sections visible, all changes work|
|2|Open node in .irPhotos mode|IR section shows, existing IR photos from snapshot display correctly|
|3|Open node in .coreAttributes mode|Core attributes + photos sections show, extraction button state correct|
|4|Open node in .collectAFData mode|Core attributes + photos + OCP sections show, all working|
|5|Open node in .collectCOMData mode|Basic info + photos show, profile photo correct|

----

h3. Test 10: Edge Cases

||Step||Action||Expected||
|1|Open a brand new node (just created, zero data)|No crash. Empty states shown for photos, IR photos|
|2|Open a node with 50+ photos|Profile photo shows correctly. No performance degradation|
|3|Rapidly background/foreground while on node detail|No crash|
|4|Open node detail → open child node sheet → edit child → return|Parent node detail still works, no crash|
|5|Create a task from node detail, return|Task list shows new task (unchanged functionality)|
|6|Create an issue from node detail, return|Issue list shows new issue (unchanged functionality)|

----

h3. Priority Order for QA

# *Test 1* (crash scenario) — This is the whole point
# *Test 7* (save integrity) — Must not lose data
# *Test 2 + 3* (profile photo + photo flow) — Most user-visible
# *Test 4 + 5* (IR photos) — Second most common path
# *Test 6* (extraction button) — Improvement, should work better than before
# *Tests 8-10* (discard, focus modes, edge cases) — Regression coverage

column1: Android | Implement Attachment Upload and Node Mapping Flow in Site Visits/Jobs
column41:
Implement flow to support uploading attachments and mapping them to nodes and show attachments using the following APIs:

# */attachment/presigned-upload*
#* Obtain a presigned URL for uploading the file (e.g., "2025 Egalvanic Assessment - Technical Report.pdf", size: 689,556 bytes).
# */attachment/create*
#* Create an attachment record with metadata including company_id, file_size, filename, key, session_id, sld_id, task_id, and type ("SLD"). see attached screenshot for types
#* Task selection is optional
# */attachment/mapping/node*
#* Map the uploaded attachment to a node using attachment_id and node_id.
#* Node selection is optional
#* can select multiple nodes
#* Selected node should show attachment in attachment section of node detail page in web.
# */attachment/?session_id=7ede368a-b9ed-4f27-9f22-bec7469b399e*
#* Get list of attachments
#* Show attachment list in UI

The iOS should provide a seamless user experience for uploading documents, tracking upload progress, and confirming successful mapping to the correct node.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* - User can select and upload a file (e.g., PDF) from the UI.-
* - On file selection, the mobile requests a presigned upload URL via {{/attachment/presigned-upload}} and uploads the file directly to storage.-
* -After successful upload, the mobile calls {{/attachment/create}} with all required metadata (company_id, file_size, filename, key, session_id, sld_id, task_id, type).-
* -Upon successful attachment creation, the mobile calls {{/attachment/mapping/node}} to map the attachment to the selected node (using attachment_id and node_id).-
* -The UI provides clear feedback for each step (upload progress, success, error handling).-
* -The uploaded attachment appears in the relevant node’s attachment list after completion if its mapped-
* -Error states (e.g., upload failure, mapping failure) are handled gracefully with user-friendly messages.-
* -All API calls use the correct payload structure as per backend requirements.-
* -Unit tests cover the upload and mapping flow, including error scenarios.-

*Supporting Attachments:*

column1: iOS | CI/CD Implementation
column41:
Implement a CI/CD pipeline for the iOS application using GitHub Actions, supporting all environments: development (dev), quality assurance (QA), staging (stage), and production (prod). The pipeline should automate building, testing, artifact generation, and deployment for each environment, ensuring environment-specific configurations and secure secrets management. This will streamline the release process.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* A GitHub Actions workflow exists that supports dev QA, stage, and prod environments.
* Automate build and test processes for all code changes.
* On every push and pull request, the workflow:
** Checks out the code.
** Installs dependencies.
** Builds the Android project for the targeted environment.
** Runs unit and instrumentation tests.
** Build and test results are visible in the GitHub Actions tab.
* Generate and store build artifacts (ipa) for each environment.
* Deploy builds to the appropriate destinations for dev, QA, stage, and prod.
* Ensure environment-specific configurations and secrets are managed securely.
* Workflow status (success/failure) is reported on pull requests and relevant branches.
* Failed builds/tests block merging until resolved.

*Additional Details:*

# Dev builds are deployed to internal testers or a dev distribution channel.
# QA builds are deployed to QA testers or a QA channel.
# Stage builds are deployed to staging testers or a staging channel.
# Prod builds are deployed to Appstore or the designated production channel.
# Deployment steps are only triggered for the appropriate branches/tags (e.g., dev branch for dev, main for prod).

column1: BE | CrossSiteScripting_BODY WAF Rule Override to Terraform
column41:
A manual change was made to the *production AWS WAF* to prevent file uploads (e.g., {{.xlsx}}) from being blocked by the AWS Managed XSS rule.

Currently, this change exists *only in AWS console / CLI* and is *not reflected in Terraform*, which risks being overwritten in future deployments.

This ticket tracks the work required to *codify the manual WAF change into Terraform*

*Root Cause Analysis*

# Users reported JSON parse errors during {{.xlsx}} uploads
# WAF logs showed:
{noformat}"terminatingRule": {
  "ruleId": "CrossSiteScripting_BODY",
  "action": "BLOCK"
}
{noformat}
# Matched data included:
{noformat}["<!", "`"]
{noformat}
which are valid characters in XML-based {{.xlsx}} files

h3. *Manual Fix Applied (Already Done)*

* WAF: {{eg-pz-prod-waf-ohio}}
* Rule: {{AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet}}
* Action Override added:
** {{CrossSiteScripting_BODY}} → {{COUNT}}

This allows uploads to proceed while still logging matches for monitoring.


*Scope of Impact*

All file upload endpoints, including but not limited to:

* {{/api/bulk-upload/validate}}
* {{/api/bulk-upload/process}}
* {{/api/photo}}
* Other multipart/form-data upload APIs

h3. *Acceptance Criteria*

* -Update Terraform WAF configuration for *prod*-
* -Add {{CrossSiteScripting_BODY}} to {{RuleActionOverrides}}-
* -Ensure it is under {{AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet}}-
* -Terraform plan shows *no drift* after apply-
* -Change is deployed without impacting existing WAF behavior-
* Upload of {{.xlsx}} files works without WAF blocking

column1: Z | BE | Sub Type not updated
column41:
When updating a shortcut using the {{PUT /api/shortcut/update/{id}}} API, the {{node_subtype}} field provided in the request payload is not returned in the response.

Although a valid {{node_subtype}} UUID is sent, the API response always returns {{node_subtype}} and {{node_subtype_id}} as {{null}}. This prevents clients from confirming whether the value was successfully persisted and causes inconsistency between request data and response payload.

Acceptance Criteria:

* -When a valid {{node_subtype}} UUID is provided in the update shortcut request, the value is persisted successfully-
* -The update shortcut API response returns the correct:-

* {{node_subtype}} *and/or*
* {{node_subtype_id}}

* -Returned {{node_subtype}} value matches the UUID sent in the request-
* -Existing shortcut fields (name, is_default, node_class_id, etc.) continue to behave as expected-
* -No regression is introduced for shortcuts that do not include {{node_subtype}} in the request-
* API behavior is consistent across environments (dev/stage/prod, if applicable)

column1: iOS | Image preview intermittently fails to load when site contains large data and multiple images
column41:
*Description:*
When a large number of images are uploaded and the same site contains a large amount of data, image preview for an asset sometimes does not load properly. A cross (✖) icon appears in the image preview. However, after closing the preview and reopening it, the image loads correctly.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open a site that contains a *large amount of data*.
# Ensure *multiple images* are uploaded across assets in the same site.
# Navigate to any *asset* within the site.
# Open the *image preview* for any uploaded image.
# Observe the image loading behavior.

*Actual Result:*

* Image does not load in preview.
* A cross (✖) icon is displayed instead of the image.
* Closing and reopening the preview loads the image correctly.
!Video_20251216_132732_835.mp4|width=592,alt="Video_20251216_132732_835.mp4"!

column1: iOS | Sync fails when zoomed camera images are uploaded to Asset, Issue, and Task in online mode
column41:
*Description:*
When an asset is created in online mode and images are captured using the camera with zoom enabled, the same images are uploaded to the Asset, Issue, and Task. After saving, the sync count on the dashboard increases, but synchronization fails in Settings. An error with code *1011* appears in the history, and due to this issue, the site does not change.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

# Go to *Online mode*.
# Create a new *Asset*.
# Capture all asset images using the *camera with zoom enabled*.
# Zoom the image and capture it.
# Add the same type of zoomed camera images to the *Issue* and *Task* under the same asset.
# Save the asset.
# Go to the *Dashboard* and observe the *WiFi sync icon count* increasing.
# Navigate to *Settings → Sync*.
# Check *Sync History*.

*Actual Result:*

* Sync fails in Settings.
* Error code *1011* appears in the sync history.
* Sync count keeps increasing on the dashboard.
* Site does not change due to sync failure.

!Video_20251216_124907_281_1.mp4|width=592,alt="Video_20251216_124907_281_1.mp4"!

column1: Z | FE | Unresolved and Pending Task Counts Are Different on Web and Mobile
column41:
The web application is currently not invoking the User Task Mapping API for tasks with the “assignee” and “owner” roles.

On the Android side, we display the count of assets assigned to the logged-in user. To maintain consistency, the web application must call the following APIs when creating a task:

# owner mapping 


--> POST [+https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai/api/mapping/user-task/create+|https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai/api/mapping/user-task/create]
Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8
Content-Length: 141
{"is_deleted":false,"mapping_type":"owner","task_id":"f8213fc7-7f49-4ff5-b280-34c8a6cb144b","user_id":"96aa4804-5303-450d-bb27-ed33b65e172d"}

# assignee mapping


--> POST [+https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai/api/mapping/user-task/create+|https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai/api/mapping/user-task/create]
Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8
Content-Length: 144
{"is_deleted":false,"mapping_type":"assignee","task_id":"f8213fc7-7f49-4ff5-b280-34c8a6cb144b","user_id":"96aa4804-5303-450d-bb27-ed33b65e172d"}
--> END POST (144-byte body)

column1: iOS: Add captions to photos
column41:
Enable users to add captions to photos across the platform.

* Add a new string field photos.caption to the database.
* Allow users on both web and mobile to add or edit a caption by long-pressing on a photo after taking or uploading it.
* This functionality should be available for all photos, regardless of where they are used (issues, tasks, nodes, etc.).

*Acceptance Criteria*

* Database schema is updated to include a string field photos.caption for all photo records.
* On mobile, users can add or edit a caption by long-pressing on a photo after taking it.
* The caption field is available and editable for all photos, regardless of context (issues, tasks, nodes, etc.).
* Captions are saved and displayed correctly wherever photos appear in the platform.
* If a photo has a caption, it is visible to users in the photo viewer or relevant UI.
* If a photo does not have a caption, users are prompted to add one via long press.
* Changes to captions are persisted and reflected across all devices and platforms.

End-to-end test automation across Web, iOS, and Infrastructure to enable continuous quality assurance with CI/CD integration.

----

h2. Objective

Build and maintain a comprehensive QA automation suite that covers all major product modules across platforms (Web and iOS), validates infrastructure configuration integrity, and integrates with CI/CD pipelines for automated regression testing on every release.

----

column1: Z | Android | Add Emergency, Normal, and Load terminals to ATS in SLD
column41:
The ATS (Automatic Transfer Switch) configuration must support three distinct terminal types: Emergency, Normal, and Load.

Currently, ATS setup does not explicitly enforce or model these terminal types. This enhancement will ensure ATS assets are configured with the required terminals for accurate representation, validation, and downstream processing.



!image-20251212-052411.png|width=474,alt="image-20251212-052411.png"!

Designs:

*ATS Detail Page – Terminals Section*

* Section title: *Terminals*
* Display as a card or panel within ATS asset details

*Layout*

* Three fixed rows (non-removable):
*# Emergency
*# Normal
*# Load

Each row contains:

* Terminal Type (label, read-only): Emergency / Normal / Load
* Terminal Name (text input)
* Rating / Capacity (optional numeric input if applicable)
* Connection Status (dropdown or indicator: Connected / Not Connected)
* Notes (optional text input)

*Behavior*

* Terminal types are pre-defined and cannot be deleted
* Fields are editable per terminal
* Validation error shown inline if any required terminal data is missing
* Read-only view mode when asset is locked; editable in edit mode


Validation:

*Empty State*

* If terminals not configured yet, rows still appear with empty inputs
* Inline helper text: “All three terminals are required for ATS”

*Error State*

* Red highlight and message under row if validation fails


AcceptanceCriteria:

* -ATS detail page displays a dedicated Terminals section-
* -Emergency, Normal, and Load terminals are always visible-
* -Terminal types are fixed and cannot be removed-
* -Users can edit terminal details in edit mode-
* -Validation and error states are clearly shown in the UI-

column1: Z | BE |  Define Issue Class business logic + seeding/backfill script for issue classification
column41:
Implement the backend “Issue Classifcation” definition + classification logic to consistently map TEGG Equipment_Problem records into eg-pz {{issues.issue_class}}, during migration and for any future imports.

Reference implementation exists in the migration repo commit feature:
[https://github.com/Egalvanic/eg-pz-tegg-migration/commit/a37cec5a4e47c260677ee6235ee8aef504be9ba5#diff-bf4a2cccea14855cc8d3e867eaba8faff402bd3803c36b3f1b23db4c6cfb7696|https://github.com/Egalvanic/eg-pz-tegg-migration/commit/a37cec5a4e47c260677ee6235ee8aef504be9ba5#diff-bf4a2cccea14855cc8d3e867eaba8faff402bd3803c36b3f1b23db4c6cfb7696|smart-link] 

Acceptance Criteria:

* Issue classification logic follows precedence: Immediate Hazard -> Category keyword -> Description keyword -> ProblemClass mapping -> Other.
* {{priority}} is derived from EP_ImmediateHazard / EP_SeverityID per defined mapping.
* {{issue_type}} and {{issue_subtype}} are generated per defined rules, with consistent normalization.
* {{status}} is computed from corrective action fields per defined rules.
* {{details}} JSONB is populated with required base keys and optional arrays/flags only when applicable.
* A seed script creates/updates canonical {{issue_classes}} records with fixed UUIDs and expected names.
* A backfill script updates existing issues safely (idempotent, supports dry-run, logs counts by class/priority).
* Unit tests cover at least: hazard override, category override, description override, class fallback, subtype mapping, and status mapping.

The goal is to automate the reporting of issues in Sentry when a specific event occurs.

h3. Context

The automation feature in Sentry has been updated. It will now trigger reports based on the frequency of events.

h3. Scope

* *Included*: Automation of issue reporting when more than 10 events occur within an hour.
* *Not Included*: Other types of event reporting or manual reporting processes.

h3. Success criteria

* Reports are generated automatically when the threshold of 10 events is reached within an hour.
* The automation functions without manual intervention.

h3. Risks and dependencies

* Potential for false positives if events spike unexpectedly.
* Dependency on the stability of the Sentry platform for accurate reporting.

column1: Android: IR Photo Section Missing in Issue Screen (Android) and IR Photo Not Linked to Issue in Site Visit (Web)
column41:
*Steps to Reproduce:*

* In the mobile app (Android), activate *Site Visit* mode.
* Create a new asset.
* Add an IR photo and save the asset.
* Now go to the *Issue* screen and add a new issue for the same asset.
* Observe that the *IR Photo upload/attach section is not visible* on Android (but this section is available in iOS).
* Save the issue.
* Open the same site in the *Web application*.
* Go to *Site Visit → IR Photo*.
* Check *Associated Issues* for the IR photo.

*Bug Description:*

* When creating an issue in Android, the *IR photo attachment section does not appear*, whereas the same feature is available in iOS.
* Because of this missing section, Android users cannot attach IR photos to an issue.
* As a result, in the *web Site Visit → IR Photo*, the IR photo does *not display the linked/associated issue* added from Android.
* The issue will only show if the user adds the IR photo from iOS (which has this feature).

*Expected Result:*

* Android app should have the *same IR photo attachment section* in the Issue screen as iOS.
* When a user attaches an IR photo to an issue from Android, it should appear properly under *Associated Issues* in the *Web → Site Visit → IR Photo*.
* The IR photo and its linked issue should sync consistently across Mobile (Android/iOS) and Web.

column1: Web | IR Photo Location Not Displayed on Web for Assets Created from Mobile App
column41:
h3. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Select a site visit  in the mobile app.
# Create a new asset.
# Add a location to the asset.
# Pair the IR photo and save the asset.
# Open the same site in the web application.

When an asset is created from the mobile app with a location and IR photo, the IR photo location does *not* display in the web application under Site Visit.
The location appears *only* after editing the asset’s location again on the web, and then checking it in Site Visit.
Without this manual location edit on the web, the IR photo location never shows.

----

h3. *Expected Result:*

The IR photo location added from the mobile app should display correctly in the web application under *Site Visit* without requiring any additional edit in the web version.

column1: Android | Connection does not delete when using long-press delete action
column41:
h3. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open the application.
# Go to *Asset Details*.
# Navigate to the *Connections* section.
# Select any connection.
# Long-press (hold) on the connection to delete it.
# Tap on *Delete*.

----

h3. *🔹 Bug Description:*

When attempting to delete a connection using the *long-press (hold)* delete action, the connection does not get deleted.
However, if the user *swipes* the connection and then taps the *Delete* icon, the connection is deleted successfully.
The issue occurs only with the hold-delete action.

----

h3. *🔹 Actual Result:*

Long-press delete does *not* delete the selected connection.

----

h3. *🔹 Expected Result:*

Long-press delete should delete the connection in the same way as the swipe-delete action.

column1: Android: Face Unlock Option Not Shown When Device Has Only Face Lock (No Fingerprint)
column41:
h3. *Priority:*

*Low*

----

h3. *Environment:*

* iOS / Android (specify if needed)
* Production App
* Device without fingerprint hardware (Face Unlock only)

----

h3. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open the app on a device that *does NOT have a fingerprint sensor*.
# Ensure the device uses *Face Lock only* (no fingerprint enrolled, no fingerprint hardware).
# Navigate to any feature or screen that prompts for biometric authentication.
# Observe the authentication prompt.

----

h3. *Expected Result:*

The app should detect that the device only supports *Face Unlock* and display the *Face Unlock* authentication prompt.

----

h3. *Actual Result:*

The app still shows an option to *unlock using fingerprint*, even though the device does not have a fingerprint sensor.

h3. *Impact:*

* Creates confusion for users on devices without fingerprint hardware.
* Minor usability issue; no functional blocking.

column1: Web: IR Photo Pair Not Created in Production

*Environment:* Production

* No IR photo pair is created
* Sentry logs HTTP 500
* Backend endpoint fails immediately
* User sees error or nothing happens

----

h2. *Expected Result*

* IR photo pair should be created successfully
* Data should be saved
* User continues workflow without interruption

# *Add an* *Asset* *inside the room.*
# *Add the required* *IR Photo filename* *and* *Visual Photo filename**.*
# *Tap on* *“Add IR Photo Pair”**.*
# *Observe the behavior.*

* The app *immediately navigates back* to the previous screen.
* No IR photo screen opens.
* No IR photo pair is created.
* User cannot proceed.

----

* The “Add IR Photo Pair” screen should open.
* User should be able to select/capture IR photo.
* IR photo pair should be created successfully.

column1: Web: The data couldn’t be read because it isn’t in the correct format” in iOS
column41:
h2. *Environment:*

* *Platform:* iOS
* *App Version:* 1.10
* *Device:* iPad
* *OS Version:* iPadOS 26.1

h2. *Severity:*

h2.  *High*

h2. *Module:*

h2.  *Data Refresh / API Integration*

----

h2. *Bug Description:*

When attempting to refresh the data in the iOS application, the operation fails and shows the error:

*“Refresh failed. Failed to refresh data: The data couldn’t be read because it isn’t in the correct format.”*

This indicates that the API response or local data being parsed is not in the expected structure (e.g., invalid JSON, null fields, incorrect data type).

----

h1. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# *On Web:*
#* *Go to* *User Management → User Profile**.*
#* *Add a Site for the user and click Save.*
# *On Mobile (Android/iOS):*
#* *Log in using the same user.*
#* *Tap on the assigned Site (the site added from Web).*
# *On Web Again:*
#* *Go back to the same user profile.*
#* *Unselect the previously added site and click Save.*
# *On Mobile Again:*
#* *Open the app and tap on the* *unselected site* *(which still remains visible on mobile).*
#* *Then tap on any site to continue login.*
*(Mobile still holds cached/previous site selection.)*
# *Back on Web:*
#* *Try to add the same site again for that user and click Save.*

----

column41:
h1. *Environment:*

* *Environment:* QA
* *Platforms:* Web, Android App, iOS App
* *Module:* User Profile → Site Assignment
* *Browser:* (Add Chrome/Edge version)

----

h1. *Priority:* *High (P1)*

Reason: This issue blocks the admin from assigning sites to the user and stops user operations across Web + Mobile.

----

h1. *Description:*

Once a site is unselected on Web for a user, and the same user logs in again on Android/iOS, the system prevents adding that site again from the Web. The "Save" action does not work, and the site remains unassigned.

----

h3. Steps to Reproduce

# Open the *Web application* and log in with an admin account.
# Navigate to *Settings → Users*.
# Create a new user *or* edit an existing user:
#* If creating: click *Create User*, fill required fields, and save the user (do not add the site yet).
#* If editing: select an existing user and open the edit dialog.
# In the user form, *Add a Site* (select *Test Site A* from the site list).
# Click *Save* on the user form to persist the user with the assigned site.
# On the *Mobile app*, log in using the same user credentials you just created/edited.
# On Mobile, navigate to *Sites* — you should see the option to *select* the assigned site (*Test Site A*) (confirm the site appears as selectable).
# Return to the *Web application* (same admin account).
# Navigate to *Settings → Users*, open the same user you edited earlier, and *remove / unselect* *Test Site A* from that user.
# Click *Save* on the user form to persist the removal.
# On the *Mobile app*, attempt to *open* the now-deselected site (*Test Site A*) — observe whether the mobile still allows opening the deselected site or shows inconsistent state.
# Back on the *Web application*, attempt to *add the same site (Test Site A)* again to the same user or create the site again (depending on your flow).
# Click *Save* on the web side to add/save the site.
# Observe whether the web allows saving the same site again or throws an error / silently fails.

----

h1. *Actual Result:*

* Web fails to re-add the site.
* Save action does nothing.
* No error message shown.

----

h1. *Expected Result:*

* Web should successfully reassign the site to the user.
* Mobile cache should not block Web updates.
* Web must override mobile active-site session.

----

h1. *Impact:*

* User cannot be assigned required sites.
* Workflow is blocked for scanning, issue creation, etc.
* Admin must involve backend team to reset assignments.
* Sync conflict between Web and Mobile.

----

column1: iOS – Sync counter shows “1 data to sync” but Pending tab is empty
column41:
*Issue Type:* Bug

----

*Description:*

On iOS devices, the Sync screen shows *“1 data to sync”*, but when the user opens the *Pending* section, no records are displayed. This creates a mismatch between the sync counter and the actual pending data list, and the user cannot identify or clear the stuck item.

This looks like a phantom/stuck sync record or a UI state issue where the counter is not updated correctly after sync.

----

*Expected Result:*

* If the Sync header shows *“1 data to sync”*, the *Pending* tab should display that corresponding pending record.
* If there are *no pending items*, then:

** The sync counter should show *0* or not show any pending count.
** There should be no discrepancy between the sync counter and the Pending list.



*Actual Result:*

* Sync header shows *“1 data to sync”*.
* *Pending* tab shows *no data / empty list*.
* User cannot see or clear the supposed pending item, leading to a permanently stuck sync count.

----

*Impact / Severity:*

* *Severity:* Major (P2)
* Confuses users about sync status.
* May hide an unsynced record that cannot be retried or fixed from the UI.
* Risk of data not being fully synced without any visible way to resolve.

----

*Logs / Attachments:*

[https://drive.google.com/file/d/11Kv-YzQHudmzGad9wGeFjl1AvToAXEaA/view?usp=sharing|https://drive.google.com/file/d/11Kv-YzQHudmzGad9wGeFjl1AvToAXEaA/view?usp=sharing|smart-link]

column1: Web: Deleted Image Still Appearing in Issue Photos After Removal on Web
column41:
*Feature:*
Issues / Attachments / Photos Management

*Priority:* High

----

h1. *Bug Title:*

Deleted image still appearing in Issue Photos after removal on web

----

h1. *Module:*

Issues → Photos / Attachments

h1. *Environment:*

* *Platform:* Web
* *Environment:* QA
* *Browser:* Chrome / Safari / Edge
* *API Environment:* QA

📎 *Video Evidence:*
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1oLtYGi_4kiNsnzKXYuc4T5Ji-PQ-U5q3/view?usp=sharing|https://drive.google.com/file/d/1oLtYGi_4kiNsnzKXYuc4T5Ji-PQ-U5q3/view?usp=sharing|smart-link] 

*Severity:* High
*Priority:* High

----

h1. *Bug Description:*

After a user deletes an image from the *Issue Photos* section on the *Web application*, the *deleted image still appears* when viewing the Issue Photos list.

Flow observed:

# User uploads a new image
# User deletes an existing image
# Deleted image continues to be displayed in Issue Photos

This indicates that:

* The image is *not fully removed* from the UI
* OR the backend/database still returns the deleted image
* OR frontend caching is still displaying old data

This issue leads to:

* Confusion for users
* Incorrect visual evidence tracking
* Audit trail discrepancies
* Inaccurate recordkeeping for issue-related photos

The deleted image *must not* appear in the system after removal.

----

h1. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Login to the *Web QA* application.
# Navigate to *Issues* module.
# Open any issue where photos are displayed.
# Upload a new image (optional step).
# Delete an existing image.
# Refresh the screen or reopen the issue.
# Observe that the deleted image *still appears* in the Issue Photos list.

----

h1. *Actual Result:*

Deleted image continues to appear in Issue Photos even after successful deletion.

----

h1. *Expected Result:*

Deleted image should *immediately disappear* from Issue Photos and *should not be returned* by the backend or displayed by the UI.

----

h1. *Attachments:*

📎 Video evidence:
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1oLtYGi_4kiNsnzKXYuc4T5Ji-PQ-U5q3/view?usp=sharing|https://drive.google.com/file/d/1oLtYGi_4kiNsnzKXYuc4T5Ji-PQ-U5q3/view?usp=sharing|smart-link]

column1: Web: Attachment View and Download Options Both Open Image Instead of Downloading (Web)
column41:
*Feature:*
Attachments / File Management

*Priority:* Low

----

h1. *Bug Title:*

Attachment View and Download Both Only Showing Image Instead of Downloading

----

h1. *Module:*

Attachments → View / Download Functionality

h1. *Environment:*

* *Platform:* Web
* *Environment:* QA
* *Browser:* Chrome / Safari / Edge

📎 Screenshot Evidence: [https://prnt.sc/BqRMPLM8LG01|https://prnt.sc/BqRMPLM8LG01|smart-link] 

*Severity:* Low
*Priority:* Low

----

h1. *Bug Description:*

When the user tries to *download* an attachment from the Web application, the system *does NOT trigger a file download*.

Instead:

* *View* → Opens the image preview
* *Download* → Also opens the image preview (same behavior as View)

This means the *Download* function is not working at all, and the system is treating both actions as *View Only*, which is incorrect.

This issue may be caused by:

* Incorrect frontend action mapping
* Wrong endpoint being triggered for download
* Improper file headers returned by backend (missing {{Content-Disposition: attachment}})
* File type not handled properly
* API response returning file URL instead of stream download
* Browser opening file inline by default due to incorrect MIME type

This bug prevents users from downloading attachments and retrieving files, causing workflow interruption.

----

h1. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Login to *Web QA*.
# Navigate to a module where attachments are listed.
# Click *View* → Opens image preview (expected).
# Click *Download* → Instead of downloading, it also opens the image preview.
# No file download occurs.

----

h1. *Actual Result:*

* *Download* action behaves the same as *View*.
* No file download is triggered.

----

h1. *Expected Result:*

* *View* should open the preview screen.
* *Download* should immediately trigger a *file download* (image/PDF/other).
* Browser should download the file using proper headers.

----

h1. *Possible Root Causes:*

h3. *1️⃣ Incorrect Frontend Mapping*

Both View and Download buttons may be linked to the same function.

h3. *2️⃣ Backend Not Sending Proper Download Headers*

Missing or incorrect:

* {{Content-Disposition: attachment}}
* {{Content-Type}}
* {{Content-Length}}

h3. *3️⃣ Download Endpoint Returning a URL Instead of File Stream*

Browser loads image directly instead of downloading it.

h3. *4️⃣ MIME Type Misconfiguration*

Images often load inline unless forced for download.

h3. *5️⃣ CORS / Security Blocking Download File Stream*

Fallback logic might redirect to image preview instead.

h3. *6️⃣ File Download Logic Disabled or Not Implemented*

Legacy or placeholder code may still be present.

----

h1. *Developer Investigation Checklist:*

✔ Verify frontend download button handler
✔ Compare View vs Download API endpoints
✔ Inspect backend response headers
✔ Confirm if attachment download supports file types (JPG, PNG, PDF, etc.)
✔ Check browser network tab → response should contain:
{{Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="file.jpg"}}
✔ Test with multiple attachments
✔ Validate if link uses {{download}} attribute or file stream

----

h1. *Attachments:*

📎 Screenshot of issue:
[https://prnt.sc/BqRMPLM8LG01|https://prnt.sc/BqRMPLM8LG01|smart-link]

column1: Web: Edit and Delete Buttons Not Visible at 100% Screen Size in Asset Table
column41:
h1. *Module:*

Assets → Asset List / Asset Table

h1. *Environment:*

* *Platform:* Web
* *Environment:* QA
* *Browser:* Chrome (Issue), Safari/Edge (verify)
* *Zoom Level:* 100% (Issue) / 90% (Works)

📎 Screenshot: [https://prnt.sc/4oAgoAUZfXvU|https://prnt.sc/4oAgoAUZfXvU|smart-link] 

*Severity:* High
*Priority:* High

----

h1. *Bug Description:*

On the *Asset Management (Asset Table)* page, the *Edit* and *Delete* action buttons are *not visible* when the browser zoom level is set to *100%*, which is the default and expected display size for users.

The action buttons only become visible when the zoom level is *reduced (e.g., 90%)*, indicating a *UI responsiveness issue*, likely caused by:

* Insufficient column width
* Overflow hidden in the action column
* Icon container collapsing at full zoom
* CSS layout not scaling correctly
* Table grid not adjusting for default viewport width

Because of this issue, users are *unable to edit or delete assets* unless they manually adjust their zoom level, which is not a normal or acceptable workflow.

This breaks usability and directly impacts asset management operations.

----

h1. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open *Web QA* environment.
# Navigate to *Assets → Asset List*.
# Ensure browser zoom level is set to *100%*.
# Observe the *Edit* and *Delete* buttons inside the asset table.
# Reduce zoom level to *90%*.
# Buttons become visible.

----

h1. *Actual Result:*

* Edit and Delete buttons are *hidden or cut off* at *100% zoom*.
* Buttons appear only when zoom is reduced.

----

h1. *Expected Result:*

* Edit and Delete action buttons should be *fully visible and accessible* at *100% zoom* in all supported browsers.
* Table layout should adapt responsively without requiring zoom adjustments.

----

h1. *Possible Root Causes:*

h3. *1️⃣ Column Width Too Small*

The action column width may not accommodate the icons at 100% zoom.

h3. *2️⃣ CSS Overflow Hidden*

The table or cell container may have:

* {{overflow: hidden}}
* {{max-width}} constraints
* Truncated or clipped icons

h3. *3️⃣ Flexbox / Grid Layout Shrink Issue*

Icons may be shrinking out of view due to:

* {{flex-shrink: 1}}
* Incorrect {{min-width}} settings

h3. *4️⃣ Table Responsiveness Bug*

Table may treat 100% zoom as a smaller viewport and remove/hide action buttons.

h3. *5️⃣ Icon Container Misalignment*

Padding, margins, or alignment rules may push icons outside view.

----

h1. *Developer Investigation Checklist:*

✔ Check CSS for table columns and action button container
✔ Verify if overflow rules are hiding icons

column1: Z | FE | No “Page Not Found” Message Displayed for Invalid URLs (Web)
column41:
*Feature:*
Web Routing / Error Handling

*Priority:* Medium

----

h1. *Bug Title:*

No “Page Not Found” Message Shown for Invalid URLs

----

h1. *Module:*

Web App – Routing / Error Handling

h1. *Environment:*

* *Platform:* Web
* *Environment:* QA
* *Browser:* Chrome / Safari / Edge
* *URL Example:*
[https://acme.egalvanic.ai/23dsfsdfasfas%20gsdf|https://acme.egalvanic.ai/23dsfsdfasfas%20gsdf|smart-link] 

*Severity:* Medium–High
*Priority:* High

----

h1. *Bug Description:*

When the user enters an *invalid or malformed URL*, the system fails to show a *“Page Not Found” (404)* message or any user-friendly error screen.

Example invalid URL:
[https://acme.egalvanic.ai/23dsfsdfasfas%20gsdf|https://acme.egalvanic.ai/23dsfsdfasfas%20gsdf|smart-link] 

Instead of showing a 404 page or an error component:

* The page loads with *no feedback*
* No “Page Not Found” message appears
* The user is left without understanding whether the page exists or not

This causes *confusion*, poor UX, and incorrect navigation behavior.

----

h1. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open any Web browser.
# Navigate to the application base URL.
# Manually enter any *invalid / random URL path*, e.g.:
{{/23dsfsdfasfas%20gsdf}}
# Press Enter.
# Observe the system response.

----

h1. *Actual Result:*

* The system loads a blank / partial page.
* No *404* or *Page Not Found* message is shown.
* User receives *no indication* that the URL is invalid.

📎 Screenshot: [https://prnt.sc/eCZVgAiSjxXa|https://prnt.sc/eCZVgAiSjxXa|smart-link] 

----

h1. *Expected Result:*

For all invalid or non-existing URLs, the system should:

* Display a *“Page Not Found” (404)* screen
* Show a user-friendly message such as:
_“The page you are looking for does not exist.”_
* Optionally provide navigation options (e.g., Home, Dashboard, Back button)
* Ensure consistent error handling across all routes

----

h1. *Possible Root Causes:*

h3. *1️⃣ Missing Frontend Fallback Route*

* React / Angular / Vue router not configured with {{"*"}} catch-all route
* 404 component not mapped properly

h3. *2️⃣ Backend Not Returning HTTP 404*

* Server responds with 200 / 304 instead of 404
* Routing not validated on backend

h3. *3️⃣ Incorrect Route Path Matching*

* App routes may be overly strict or overly permissive
* Invalid paths not triggering fallback

h3. *4️⃣ Missing Global Error Component*

* UI may not have a global 404 page
* Older version of error handler may have been removed

h3. *5️⃣ Build/Deployment Misconfiguration*

* Routing fallback not included in production / QA build
* Missing configuration in Nginx / CloudFront / Load balancer

----

h1. *Developer Investigation Checklist:*

✔ Verify SPA router fallback ({{*}} route in React Router / Angular / Vue)
✔ Check backend for correct *404 HTTP status code*
✔ Ensure deployment server redirects invalid paths to 404 page
✔ Test multiple invalid URL formats (random strings, encoded paths, nested routes)
✔ Confirm 404 component loads correctly when directly accessed
✔ Validate behavior across different browsers

----

h1. *Attachments:*

📎 Screenshot: [https://prnt.sc/eCZVgAiSjxXa|https://prnt.sc/eCZVgAiSjxXa|smart-link]

column1: Android | Sync fails when user creates a location & asset offline, then deletes the location before sync
column41:
*Module:*
Sync Engine / Offline Mode / Location & Asset Management

*Environment:*

* *Device:* (Android / iOS)
* *App Environment:* QA
* *Network:* Offline 
* *API Environment:* QA

*Severity:* Critical
*Priority:* High

----

h1. *Bug Description:*

When the user goes *offline*, then:

# Creates a *Location*
# Creates an *Asset* inside that Location
# Deletes the same Location (either from mobile or from Sync screen) *before performing full sync*

… then tries to *sync*, the sync process fails with an error.

The failure occurs because the asset is still linked to the *deleted location*, so when sync tries to push the asset to the server, the reference location no longer exists.

This results in:

* Sync not completing
* Pending offline records stuck
* User unable to proceed
* Possible data inconsistency

This is a *major edge-case failure* in offline sync and needs immediate handling.

----

h1. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Turn *Internet OFF*.
# Open app → Go to *Locations*.
# Create a *new Location* (offline).
# Inside that Location, create an *Asset* (offline).
# Now delete that same Location:
#* Either from *Sync screen*, OR
#* From *Location module* (offline)
# Turn *Internet ON* again.
# Tap *Sync All*.

----

h1. *Actual Result:*

* Sync fails with an error.
* Asset linked to the deleted location cannot sync.
* Offline queue gets stuck.
* User cannot proceed without clearing offline data.

----

h1. *Expected Result:*

App should *handle this scenario gracefully*, such as:

* If Location is deleted, all related offline assets should be automatically marked for deletion or skipped
* Sync should NOT fail
* User should NOT see unresolvable sync errors
* Dependency validation should occur before syncing

----

h1. *Root Cause Hypothesis (Technical):*

h3. *1️⃣ Broken parent-child relationship*

Asset references {{locationId}} that no longer exists (deleted offline).

h3. *2️⃣ Sync engine not handling dependency validation*

Sync does not check if parent was deleted before processing child records.

h3. *3️⃣ Incorrect sync order*

Deleting location should be synced *before* syncing assets.

h3. *4️⃣ Offline database not updating child records*

When Location is deleted offline, associated assets still remain in local DB pending sync.

h3. *5️⃣ No fail-safe for orphaned offline data*

System tries to push invalid asset record to server → server rejects → sync error.

----

h1. *Developer Investigation Checklist:*

✔ Validate offline DB state after deleting location
✔ Check sync order (location delete should sync before asset create)
✔ Implement cascading delete or rule-based cleanup
✔ Ensure orphan assets are auto-removed or flagged invalid
✔ Add pre-sync validation for parent-child dependencies
✔ Confirm API error code when syncing asset with non-existing location

----

h1. *Proposed Fix Approaches (Recommended):*

h3. *Option A — Auto-cleanup*

When a location is deleted offline → delete all unsynced assets under it.

h3. *Option B — Pre-sync validation*

Before syncing, check if any asset points to a deleted location.

h3. *Option C — Sync order correction*

Process location deletion *before* asset creation.

h3. *Option D — Graceful skip*

Skip invalid child objects instead of failing whole sync.

----

h1. *Attachments:*

[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1kXY6siFqd_wurm6vsseIhMbIcvbTJWgT/view?usp=sharing|https://drive.google.com/file/d/1kXY6siFqd_wurm6vsseIhMbIcvbTJWgT/view?usp=sharing|smart-link]

column1: iOS: “Create Connection” Button Not Visible in Portrait Mode on iPad Air 5th Generation (iPadOS 26.1)
column41:
*Feature:*
Connections

*Priority:* High

----

h1. *Module:*

Connections / Create Connection Screen

h1. *Environment:*

* *Device:* iPad Air 5th Generation
* *OS:* iPadOS 26.1
* *Mode:* Portrait (Issue) / Landscape (Works)
* *App Environment:* QA (iOS Build)

*Severity:* High
*Priority:* High

----

h1. *Bug Description:*

On *iPad Air 5th Gen (iPadOS 26.1)*, the *Create Connection* button does *NOT* appear when the device is in *Portrait mode* inside the *Connections module*.

However, when rotating the device to *Landscape mode*, the Create Connection button becomes visible normally.

This is a clear *UI responsiveness / auto-layout constraint issue* similar to the Assets module. The button is likely:

* Hidden due to vertical or horizontal space constraints
* Positioned outside visible frame
* Overlapping with another UI element
* Not handled correctly in compact width (portrait)
* Affected by safe areas / bottom padding

This directly blocks users from creating new connections in Portrait mode, impacting workflow and usability.

----

h1. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open the app on *iPad Air 5th Gen – iPadOS 26.1*
# Navigate to *Connections* module
# Hold device in *Portrait mode*
# Observe that *Create Connection* button is *not visible*
# Rotate device to *Landscape mode*
# Observe that *Create Connection* button appears correctly

----

h1. *Actual Result:*

Create Connection button is *not visible or hidden* when viewing the screen in Portrait mode.

----

h1. *Expected Result:*

Create Connection button should be *visible and accessible in both Portrait and Landscape modes*.

----

h1. *Possible Root Causes:*

h3. *1️⃣ Auto-Layout Constraint Issue*

* Width constraint exceeds portrait width
* Incorrect vertical spacing pushing button off-screen
* Low-priority constraints allowing button to collapse

h3. *2️⃣ Orientation-Based Visibility Logic*

* Button might be hidden for compact width size classes
* UI checks may be incorrectly identifying iPad portrait mode

h3. *3️⃣ ScrollView Height / Content Size Issue*

* Button is pushed outside scrollable frame
* Scroll not enabled, so user cannot reach button

h3. *4️⃣ Safe Area Insets / Padding*

* Portrait has larger safe-area at bottom/top
* Button anchored too close to bottom of screen

h3. *5️⃣ iPad Layout Class Overriding*

* iPad in portrait might be treated as an iPhone layout
* Tablet-specific constraints missing or not applied

----

h1. *Developer Investigation Checklist:*

✔ Check auto-layout logs for conflicts in *portrait orientation*
✔ Inspect *horizontalSizeClass* and *verticalSizeClass* logic
✔ Validate if button is inside scrollView and whether scrolling is enabled
✔ Review safeAreaInsets in portrait vs landscape
✔ Confirm the button is not manually hidden using code during rotation
✔ Test on *iPad Simulators* (Air / Pro) to confirm reproducibility
✔ Compare constraints applied in Landscape vs Portrait

----

h1. *Attachments:*

!Screenshot 2025-12-08 at 3.36.28 PM.png|width=709,alt="Screenshot 2025-12-08 at 3.36.28 PM.png"!

column1: Web - Connection ID Appears Briefly When Switching Sites in Connections Module
column41:
*Module:*
Connections → Site Switch

*Environment:*

* *Platform:* Web (QA)
* *Browser:* (Chrome recommended; specify version if needed)
* *API Environment:* QA

*Severity:* Medium
*Priority:* low

*Video Evidence:*
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1OOzmogq7YUfGBsseJ73x9dNmncRqAm05/view?usp=sharing|https://drive.google.com/file/d/1OOzmogq7YUfGBsseJ73x9dNmncRqAm05/view?usp=sharing|smart-link] 

----

h1. *Bug Description:*

When the user switches from one *Site* to another inside the *Connections module* on the Web application, the *Connection ID* (internal backend ID) is briefly displayed for a few seconds before the UI updates to the proper user-friendly formatted values.

The flicker exposes internal system IDs that should *never be shown to end-users* and creates an unpolished user experience.

This happens due to a delay in UI rendering where the component displays *raw API response data* before applying formatting, mapping, or UI-binding.

----

h1. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Login to the *Web QA* application.
# Navigate to *Connections* module.
# Open the *Site selector* and switch from Site A to Site B.
# Observe the connection list immediately after switching.

----

h1. *Actual Result:*

* For 1–3 seconds, the list shows *raw Connection IDs*.
* After the API call completes, UI updates to the formatted values.
* Flicker clearly visible (refer video).

----

h1. *Expected Result:*

* UI should never show internal or raw IDs at any moment.
* When switching sites, the UI should:
** Clear previous state OR
** Show a loader/skeleton until the new data is ready
* Only properly formatted values should be visible.

----

h1. *Possible Root Causes:*

h3. *1️⃣ Slow State Initialization*

UI renders the previous state's raw array before formatting.

h3. *2️⃣ Component Re-Rendering Earlier Than Mapping*

Mapping logic executes _after_ initial render, causing IDs to appear first.

h3. *3️⃣ Race Condition*

API fetch completes after UI mount, leaving a short period with unmapped values.

h3. *4️⃣ Missing Loader / Skeleton*

Without a loading skeleton, React (or Vue/Angular) shows whatever is available immediately.

h3. *5️⃣ Old State Not Cleared*

Old site’s connection data is shown briefly before being overwritten.

h3. *6️⃣ Inconsistent Mapping Logic Between Initial and Final Render*

Some fields (like Connection ID) may not be conditionally hidden on first render.

----

h1. *Developer Investigation Checklist:*

✔ Check if component state is reset (e.g., {{setConnections([])}}) before fetching new site data
✔ Confirm mapping logic runs *before* UI render
✔ Add loading skeleton when API is fetching
✔ Validate whether API returns raw IDs (expected) but UI must hide them until mapping is complete
✔ Check for async race conditions between {{useEffect}} and mapping functions

----

h1. *Attachments:*

📎 Video demonstrating issue:
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1OOzmogq7YUfGBsseJ73x9dNmncRqAm05/view?usp=sharing|https://drive.google.com/file/d/1OOzmogq7YUfGBsseJ73x9dNmncRqAm05/view?usp=sharing|smart-link]

column1: Android: Unresolved and Pending Task Counts Are Different on Web and Mobile
column41:
*Module:*
Tasks / Issues Dashboard

*Environment:*

* *Web:* QA
* *Mobile:* QA (Android/iOS)
* *API Environment:* QA

*Severity:* High
*Priority:* High

----

h1. *Bug Description:*

On the *Tasks/Issues dashboard*, the *Unresolved Task Count* and *Pending Task Count* displayed on the *Web application* do *not match* the counts shown on the *mobile application* for the same project/workspace.

Users are seeing *different numbers* on Web vs Mobile, resulting in confusion, inaccurate reporting, and inconsistent workflow tracking.

The issue seems to be caused by differences in data sources or logic used across platforms.

*Screenshot:* [https://prnt.sc/nR8awWmSm8qH|https://prnt.sc/nR8awWmSm8qH|smart-link] 

----

h1. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Login to both *Web* and *Mobile* QA environments.
# Navigate to *Tasks / Issues Dashboard*.
# Compare *Unresolved* and *Pending* task counts on both platforms.
# Observe that the numbers are *not the same*, even for the same workspace or project.

----

h1. *Actual Result:*

* Web shows *different counts* for unresolved and pending tasks.
* Mobile displays *different task counts* for the same workspace.
* Filters/status mapping appear inconsistent between Web and Mobile.

----

h1. *Expected Result:*

Unresolved and Pending task counts should be *identical on both Web and Mobile*, using the *same API logic, filters, and status mapping*.

----

h1. *Possible Root Causes:*

h3. *1️⃣ Different API Endpoints*

Web and Mobile may be calling different APIs, resulting in mismatched data sets.

h3. *2️⃣ Mobile Caching Old Data*

Mobile may be returning previously cached results instead of refreshing live data.

h3. *3️⃣ Filter Logic Difference*

Status values (Pending, Unresolved, In Progress, etc.) may be mapped differently on each platform.

h3. *4️⃣ Backend Returning Different Results*

Query parameters or filters passed by Web vs Mobile may differ (e.g., assignedTo, siteId, statusIds).

h3. *5️⃣ Sync Delay*

Mobile may fetch from local storage first, causing delayed updates.

h3. *6️⃣ Web & Mobile Logic Out of Sync*

Web may have the latest categorization logic, while Mobile still uses older logic.

----

h1. *Developer Investigation Checklist:*

* Compare the *API endpoints* used by Web and Mobile.
* Inspect query parameters and filters used by each platform.
* Review mobile caching mechanism (local DB / SharedPreferences).
* Validate status mapping consistency across platforms.
* Check backend logs for differences in returned task sets.
* Confirm if both platforms use the *same business rules* to calculate counts.

----

h1. *Attachments:*

📎 Screenshot: [https://prnt.sc/nR8awWmSm8qH|https://prnt.sc/nR8awWmSm8qH|smart-link]

column1: Android: Site selection fails on Android device (Unable to select site)
column41:
*Environment:*

* *Mobile App:* Android QA Build
* *Web:* QA Environment (works fine)
* *Module:* Site Selection / Sites Dropdown
* *OS:* Android (version specify if needed)

----

h2. *Severity:* Critical

h2. *Priority:* High

----

h1. *Bug Description:*

On the Android QA app, users are *unable to select a site* from the Site Selection dropdown/list. When selecting any site, the app does *not update* the selected site and continues to show the previous/default site.

Site selection works fine on *iOS* and *Web*, but consistently fails only on *Android devices*.

----

h1. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Login to the *Android QA app*.
# Open the *Site Selection* screen (header dropdown o).
# Tap on Test site from the dropdown/list.
# Observe the UI after selection.

----

h1. *Actual Result:*

* Tap is registered, but *site does not change*.
* No update in UI.
* Data for selected site is *not loading*.
* Sometimes dropdown closes with no change.

----

h1. *Expected Result:*

When a user selects a site on Android:

* The selected site should update immediately.
* Data should load for the newly selected site.
* UI should reflect the change (header/site label must update).

----

h1. *Possible Root Causes (Developer Reference):*

h3. *1️⃣ Android API call failing*

* Site change API not triggered
* Token invalid / expired
* Wrong payload sent from Android

h3. *2️⃣ UI event not binding properly*

* onClick listener not working
* RecyclerView item not sending callback
* State not updating after selection

h3. *3️⃣ Local cache issue*

* Android storing old site in local storage
* Sync not happening with server

h3. *4️⃣ Backend environment mismatch*

* Android build pointing to Dev/Stage instead of QA
* Site list mismatched between Web & Android

h3. *5️⃣ Error but not shown on UI*

* API 400/500 hidden
* Silent error in logs
* Missing error handling

----

h1. *Attachments*

[https://drive.google.com/file/d/168dP4o8gcH10LZXSXWYgYURmZSplQJOr/view?usp=sharing|https://drive.google.com/file/d/168dP4o8gcH10LZXSXWYgYURmZSplQJOr/view?usp=sharing|smart-link]

column1: iOS | Implement Attachment Upload and Node Mapping Flow in Site Visits/Jobs
column41:
Implement flow to support uploading attachments and mapping them to nodes and show attachments using the following APIs:

# */attachment/presigned-upload*
#* Obtain a presigned URL for uploading the file (e.g., "2025 Egalvanic Assessment - Technical Report.pdf", size: 689,556 bytes).
# */attachment/create*
#* Create an attachment record with metadata including company_id, file_size, filename, key, session_id, sld_id, task_id, and type ("SLD"). see attached screenshot for types
#* Task selection is optional
# */attachment/mapping/node*
#* Map the uploaded attachment to a node using attachment_id and node_id.
#* Node selection is optional
#* can select multiple nodes
#* Selected node should show attachment in attachment section of node detail page in web.
# */attachment/?session_id=7ede368a-b9ed-4f27-9f22-bec7469b399e*
#* Get list of attachments
#* Show attachment list in UI

The iOS should provide a seamless user experience for uploading documents, tracking upload progress, and confirming successful mapping to the correct node.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* - User can select and upload a file (e.g., PDF) from the UI.-
* - On file selection, the mobile requests a presigned upload URL via {{/attachment/presigned-upload}} and uploads the file directly to storage.-
* -After successful upload, the mobile calls {{/attachment/create}} with all required metadata (company_id, file_size, filename, key, session_id, sld_id, task_id, type).-
* -Upon successful attachment creation, the mobile calls {{/attachment/mapping/node}} to map the attachment to the selected node (using attachment_id and node_id).-
* -The UI provides clear feedback for each step (upload progress, success, error handling).-
* -The uploaded attachment appears in the relevant node’s attachment list after completion if its mapped-
* -Error states (e.g., upload failure, mapping failure) are handled gracefully with user-friendly messages.-
* -All API calls use the correct payload structure as per backend requirements.-
* Unit tests cover the upload and mapping flow, including error scenarios.

*Supporting Attachments:*

column1: Deleted assets in Room module still visible on Web (QA environment)
column41:
*Environment:*

* *Mobile App:* QA Build
* *Web:* QA Environment
* *API Environment:* QA
* *Module:* Assets / Room

----

h3. *Severity: High*

h3. *Priority: Medium–High*

----

h1. *Bug Description:*

When a user deletes an asset from the *mobile app (Room module)*, the asset is deleted locally on mobile but still appears on the *web application in QA environment*. The issue seems related to API synchronization or server caching, where delete actions are not reflected on the backend.

----

h1. *Actual Result:*

Deleted asset *still appears on the Web QA application*, even after refreshing.

----

h1. *Expected Result:*

Once an asset is deleted from mobile, it should also be removed from the *server database*, and should *not be visible on Web QA*.

----

h1. *Possible Root Causes (for Developer Reference):*

* Delete API not updating database
* Local delete only (no server sync)
* Web fetching cached data
* Soft delete flag not updated
* Mobile and web pointing to different environments
* Background queue delaying deletion

----

column1: FE | Broken image URL with random text is shown
column41:
*Steps:*

# Login into Mobile app
# Go to Site Visit > Go to Locations
# Go to Add Asset Screen > Go with Quick Count / Photo Walkthrough flow to Create Asset
# In Create asset, Add Photos to Task, Issues enc
# Now Go to Web & Go to Site Visit
# Click on Tasks & Open Task Detail side panel and Observe

*Existing Behaviour:* Observed that, Broken image URL with random text is shown

*Existing Behaviour:* The uploaded image to appear.

*Root Cause Analysis*

*Web:*
Due to security considerations, the software no longer uses public URLs and instead uses signed URLs. Currently, all images uploaded through the web platform are stored in a single bucket named *“photo”*, which is also used by other modules such as Issues, Tasks, etc.

*Mobile:*
On the mobile platform, different modules use different bucket names. As a result, when loading images on the web, the signed URL fails because the bucket name differs from what the web application expects. This mismatch in bucket naming is causing the issue. We need to make them synchronised.

*Video Link:* [https://nirali-egalvanic.tinytake.com/msc/MTEzMTY0NzFfMjUwNTgzNDU|https://nirali-egalvanic.tinytake.com/msc/MTEzMTY0NzFfMjUwNTgzNDU|smart-link]

column1: iOS: Sync Queue Export Service
column41:
h3. *Summary*

A service that exports all pending sync-queue items into a structured JSON report, including metadata, queue item details, and device information. The export is saved as a shareable {{.json}} file.

* -*Generate Export Report*
Create an {{ExportReport}} object containing metadata, device info, and all queue items.-

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* System must capture the current date/time as {{exportDate}}.
* System must count all queue items and set {{totalItems}}.
* Device info must include device model, OS version, app version, and logged-in user ID.
* Each queue item must be mapped into the {{ExportedQueueItem}} structure.
* All local database lookups (e.g., entity status) must be resolved and added in the report.

* - *Include Supported Entity Types*-

       Support export for all queue items across predefined entity types.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* Export must support queue items for these targets: node, edge, photo, userTask, irPhoto, irSession, issue, quote, mappings, building/floor/room.
* Export must validate supported HTTP Methods (POST, PUT, DELETE).
* Export must include the correct endpoint path for each entity according to the mapping table.

* -*Exported Queue Item Structure* -

 Every queue item must be exported with full details.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* Exported item must include: queueItemId, target, operation, createdAt, retryCount, httpMethod, endpoint, headers, bodyJSON, entityId, entityStatus.
* bodyJSON must be exported as _pretty-printed_ JSON text.
* Headers must include all request headers (Authorization, Content-Type, etc.).
* Entity status must reflect whether the entity exists in local database.

* -*Generate JSON String*-

Convert the {{ExportReport}} object to a JSON-formatted string.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* JSON string must be valid, pretty-formatted, and UTF-8 encoded.
* JSON must preserve nested structures exactly.
* JSON must not include nil/empty properties unless required by schema.

* -*Save Export File to Temp Directory*-

Save generated JSON to a local temp file for sharing.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* File must be saved in a temporary directory accessible by the app.
* If file generation fails, service must return nil and log error.
* Output must be shareable via system share sheet.

*Sample Output:* 

{noformat}ExportReport Structure:
  {
      "exportDate": "2025-11-28T10:30:00Z",
      "totalItems": 5,
      "deviceInfo": {
          "deviceModel": "iPhone",
          "osVersion": "18.1",
          "appVersion": "1.08",
          "userId": "uuid-string"
      },
      "queueItems": [...]
  }

  ExportedQueueItem Structure:
  {
      "queueItemId": "uuid",
      "target": "node",
      "operation": "create",
      "createdAt": "2025-11-28T10:30:00Z",
      "retryCount": 0,
      "httpMethod": "POST",
      "endpoint": "https://api.example.com/node/create",
      "headers": {
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
          "Authorization": "Bearer <ACCESS_TOKEN>",
          "X-Company-Name": "company"
      },
      "bodyJSON": "{ ... pretty-printed request body ... }",
      "entityId": "uuid",
      "entityStatus": "Found in local database"
  }{noformat}

column1: iOS | App Hanging
column41:
The hang likely stems from deep recursion or blocking operations within SwiftUI's style resolution, specifically around AnyShapeStyle.init.

[http://egalvanic-yb.sentry.io/issues/7087081529/?project=4510436760748032&query=is%3Aunresolved&referrer=issue-stream|http://egalvanic-yb.sentry.io/issues/7087081529/?project=4510436760748032&query=is%3Aunresolved&referrer=issue-stream]
column211:
[~accountid:712020:b43bccaf-6e8f-47ef-82f7-5439c6765fdb] *App Hanging* - The app is freezing for at least 2000ms (2 seconds). *This is a UI responsiveness issue*, not a crash.



 From the breadcrumbs, the hang occurred after these API calls completed:

  | Endpoint                      | Response Size |

  |-------------------------------|---------------|

  | /api/forms                    | *5.4 MB*        |

  | /api/sld/v2/{UUID}            | *1.2 MB*        |

  | /api/node_classes/user/{UUID} | 85 KB         |



*The problem*: Large API responses (especially the 5.4 MB /api/forms) are being parsed and likely saved to SwiftData on the *main thread*, blocking the UI.

The issue occurs *only for one specific asset*.
For all other assets scanned offline, the Wi-Fi icon does *not* appear, which is the correct behavior.

----
