# Open the app in *offline mode*.
# Scan the QR code of the specific asset where the issue occurs.
# Open the asset details screen.
# Check the profile picture.

----

h3. *Actual Result:*

The *Wi-Fi icon* is visible on the Asset Profile Picture *only for one asset* when opened in offline mode.

----

h3. *Expected Result:*

In offline mode:

* The Asset Profile Picture should *NOT* show any Wi-Fi icon.
* All assets should behave consistently in offline mode.

----

h3. *Additional Note:*

* Issue occurs *only in one specific asset*, so the problem may be related to asset-specific data or local caching.
* Other assets work fine in offline mode.

column1: System Allows Loop Creation (A → B → C → A) in Connection Mapping
column41:
h3. *Bug Description:*

While creating connections between points/nodes, the system currently allows creating a *loop* (A → B → C → A).
This behavior is incorrect.

In the *old system*, loop formation was *restricted*.
If a user tried to connect C → A after A → B and B → C, the system would show an *error message* and prevent the loop.

But in the *new system*, loop creation is allowed, which can cause wrong network flow and data inconsistency.

----

h3. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Create a connection from *A → B*.
# Create a connection from *B → C*.
# Now create a connection from *C → A*.
# Observe system behavior.

----

h3. *Actual Result:*

The system allows *C → A* connection, creating a *loop* automatically.

----

h3. *Expected Result:*

The system should *not allow loops*.
When user tries to connect *C → A*, the system should:

* Show an error message (e.g., _"Loop connection not allowed"_)
* Prevent creation of the connection.

----

h3. *Additional Note:*

This issue did *not* happen in the old system — loop creation was *restricted properly* before.

column1: Web: Location Deletion Not Working
column41:
When a user attempts to delete a Location from the *Locations list*, the system fails to perform the delete action.
After clicking the *Delete icon/button*, the following issues occur:

* No *confirmation popup* is displayed.
* No *success* or *error* message appears.
* The selected *location remains visible* in the list.
* The delete action appears to be *not sent or not processed* by the system.

This indicates that the delete operation is either not triggered or is failing silently without any UI feedback.

----

h3. *Impact:*

Users are unable to remove *unwanted or outdated locations*, leading to:

* Incorrect or stale data in the system.
* Poor user experience due to lack of feedback.
* Potential issues in workflows where location data is critical.

----

h3. *Possible Root Causes:*

Based on the behavior, the issue may be caused by one or more of the following:

# *Delete API is not being triggered* from the frontend.
# *Incorrect location ID* or payload is being sent in the API request.
# *Wrong API endpoint* mapped to the delete action.
# *Backend validation failure*, preventing deletion.
# *Frontend state not refreshing* after a successful delete.
# *Missing toast/snackbar alerts* for success or error handling.
# *UI components not re-rendering* after the deletion response.

----

h3. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Navigate to *Locations* module.
# Select any existing location from the list.
# Click on the *Delete* icon/button.
# Observe that:
#* No popup or message appears.
#* The location still remains in the list.

----

h3. *Expected Result:*

* A *confirmation popup* should appear before deletion.
* On confirming the action:
** The delete API should be triggered with the *correct location ID*.
** On success:
*** The location should be removed from the list.
*** A *success toast message* should be displayed.
** On failure:
*** An *error toast/message* should appear.

----

h3. *Actual Result:*

* No popup, confirmation, or message displayed.
* No visible API call being made (as per UI behavior).
* Location remains in the list.

----

h3. *Video Evidence:*

📎 *Video Link:*
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1O2rUVgPpXvsiWBeSNRVrmXFTRUQTIeIZ/view?usp=sharing|https://drive.google.com/file/d/1O2rUVgPpXvsiWBeSNRVrmXFTRUQTIeIZ/view?usp=sharing|smart-link] 

----

h3. *Priority:*

*High*

column1: iOS | Face ID Gets Removed After 3 Failed Attempts on iOS
column41:
h3. *Bug Description:*

When Face ID is already enabled in the iOS app, and the user enters the wrong Face ID three times, the app asks for the password. If the user cancels the password screen, the Face ID setting automatically gets removed. This should not happen—Face ID should remain enabled unless the user manually disables it.

----

h3. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Open the iOS app and enable *Face ID* login.
# Lock the app.
# Try to log in using Face ID.
# Enter an incorrect Face ID three times.
# App displays *"Enter Password"* screen.
# Tap on *Cancel* instead of entering the password.
# Go to settings and check Face ID status.

----

h3. *Actual Result:*

Face ID automatically gets removed/disabled after cancelling the password screen.

----

column1: Location Dropdown Not Visible Intermittently
column41:
In the *Locations module*, the dropdown menu (used for selecting Country, State, or other dependent fields) *intermittently fails to open* when the user clicks on it.
The issue is inconsistent and random, making it difficult to reproduce reliably.

When the dropdown does not appear:

* The user *cannot select any value*.
* Form submission becomes blocked due to required fields remaining unfilled.
* The workflow is interrupted, causing delays and confusion.

The intermittent nature suggests a possible issue with rendering, event handling, or UI layering.

----

h3. *Impact:*

* Prevents users from completing or editing location forms.
* Causes workflow disruptions and slows down data entry.
* Leads to user frustration due to unpredictable behavior.
* May result in incorrect or incomplete location records.

----

h3. *Steps to Reproduce:*

_(Intermittent behavior — may not occur every time)_

# Navigate to the *Locations* module.
# Open the *Create Location* or *Edit Location* form.
# Click on any dropdown (e.g., Country, State, City).
# Observe that sometimes:
#* The dropdown does *not* open.
#* No loading indicator appears.
#* No error message is shown.

----

h3. *Expected Result:*

* Dropdown should *always* open when the user clicks on it.
* Options should load consistently without delay.
* UI should not block the selection process.
* Any errors should trigger clear feedback messages.

----

h3. *Actual Result:*

* Dropdown intermittently *fails to appear*.
* User is unable to select any value.
* Workflow is blocked until page refresh or retry.

----

h3. *Video Evidence:*

📎 *Video Link:*
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1O2rUVgPpXvsiWBeSNRVrmXFTRUQTIeIZ/view?usp=sharing|https://drive.google.com/file/d/1O2rUVgPpXvsiWBeSNRVrmXFTRUQTIeIZ/view?usp=sharing|smart-link] 

----

h3. *Priority:*

*Medium* — Blocks form submission and affects core functionality.

column211:
*1. Quick Count View (New Feature)*

  *File:* Views/QuickCount/QuickCountView.swift (2080 lines)

  *Purpose*

  Bulk asset creation with simplified count-based workflow for rapid field

  data collection.



  *Data Models*

  QuickCountEntry

  ├── nodeClass: NodeClass

  ├── nodeSubtype: NodeSubtype? (optional)

  ├── quantity: Int (default: 1)

  └── photosets: [QuickCountPhotoset]



  QuickCountPhotoset

  ├── assetIndex: Int (1-based, which asset this belongs to)

  ├── photos: [QuickCountStagedPhoto]

  ├── ocpAssets: [QuickCountOCPAsset] (OCPDs with photos)

  └── ocpCountEntries: [QuickCountOCPCountEntry] (OCPDs without photos)



  QuickCountOCPAsset

  ├── nodeClass: NodeClass

  ├── nodeSubtype: NodeSubtype?

  ├── photos: [QuickCountStagedPhoto]

  └── quantity: Int



  QuickCountOCPCountEntry

  ├── nodeClass: NodeClass

  ├── nodeSubtype: NodeSubtype?

  └── quantity: Int



  QuickCountStagedPhoto

  ├── imageData: Data

  ├── filename: String

  └── localFilepath: String



  *UI Flow*

# *Entry Point:* Room detail view → "Create Quick Count" button

# *Add Asset Types:* User selects NodeClass from list

# *Subtype Selection:* If NodeClass has subtypes, show subtype picker

  (optional, can skip)

# *Quantity Adjustment:* Stepper to set quantity (1+)

# *Expand/Collapse:* Each entry row is expandable

# *Add Photos:* Optional - add photos for specific assets (by index)

# *Add OCPDs:* Two modes:

* *Photo-based:* Take photos, select OCP NodeClass/Subtype

* *Count-based:* Just add quantity without photos

# *Summary Bar:* Shows total assets and photos count

# *Create:* Creates all assets with progress indicator



  *Key UI Components*

* Room breadcrumb header

* Asset type list with stepper controls

* "Add Asset Type" button with NodeClass picker

* Subtype selection sheet

* Summary bar with "Create X Assets" button

* Progress overlay during creation



  *Asset Creation Logic*

* For each entry, create quantity number of NodeV2 assets

* If asset has OCPDs, set type = "group" (otherwise use NodeClass style)

* Create child OCPs with parent_id set to parent asset

* Photos are saved locally and marked upload_needed = true

* Assets marked with needsSync = true
column212:
# *Photo Walkthrough View (New Feature)*

  *File:* Views/PhotoWalkthrough/PhotoWalkthroughView.swift (1115 lines)

  *Purpose*

  Camera-first bulk asset creation with 1:1 photo-to-asset mapping. Guided

  workflow for systematic documentation.



  *Data Models*

  WalkthroughParentAsset

  ├── nodeClass: NodeClass

  ├── nodeSubtype: NodeSubtype?

  ├── photos: [WalkthroughStagedPhoto]

  └── children: [WalkthroughChildAsset]



  WalkthroughChildAsset

  ├── nodeClass: NodeClass

  ├── nodeSubtype: NodeSubtype?

  └── photos: [WalkthroughStagedPhoto]



  WalkthroughStagedPhoto

  ├── imageData: Data

  ├── filename: String

  └── localFilepath: String



  *State Machine*

  WalkthroughState:

# capturingParentPhotos    → Camera open, taking photos of parent

# classifyingParent        → Select NodeClass/Subtype for parent

# promptingForChildren     → "Add OCPD photos?" (only if NodeClass.ocp ==

   true)

# capturingChildPhotos     → Camera open for OCPD

# classifyingChild         → Select NodeClass/Subtype for OCPD

# decidingChildAction      → "Add Another OCPD" or "Done with OCP"

# decidingNextAction       → "Add Another Asset" or "Finish"

# reviewing                → Review all captured assets before creation

# creating                 → Creating assets (show progress)



  *UI Flow*

# *Photo Capture View:*

* Camera button (opens native camera)

* Gallery button (PhotosPicker)

* Thumbnail strip of captured photos

* Delete button on each thumbnail

* "Done with this asset/OCPD" button

# *Classification View:*

* Photo thumbnails strip at top

* NodeClass dropdown picker

* NodeSubtype dropdown (optional, only if subtypes exist)

* "Continue" button

# *Decision Views:*

* Child Prompt: "Add OCPD?" → Yes/No buttons

* Child Decision: "Add Another OCPD?" / "Done with OCPs"

* Next Action: "Add Another Asset" / "Finish & Review"

# *Review View:*

* List of all parent assets with children

* Edit/delete capability

* "Create All" button

# *Creating View:*

* Progress indicator

* Count of created assets



  *Key Differences from Quick Count*

  | Quick Count                | Photo Walkthrough             |

  |----------------------------|-------------------------------|

  | Count-first approach       | Camera-first approach         |

  | Batch add by quantity      | One asset at a time           |

  | Photos optional            | Photos required (1:1 mapping) |

  | OCPD count entries allowed | OCPD must have photos         |

column218:
*8. Arc Flash Completion Calculation Update*

  *File:* Views/ArcFlash/ArcFlashCompletionView.swift



  *Changes to Asset Details Completion*

  *Old Logic:* Average of node.af_completion across all nodes

  *New Logic:*

* Count total required fields vs completed fields across ALL nodes

* Unclassified nodes (no NodeClass) penalized as 0% (1 required, 0

  completed)

* Uses attribute lookup map with multiple keys (by ID, name, property ID)

* Matches web app calculation logic



  *Changes to Source/Target Completion*

  *Old Logic:* Only checked direct children for inbound connections

  *New Logic:*

* Recursive check: hasDescendantWithSource() function

* Checks children AND descendants for inbound connections

* Node counts as "having source" if:

* Direct target of an edge, OR

* Has any descendant that is a target



  *Changes to Connection Details Completion*

* Same field-counting logic as Asset Details

* Counts total required fields vs completed across all edges

column1: Android: Node Connections / Edges Section
column41:
As a field engineer want to view and manage power connections (edges) for a selected node which is having ability to add OCP and have box is true.

So that he can correctly configure how power flows into and out of the asset.

Acceptance Criteria

* -View Connections-
** -Show Connections section in EditNodeDetailView-
** - Show badge count for total relevant connections-
** - Display connections sorted: Lineside → Loadside → Name-
** - Lineside connections show blue icon + correct Source → Target-
** - Loadside connections show orange icon + correct Source → Target-
** - Internal edges (if any) show gray icon-
** - Incomplete edges show warning indicator-
* -Create Lineside Connection-
** - Via “+” menu, user can select New Lineside Connection-
** - Target must be pre-selected to the context node or its children-
** - Source must be selectable from all nodes-
** - Inline dropdown picker with expand + search-
** - Validation: Source ≠ Target-
** - On creation → edge appears in the list immediately-
* -Create Loadside Connection-
** - Via “+” menu, user can select New Loadside Connection-
** - Source must be pre-selected to the context node or its children-
** - Target must be selectable from all nodes-
** - Validation: Source ≠ Target-
** - New connection appears in list under Loadside-
* -Delete Connection-
** - Swipe left shows Delete action-
** - Confirmation dialog appears before final removal-
** - On confirmation, connection removed from section-
* -Edge Filtering / Relevance-
** - Only edges where source or target is context node or its children are shown-
** - Edges marked as deleted must NOT be shown-

column1: Android: Sync Queue Export Service
column41:
h3. *Summary*

A service that exports all pending sync-queue items into a structured JSON report, including metadata, queue item details, and device information. The export is saved as a shareable {{.json}} file.

* -*Generate Export Report*
Create an {{ExportReport}} object containing metadata, device info, and all queue items.-

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* System must capture the current date/time as {{exportDate}}.
* System must count all queue items and set {{totalItems}}.
* Device info must include device model, OS version, app version, and logged-in user ID.
* Each queue item must be mapped into the {{ExportedQueueItem}} structure.
* All local database lookups (e.g., entity status) must be resolved and added in the report.

* - *Include Supported Entity Types*-

       Support export for all queue items across predefined entity types.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* Export must support queue items for these targets: node, edge, photo, userTask, irPhoto, irSession, issue, quote, mappings, building/floor/room.
* Export must validate supported HTTP Methods (POST, PUT, DELETE).
* Export must include the correct endpoint path for each entity according to the mapping table.

* -*Exported Queue Item Structure* -

 Every queue item must be exported with full details.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* Exported item must include: queueItemId, target, operation, createdAt, retryCount, httpMethod, endpoint, headers, bodyJSON, entityId, entityStatus.
* bodyJSON must be exported as _pretty-printed_ JSON text.
* Headers must include all request headers (Authorization, Content-Type, etc.).
* Entity status must reflect whether the entity exists in local database.

* -*Generate JSON String*-

Convert the {{ExportReport}} object to a JSON-formatted string.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* JSON string must be valid, pretty-formatted, and UTF-8 encoded.
* JSON must preserve nested structures exactly.
* JSON must not include nil/empty properties unless required by schema.

* -*Save Export File to Temp Directory*-

Save generated JSON to a local temp file for sharing.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* Export file must follow naming format: {{sync_queue_export_MM-DD-YYYY_HH-MM.json}}.
* File must be saved in a temporary directory accessible by the app.
* If file generation fails, service must return nil and log error.
* Output must be shareable via system share sheet.

*Sample Output:* 

{noformat}ExportReport Structure:
  {
      "exportDate": "2025-11-28T10:30:00Z",
      "totalItems": 5,
      "deviceInfo": {
          "deviceModel": "iPhone",
          "osVersion": "18.1",
          "appVersion": "1.08",
          "userId": "uuid-string"
      },
      "queueItems": [...]
  }

  ExportedQueueItem Structure:
  {
      "queueItemId": "uuid",
      "target": "node",
      "operation": "create",
      "createdAt": "2025-11-28T10:30:00Z",
      "retryCount": 0,
      "httpMethod": "POST",
      "endpoint": "https://api.example.com/node/create",
      "headers": {
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
          "Authorization": "Bearer <ACCESS_TOKEN>",
          "X-Company-Name": "company"
      },
      "bodyJSON": "{ ... pretty-printed request body ... }",
      "entityId": "uuid",
      "entityStatus": "Found in local database"
  }{noformat}

column1: Android: Ability to add/modify/Delete OCP in asset details
column41:
User want to view and manage child assets inside an enclosure, So that can structure OCP devices under their parent enclosure.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* -*Child Nodes Section Visibility*-

* The Child Nodes section must be displayed only when:
** The current node has no parent ({{parent_id == nil}}), and
** The node’s class has {{box == true}}.

* - *Child Node Listing Logic*-

* Only direct children of the parent node should be listed.
* Each child node must show completion percentage and be selectable for:
** Viewing details
** Unlinking
** Deleting

* -*Create Child Node – Logical Rules*-

* Asset Class list must include only classes where:
** {{is_deleted == false}}
** {{ocp == true}}
* On child creation:
** Set {{parent_id = parent.id}}
** Set position defaults: {{x = 0}}, {{y = 0}}
** Inherit {{location}} and {{room}} from parent
** Set width and height from the selected node class
* If the parent node type is not already {{"group"}}, update it to {{"group"}}.
* If an active IR session exists, automatically link the newly created child node to the session.

* -*Link Existing Node – Logical Rules*-

* Eligible nodes for linking must satisfy:
** {{is_deleted == false}}
** {{parent_id == nil}} (not linked elsewhere)
** Their ID must not match the parent ID (cannot link a node to itself)
** Their node class must have {{ocp == true}}
* When linking:
** Set {{child.parent_id = parent.id}}
** Reset child position: {{x = 0}}, {{y = 0}}
** Mark child as {{needsSync = true}}
* If the parent node is not {{"group"}}, update it to {{"group"}}.

* -*Unlink Child Node – Logical Rules*-

* On unlink:
** Clear {{parent_id}} (set to {{nil}})
** Move the node visually outside the parent boundary by setting:
*** {{x = parent.x + parent.width + 50}}
*** {{y = parent.y}}
** Mark the node as {{needsSync = true}}.

* -*Delete Node – Logical Rules*-

* When deleting a node:
** Perform a cascade delete:
*** Recursively identify all child nodes under the node.
*** Soft-delete all nodes in the hierarchy ({{is_deleted = true}}).

* -Check all above cases in site visits node -

column1: DevOps: Update configuration using terraform
column41:
{noformat} DevOps Notes - Manual AWS Changes to be added to Terraform

## 2024-11-30: Added GOTENBERG_URL to Backend ECS Services

Added `GOTENBERG_URL` environment variable to all backend ECS services so they can invoke the Gotenberg PDF service.

**Changes:**
1. Added `GOTENBERG_URL` to AWS Secrets Manager for all environments
2. Registered new task definition revisions (dev/qa/staging/prod: rev 7, bces-iq: rev 5)
3. Deployed updated services

**Values by environment:**
- Dev: `http://gotenberg.egpz.dev:3000`
- QA: `http://gotenberg.egpz.qa:3000`
- Staging: `http://gotenberg.egpz.staging:3000`
- Prod: `http://gotenberg.egpz.prod:3000`
- BCES-IQ Prod: `http://gotenberg.bces-iq.prod:3000`

**Terraform update needed:** Add GOTENBERG_URL to task definitions and secrets.

## 2024-12-01: WAF Rule Exclusion for Large Request Bodies

Excluded `SizeRestrictions_BODY` rule from `AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet` in the prod WAF (`eg-pz-prod-waf-ohio`).

**Issue:** Requests with body size > 8KB were being blocked with 403 errors from CloudFront. This affected bulk API endpoints like `/api/bulk/create-unary-tasks` when creating tasks for many nodes.

**Fix:** Added rule exclusion to allow larger request bodies.

**Before:**
```json
{
  "Name": "AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet",
  "Statement": {
    "ManagedRuleGroupStatement": {
      "VendorName": "AWS",
      "Name": "AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet"
    }
  }
}
```

**After:**
```json
{
  "Name": "AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet",
  "Statement": {
    "ManagedRuleGroupStatement": {
      "VendorName": "AWS",
      "Name": "AWSManagedRulesCommonRuleSet",
      "ExcludedRules": [
        {"Name": "SizeRestrictions_BODY"}
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

**Terraform update needed:** Add `SizeRestrictions_BODY` to excluded rules for the prod WAF managed rule group.{noformat}

column1: iOS - iPad portrait keyboard closes when focusing email input
column41:
On iPad devices in portrait orientation, tapping the email input field causes the keyboard to open briefly and then immediately close. This prevents the user from entering an email address and effectively traps them on the screen.  
  Issue does not occur in landscape mode.

  Steps to Reproduce:

# Open the app on an iPad in portrait mode.
# Navigate to the screen with the email input box.
# Tap the email input field.
# Keyboard opens then immediately closes.
# User cannot proceed.

column1: iOS: Update asset creation workflow for Quick Add and Detailed Add
column41:
Implement the new asset creation workflow on iOS as per EGPZ-164. Requirements:

* Allow users to select between Quick Add (basic fields) and Detailed Add (full form with class-based core attributes and editable detail fields) during asset creation.
* Enable switching between modes before submission.

h2. {color:#ff5630}NOTE: There is an existing IR implementation of this in UnifiedEntityEditor.jsx, but it is quite poorly implemented. Please either improve or replace this implementation.{color}


*Issues*

When an IR photo is linked to an issue, this should be shown in an IR photos section within UnifiedEntityEditor.jsx. 

When accessed from within a Site Visit, the user should be able to:

* Link or unlink IR photos to issues (if the issue is linked to a node, then the options for linking/unlinking should be limited to the IR photos that belong to both that node and that site visit/session)

When accessed from the Issues tab, the user should be able to see IR photos that are associated but not edit them.

Also let’s make this component better looking, it’s a little bit ridiculous right now



*Nodes*

When an IR photo is linked to a node (as they all are), this should be shown in an IR photos section within UnifiedEntityEditor.jsx

When accessed from within a Site Visit, the user should be able to:

* Create or soft-delete IR photos
* Created photos should also be linked to the Site Visit from which the editor has been accessed
* The photos that are shown must all also be linked to the Site Visit that is active

When accessed from the Asset detail page / asset list, the user should be able to:

* View only all of the IR photos that are linked to the node, regardless of the site visit they’re associated with

Re-use same IR photos component as in issues version of UnifiedEntityEditor



*Tasks*

Tasks can be linked to 0-n nodes. Right now, we are simply allowing a node selection and displaying read-only chips when multi-node tasks are displayed. We need to implement a proper multi-select for nodes which create or nullify multiple mappings on save.

column1: User should be able to easily generate Standard Quotes by adding bulk procedures (i.e., IR Scan for all assets) in the same way that tasks can be bulk-created within Site Visits
column41:
!Screenshot 2025-11-21 at 11.45.40 AM.png|width=752,alt="Screenshot 2025-11-21 at 11.45.40 AM.png"!



*HOW IT SHOULD WORK:*

* First, you have an option to select any procedure master that is listed as unary=true, or to select “Class-Specific” instead

!Screenshot 2025-11-21 at 12.06.49 PM.png|width=629,alt="Screenshot 2025-11-21 at 12.06.49 PM.png"!

* The options in the procedure dropdown should come from the procedure masters table. Show an option for all procedures masters that have unary=true, and then show an additional option “Class-Specific”

!image-20251121-200836.png|width=347,alt="image-20251121-200836.png"!

* Give the user the option to add this procedure to all or a selection of the assets within the SLD



*LOGIC FOR ADDING UNARY PROCEDRUES*

* What unary means is basically “every procedure that belongs to this procedure master within a given node class / node subtype pair is basically the same, so we can safely grab and apply the first such procedure to all assets that it’s being quoted for”
* Example: “Infrared Thermography” (unary=true)
** For each asset, check if that asset has a node class and node subtype
*** If no node class, skip
*** If there is a node class
**** Check if there is a node subtype
***** If there is a node subtype
****** Try to find procedure that has a procedure master of infrared thermography and that matches both the node class and node subtype
****** If a match is found, pull this procedure into the quote
****** If no match is found, try to match on node class only
****** If a match is found, pull this procedure into the quote
****** If no match is found, skip
**** If no node subtype, try to match only on node class
***** If there is a match
****** Pull this procedure into the quote
***** If there is no match
****** Skip



*LOGIC FOR ADDING CLASS-SPECIFIC PROCEDURES*

* A bit simpler; select node class first
* Then select node subtype (note this is different from screenshot)
** *Important: show counts for assets in each node class and node subtype. Don’t show (or grey out) options for asset classes or node subtypes that don’t have any associated assets on them.*
* Then select procedure
* Then select the assets (all or specific) you want to apply these records to

!Screenshot 2025-11-21 at 11.53.11 AM.png|width=752,alt="Screenshot 2025-11-21 at 11.53.11 AM.png"!

!Screenshot 2025-11-21 at 12.11.27 PM.png|width=623,alt="Screenshot 2025-11-21 at 12.11.27 PM.png"!

*WHAT HAPPENS ON  “PROCEDURE ADD TO QUOTE”*

* You are creating quote_workorder_line records
** quote_id → linked to quote that’s being worked on
** planned_workorder_id → linked to the one planned workorder in the standard quote
** node_id → linked to nodes
** procedure_id → linked to procedure
** shortcut_id → null
** task_id → null
** description → derived from procedures
** window_start → null
** window_end → null
** est_mins → derived from procedures
** subcontracted → false
** is_deleted → false
** created_at → NOW()
** modified_at → null

column1: Mobile App Update Management (Force & Optional Updates with Remind Me Later)
column41:
h2. *Summary*

Implement a unified update management system for Android and iOS apps where the app calls a API that returns force update, optional update, or no update, and the app behaves accordingly. The solution must support “Remind Me Later” for optional updates, follow platform guidelines, and handle API failures.

h2. *Description*

To ensure users remain on secure, compatible, and stable versions of the mobile application, the app must check for updates on every launch (and periodically in the session) using an API. This API will dynamically return whether the user requires a force update, can perform an optional update, or can continue without updating.

The app will show a non-dismissible force popup screen when the current version is below the minimum supported version.
For optional updates, the app will show an update prompt with Update Now and Remind Me Later actions. The “Remind Me Later” will follow a configurable cooldown period returned from the API.
If no update is required, the app continues normally.

The system must support both Android and iOS.

Notes:

# User can keep using app if he is in offline mode and don’t go online and use dashboard screen.

column1: Android: Face and Fingerprint Login
column41:
The Android application will implement face and fingerprint login features to enhance security and user convenience. This allows users to authenticate their identity quickly and securely using biometric data. The system will ensure that the biometric data is stored securely and complies with privacy regulations.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

# Users can enable or disable face and fingerprint login in the app settings.
# The app must successfully log in users who provide valid biometric data.
# If biometric authentication fails, the app should provide an option to log in using a password.
# The app should notify users if their biometric data cannot be recognized after multiple attempts.
# The app must comply with all relevant data protection and privacy regulations regarding biometric data.

*Error Handling:*

# If the biometric sensor is not available or malfunctioning, the app should display a message indicating that the feature is unavailable and prompt the user to use an alternative login method.
# If the user cancels the biometric authentication, the app should return to the login screen without any errors.
# Any unexpected errors during the biometric authentication process should be logged, and a user-friendly error message should be displayed.

column1: Allow user to override globals
column41:
* Remove feature flags to hide everything:
** node_classes
** edge_classes
** issue_classes
** procedures
** shortcuts
** forms
* Add “is_override” property to all of the above tables, plus a self-referencing foreign key “for_entity” which points to the table’s own id column
* When fetching any of the above, including in the get_sld_full_v2 endpoint for mobile, if an is_override=true entity exists, omit the thing that it overrides but maintain a reference to the thing that it overrides for use in the next step (this will require modifying DTOs / SwiftData models)
* When upserting into mobile, if any local refrerences to the overridden entities are located, they need to be converted to the overriden version
* In Frontend, we need to add options in settings for all of the above classes to “create override” and warn them that this cannot be undone
* That should then essentially create a new class record with is_global=false, company_id= that user’s company ID, is_override=true, and for_entity= whatever entiity (i.e. class or procedure) id it is overriding, *and it should force-change the class of all entities within the user’s company that currently reference the old class*



{noformat} Here is Claude's plan:
╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 Entity Override System - Revised Implementation Plan

 Phase 1: Database Schema Changes (Direct SQL)

 Execute via psql on each environment:
 -- Node classes
 ALTER TABLE node_class ADD COLUMN is_override BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL;
 ALTER TABLE node_class ADD COLUMN for_entity UUID REFERENCES node_class(id);

 -- Edge classes
 ALTER TABLE edge_class ADD COLUMN is_override BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL;
 ALTER TABLE edge_class ADD COLUMN for_entity UUID REFERENCES edge_class(id);

 -- Issue classes  
 ALTER TABLE issue_class ADD COLUMN is_override BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL;
 ALTER TABLE issue_class ADD COLUMN for_entity UUID REFERENCES issue_class(id);

 -- Procedures
 ALTER TABLE procedure ADD COLUMN is_override BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL;
 ALTER TABLE procedure ADD COLUMN for_entity UUID REFERENCES procedure(id);

 -- Shortcuts
 ALTER TABLE shortcut ADD COLUMN is_override BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL;
 ALTER TABLE shortcut ADD COLUMN for_entity UUID REFERENCES shortcut(id);

 -- Forms
 ALTER TABLE form ADD COLUMN is_override BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE NOT NULL;
 ALTER TABLE form ADD COLUMN for_entity UUID REFERENCES form(id);

 ---
 Phase 2: Update SQLAlchemy Models

 Add to each model (NodeClass, EdgeClass, IssueClass, Procedure, Shortcut, Form):
 is_override = db.Column(db.Boolean, default=False, nullable=False)
 for_entity = db.Column(UUID(as_uuid=True), db.ForeignKey('table_name.id'), nullable=True)
 original = db.relationship('ClassName', remote_side=[id], backref='overrides')

 Update to_dict() methods to include:
 - is_override
 - for_entity (as string)

 ---
 Phase 3: Create Override Endpoints with DB Reference Migration

 New file: app/routes/override_routes.py

 Endpoint example: POST /override/node_class/<class_id>

 Override creation logic:
 1. Verify entity is global and exists
 2. Check if override already exists (prevent duplicates)
 3. Copy entity with all properties:
   - For node_class: Also copy all node_class_property records with new IDs
   - Set is_override=True, company_id=user.company_id, for_entity=original_id
 4. Database reference migration (critical for mobile sync):
 -- Update all company nodes to reference new override
 UPDATE nodes
 SET node_class_id = :override_id
 WHERE node_class_id = :global_id
   AND sld_id IN (SELECT id FROM slds WHERE company_id = :company_id);
 5. Similar updates for edges, issues, procedures, forms, shortcuts
 6. Return override + count of migrated references

 All 6 endpoint variants:
 - /override/node_class/<id> → Update nodes.node_class_id
 - /override/edge_class/<id> → Update edges.edge_class_id
 - /override/issue_class/<id> → Update issues.issue_class_id
 - /override/procedure/<id> → Update various procedure references
 - /override/shortcut/<id> → Update mapping_procedure_shortcut
 - /override/form/<id> → Update tasks.form_id, procedures.form_id

 ---
 Phase 4: Update Fetch Filtering (Hide Overridden Globals)

 Pattern for all entity fetch endpoints:
 # Return company entities + global entities that haven't been overridden
 from sqlalchemy import exists

 override_exists = exists().where(
     and_(
         EntityClass.for_entity == EntityClass.id,
         EntityClass.company_id == user.company_id,
         EntityClass.is_deleted == False
     )
 )

 query = EntityClass.query.filter(
     and_(
         or_(
             EntityClass.company_id == user.company_id,  # Company entities (including 
 overrides)
             and_(
                 EntityClass.is_global == True,  # Global entities...
                 ~override_exists  # ...that aren't overridden
             )
         ),
         EntityClass.is_deleted == False
     )
 )

 Files to update:
 - app/routes/class_routes.py (3 endpoints)
 - app/controllers/node_controller.py
 - app/controllers/form_controller.py
 - app/controllers/shortcut_controller.py (2 methods)
 - app/controllers/lookup_controller.py
 - app/routes/sld_routes.py - get_sld_full_v2

 ---
 Phase 5: Mobile Sync - Remap Logic in iOS

 Critical: Mobile may have local nodes referencing old global class IDs that are now hidden by
 overrides.

 Update: SLDSyncService.swift upsert logic

 In upsertSwiftDataObjects() during node/edge/issue processing:
 // Before processing each entity, check if its class_id exists in fetched classes
 if let nodeClassId = nodeDTO.node_class_id {
     // Check if class exists in availableClasses
     let classExists = availableClasses.contains { $0.id == nodeClassId }

     if !classExists {
         // Check if an override exists for this global class
         if let override = availableClasses.first(where: { $0.for_entity == nodeClassId }) {
             // Remap to override
             nodeDTO.node_class_id = override.id
             print("⚠️ Remapped node \(nodeDTO.id) from global class \(nodeClassId) to override 
 \(override.id)")
         }
     }
 }

 Apply same logic for:
 - Nodes → node_class_id
 - Edges → edge_class_id
 - Issues → issue_class_id
 - Tasks → form_id
 - Procedures → form_id

 ---
 Phase 6: Update DTOs and Swift Models

 Backend DTOs - Add fields to:
 - NodeClassDTO
 - EdgeClassDTO
 - IssueClassDTO
 - ProcedureDTO
 - ShortcutDTO
 - FormDTO

 Add to each:
 is_override: bool
 for_entity: Optional[str]  # UUID as string

 iOS Models - Update SwiftData models:
 - NodeClass
 - EdgeClass
 - IssueClass
 - Form
 - Procedure
 - Shortcut

 Add to each:
 @Attribute var is_override: Bool = false
 @Attribute var for_entity: UUID? = nil

 ---
 Phase 7: Frontend Override Management UI

 New Settings Section: "Customize Global Entities"

 For each entity type, show:
 - List of global entities with "Create Custom Version" button
 - Warning modal:
 "This will create a company-specific copy of '[Entity Name]' and automatically update all 
 existing [nodes/edges/etc] in your organization to use the custom version. This cannot be 
 undone. Continue?"
 - Display count: "This will affect X [nodes/edges/etc]"
 - After creation: Show success with migration count

 Files to create:
 - eg-pz-frontend/src/pages/Settings/OverrideManagement.jsx
 - Add navigation route for override management
 - Update entity display to show "(Custom)" badge for overrides

 ---
 Phase 8: Testing Checklist

 Backend Tests:
 1. ✓ Create override for node_class → Verify nodes.node_class_id updated in DB
 2. ✓ Fetch node_classes after override → Verify global hidden, override shown
 3. ✓ Prevent duplicate overrides → Should fail gracefully
 4. ✓ Test all 6 entity types

 Mobile Tests:
 1. ✓ Sync with existing local nodes referencing global class
 2. ✓ Create override on backend
 3. ✓ Mobile fetches classes → Global class missing, override present
 4. ✓ Upsert remaps local node references → Verify nodes now reference override
 5. ✓ No orphaned references remain

 Frontend Tests:
 1. ✓ Create override via UI → See warning, confirm, see success
 2. ✓ Verify override appears in dropdowns with "(Custom)" badge
 3. ✓ Verify global entity no longer appears

 ---
 Summary

 SQL Scripts: 6 ALTER TABLE commands (per environment)
 Backend Changes: 6 models updated, 7 fetch endpoints updated, 1 new override_routes.py file
 Mobile Changes: Update 6 SwiftData models, add remap logic in SLDSyncService
 Frontend Changes: 1 new override management page

 Key Innovation: Mobile remap logic during upsert handles the sync edge case automatically.{noformat}

h2. User story

* As a developer, when integrating an alliance partner, I want to retrieve the sub-domain using the company code so that I can ensure the correct configuration.

h2. Context

This =involves integrating an alliance partner by retrieving necessary information from the company and alliance partner tables. The integration requires handling cases where the alliance partner key may be absent.

h2. Acceptance criteria

* Retrieve the sub-domain from the company table using the company code.
* Retrieve the invoke URL (main domain) from the alliance partner table.
* If the alliance partner key is missing, default to 'egalvanic' for the integration.

h2. Other information

column41:
User want to view and manage child assets inside an enclosure, So that can structure OCP devices under their parent enclosure.

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* -*Child Nodes Section Visibility*-

* The Child Nodes section must be displayed only when:
** The current node has no parent ({{parent_id == nil}}), and
** The node’s class has {{box == true}}.

* - *Child Node Listing Logic*-

* Only direct children of the parent node should be listed.
* Each child node must show completion percentage and be selectable for:
** Viewing details
** Unlinking
** Deleting

* -*Create Child Node – Logical Rules*-

* Asset Class list must include only classes where:
** {{is_deleted == false}}
** {{ocp == true}}
* On child creation:
** Set {{parent_id = parent.id}}
** Set position defaults: {{x = 0}}, {{y = 0}}
** Inherit {{location}} and {{room}} from parent
** Set width and height from the selected node class
* If the parent node type is not already {{"group"}}, update it to {{"group"}}.
* If an active IR session exists, automatically link the newly created child node to the session.

* -*Link Existing Node – Logical Rules*-

* Eligible nodes for linking must satisfy:
** {{is_deleted == false}}
** {{parent_id == nil}} (not linked elsewhere)
** Their ID must not match the parent ID (cannot link a node to itself)
** Their node class must have {{ocp == true}}
* When linking:
** Set {{child.parent_id = parent.id}}
** Reset child position: {{x = 0}}, {{y = 0}}
** Mark child as {{needsSync = true}}
* If the parent node is not {{"group"}}, update it to {{"group"}}.

* -*Unlink Child Node – Logical Rules*-

* On unlink:
** Clear {{parent_id}} (set to {{nil}})
** Move the node visually outside the parent boundary by setting:
*** {{x = parent.x + parent.width + 50}}
*** {{y = parent.y}}
** Mark the node as {{needsSync = true}}.

* -*Delete Node – Logical Rules*-

* When deleting a node:
** Perform a cascade delete:
*** Recursively identify all child nodes under the node.
*** Soft-delete all nodes in the hierarchy ({{is_deleted = true}}).

*Connections Acceptance Criteria*

As a field engineer want to view and manage power connections (edges) for a selected node which is having ability to add OCP and have box is true.

So that he can correctly configure how power flows into and out of the asset.

* View Connections
** Show Connections section in EditNodeDetailView
**  Show badge count for total relevant connections
**  Display connections sorted: Lineside → Loadside → Name
**  Lineside connections show blue icon + correct Source → Target
**  Loadside connections show orange icon + correct Source → Target
**  Internal edges (if any) show gray icon
**  Incomplete edges show warning indicator
* Create Lineside Connection
**  Via “+” menu, user can select New Lineside Connection
**  Target must be pre-selected to the context node or its children
**  Source must be selectable from all nodes
**  Inline dropdown picker with expand + search
**  Validation: Source ≠ Target
**  On creation → edge appears in the list immediately
* Create Loadside Connection
**  Via “+” menu, user can select New Loadside Connection
**  Source must be pre-selected to the context node or its children
**  Target must be selectable from all nodes
**  Validation: Source ≠ Target
**  New connection appears in list under Loadside
* Delete Connection
**  Swipe left shows Delete action
**  Confirmation dialog appears before final removal
**  On confirmation, connection removed from section
* Edge Filtering / Relevance
**  Only edges where source or target is context node or its children are shown
**  Edges marked as deleted must NOT be shown

* Check all above cases in site visits node 

*SLD Changes Acceptance Criteria*

* Sub component should not be drag outside its parent node
* Once its removed/linked from parent can be dragged outside
* Show edge details and connection class details on edge line based on toggle button in box

column1: Migrate RND Customers to egalvanic.ai
column41:
h2. Summary

This task covers the migration of RND customers and their associated data and assets to the eg-ai platform. The process ensures a seamless transition with minimal disruption to users. The new iOS and Android apps, must be live by Tuesday, 2025-11-25.

h2. Description

The migration involves several key steps:

# *S3 Image Incremental Migration*
#* Incrementally transfer all customer images stored in the RND AWS S3 buckets to the new S3 buckets under the [+egalvanic.ai+|http://egalvanic.ai/] AWS account. This approach minimizes downtime and ensures that new or updated images are not missed during the migration window.
# *Data Migration from RND to* [+*egalvanic.ai*+|http://egalvanic.ai/]
#* Migrate all relevant customer data, including user profiles, records, and metadata, from the RND environment to the [+egalvanic.ai+|http://egalvanic.ai/] platform. This step should include data validation and integrity checks to ensure completeness and accuracy.
# *Image URL Path Update for RND Instance*
#* Update all image URL references in the database and application configuration to point to the new S3 bucket paths under [+egalvanic.ai+|http://egalvanic.ai/]. This ensures that all applications and users access images from the correct location after migration.
# *Routing from RND AWS Account to* [+*egalvanic.ai*+|http://egalvanic.ai/] *AWS Account*
#* Reconfigure routing rules and permissions to allow seamless access and integration between the old RND AWS account and the new [+egalvanic.ai+|http://egalvanic.ai/] AWS account. This may involve updating IAM roles, VPC peering, or other network configurations as needed.
# *API Routing for Old App API URL to New API URL*
#* Review and update API endpoints so that requests to the old application API URLs are correctly routed to the new API endpoints on [+egalvanic.ai+|http://egalvanic.ai/].
# *Go-Live Requirement*
#* The new iOS with new bundle id and Android and Web apps, must be live and available to users by Tuesday, 2025-11-25.

h2. Acceptance Criteria

* All images and data are successfully migrated and accessible from the new platform.
* Image URLs and API endpoints are updated and verified.
* No data loss or service disruption occurs during migration.
* All routing and permissions are correctly configured.
* New iOS and Android apps should be live with new bundle IDs by the deadline.

h2. Additional Notes

* Perform thorough testing after each migration step.
* After migration customer will use new iOS and Android app and current RND instance will be stopped.
* Customer will sync all offline data using RND app before data migration take place.

column1: FE |Add captions to photos
column41:
Enable users to add captions to photos across the platform.

* Add a new string field photos.caption to the database.
* Allow users on both web and mobile to add or edit a caption, provide 3dot option and provide option to add/edit caption.
* It should also be done through photos section and edit asset both.
* This functionality should be available for all photos, regardless of where they are used (issues, tasks, nodes, etc.).

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -Database schema is updated to include a string field photos.caption for all photo records.-
* -On web, users can add or edit a caption by clicking on 3dot.-
* -The caption field is available and editable for all photos, regardless of context (issues, tasks, nodes, etc.).-
* -Captions are saved and displayed correctly wherever photos appear in the platform.-
* -If a photo has a caption, it is visible to users in the photo viewer or relevant UI.-
* If a photo does not have a caption, users are prompted to add one via long press.
* -Changes to captions are persisted and reflected across all devices and platforms.-

FE: Multiday support
column41:
h3. Summary

The task is about implementing multiday support for work blocks. Currently, the system only asks for a single day’s details.

h3. Context

The current functionality requires users to input:

* The day
* The number of hours
* The start time

With multiday support, additional details will be needed for consecutive days of work.

h3. Acceptance criteria

* Ask for the following when adding a work block:
** Start day
** Number of hours
** Start time
** Number of consecutive days for the same work block
* Default settings should be:
** Start time: 7:00 AM
** Work length: 9.5 hours
